## **14.10.3 Use of DOS File Paths**

The following fields allow the use of a DOS file path in place of the (preferred) IRI syntax:

- INCLUDEPICTURE (Part 1, §17.16.5.27)

- INCLUDETEXT (Part 1, §17.16.5.28)

When a DOS file path is specified in a _field-argument_ , each backslash character shall be preceded directly by another backslash character [ _Example_ : E:\\example.docx _end example_ ] If _field-argument_ contains white space, it shall be enclosed in double quotes.

## **14.10.4 Field definitions**

## 14.10.4.1 AUTONUM

**Syntax** :

`AUTONUM` [ _switches_ ]

**Description:** In paragraphs formatted with one of the nine built-in heading styles, paragraph numbering restarts at 1 in each successive heading level. If headings that contain `AUTONUM` fields are followed by body text paragraphs that also contain `AUTONUM` fields, the paragraph numbering of the body text is restarted at 1 after each heading. If the headings don't contain `AUTONUM` fields, body text paragraphs that contain `AUTONUM` fields are numbered in a continuous, sequential series throughout the document. [ _Note_ : This field is supported for legacy reasons, It is recommended that `LISTNUM` (Part 1, §17.16.5.33) be used instead. _end note_ ]

The XML generated for a complex field implementation shall not have the optional field value stored.

**Field Value** : A new paragraph number in ascending sequential order.

**Switches** : Zero or one of the _general-formatting-switch_ es, or zero or more of the following _field-specificswitch_ es.

`\s` _field-argument text_ in this switch's _field-argument_ specifies the separator character to be used. If `\s` is omitted, a period (.) is used.

[ _Example_ : When the following fields are updated:

```
AUTONUM
AUTONUM \* Arabic \s :
AUTONUM \* alphabetic \s " "xxx
AUTONUM \* ROMAN
AUTONUM \* OrdText
```

The results are:

```
1.
2:
c xxx
IV.
fifth.
```

_end example_ ]

14.10.4.2 AUTONUMLGL

**Syntax** :

`AUTONUMLGL` [ _switches_ ]

**Description:** For legal and technical publications, use the nine built-in heading styles to format headings in the document, and then insert an `AUTONUMLGL` field at the beginning of each heading paragraph. The numbers reflect the heading levels that correspond to the heading styles. If an `AUTONUMLGL` field is inserted in paragraphs of body text paragraphs not formatted with built-in heading styles, the number of the preceding heading is included in the paragraph number. [ _Note_ : This field is supported for legacy reasons, It is recommended that `LISTNUM` (Part 1, §17.16.5.33) be used instead. _end note_ ]

This field only makes sense in terms of multi-level headings. Given the following headings:

Heading 1 Heading 2 Heading 2 Heading 1

this field allows

1. Heading 1

1.1. Heading 2

1.2. Heading 2

2. Heading 1

At each level, the numbering sequence does two things—it increments specific to that level, and it includes the value from the previous level.

The XML generated for a complex field implementation shall not have the optional field value stored.

**Field Value** : A new paragraph number in ascending sequential order.

**Switches** : Zero or one of the _general-formatting-switch_ es, or zero or more of the following _field-specificswitch_ es.

|`\e`|Removes the trailing separator (period).|
|---|---|
|`\s`_field-argument_|_text_in this switch's_field-argument_specifies the separator character to be<br>used. If`\s`is omitted, a period (.) is used.|

[ _Example_ : When the following fields are updated:

```
AUTONUMLGL
AUTONUMLGL \* Arabic \s :
AUTONUMLGL \* alphabetic \s " "xxx
AUTONUMLGL \* ROMAN
AUTONUMLGL \e xxx
```

The results are:

```
1.
2:
c xxx
IV.
5xxx
```

_end example_ ]

14.10.4.3 AUTONUMOUT

**Syntax** :

`AUTONUMOUT`

**Description:** Use the nine built-in heading styles to format headings in the document, and then insert an `AUTONUMOUT` field at the beginning of each heading paragraph. The numbers reflect the heading levels that

correspond to the heading styles. [ _Note_ : This field is supported for legacy reasons, It is recommended that `LISTNUM` (Part 1, §17.16.5.33) be used instead. _end note_ ]

The XML generated for a complex field implementation shall not have the optional field value stored.

This field allows the numbering to be incremented based on the heading level. Given the following:

`{AutoNumOut}` Heading 1 `{AutoNumOut}` Heading 2 `{AutoNumOut}` Heading 2 `{AutoNumOut}` Heading 1

results in

I. Heading 1

A. Heading 2

B. Heading 2

II. Heading 1

**Field Value** : A paragraph number.

**Switches** : None.

[ _Example_ : When the following fields are updated:

```
AUTONUMOUT
AUTONUMOUT
```

The results are:

```
1.
```

```
2.
```

_end example_ ]

## 14.10.4.4 BARCODE

**Syntax** :

`BARCODE` _field-argument_ [ _switches_ ]

**Description:** Produces a postal bar code in a machine-readable form of address used by the U.S. Postal Service. The barcode is in the form of either a POSTNET delivery-point bar code or a Facing Identification Mark (FIM). _text_ in _field-argument_ can be either a postal address or a bookmark name. In the case of a postal address, all that is needed is a 5-digit or 9-digit ZIP code; the rest of the address is superfluous.

**Field Value** : A postal bar code.

**Switches** : Zero or more of the following _field-specific-switch_ es.

|`\b`|Indicates that_text_in_field-argument_is the name of a bookmark.|
|---|---|
|`\f`_field-argument_|Inserts a Facing Identification Mark (FIM)._text_in this switch's_field-argument_<br>shall be either "A" (courtesy reply mark) or "C" (business reply mark).|
|`\u`|Indicates that_text_in_field-argument_is a U.S. postal address.|

[ _Example_ : Consider the case in which `PostalAddress` is the name of a bookmark for the text "2051 Swans Neck Way, Reston VA 20191". When the following fields are updated:

```
BARCODE 20191
BARCODE 20191 \u
BARCODE 20191-4023 \u
BARCODE "2051 Swans Neck Way, Reston VA 20191" \u
BARCODE "2051 Swans Neck Way, Reston VA 20191" \f A
BARCODE 20191 \f C
BARCODE PostalAddress \b \f A
```

The results are:

_end example_ ]

## 14.10.4.5 BIDIOUTLINE

**Syntax** :

`BIDIOUTLINE`

**Description:** This field is identical to the `AUTONUMLGL` field (§14.10.4.3), except for the separator that delimits each level of the paragraph numbering (this field uses a hyphen-minus (U+002D) instead of a full stop (U+002E) character as the default separator character).

**Field Value** : A new paragraph number in ascending sequential order, as defined by the description in §14.10.4.3.

**Switches** : None.

## 14.10.4.6 EQ

**Syntax** :

`EQ` [ _switches_ ] ( _eq-argument-list_ ) [ _switches_ ]

_eq-argument-list_ is a list of arguments separated using a separator character. For implementations using a period (.) as the radix point, the separator character is a comma (,). For implementations using a comma (,) as the radix point, the separator character is a semicolon (;).

**Description:** Computes the specified mathematical equation.

**Field Value** : The result of the specified mathematical equation. [ _Note_ : The result of an `EQ` field can be used as an argument in another `EQ` field's _eq-argument-list_ . _end note_ ]

**Switches** : The left-hand _switch_ es can only be one of the following: `\a` , `\b` , `\d` , `\f` , `\i` , `\l` , `\o` , `\r` , `\s` , and `\x` . Each of these switches has one or more subswitches, as shown below.

`\a` produces an array using the argument values in _eq-argument-list_ (which are in row-major order) and the _field-specific-switch_ es below:

|`\ac`|Alignment is centered in each array column.|
|---|---|
|`\al`|Alignment is left in each array column.|
|`\ar`|Alignment is right in each array column.|
|`\co`_field-argument_|The number of columns in the array is specified by_text_in this switch's<br>_field-argument_. In the absence of this switch, the number is 1.|
|`\hs`_field-argument_|Adds the integral number of points of horizontal spacing specified by<br>_text_in this switch's_field-argument_between columns.|
|`\vs`_field-argument_|Adds the integral number of points of vertical spacing specified by_text_in<br>this switch's_field-argument_between lines.|

`\b` brackets the single element in _eq-argument-list_ in a size appropriate for that element. The default form of brackets is parentheses. The _field-specific-switch_ es below can be used:

|`\bc \`_char_|Uses the character designated by_char_as both the left and right bracket<br>character. However, if_char_is`{`,`[`,`(`, or`<`, that character is used for the<br>left bracket, and`}`,`]`,`)`, or`>`, respectively, is used for right bracket.|
|---|---|
|`\lc \`_char_|Uses the character designated by_char_as the left bracket character.|
|`\rc \`_char_|Uses the character designated by_char_as the right bracket character.|

`\d` Controls where the next character following the `EQ` field is drawn (that is, the displacement). _eq-argumentlist_ shall have no arguments. The _field-specific-switch_ es below can be used:

|`\ba`_field-argument_|Draws to the left (backward) the integral number of points specified by<br>_text_in this switch's_field-argument_.|
|---|---|
|`\fo`_field-argument_|Draws to the right (forward) the integral number of points specified by<br>_text_in this switch's_field-argument_.|
|`\li`|Underlines the space up to the next character.|

`\f` Creates a fraction with the first argument as numerator and the second argument as denominator, centered above and below the division line, respectively. _eq-argument-list_ shall have exactly two arguments. There are no _field-specific-switch_ es for this switch.

`\i` Creates an integral using the specified or default symbol and three elements. The first argument is the lower limit, the second is the upper limit, and the third is the integrand. _eq-argument-list_ shall have exactly three arguments. The _field-specific-switch_ es below can be used:

|`\fc \`_char_|Uses the character designated by_char_as the fixed-height character for<br>the symbol.|
|---|---|
|`\in`|Uses an inline format with the limits displayed to the right of the symbol<br>instead of above and below it.|
|`\pr`|Uses the symbol Capital pi and creates a product.|
|`\su`|Uses the symbol Capital sigma and creates a summation.|
|`\vc \`_char_|Uses the character designated by_char_as the variable-height character<br>for the symbol. The symbol matches the height of the third argument.|

`\lf` Creates a list from an arbitrary number of arguments. There are no _field-specific-switch_ es for this switch.

`\o` Using an arbitrary number of arguments, displays each successive argument on top of the previous one. Each character is displayed within an invisible character box, with the switches being available to align the boxes on top of one another. The _field-specific-switch_ es below can be used:

|`\ac`|Alignment character box center (the default).|
|---|---|
|`\al`|Alignment character box left.|
|`\ar`|Alignment character box right.|

`\r` Creates a radical. _eq-argument-list_ shall have either one or two arguments. If it has one argument, the result is the square root of that argument. If it has two arguments, the result is the _n_ th root of the second argument, where _n_ is the first argument. There are no _field-specific-switch_ es for this switch.

`\s` Creates a subscript or superscript. One or more arguments are permitted. If more than one element is specified, the elements are stacked and left-aligned. The _field-specific-switch_ es below can be used:

|`\ai`_field-argument_|Adds space above a line in a paragraph by the integral number of points<br>specified by_text_in this switch's_field-argument_. The default is 2 points.|
|---|---|
|`\di`_field-argument_|Adds space below a line in a paragraph by the integral number of points<br>specified by_text_in this switch's_field-argument_.|
|`\do`_field-argument_|Moves a single argument below the adjacent text by the integral<br>number of points specified by_text_in this switch's_field-argument_. The<br>default is 2 points.|
|`\up`_field-argument_|Moves a single argument above the adjacent text by the integral<br>number of points specified by_text_in this switch's_field-argument_.|

`\x` Creates one or more border segments around a single argument. By default, all four borders are added. _eqargument-list_ shall have no arguments. The _field-specific-switch_ es below can be used:

|`\bo`|Draws a horizontal border below the argument.|
|---|---|
|`\le`|Draws a vertical border to the left of the argument.|
|`\ri`|Draws a vertical border to the right of the argument.|
|`\to`|Draws a horizontal border above the argument.|

[ _Example_ : When the following fields are updated:

EQ \a \co 2 \ac \hs 10 ( 1000, 20, A, Sunday ) EQ \b \bc \| ( -100 )  EQ \b \bc \| ( \r(3, a + b) xx EQ \d \fo 20 () xx EQ \d \fo 30 \li ()xx EQ \f ( 1, 32 )  EQ \f ( 7, 64 ) EQ \i ( 0, ∞, x )  EQ \i \su \in ( 0, 10, x )  EQ \i \pr \in ( 0, 5, x ) EQ \i \fc \{ ( 0, 5, \f (x, 0.34) )  EQ \i \vc \{ ( 0, 5, \f (x, 0.34) )

EQ \l ( 0, 10 ) EQ \b \lc \[ \rc \) (\l (0, 10))

EQ \o ( 0, 0, 0 )   EQ \o ( 0, + )  EQ \o \ar ( 0, |, _ ) EQ \r ( 2 )   EQ \r ( 2, x ) a EQ \s \up (2) + b EQ \s \up (2) a EQ \x ( + ) b  a EQ \x \to \le ( + ) b  a EQ \x \bo \ri ( + ) b

The results are:

1000 20 A Sunday  3  | -100    |  a + b    xx xx xx 1[ 7] 32 64 ∞ [] x     10 x     0  0 5 x 0 5 5 {[x] [x] 0.34  0.34 0 0

0, 10 [0, 10)

0     0 **0** +     0| _ 2 2 x a[2] + b[2] a + b  a + b  a + b

_end example_ ]

## 14.10.4.7 INFO

**Syntax** :

`INFO info-category` [ _field-argument_ ] [ _switches_ ]

This field is documented for purposes of backwards compatibility. Each permitted value for _info-category_ is also permitted as a _field-type_ .  Instances of the INFO field shall be treated as an instance of the _field-type_ with the same value as _info-category_ ; that is, as if the INFO token was not present.

## 14.10.4.8 QUOTE

This field retrieves the text specified by _text_ in _field-argument_ . In strict conformance mode, this text may include any other fields except SYMBOL. However, in transitional conformance mode, this text may include any other fields except AUTONUM, AUTONUMLGL, AUTONUMOUT, and SYMBOL.

## **14.10.5 fldData (Custom Field Data)**

This element specifies custom field data which shall be associated with the parent field. No information or semantics are applied to the contents of this data by ECMA-376, and therefore this field can be used as desired to store additional application-defined data with the field. However, applications should not lose the contents of this custom data if they do not understand or utilize it (i.e. the information should continue to be saved with the file).

If this element is omitted, then no custom field data is stored with the parent field. If the type attribute of the current field character is not `start` , then his setting can be ignored.

[ _Example_ : Consider the following WordprocessingML fragment for a complex field:

```
<w:r>
```

```
  <w:fldChar w:fldCharType="start">
    <w:fldData xml:space="preserve">///3645ERKJHE</w:fldData>
```

```
  </w:fldChar>
</w:r>
```

```
<w:r>
  <w:instrText>PRIVATE</w:instrText>
</w:r>
<w:r>
  <w:fldChar w:fldCharType="separate" />
</w:r>
…
```

The fldData element contains custom data stored with this `PRIVATE` field (Part 1, §17.16.5.48), the contents of which are determined by a hosting application. _end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|xml:space(Content<br>Contains Significant<br>Whitespace)<br>Namespace:<br>http://www.w3.or|Specifies how white space should be handled for the contents of this element using the<br>W3C space preservation rules.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following run contained within a WordprocessingML document:<br>`<w:r>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|g/XML/1998/nam<br>espace|`<w:t>   significant whitespace   </w:t>`<br>`</w:r>`<br>Although there are three spaces on each side of the text content in the run, that<br>whitespace has not been specifically marked as significant, therefore it is subject to the<br>space preservation rules currently specified in that run's scope._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by §2.10 of the XML 1.0 specification.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Text) is located in §A.2. _end note_ ]

## **14.10.6 fldData (Custom Field Data)**

This element specifies custom field data which shall be associated with the parent field. No information or semantics are applied to the contents of this data by ECMA-376, and therefore this field can be used as desired to store additional application-defined data with the field. However, applications should not lose the contents of this custom data if they do not understand or utilize it (i.e. the information should continue to be saved with the file).

If this element is omitted, then no custom field data is stored with the parent field.

[ _Example_ : Consider the following WordprocessingML fragment for a simple field:

- `<w:fldSimple w:instr="PRIVATE">`

- `<w:fldData xml:space="preserve">///3645ERKJHE</w:fldData> </w:fldSimple>`

The fldData element contains custom data stored with this `PRIVATE` field (Part 1, §17.16.5.48), the contents of which are determined by a hosting application. _end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|xml:space(Content<br>Contains Significant<br>Whitespace)<br>Namespace:<br>http://www.w3.or<br>g/XML/1998/nam<br>espace|Specifies how white space should be handled for the contents of this element using the<br>W3C space preservation rules.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following run contained within a WordprocessingML document:<br>`<w:r>`<br>`<w:t>   significant whitespace   </w:t>`<br>`</w:r>`<br>Although there are three spaces on each side of the text content in the run, that<br>whitespace has not been specifically marked as significant, therefore it is subject to the<br>space preservation rules currently specified in that run's scope._end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by §2.10 of the XML 1.0 specification.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Text) is located in §A.2. _end note_ ]

## **14.10.7 hyperlink (Hyperlink) (Part 1, §17.16.22)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Hyperlink<br>Target)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|The same as the id attribute in Part 1, §17.16.22.|

## **14.11 Simple Types**

The following additional simple type information in the

http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/wordprocessingml/2006/main namespace is used for documents of a transitional conformance class.

## **14.11.1 Additional member types for the union in ST_DecimalNumberOrPercent (Part 1, §17.18.11)**

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

 The ST_UnqualifiedPercentage simple type (§14.11.10).

## **14.11.2 Additional enumeration values for ST_Jc (Part 1, §17.18.44)**

The following addtional enumeration values can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|left(Align to Leading Edge)|Semantically equivalent tostart.|
|right(Align to Trailing Edge)|Semantically equivalent toend.|

## **14.11.3 Additional enumeration values for ST_JcTable (Part 1, §17.18.45)**

The following addtional enumeration values can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|left(Align to Starting Edge)|Specifies that the table shall be aligned to the leading<br>edge of the text flow – the left text margin (for a left-|

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||to-right table); or the right text margin (for a right-to-<br>left table) in the document. (See Part 1, §17.4.1)|
|right(Align to Trailing Edge)|Specifies that the table shall be aligned to the trailing<br>edge of the text flow – the right text margin (for a left-<br>to-right table); or the left text margin (for a right-to-<br>left table) in the document. (See Part 1, §17.4.1)|

## **14.11.4 Additional enumeration values for ST_NumberFormat (Part 1, §17.18.59)**

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|decimalFullWidth2(Full Width Arabic Numerals Alternate)|Specifies that the sequence shall consist of a set of full-<br>width Arabic numbering.<br>To determine the text that is displayed for any value, this<br>sequence specifies a set of characters that represent<br>positions 1–9 and then those same characters are combined<br>with each other and`０`(represents the number zero) to<br>construct the remaining values.<br>The set of characters used by this numbering format for<br>values 0–9 is U+FF10–U+FF19, respectively.<br>For values greater than the size of the set, the number is<br>constructed by following these steps:<br>**1) **Divide the value by 10 and write the symbol which<br>represents the remainder.<br>**2) **Divide the quotient of the previous division by 10<br>and write the symbol, which represents the<br>remainder, to the left of the existing position.<br>**3) **Repeat step 2 until the remaining value is equal to<br>zero.<br>[_Example_: The numbering for the items should be<br>represented by the following pattern:`１`,`２`,`３`, …,`８`,`９`,<br>`１０`,`１１`,`１２`, …,`１８`,`１９`,`２０`,`２１`, …_end_<br>_example_]|

## **14.11.5 Additional enumeration values for ST_StyleSort (Part 1, §17.18.82)**

The following addtional enumeration values can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|0000(Sort by Style Name)|Specifies that styles which are visible should be sorted<br>by their names.|
|0001(Sort by Style Priority)|Specifies that styles which are visible should be sorted|

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||by their UI priority using theuiPriorityelement (Part<br>1, §**17.7.4.19**).|
|0002(Sort by Default Method)|Specifies that styles which are visible should be sorted<br>by the default sorting of the host application.|
|0003(Sort by Font)|Specifies that styles which are visible should be sorted<br>by the font which they apply.|
|0004(Sort by Based On Style)|Specifies that styles which are visible should be sorted<br>by the style on which they are based using the<br>basedOnelement (Part 1, §**17.7.4.3**).|
|0005(Sort by Style Type)|Specifies that styles which are visible should be sorted<br>by their style types (i.e. character, linked, paragraph).|

## **14.11.6 Additional enumeration values for ST_TabJc (Part 1, §17.18.84)**

The following addtional enumeration values can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|left(Leading Tab)|Semantically equivalent tostart.|
|right(Trailing Tab)|Semantically equivalent toend.|

## **14.11.7 Additional enumeration values for ST_TextDirection (Part 1, §17.18.93)**

The following addtional enumeration values can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|btLr(Lines Flow From Left to Right)|Semantically equivalent tolr.|
|lrTb(Lines Flow From Top To Bottom)|Semantically equivalent totb.|
|lrTbV(Lines Flow From Top to Bottom, Rotated)|Semantically equivalent totbV.|
|tbLrV(Lines Flow From Left to Right, Rotated)|Semantically equivalent tolrV.|
|tbRl(Lines Flow From Right to Left)|Semantically equivalent torl.|
|tbRlV(Lines Flow From Right to Left, Rotated)|Semantically equivalent torlV.|

**14.11.8 Additional member types for the union in ST_TextScale (Part 1, §17.18.95)** The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

The ST_TextScaleDecimal simple type (§14.11.11).

## **14.11.9 ST_Cnf (Conditional Formatting Bitmask)**

This simple type specifies the format for the set of conditional formatting properties that have been applied to this object.

These properties are expressed using a string serialization of a binary bitmask for each of the following properties (reading from the first character position right):

- First Row - Is this the first row of the table?

- Last Row - Is this the last row of the table?

- First Column - Does this belong to the first column of the table?

- Last Column - Does this belong to the last column of the table?

- Band 1 Vertical - Does this belong to a column which should receive band 1 formatting? This property specifies whether the cell should receive the formatting specified for odd-numbered columns (e.g. 1,3,5,…)

- Band 2 Vertical - Does this belong to a column which should receive band 2 formatting? This property specifies whether the cell should receive the formatting specified for even-numbered columns (e.g. 2,4,6…)

- Band 1 Horizontal - Does this receive band 1 formatting? This property specifies whether the cell should receive the formatting specified for odd-numbered rows (e.g. 1,3,5,…)

- Band 2 Horizontal - Does this receive band 2 formatting? This property specifies whether the cell should receive the formatting specified for even-numbered rows (e.g. 2,4,6…)

- NE Cell - Is this part of the top-right corner of the table?

- NW Cell - Is this part of the top-left corner of the table?

- SE Cell - Is this part of the bottom-right corner of the table?

- SW Cell -  Is this part of the bottom-left corner of the table?

For each of these properties, a value of `1` in the specified character position in the string means that the value is true, a value of `0` means false. All values shall be specified.

[ _Example_ : Consider a paragraph in the top right corner of a table with a table style applied. This paragraph would need to specify the following WordprocessingML:

```
<w:p>
```

```
  <w:pPr>
```

```
    <w:cnfStyle w:val="101000000100" />
```

```
    …
```

```
  </w:pPr>
```

```
  …
```

```
</w:p>
```

This paragraph specifies that it has the conditional properties from the table style for the first column, first row, and the NW corner of the parent table by setting the appropriate bits in the val attribute. _end example_ ]

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

- This simple type's contents have a length of exactly 12 characters.

- This simple type's contents shall match the following regular expression pattern: [01]*

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_Cnf) is located in §A.2. _end note_ ]

## **14.11.10 ST_UnqualifiedPercentage (Percentage Value Without Percent Sign)**

This simple type specifies additional formats for percentage-based values which can only be used within the transitional conformance class.

Specifically, this value allows percentage-based values to be specified as follows:

- For the w attribute in CT_TblWidth (Part 1, §17.4.88), the value is stored in 50ths of a percent.

- For all other uses, the value is stored in whole percentage points.

[ _Example_ : Consider the following WordprocessingML fragment:

```
<w:tblW w:w="1000" w:type="pct" />
```

The tblW element is based on the CT_TblWidth complex type, and the type attribute's value is `pct` , which means that this value is measured in 50ths of a percent (i.e. `1000` is equal to 20%). _end example_ ]

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema integer datatype.

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_UnqualifiedPercentage) is located in §A.2. _end note_ ]

## **14.11.11 ST_TextScaleDecimal (Text Expansion/Compression Percentage)**

This simple type specifies that the percentage by which the contents of a run shall be expanded or compressed with respect to its normal (100%) character width, with a minimum width of 1% and maximum width of 600%.

[ _Example_ : Consider a run of text which must be expanded to 300% when displaying each character within the contents of the run. This constraint is specified using the following WordprocessingML:

```
<w:rPr>
```

```
  <w:w w:val="300"/>
```

```
</w:rPr>
```

This run explicitly declares that the w value is `300` , so the contents of this run appear at 300% of their normal character width by expanding the width of each character. _end example_ ]

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema integer datatype.

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to 0 . This simple type has a maximum value of less than or equal to 600 .

## **14.11.12 Changed enumeration value for ST_BrType (Part 1, §17.18.4)**

The enumeration value row for this type is replaced by the following:

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|page(Page Break)|Specifies that the current break shall restart itself on<br>the next page of the document.<br>[_Note_: For information on the interaction of page<br>breaks in frames and the showBreaksInFrames<br>element, see §14.8.3.36._end note_]|

## **14.12.1 General**

The following attributes, which are defined in subclauses within Part 1, §17, “WordprocessingML”, have different source relationships when used in documents of the Transitional conformance class:

||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

This property bag is contained in a separate part within the Office<br>Open XML package.<br>The relationship explicitly targeted by this attribute shall be of type<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/control<br>or the document shall be considered non-conformant.<br>If this attribute is omitted, then the embedded control shall be given no property bag<br>when instantiated.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following WordprocessingML markup for an embedded control in<br>a document:<br>`<w:control r:id="rId5" w:name="CheckBox1"`<br>`w:shapeid="_x0000_s1027" />`<br>Theidattribute in the relationship reference namespace specifies that the relationship<br>with relationship ID`rId5`must contain the property data for this embedded control._end_<br>_example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bottomLeft<br>(Custom Defined<br>Bottom Left Border<br>Relationship<br>Reference)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship ID for the relationship that contains the custom bottom left<br>border image for the parent element. This custom border image is contained in a<br>separate part within the WordprocessingML package.<br>The relationship explicitly targeted by this attribute shall be of type<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/imageor<br>the document shall be considered non-conformant.<br>If this attribute is omitted, then no custom bottom left border shall be used.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following WordprocessingML markup for a custom bottom left<br>border in a document:<br>`<w:bottom w:val="custom"`<br>`r:bottomLeft="rIdCustomBottomLeftBorder" …/>`<br>Theidattribute in the relationship reference namespace specifies that the relationship<br>with relationship ID`rIdCustomBottomLeftBorder`must contain the custom bottom left<br>border image for the document._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|
|bottomRight<br>(Custom Defined|Specifies the relationship ID for the relationship that contains the custom bottom right<br>border image for the parent element. This custom border image is contained in a|

This custom border image is contained in a separate part within<br>the WordprocessingML package.<br>The relationship explicitly targeted by this attribute shall be of type<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/imageor<br>the document shall be considered non-conformant.<br>If this attribute is omitted, then no custom border shall be used.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following WordprocessingML markup for a custom bottom<br>border in a document:<br>`<w:bottom w:val="custom" r:id="rIdCustomBottomBorder" …/>`<br>Theidattribute in the relationship reference namespace specifies that the relationship<br>with relationship ID`rIdCustomBottomBorder`must contain the custom bottom border<br>image for the document._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **14.12.23 Changed attribute for saveThroughXslt element (Part 1, §17.15.1.76)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(XSL<br>Transformation<br>Location)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies an explicit relationship to the location of the XSL Transformation which shall be<br>applied.<br>The relationship targeted by this element shall be of type<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/transform<br>, or this document shall be declared non-conformant.<br>[_Example_: Consider a XML document that must have the XSL transform located at<br>c`:\Example Transform.xslt`applied when the document is saved as a single XML file.<br>This requirement would be specified using the following WordprocessingML in the<br>document settings:<br>`<w:saveThroughXslt r:id="rId5" />`<br>ThesaveThroughXsltelement specifies that the relationship located at`rId5`must be<br>used when saving as a single XML file in this case, that relationship must target<br>c`:\Example Transform.xslt`_. end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship to<br>Part)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship ID to a specified part.<br>The specified relationship shall match the relationship type required by the parent<br>element:<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/customXmlfor the<br>contentPartelement<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/footer|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||for thefooterReferenceelement<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/heade<br>rfor theheaderReferenceelement<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/font<br>for theembedBold,embedBoldItalic,embedItalic, orembedRegularelements<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/printe<br>rSettingsfor theprinterSettingselement<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/hyper<br>linkfor thelongDescorhyperlinkelement<br>[_Example_: Consider an XML element which has the followingidattribute:<br>`<… r:id="rId1" />`<br>The markup specifies the associated relationship part with relationship ID`rId1`contains<br>the corresponding relationship information for the parent XML element._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.3 Workbook**

## **15.3.1 Additional attribute for fileSharing element (Part 1, §18.2.12)**

The following additional attributes can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class:

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|reservationPasswo<br>rd(Write<br>Reservation<br>Password)|Specifies the legacy hash of the password required for editing this workbook.<br>`The hash is generated using the logic defined in the`<br>revisionsPassword`attribute of the`wookbookProtection`element (Part`<br>`1, §18.2.29).`<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_UnsignedShortHexsimple<br>type (§15.8.2).|

## **15.3.2 Additional attribute for webPublishing element (Part 1, §18.2.24)**

The following additional attributes can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class:

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|codePage(Code<br>Page)|This attribute is used only for compatibility with the existing corpus of binary documents,<br>and is ignored if thecharacterSetattribute is present. Specifies the encoding the<br>application uses when a Web page is saved. A code page is a table that relates the binary<br>character codes used by a program to keys on the keyboard or to the appearance of<br>characters on the display. Code pages are a means of providing support for the languages<br>used in different countries.<br>[_Note_: There are a number of code page technologies. One example of potential values<br>can be found at:http://www.unicode.org/Public/MAPPINGS/ _end note_]<br>The default value for this attribute is the workbook's encoding.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML SchemaunsignedInt<br>datatype.|

## **15.3.3 Additional attributes for workbookProtection element (Part 1, §18.2.29)**

The following additional attributes can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class:

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|revisionsPassword<br>(Legacy Revisions<br>Password)|Specifies the legacy hash of the password required for unlocking revisions in this<br>workbook. The hash is generated from an 8-bit wide character. The input string shall be<br>in UTF-16LE format (if there is a leading BOM character (U+FEFF) in the encoded<br>password it is removed before hash calculation), and these 16-bit Unicode characters<br>shall be converted down to 8 bits before the hash is computed, using the following logic:<br>[_Note_: This legacy conversion attempts to fit UTF-16 encoded characters into a single-<br>byte character set. As such, if the input string uses characters from multiple character<br>sets, many characters are unmapped in the destination character set and take on the<br>default value, 0x3F. For this reason, it is recommended that applications choose a<br>character set which maps the maximum number of characters from the input string and<br>explicitly declare the character set used in therevisionsCharacterSetattribute. Not<br>doing so will inhibit interoperability._end note_]<br>For SpreadsheetML password hash purposes, Unicode UTF-16 input code points are<br>converted to a single or double byte character set.<br>Code points with no representation in the target character set are replaced with Unicode<br>character 0x3f (?).<br>The values permitted by this attribute are names and aliases listed in the IANA character<br>set listing found at http://www.iana.org/assignments/character-sets.<br>For single byte character sets, each Unicode code point is replaced by a single byte or<br>0x3f if an appropriate character doesn’t exist in the character set.<br>For double byte character sets, each Unicode code point is replaced by either a single|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||byte, or a two byte sequence, depending on the input character, or 0x3f if an appropriate<br>character doesn’t exist in the character set.  In our tables the target is a single byte<br>sequence if the most significant byte is 0x00, otherwise it is a double byte sequence, with<br>the lead byte being the most significant byte.<br>To convert, first check if conversion is being done to a single or double byte code page<br>and load the appropriate WCTABLE code page table.<br>For each input character, look up the code point in the WCTABLE.  There are 3<br>possibilities: Not found, single byte, or double byte.<br><br>If the input character is not found, append 0x3f and continue to the next<br>character.<br><br>If the result is a single byte, check to make sure the entry in the MBTABLE<br>matches the input.  If it matches, append the single byte to the output.  If it does<br>not match, append 0x3f to the output.<br><br>If the result is a double byte,check to make sure the entry in the DBCSENTRY<br>table for the appropriate lead byte matches the input character.  If it matches,<br>append the lead byte and trail byte to the output.  If it does not match, append<br>0x3f to the output.<br>The following pseudocode describes how this conversion should be done:<br>`int WideCharToMultiByte(wchar_t* wszInput, byte* szOutput)`<br>`{`<br>`// Remember output start so we can return length`<br>`byte* szOutputStart = szOutput;`<br>`// Load Character Set Tables and determine`<br>`// double/single byte nature.`<br>`// This will depend on how the character sets are`<br>`represented on`<br>`// the target machine.  TABLECLASS represents some abstract`<br>`// representation of this structure here.`<br>`TABLECLASS pTables = LoadCharacterSetTables();`<br>`Bool bDoubleByte = IsCharacterSetDoubleByte();`<br>`while (*wszInput != 0)`<br>`{`<br>`if (bDoubleByte)`<br>`szOutput = AppendDoubleByte(pTables, *wszInput,`<br>`szOutput);`<br>`else`<br>`szOutput = AppendSingleByte(pTables, *wszInput,`<br>`szOutput);`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`// Read next input wchar_t`<br>`wszInput++;`<br>`}`<br>`// Null terminate the output`<br>`*szOutput = 0;`<br>`// Return output length`<br>`return szOutput – szOutputStart;`<br>`}`<br>`byte* AppendSingleByte(TABLECLASS pTables, wchar_t wcIn, byte*`<br>`szOutput)`<br>`{`<br>`// Look up byte that we want to append.`<br>`byte bOut = pTables->LookUpSingleByte(wcIn);`<br>`// Make sure that bOut matches the input, otherwise use ?`<br>`// (ie: no best fit behavior allowed)`<br>`if (wcIn != pTables->LookUpWideChar(bOut))`<br>`bOut = 0x3f;`<br>`*szOutput = bOut;`<br>`szOutput++;`<br>`return szOutput;`<br>`}`<br>`byte* AppendDoubleByte(TABLECLASS pTables, wchar_t wcIn, byte*`<br>`szOutput)`<br>`{`<br>`// Look up bytes that we want to append.`<br>`UINT16 bytesOut = pTables->LookUpDoubleByte(wcIn);`<br>`// See if it is a single or double byte sequence`<br>`if (bytesOut & 0xFF00)`<br>`{`<br>`// It is a double byte sequence`<br>`// Make sure that bytesOut matches the input, otherwise`<br>`use ?`<br>`// (ie: no best fit behavior allowed)`<br>`if (wcIn != pTables->LookUpWideChar(bytesOut))`<br>`{`<br>`// Use ?, it will be added below`<br>`bytesOut = 0x003f;`<br>`}`<br>`else`<br>`{`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`// It matched, use the lead byte we found`<br>`// trail byte will be added below`<br>`*szOutput = bytesOut >> 8;`<br>`szOutput++;`<br>`}`<br>`else`<br>`{`<br>`// It is a single byte sequence`<br>`// Make sure that bytesOut matches the input, otherwise`<br>`use ?`<br>`// (ie: no best fit behavior allowed)`<br>`if (wcIn != pTables->LookUpWideChar(bytesOut & 0xFF))`<br>`bytesOut = 0x003f;`<br>`}`<br>`// Add the single or trail byte`<br>`*szOutput = bytesOut & 0xFF;`<br>`szOutput++;`<br>`return szOutput;`<br>`}`<br>`class pTables`<br>`{`<br>`// Construction depends on how you choose to store & load`<br>`the`<br>`// table files`<br>`byte LookUpSingleByte(wchar_t wcIn)`<br>`{`<br>`// How you access the table depends on your storage`<br>`mechanism.`<br>`// Look up the line in WCTABLE where the first column`<br>`matches wcIn,`<br>`// and then return the byte value from the second`<br>`column.`<br>`if (exists WCTABLE{wcIn})`<br>`return WCTABLE{wcIn}.SecondColumn;`<br>`// If it doesn’t exist, return ?`<br>`return 0x3f;`<br>`}`<br>`UINT16 LookUpDoubleByte(wchar_t wcIn)`<br>`{`<br>`// How you access the table depends on your storage`<br>`mechanism.`<br>`// Look up the line in WCTABLE where the first column`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`matches wcIn,`<br>`// and then return the double byte value from the`<br>`second column.`<br>`if (exists WCTABLE{wcIn})`<br>`return WCTABLE{wcIn}.SecondColumn;`<br>`// If it doesn’t exist, return ?`<br>`return 0x003f;`<br>`}`<br>`// Overload that looks up wide chars from single byte code`<br>`points.`<br>`wchar_t LookUpWideChar(byte bIn)`<br>`{`<br>`// How you access the table depends on your storage`<br>`mechanism.`<br>`// Look up the line in MBTABLE where the first column`<br>`matches bIn,`<br>`// and then return the wchar_t value from the second`<br>`column.`<br>`if (exists MBTABLE{bIn})`<br>`return MBTABLE{bIn}.SecondColumn;`<br>`// If it doesn’t exist, return ?`<br>`return 0x003f;`<br>`}`<br>`// Overload that looks up wide chars from double byte code`<br>`points`<br>`wchar_t LookUpWideChar(UINT16 bytesIn)`<br>`{`<br>`// How you access the table depends on your storage`<br>`mechanism.`<br>`// First find the DBCSTABLE where the LeadByte matches`<br>`// the lead (most significant) input byte.`<br>`if (exists DBCSTABLE{bytesIn >> 8))`<br>`{`<br>`DbcsTable = DBCSTABLE{bytesIn >> 8);`<br>`// Look up the line in DbcsTable where the first`<br>`column`<br>`// matches the input trail (least significant)`<br>`byte,`<br>`// and then return the wchar_t value from the`<br>`second column.`<br>`if (exists DbcsTable{bytesIn & 0xFF})`<br>`return DbcsTable{bytesIn & 0xFF}.SecondColumn;`<br>`}`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`// Either the lead byte table or specific trail byte`<br>`// doesn’t exist in the table, return ?`<br>`return 0x003f;`<br>`}`<br>`}`<br>The resulting value is hashed using the low-order word algorithm defined in §14.8.1. This<br>step assumes that all words are unsigned, the word size is two bytes, and that bit-level<br>shift-left/shift-right operations shift in the direction of the highest-order and lowest-<br>order bit, respectively. [_Example_:`0x61 SHR 1`is`0xC2`, as`01100001`shifted one<br>position in the direction of its highest-order bit is`11000010`._end example_]<br>[_Example_: This algorithm can be represented by the following pseudocode:<br>`// Function Input:`<br>`//    szPassword: NULL terminated C-Style string`<br>`//    cchPassword: The number of characters in szPassword (not`<br>`including the NULL terminator)`<br>`unsigned_short GetPasswordHash(const char *szPassword, int`<br>`cchPassword) {`<br>`unsigned_short wPasswordHash;`<br>`const char *pch;`<br>`wPasswordHash = 0;`<br>`if (cchPassword > 0)`<br>`{`<br>`pch = &szPassword[cchPassword];`<br>`while (pch-- != szPassword)`<br>`{`<br>`wPasswordHash = ((wPasswordHash >> 14) &`<br>`0x01) | ((wPasswordHash << 1) & 0x7fff);`<br>`wPasswordHash ^= *pch;`<br>`}`<br>`wPasswordHash = ((wPasswordHash >> 14) & 0x01) |`<br>`((wPasswordHash << 1) & 0x7fff);`<br>`wPasswordHash ^= cchPassword;`<br>`wPasswordHash ^= (0x8000 | ('N' << 8) | 'K');`<br>`}`<br>`return(wPasswordHash);`<br>`}`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_UnsignedShortHexsimple<br>type (§15.8.2).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|revisionsPassword<br>CharacterSet<br>(Revisions Password<br>Character Set)|Name of the character set associated with the legacyrevisionsPasswordhash. The<br>values permitted by this attribute are names and aliases listed in the IANA CHARACTER<br>SETS listing found athttp://www.iana.org/assignments/character-sets.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|workbookPasswor<br>d(Legacy Workbook<br>Password)|Specifies the legacy hash of the password required for unlocking revisions in this<br>workbook.<br>The hash is generated using the logic defined in the precedingrevisionsPassword<br>attribute.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_UnsignedShortHexsimple<br>type (§15.8.2).|
|workbookPasswor<br>dCharacterSet<br>(Workbook<br>Password Character<br>Set)|Name of the character set associated with theworkbookPasswordhash. The values<br>permitted by this attribute are the names and aliases listed in the IANA CHARACTER SETS<br>listing found athttp://www.iana.org/assignments/character-sets.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

## **15.3.4 Modified content for Date Conversion for Serial Date-Times (Part 1, §18.17.4.1)**

When interpreting a document of a transitional conformance class, Part 1, §18.17.4.1 is replaced by the following text:

A _serial date-time_ is a number that represents a date and time.  This signed value is in units of days relative to the base date for the selected date system. Serial date-times increase by 1 into each successive day, and decrease by 1 into each preceding day. Fractional portions of serial date-times represent fractions of a single day. [ _Example_ : When using the 1900 date system, which has a base date of 30[th] December 1899, a serial datetime of 1.5 represents midday on the 31[st] December 1899 (serial date-time day 1); that is, 1899-12-31T12:00. A serial date-time of -4.25 represents 6 pm on the 25[th] December 1899; that is, 1899-12-25T18:00. _end example_ ] The base dates and the related serial date-times represent local date and time.

Two different bases are used for converting dates to and from serial date-times:

- In the _1900 date system_ , the lower limit is January 1, 1900, 00:00:00, which has a serial date-time of 1. The upper limit is December 31, 9999, 23:59:59, which has a serial date-time of 2,958,465.9999884. The base date for this date base system is December 31, 1899, which has a serial date-time of 0.

- In the _1904 date system_ , the lower limit is January 1[st] , 0001, 00:00:00, which has a serial date-time of - 695055. The upper limit is December 31[st] , 9999, 23:59:59.999, which has a serial date-time of

2,957,003.9999884.  The base date for this system is midnight (00:00:00) on the morning of January 1[st] , 1904, which has a serial date-time of 0.

A serial date-time outside the temporal range for the selected date system is invalid.

The date system is specified by the value of the date1904 attribute of the workbookPr element. [ _Example_ :

1900 date system: `<workbookPr showObjects="all"/>`

1904 date system: `<workbookPr date1904="1" showObjects="all"/>`

_end example_ ]

## **15.4 Worksheets**

## **15.4.1 Worksheets**

## 15.4.1.1 legacyDrawing (Legacy Drawing Reference)

This element is present when the sheet contains drawing shapes defined by VML. In this case, the element contains an explicit relationship whose ID points to the part containing the VML definitions.

[ _Example_ :

```
<drawing r:id="rId1"/>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|This value references a relationship Id for the sheet. The relationship shall point to the<br>part containing the VML definition.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_LegacyDrawing) is located in §A.3. _end note_ ]

## 15.4.1.2 legacyDrawingHF (Legacy Drawing Reference in  Header Footer)

This element specifies the explicit relationship to the part containing the VML defining pictures rendered in the header / footer of the sheet.

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship Id)<br>Namespace:|This value references a relationship Id for the sheet. The relationship shall point to the<br>part containing the VML definition.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_LegacyDrawing) is located in §A.3. _end note_ ]

## 15.4.1.3 Additional attribute for dataConsolidate element (Part 1, §18.3.1.29)

The following additional attributes can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class:

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|leftLabels(Starting<br>Column Labels)|Semanticaly equivlent tostartLabels.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

## 15.4.1.4 Additional attributes for protectedRange element (Part 1, §18.3.1.71)

The following additional attributes can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class:

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|password(Legacy<br>Password)|Specifies the legacy hash of the password required for editing this range.<br>The hash is generated using the logic defined in therevisionsPasswordattribute of the<br>workbookProtectionelement (Part 1, §18.2.29).<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_UnsignedShortHexsimple<br>type (§15.8.2).|
|securityDescriptor<br>(Security<br>Descriptor)|Optional setting to specify the relative security descriptor. The security descriptor defines<br>user accounts who can edit this range without providing a password to access the range.<br>The format of asecurityDescriptoris application defined; however, it is recommended<br>that the following format be used for interoperability between implementations:<br><br>username@domain<br>If multiple user accounts are specified in thesecurityDescriptorattribute, each account<br>shall be delimited by parentheses.<br>[_Example_: This example demonstrates two user accounts in the security descriptor<br>attribute:<br>`<protectedRanges>`<br>`<protectedRange sqref="A1:C5" name="Range1"`<br>`securityDescriptor="(user1@iso.org)(user2@iso.org)"/>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`</protectedRanges>`<br>_end example_]<br>If an application is unable to resolve the meaning of thesecurityDescriptor, it shall treat<br>the attribute as if it had been removed.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

## 15.4.1.5 Additional attribute for sheetProtection element (Part 1, §18.3.1.84)

The following additional attributes can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class:

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|password<br>(Password)|Specifies the hash of the password required for editing this chart sheet.<br>`The hash is generated using the logic defined in the`<br>revisionPassword`attribute of the`workbookProtection`element (Part`<br>`1, §18.2.29).`<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_UnsignedShortHexsimple<br>type (§15.8.2).|

## 15.4.1.6 Additional attribute for sheetProtection element (Part 1, §18.3.1.85)

The following additional attributes can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class:

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|password(Legacy<br>Password)|Specifies the legacy hash of the password required for editing this worksheet.<br>`The hash is generated using the logic defined in the`<br>revisionsPassword`attribute of the`workbookProtection`element (Part`<br>`1, §18.2.29).`<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_UnsignedShortHexsimple<br>type (§15.8.2).|

## **15.4.2 AutoFilter Settings**

15.4.2.1 Attributes with modified descriptions for dynamicFilter element (Part 1, §18.3.2.5)

The following attributes have modified descriptions when specified for a document of a transitional conformance class:

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|maxVal(Max Value)|A maximum value for dynamic filter.maxVal/maxValIsoshall be required for today,|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||yesterday, tomorrow, nextWeek, thisWeek, lastWeek, nextMonth, thisMonth, lastMonth,<br>nextQuarter, thisQuarter, lastQuarter, nextYear, thisYear, lastYear, and yearToDate.<br>The above criteria are based on a value range.  [_Example_: If today's date is September<br>22nd, then the range for thisWeek  is the values greater than or equal to September 17<br>and less than September 24._end example_]  In the thisWeek range, the lower value is<br>expressed usingvalorvalIso. The higher value is expressed usingmaxValormaxValIso.<br>These dynamic filter shall not requireval/valIsoormaxVal/maxValIso:<br>Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4,<br>M1, M2, M3, M4, M5, M6, M7, M8, M9, M10, M11 and M12.<br>The above criteria shall not specify the range usingval/valIsoandmaxVal/maxValIso<br>because Q1 always starts from M1 to M3, and M1 is always January.<br>These types of dynamic filters shall usevaland shall not usemaxVal/maxValIso:<br>aboveAverage and belowAverage.<br>IfmaxValIsoandmaxValare both present,maxValIsoshall take precedence.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemadouble<br>datatype.|
|val(Value)|A minimum numeric or serial date value for dynamic filter. (See description ofValIsoto<br>understand whenvalis required.)<br>IfvalIsoandvalare both present,valIsoshall take precedence.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemadouble<br>datatype.|
|valIso(ISO Value)|A minimum date value for dynamic filter. (See description ofmaxVal/maxValIsoto<br>understand whenval/valIsois required.)<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML SchemadateTime<br>datatype.|

## **15.5 Styles**

## **15.5.1 left (Leading Edge Border)**

Semantically equivalent to start (Part 1, §18.8.37).

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|style(Line Style)|The line style for this border.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BorderStylesimple type (Part|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||1, §18.18.3).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_BorderPr) is located in §A.3. _end note_ ]

## **15.5.2 right (Trailing Edge Border)**

Semantically equivalent to end (Part 1, §18.8.16).

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|style(Line Style)|The line style for this border.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BorderStylesimple type (Part<br>1, §18.18.3).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_BorderPr) is located in §A.3. _end note_ ]

## **15.6 Pivot Tables**

## **15.6.1 Pivot Tables**

15.6.1.1 Additional attribute for pivotCacheDefinition element (Part 1, §18.10.1.67)

The following additional attributes can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class:

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|refreshedDate<br>(PivotCache Last<br>Refreshed Date)|Specifies the date when the cache was last refreshed. This attribute depends on whether<br>the application exposes mechanisms via the user interface whereby the end-user can<br>refresh the cache.<br>IfrefreshedDateIsoandrefreshedDateare both present,refreshedDateIsoshall take<br>precedence.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemadouble<br>datatype.|

## **15.7 External Data Connections**

## **15.7.1 Additional attribute for textPr element (Part 1, §18.13.12)**

The following additional attributes can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class:

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|codePage(Code<br>Page)|Code page associated with the text file. This attribute is used only for backwards<br>compatibility, and is ignored if the characterSet attribute is present.<br>[_Note_: There are a number of code page technologies. One example of potential values<br>can be found at:http://www.unicode.org/Public/MAPPINGS _end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML SchemaunsignedInt<br>datatype.|

## **15.8 Simple Types**

The following additional simple type information in the

http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main namespace is used for documents of a transitional conformance class.

## **15.8.1 Additional enumeration values for ST_PivotAreaType (Part 1, §18.18.58)**

The following addtional enumeration values can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|topRight(Top Corner, Trailing Edge)|Semantically equivalent totopEnd.|

## **15.8.2 ST_UnsignedShortHex (Unsigned Short Hex)**

This simple type defines the Hex representation of an unsigned short.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema hexBinary datatype.

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

- This simple type's contents have a length of exactly 4 hexadecimal digit(s).

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_UnsignedShortHex) is located in §A.3. _end note_ ]

## **15.8.3 Removed enumeration values for ST_CellType (Part 1, §18.18.11)**

For transitional documents, the restriction on the simple type ST_CellType having the value “d” (ISO 8601 format) is removed.

## **15.9 Formulas**

## **15.9.1 Attribute synonym for c element (Part 1, §18.6.1)**

The following additional attribute can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class:

**Attributes Description**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|ref(Cell Reference)|An A-1 style reference to a cell. The possible values for this attribute are defined by the<br>ST_CellRef simple type (Part 1, §18.18.7).|

This attribute is semantically equivalent to r (Part 1, §18.6.1).

Only one or the other of r and ref can be defined in any given instance.

## **15.9.2 Additional representation for dates and times (Part 1, §18.17.4 )**

For a document of a transitional conformance class, each unique instant in SpreadsheetML time shall be stored as an ISO 8601-formatted string or as a serial value.

## **15.10 Changed attributes**

The following attributes, which are defined in subclauses within Part 1, §18, “SpreadsheetML”, have different source relationships when used in documents of the Transitional conformance class:

## **15.10.1 Changed attribute for externalReference element (Part 1, §18.2.8)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies a unique identifier that is used to identify a relationship to another part in the<br>file. Relationship identifiers link the element definition with the part where data for the<br>element is stored.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.2 Changed attribute for pivotCache element (Part 1, §18.2.17)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the identifier to a pivot cache definition part where cached data is stored.<br>This attribute is required.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.3 Changed attribute for sheet element (Part 1, §18.2.19)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the identifier of the sheet part where the definition for this sheet is stored.<br>This attribute is required.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.4 Changed attribute for control element (Part 1, §18.3.1.19)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|This relationship ID references an Embedded Control Data part that contains control-<br>specific properties and state information about this particular embedded control.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.5 Changed attribute for controlPr element (Part 1, §18.3.1.20)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship ID<br>for Embedded<br>Control Properties)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship ID for the relationship which contains the properties for this<br>embedded control. This property bag is contained in a separate part within the package.<br>The relationship explicitly targeted by this attribute shall be of relationship type<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/controlor<br>the document shall be considered non-conformant.<br>If this attribute is omitted, then the embedded control shall be given no property bag<br>when instantiated.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following WordprocessingML markup for an embedded control in<br>a document:<br>`<w:control r:id="rId5" w:id="CheckBox1" w:name="CheckBox1"`<br>`w:shapeid="_x0000_s1027" w:class="shape" w:w="145" w:h="28"`<br>`w:align="left" />`<br>Theidattribute in the relationship reference namespace specifies that the relationship<br>with relationship ID`rId5`must contain the property data for this embedded control._end_<br>_example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.6 Changed attribute for customPr element (Part 1, §18.3.1.22)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|This relationship references the binary part containing the specified custom properties.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.7 Changed attribute for dataRef element (Part 1, §18.3.1.30)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(relationship Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Used only when the source range is external to this workbook.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.8 Changed attribute for drawing element (Part 1, §18.3.1.36)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Relationship Id referencing a part containing DrawingML definitions for this worksheet.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.9 Changed attribute for drawingHF element (Part 1, §18.3.1.37)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship ID<br>for Embedded<br>Control Properties)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship ID for the relationship to the DrawingML part that contains the<br>drawing objects used in the header and footer. This DrawingML part is a separate part<br>within the package.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<drawingHF r:id="rId2" lho="7" lhf="6"/>`<br>Theidattribute in the relationship reference namespace specifies that the relationship<br>with relationship ID`rId5`must contain the drawing objects used in the header and<br>footer._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.10 Changed attribute for hyperlink element (Part 1, §18.3.1.47)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Relationship Id in this sheet's relationships part, expressing the target location of the<br>resource.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.11 Changed attribute for objectPr element (Part 1, §18.3.1.56)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship ID<br>to Embedded<br>Object Data)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship ID for the relationship that targets the Embedded Object Part<br>containing the embedded object data.<br>The specified relationship shall be of type<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/oleObjector the<br>document shall be considered non-conformant.<br>[_Example_: Consider an XML element which has the followingidattribute:<br>`<… r:id="rId1" />`<br>The markup specifies the associated relationship part with relationship ID`rId1`targets<br>the part containing the corresponding embedded object information._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.12 Changed attribute for oleObject element (Part 1, §18.3.1.59)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Relationship Id of the relationship pointing to the object persistence part.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.13 Changed attribute for pageSetup element (Part 1, §18.3.1.63)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Relationship Id of the devMode printer settings part.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.14 Changed attribute for pageSetup element (Part 1, §18.3.1.64)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi|Relationship Id of the devMode printer settings part.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|ps||

## **15.10.15 Changed attribute for picture element (Part 1, §18.3.1.67)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Relationship Id pointing to the image part.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.16 Changed attribute for pivotSelection element (Part 1, §18.3.1.69)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Relationship Id pointing to the particular PivotTable Part corresponding to this selection.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.17 Changed attribute for tablePart element (Part 1, §18.3.1.94)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|This relationship Id is used to locate a particular table definition part.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.18 Changed attribute for pivotCacheDefinition element (Part 1, §18.10.1.67)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the unique identifier that corresponds to the related`pivotCacheRecords`part.<br>See (Part 1, §18.10.1.68) for more information.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.19 Changed attribute for rangeSet element (Part 1, §18.10.1.79)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship Id)|Specifies the unique identifier of the Workbook part where the range set is stored. See|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Workbook (Part 1, §18.2) for more information.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.20 Changed attribute for worksheetSource element (Part 1, §18.10.1.95)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship Id)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the identifier to the Sheet part whose data is stored in the cache. See the Sheet<br>section (Part 1, §18.2) for more information.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.21 Changed attribute for header element (Part 1, §18.11.1.1)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship ID)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|This is the ID that is used to find the corresponding log record of the changes made for<br>this header.<br>Use the corresponding relationship expressed in the revisionHeaders part to locate the<br>log record that lists the specific changes.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.22 Changed attribute for externalBook element (Part 1, §18.14.7)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship to<br>supporting book file<br>path)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Relationship ID that references a link in therelationshipscollection.  Thetargetattribute<br>in the associatedrelationshipwill specify the worksheet XML file in the current<br>SpreadsheetML document ZIP archive that makes use of thisexternalbook.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **15.10.23 Changed attribute for oleLink element (Part 1, §18.14.11)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Object Link<br>Relationship)|Relationship ID that references a link in therelationshipscollection.  Thetargetattribute<br>in the associatedrelationshipwill specify the external file name used for thisoleLink.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|
