|16.6.25|Changed attribute for spPr element (Part 1, §19.3.1.44) .................................................................. 251|
|16.6.26|Changed attribute for tags element (Part 1, §19.3.1.47) .................................................................. 252|
|16.6.27|Changed attribute for xfrm element (Part 1, §19.3.1.53) ................................................................. 252|
|16.6.28|Changed attribute for control element (Part 1, §19.3.2.1) ............................................................... 253|
|16.6.29|Changed attribute for oleObj element (Part 1, §19.3.2.4) ................................................................ 253|
|16.6.30|Changed attribute for pos element (Part 1, §19.4.5) ........................................................................ 253|
|16.6.31|Changed attribute for snd element (Part 1, §19.5.68) ...................................................................... 254|
|16.6.32|Changed attribute for sndTgt element (Part 1, §19.5.70) ................................................................. 254|

**End of informative text.**

## **16.3 Presentation**

## **16.3.1 Presentation Properties**

## 16.3.1.1 htmlPubPr (HTML Publishing Properties)

This element specifies the publishing properties to be used when publishing this presentation document to the HTML file format. The target output profile is identified by the contents of the target attribute.

|**Attributes**||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|id(Publish Path)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps||Specifies the path that should be used when publishing.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).||
|showSpeakerNotes<br>(Show Speaker<br>Notes)||Specifies whether to show speaker notes when publishing.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.||
|target(Target<br>Output Profile)||Specifies the version of HTML output targeted by the output of any web page produced<br>by this document. This attribute shall only contain a string that represents an output<br>profile defined by published standards and W3C recommendations. Product names shall<br>not be used to define a profile.<br>The following reserved values and their targets are listed below:<br>**Value**<br>**Target**||
|||**Value**|**Target**|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|||`W3C XHTML+CSS1`|W3C XHTML 1.0 + CSS 1|
|||`W3C HTML4+CSS1`|W3C HTML 4.01 + CSS 1|
|||`W3C XHTML+CSS2`|W3C XHTML 1.0 + CSS 2|
|||`W3C HTML4+CSS2`|W3C HTML 4.01 + CSS 2|
|title(HTML Output<br>Title)||Specifies a title for the HTML output file.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.||

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_HtmlPublishProperties) is located in §A.4. _end note_ ]

## 16.3.1.2 webPr (Web Properties)

This element specifies all general output properties that pertain to generating a web format version of the presentation document.

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|allowPng(Allow<br>PNG in HTML<br>output)|Specifies whether to allow the output of PNG format pictures in the HTML document.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|
|clr(Slide Navigation<br>Colors for HTML<br>output)|Specifies the color constraints that are to be used when generating HTML output.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_WebColorTypesimple type<br>(§16.5.1).|
|encoding(Encoding<br>for HTML output)|Specifies the particular HTML character set encoding that should be used when<br>generating output.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_WebEncodingsimple type<br>(§16.5.2).|
|imgSz(Image size<br>for HTML output)|Specifies the screen size for which the images in the HTML output should be optimized.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_WebScreenSizesimple type<br>(§16.5.3).|
|organizeInFolders<br>(Organize HTML<br>output in folders)|Specifies whether the supporting output files should be automatically organized into a<br>folder.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|
|relyOnVml(Rely on<br>VML for HTML<br>output)|Specifies whether graphics should be output in VML within the HTML.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|
|resizeGraphics<br>(Resize graphics in<br>HTML output)|Specifies whether to resize graphics to fit within the browser window when generating<br>the HTML output.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|
|showAnimation<br>(Show animation in<br>HTML output)|Specifies whether to show presentation animation in the HTML output file.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|
|useLongFilenames<br>(Use long file names<br>in HTML output)|Specifies whether to allow the use of long file names when generating the HTML output.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_WebProperties) is located in §A.4. _end note_ ]

## 16.3.1.3 Additional attributes for modifyVerifier element (Part 1, §19.2.1.19)

The following additional attributes can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class:

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|algIdExt<br>(Cryptographic<br>Algorithm<br>Extensibility)|Specifies that a cryptographic algorithm which was not defined by ECMA-376 has been<br>used to generate the hash value stored with this document.<br>This value, when present, shall be interpreted based on the value of thealgIdExtSource<br>attribute in order to determine the algorithm used, which shall be application-defined.<br>[_Rationale_: This extensibility affords the fact that with exponentially increasing|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||computing power, documents created in the future might need to utilize as yet<br>undefined hashing algorithms in order to remain secure._end rationale_]<br>If this value is present, thecryptAlgorithmClass, cryptAlgorithmType, and<br>cryptAlgorithmSidattribute values shall be ignored in favor of the algorithm defined by<br>this attribute.<br>[_Example_: Consider a PresentationML document with the following information stored in<br>its protection element:<br>`<… p:algIdExt="0000000A"`<br>`p:algIdExtSource="futureCryptography"`<br>`p:hashData="9oN7nWkCAyEZib1RomSJTjmPpCY=" />`<br>ThealgIdExtattribute value of`0000000A`specifies that the algorithm with hex code`A`<br>shall be used as defined by the`futureCryptography`application._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML SchemaunsignedInt<br>datatype.|
|algIdExtSource<br>(Algorithm<br>Extensibility Source)|Specifies the application which defined the algorithm value specified by thealgIdExt<br>attribute.<br>[_Example_: Consider a PresentationML document with the following information stored in<br>one its protection element:<br>`<… p:algIdExt="0000000A"`<br>`p:algIdExtSource="futureCryptography"`<br>`p:hashData="9oN7nWkCAyEZib1RomSJTjmPpCY=" />`<br>ThealgIdExtSourceattribute value of`futureCryptography`specifies that the algorithm<br>used here was published by the`futureCryptography`application._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|cryptAlgorithmCla<br>ss(Cryptographic<br>Algorithm Class)|Specifies the class of cryptographic algorithm used by this protection. [_Note_: The initial<br>version of ECMA-376 only supports a single version -`hash`- but future versions can<br>expand this as necessary._end note_]<br>[_Example_: Consider a PresentationML document with the following information stored in<br>its protection element:<br>`<… p:cryptAlgorithmClass="hash"`<br>`p:cryptAlgorithmType="typeAny"`<br>`p:cryptAlgorithmSid="1"`<br>`p:hashData="9oN7nWkCAyEZib1RomSJTjmPpCY=" />`|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|
|---|---|---|
|||ThecryptAlgorithmClassattribute value of`hash`specifies that the algorithm used for<br>the password is a hashing algorithm._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_AlgClasssimple type<br>(§20.1.2.1).|
|cryptAlgorithmSid<br>(Cryptographic<br>Hashing Algorithm)||Specifies the specific cryptographic hashing algorithm which shall be used along with the<br>saltDataattribute and user-supplied password in order to compute a hash value for<br>comparison.<br>Thepossible values for this attribute shall be interpreted as follows:<br>**Value**<br>**Algorithm**<br>1<br>MD2<br>2<br>MD4<br>3<br>MD5<br>4<br>SHA-1<br>5<br>MAC<br>6<br>RIPEMD<br>7<br>RIPEMD-160<br>8<br>Undefined. Shall not be used.<br>9<br>HMAC<br>10<br>Undefined. Shall not be used.<br>11<br>Undefined. Shall not be used.<br>12<br>SHA-256<br>13<br>SHA-384<br>14<br>SHA-512<br>Any other<br>value<br>Undefined. Shall not be used.<br>[_Example_: Consider a PresentationML document with the following information stored in<br>its protection element:<br>`<… p:cryptAlgorithmClass="hash"`<br>`p:cryptAlgorithmType="typeAny"`<br>`p:cryptAlgorithmSid="1"`<br>`p:hashData="9oN7nWkCAyEZib1RomSJTjmPpCY=" />`<br>ThecryptAlgorithmSidattribute value of`1`specifies that the SHA-1 hashing algorithm<br>shall be used to generate a hash from the user-defined password._end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML SchemaunsignedInt<br>datatype.|
|cryptAlgorithmTyp<br>e(Cryptographic<br>Algorithm Type)|Specifies the kind of cryptographic algorithm used by this protection. [_Note_: The initial<br>version of ECMA-376 only supports a single type -`typeAny`- but future versions can<br>expand this as necessary._end note_]<br>[_Example_: Consider a PresentationML document with the following information stored in<br>its protection element:<br>`<… p:cryptAlgorithmClass="hash"`<br>`p:cryptAlgorithmType="typeAny"`<br>`p:cryptAlgorithmSid="1"`<br>`p:hashData="9oN7nWkCAyEZib1RomSJTjmPpCY=" />`<br>ThecryptAlgorithmTypeattribute value of`typeAny`specifies that any algorithm type<br>might have been used for the password._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_AlgTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.2).|
|cryptProvider<br>(Cryptographic<br>Provider)|Specifies the cryptographic provider which was used to generate the hash value stored in<br>this document. If the user provided a cryptographic provider which was not the system's<br>built-in provider, then that provider shall be stored here so it can subsequently be used if<br>available.<br>If this attribute is omitted, then the built-in cryptographic provider on the system shall be<br>used.<br>[_Example_: Consider a PresentationML document with the following information stored in<br>its protection element:<br>`<… p:cryptProvider="Krista'sProvider"`<br>`p:hashData="9oN7nWkCAyEZib1RomSJTjmPpCY=" />`<br>ThecryptProviderattribute value of`Krista'sProvider`specifies that the cryptographic<br>provider with name "Krista's Provider" shall be used if available._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|cryptProviderType<br>(Cryptographic<br>Provider Type)|Specifies the kind of cryptographic provider to be used.<br>[_Example_: Consider a PresentationML document with the following information stored in<br>its protection element:<br>`<… p:cryptProviderType="rsaAES"`<br>`p:hashData="9oN7nWkCAyEZib1RomSJTjmPpCY=" />`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||ThecryptProviderTypeattribute value of`rsaAES`specifies that the cryptographic<br>provider type shall be an Advanced Encryption Standard provider._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_CryptProvsimple type<br>(§20.1.2.4).|
|cryptProviderType<br>Ext(Cryptographic<br>Provider Type<br>Extensibility)|Specifies that a cryptographic provider type which was not defined by ECMA-376 has<br>been used to generate the hash value stored with this document.<br>This value, when present, shall be interpreted based on the value of the<br>cryptProviderTypeExtSourceattribute in order to determine the provider type used,<br>which shall be application-defined. [_Rationale_: This extensibility affords the fact that with<br>exponentially increasing computing power, documents created in the future might need<br>to utilize as yet undefined cryptographic provider types in order to remain secure._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>If this value is present, thecryptProviderTypeattribute value shall be ignored in favor of<br>the provider type defined by this attribute.<br>[_Example_: Consider a PresentationML document with the following information stored in<br>its protection element:<br>`<… p:cryptProviderTypeExt="00A5691D"`<br>`p:cryptProvideTypeExtSource="futureCryptography"`<br>`p:hashData="9oN7nWkCAyEZib1RomSJTjmPpCY=" />`<br>ThecryptProviderTypeExtattribute value of`00A5691D`specifies that the provider type<br>associated with hex code`A5691D`shall be used as defined by the`futureCryptography`<br>application._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML SchemaunsignedInt<br>datatype.|
|cryptProviderType<br>ExtSource(Provider<br>Type Extensibility<br>Source)|Specifies the application which defined the provider type value specified by the<br>cryptProviderTypeExtattribute.<br>[_Example_: Consider a PresentationML document with the following information stored in<br>its protection element:<br>`<… p:cryptProviderTypeExt="00A5691D"`<br>`p:cryptProvideTypeExtSource="futureCryptography"`<br>`p:hashData="9oN7nWkCAyEZib1RomSJTjmPpCY=" />`<br>ThecryptProvideTypeExtSourceattribute value of`futureCryptography`specifies that<br>the provider type used here was published by the`futureCryptography`application._end_<br>_example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|hashData<br>(Password Hash)|Specifies the hash value for the password stored with this document. This value shall be<br>compared with the resulting hash value after hashing the user-supplied password using<br>the algorithm specified by the preceding attributes and parent XML element, and if the<br>two values match, the protection shall no longer be enforced.<br>If this value is omitted, then no password shall be associated with the protection, and it<br>can be turned off without supplying any password.<br>[_Example_: Consider a PresentationML document with the following information stored in<br>its protection element:<br>`<… p:cryptAlgorithmClass="hash"`<br>`p:cryptAlgorithmType="typeAny"`<br>`p:cryptAlgorithmSid="1"`<br>`p:hashData="9oN7nWkCAyEZib1RomSJTjmPpCY=" />`<br>ThehashDataattribute value of`9oN7nWkCAyEZib1RomSJTjmPpCY=`specifies that the<br>user-supplied password shall be hashed using the pre-processing defined by the parent<br>element (if any) followed by the SHA-1 algorithm (specified via thecryptAlgorithmSid<br>attribute value of`1`) and that the resulting has value must be<br>`9oN7nWkCAyEZib1RomSJTjmPpCY=`for the protection to be disabled._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemabase64Binary<br>datatype.|
|saltData(Salt for<br>Password Verifier)|Specifies the salt which was prepended to the user-supplied password before it was<br>hashed using the hashing algorithm defined by the preceding attribute values to generate<br>thehashDataattribute, and which shall also be prepended to the user-supplied<br>password before attempting to generate a hash value for comparison. A_salt_is a random<br>string which is added to a user-supplied password before it is hashed in order to prevent<br>a malicious party from pre-calculating all possible password/hash combinations and<br>simply using those precalculated values (often referred to as a "dictionary attack").<br>If this attribute is omitted, then no salt shall be prepended to the user-supplied password<br>before it is hashed for comparison with the stored hash value.<br>[_Example_: Consider a PresentationML document with the following information stored in<br>its protection element:<br>`<… p:saltData="ZUdHa+D8F/OAKP3I7ssUnQ=="`<br>`p:hashData="9oN7nWkCAyEZib1RomSJTjmPpCY=" />`<br>ThesaltDataattribute value of`ZUdHa+D8F/OAKP3I7ssUnQ==`specifies that the user-|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||supplied password shall have this value prepended before it is run through the specified<br>hashing algorithm to generate a resulting hash value for comparison._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemabase64Binary<br>datatype.|
|spinCount<br>(Iterations to Run<br>Hashing Algorithm)|Specifies the number of times the hashing function shall be iteratively run (runs using<br>each iteration's result plus a 4 byte value (0-based, little endian) containing the number<br>of the iteration as the input for the next iteration) when attempting to compare a user-<br>supplied password with the value stored in thehashDataattribute. [_Rationale_: Running<br>the algorithm many times increases the cost of exhaustive search attacks<br>correspondingly. Storing this value allows for the number of iterations to be increased<br>over time to accommodate faster hardware (and hence the ability to run more iterations<br>in less time)._end rationale_]<br>[_Example_: Consider a PresentationML document with the following information stored in<br>its protection element:<br>`<… p:spinCount="100000"`<br>`p:hashData="9oN7nWkCAyEZib1RomSJTjmPpCY=" />`<br>ThespinCountattribute value of`100000`specifies that the hashing function shall be run<br>one hundred thousand times to generate a hash value for comparison with thehash<br>attribute._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML SchemaunsignedInt<br>datatype.|

## **16.4 Slides**

## **16.4.1 Embedded Objects**

16.4.1.1 Additional attribute for control element (Part 1, §19.3.2.1)

The following additional attributes can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class:

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|spid(Embedded<br>object Shape ID)|Specifies the identifier of the shape associated with this Embedded object.  The shape<br>contains all coordinate anchoring information.<br>This optional attribute shall be present if the parent element does not contain a childpic<br>element.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ShapeIDsimple type (Part 1,<br>§20.1.10.55).|

## 16.4.1.2 Additional attribute for oleObj element (Part 1, §19.3.2.4)

The following additional attributes can be specified for a document of a transitional conformance class:

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|spid(Embedded<br>object Shape ID)|Specifies the identifier of the shape associated with this Embedded object.  The shape<br>contains all coordinate anchoring information.<br>This optional attribute shall be present if the parent element does not contain a child pic<br>element.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ShapeIDsimple type (Part 1,<br>§20.1.10.55).|

## **16.5 Simple Types**

The following additional simple type information in the

http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/presentationml/2006/main namespace is used for documents of a transitional conformance class.

## **16.5.1 ST_WebColorType (HTML Slide Navigation Control Colors)**

This simple type specifies the coloring that should be used when outputting to web formats.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema token datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|blackTextOnWhite(Black Text on White Colors)|Black Text on White coloring should be used.|
|browser(Browser Colors)|Browser coloring should be used.|
|none(Non-specific Colors)|No specific coloring has been specified.|
|presentationAccent(Presentation Accent Colors)|Presentation accent coloring should be used.|
|presentationText(Presentation Text Colors)|Presentation text coloring should be used.|
|whiteTextOnBlack(White Text on Black Colors)|White text on black coloring should be used.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_WebColorType) is located in §A.4. _end note_ ]

## **16.5.2 ST_WebEncoding (Web Encoding)**

This simple type specifies a string representing the HTML character set used when outputting to web formats.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_WebEncoding) is located in §A.4. _end note_ ]

## **16.5.3 ST_WebScreenSize (HTML/Web Screen Size Target)**

This simple type specifies the intended screen resolution for output to web formats.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema token datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|1024x768(HTML/Web Size Enumeration 1024x768)|Screen size is 1024x768 pixels|
|1152x882(HTML/Web Size Enumeration 1152x882)|Screen size is 1152x882 pixels|
|1152x900(HTML/Web Size Enumeration 1152x900)|Screen size is 1152x900 pixels|
|1280x1024(HTML/Web Size Enumeration<br>1280x1024)|Screen size is 1280x1024 pixels|
|1600x1200(HTML/Web Size Enumeration<br>1600x1200)|Screen size is 1600x1200 pixels|
|1800x1400(HTML/Web Size Enumeration<br>1800x1400)|Screen size is 1800x1400 pixels|
|1920x1200(HTML/Web Size Enumeration<br>1920x1200)|Screen size is 1920x1200 pixels|
|544x376(HTML/Web Size Enumeration 544x376)|Screen size is 544x376 pixels|
|640x480(HTML/Web Size Enumeration 640x480)|Screen size is 640x480 pixels|
|720x512(HTML/Web Size Enumeration 720x515)|Screen size is 720x512 pixels|
|800x600(HTML/Web Size Enumeration 800x600)|Screen size is 800x600 pixels|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_WebScreenSize) is located in §A.4. _end note_ ]

## **16.6 Changed attributes**

The following attributes, which are defined in subclauses within Part 1, §19, “PresentationML”, have different source relationships when used in documents of the Transitional conformance class:

## **16.6.1 Changed attribute for bold element (Part 1, §19.2.1.1)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship identifier that is used in conjunction with a corresponding<br>relationship file to resolve the location of this embedded font that is referenced in a<br>presentation.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

**16.6.2 Changed attribute for boldItalic element (Part 1, §19.2.1.2)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship identifier that is used in conjunction with a corresponding<br>relationship file to resolve the location of this embedded font that is referenced in a<br>presentation.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

**16.6.3 Changed attribute for handoutMasterId element (Part 1, §19.2.1.14)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship identifier that is used in conjunction with a corresponding<br>relationship file to resolve the location within a presentation of thehandoutMaster<br>element defining this handout master.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

**16.6.4 Changed attribute for italic element (Part 1, §19.2.1.16)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship identifier that is used in conjunction with a corresponding<br>relationship file to resolve the location of this embedded font that is referenced in a<br>presentation.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **16.6.5 Changed attribute for notesMasterId element (Part 1, §19.2.1.20)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship identifier that is used in conjunction with a corresponding<br>relationship file to resolve the location within a presentation of thenotesMasterelement<br>defining this notes master.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **16.6.6 Changed attribute for notesSz element (Part 1, §19.2.1.22)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|cx(Extent Length)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the length of the extents rectangle in EMUs. This rectangle shall dictate the size<br>of the object as displayed (the result of any scaling to the original object).<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object specified as follows:<br>`<… cx="1828800" cy="200000"/>`<br>Thecxattributes specifies that this object has a height of`1828800`EMUs (English Metric<br>Units)._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_PositiveCoordinatesimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.42).|
|cy(Extent Width)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the width of the extents rectangle in EMUs. This rectangle shall dictate the size<br>of the object as displayed (the result of any scaling to the original object).<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object specified as follows:<br>`< … cx="1828800" cy="200000"/>`<br>Thecyattribute specifies that this object has a width of`200000`EMUs (English Metric<br>Units)._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_PositiveCoordinatesimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.42).|

**16.6.7 Changed attribute for regular element (Part 1, §19.2.1.29)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship identifier that is used in conjunction with a corresponding<br>relationship file to resolve the location of this embedded font that is referenced in a<br>presentation.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **16.6.8 Changed attribute for sld element (Part 1, §19.2.1.31)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship ID)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|This attribute specifies the relationship id that is used to reference to the actual slide<br>XML file that contains all the information to the slide listed within the slide list.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

**16.6.9 Changed attribute for sldId element (Part 1, §19.2.1.33)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship identifier that is used in conjunction with a corresponding<br>relationship file to resolve the location within a presentation of thesldelement defining<br>this slide.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **16.6.10 Changed attribute for sldMasterId element (Part 1, §19.2.1.36)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship identifier that is used in conjunction with a corresponding<br>relationship file to resolve the location within a presentation of thesldMasterelement<br>defining this slide master.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **16.6.11 Changed attribute for SmartTags element (Part 1, §19.2.1.40)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship identifier that is used in conjunction with a corresponding<br>relationship file to resolve the location of this smart tag.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **16.6.12 Changed attribute for gridSpacing element (Part 1, §19.2.2.3)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|cx(Extent Length)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the length of the extents rectangle in EMUs. This rectangle shall dictate the size<br>of the object as displayed (the result of any scaling to the original object).<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object specified as follows:<br>`<… cx="1828800" cy="200000"/>`<br>Thecxattributes specifies that this object has a height of`1828800`EMUs (English Metric<br>Units)._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_PositiveCoordinatesimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.42).|
|cy(Extent Width)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the width of the extents rectangle in EMUs. This rectangle shall dictate the size<br>of the object as displayed (the result of any scaling to the original object).<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object specified as follows:<br>`< … cx="1828800" cy="200000"/>`<br>Thecyattribute specifies that this object has a width of`200000`EMUs (English Metric<br>Units)._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_PositiveCoordinatesimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.42).|

## **16.6.13 Changed attribute for origin element (Part 1, §19.2.2.9)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|x(X-Axis<br>Coordinate)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a coordinate on the x-axis. The origin point for this coordinate shall be specified<br>by the parent XML element.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following point on a basic wrapping polygon for a DrawingML<br>object:<br>`<… x="0" y="100" />`<br>Thexattribute defines an x-coordinate of`0`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Coordinatesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.16).|
|y(Y-Axis<br>Coordinate)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a coordinate on the y-axis. The origin point for this coordinate shall be specified<br>by the parent XML element.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following point on a basic wrapping polygon for a DrawingML<br>object:<br>`<… x="0" y="100" />`<br>Theyattribute defines a y-coordinate of`100`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Coordinatesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.16).|

## **16.6.14 Changed attribute for sld element (Part 1, §19.2.2.14)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship identifier that is used in conjunction with a corresponding<br>relationship file to resolve the location of this presentation slide within a presentation.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **16.6.15 Changed attribute for bgRef element (Part 1, §19.3.1.3)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|idx(Style Matrix<br>Index)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the style matrix index of the style referred to.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StyleMatrixColumnIndex<br>simple type (Part 1, §20.1.10.57).|

## **16.6.16 Changed attribute for blipFill element (Part 1, §19.3.1.4)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|dpi(DPI Setting)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the DPI (dots per inch) used to calculate the size of the blip. If not present or<br>zero, the DPI in the blip is used.<br>[_Note_: This attribute is primarily used to keep track of the picture quality within a<br>document. There are different levels of quality needed for print than on-screen viewing<br>and thus a need to track this information._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML SchemaunsignedInt<br>datatype.|
|rotWithShape<br>(Rotate With Shape)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies that the fill should rotate with the shape. That is, when the shape that has been<br>filled with a picture and the containing shape (say a rectangle) is transformed with a<br>rotation then the fill is transformed with the same rotation.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

**16.6.17 Changed attribute for clrMap element (Part 1, §19.3.1.6)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|accent1(Accent 1)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined which is associated as the accent 1 color.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|
|accent2(Accent 2)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined which is associated as the accent 2 color.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|
|accent3(Accent 3)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined which is associated as the accent 3 color.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|accent4(Accent 4)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined which is associated as the accent 4 color.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|
|accent5(Accent 5)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined which is associated as the accent 5 color.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|
|accent6(Accent 6)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined which is associated as the accent 6 color.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|
|bg1(Background 1)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|A color defined which is associated as the first background color.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|
|bg2(Background 2)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined which is associated as the second background color.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|
|folHlink(Followed<br>Hyperlink)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined which is associated as the color for a followed hyperlink.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|
|hlink(Hyperlink)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined which is associated as the color for a hyperlink.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|
|tx1(Text 1)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined which is associated as the first text color.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|tx2(Text 2)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined which is associated as the second text color.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|

## **16.6.18 Changed attribute for cNvPicPr element (Part 1, §19.3.1.11)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|preferRelativeResi<br>ze(Relative Resize<br>Preferred)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies if the user interface should show the resizing of the picture based on the<br>picture's current size or its original size. If this attribute is set to true, then scaling is<br>relative to the original picture size as opposed to the current picture size.<br>[_Example_: Consider the case where a picture has been resized within a document and is<br>now 50% of the originally inserted picture size. Now if the user chooses to make a later<br>adjustment to the size of this picture within the generating application, then the value of<br>this attribute should be checked.<br>If this attribute is set to true then a value of 50% is shown. Similarly, if this attribute is set<br>to false, then a value of 100% should be shown because the picture has not yet been<br>resized from its current (smaller) size._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

## **16.6.19 Changed attribute for cNvPr element (Part 1, §19.3.1.12)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|descr(Alternative<br>Text for Object)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies alternative text for the current DrawingML object, for use by assistive<br>technologies or applications that do not display the current object.<br>If this element is omitted, then no alternative text is present for the parent object.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`<… descr="A picture of a bowl of fruit">`<br>Thedescrattribute contains alternative text that can be used in place of the actual<br>DrawingML object._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|hidden(Hidden)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies whether this DrawingML object is displayed. When a DrawingML object is<br>displayed within a document, that object can be hidden (i.e., present, but not visible).<br>This attribute determines whether the object is rendered or made hidden. [_Note_: An<br>application can have settings which allow this object to be viewed._end note_]<br>If this attribute is omitted, then the parent DrawingML object shall be displayed (i.e., not<br>hidden).<br>[_Example_: Consider an inline DrawingML object that must be hidden within the<br>document's content. This setting would be specified as follows:<br>`<… hidden="true" />`<br>Thehiddenattribute has a value of`true`, which specifies that the DrawingML object is<br>hidden and not displayed when the document is displayed._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|
|id(Unique<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a unique identifier for the current DrawingML object within the current<br>document. This ID can be used to assist in uniquely identifying this object so that it can<br>be referred to by other parts of the document.<br>If multiple objects within the same document share the sameidattribute value, then the<br>document shall be considered non-conformant.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`<… id="10" … >`<br>Theidattribute has a value of`10`, which is the unique identifier for this DrawingML<br>object._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_DrawingElementIdsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.21).|
|name(Name)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the name of the object. [_Note_: Typically, this is used to store the original file<br>name of a picture object._end note_]<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`< … name="foo.jpg" >`<br>Thenameattribute has a value of`foo.jpg`, which is the name of this DrawingML object.<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||datatype.|
|title(Title)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the title (caption) of the current DrawingML object.<br>If this attribute is omitted, then no title text is present for the parent object.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`<… title="Process Flow Diagram">`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

## **16.6.20 Changed attribute for cNvSpPr element (Part 1, §19.3.1.13)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|txBox(Text Box)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies that the corresponding shape is a text box and thus should be treated as such<br>by the generating application. If this attribute is omitted then it is assumed that the<br>corresponding shape is not specifically a text box.<br>[_Note_: Because a shape is not specified to be a text box does not mean that it cannot<br>have text attached to it. A text box is merely a specialized shape with specific properties.<br>_end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

## **16.6.21 Changed attribute for contentPart element (Part 1, §19.3.1.14)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship to<br>Part)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship ID to a content part.<br>[_Example_: Consider an XML element which has the followingidattribute:<br>`<… r:id="rId1" />`<br>The markup specifies the associated relationship part with relationship ID`rId1`contains<br>the corresponding relationship information for the parent XML element._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

**16.6.22 Changed attribute for custData element (Part 1, §19.3.1.17)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship ID)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|This attribute specifies the relationship id for referencing other resources outside the<br>scope of the current PresentationML file.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **16.6.23 Changed attribute for grpSpPr element (Part 1, §19.3.1.23)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bwMode(Black and<br>White Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies that the group shape should be rendered using only black and white coloring.<br>That is the coloring information for the group shape should be converted to either black<br>or white when rendering the corresponding shapes.<br>No gray is to be used in rendering this image, only stark black and stark white.<br>[_Note_: This does not mean that the group shapes themselves are stored with only black<br>and white color information. This attribute instead sets the rendering mode that the<br>shapes use when rendering._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BlackWhiteModesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.10).|

**16.6.24 Changed attribute for sldLayoutId element (Part 1, §19.3.1.40)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(ID Tag)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship id value that the generating application can use to resolve<br>which slide layout is used in the creation of the slide. This relationship id is used within<br>the relationship file for the master slide to expose the location of the corresponding<br>layout file within the presentation.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **16.6.25 Changed attribute for spPr element (Part 1, §19.3.1.44)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bwMode(Black and<br>White Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies that the picture should be rendered using only black and white coloring. That is<br>the coloring information for the picture should be converted to either black or white<br>when rendering the picture.<br>No gray is to be used in rendering this image, only stark black and stark white.<br>[_Note_: This does not mean that the picture itself that is stored within the file is|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||necessarily a black and white picture. This attribute instead sets the rendering mode that<br>the picture has applied to when rendering._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BlackWhiteModesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.10).|

## **16.6.26 Changed attribute for tags element (Part 1, §19.3.1.47)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship ID)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|This attribute specifies the relationship identifier for the customer data tag. This allows<br>for a link to a resource that is external from the current XML document but still contained<br>within the presentation document.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **16.6.27 Changed attribute for xfrm element (Part 1, §19.3.1.53)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|flipH(Horizontal<br>Flip)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a horizontal flip. When true, this attribute defines that the shape is flipped<br>horizontally about the center of its bounding box.<br>[_Example_: The following illustrates the effect of a horizontal flip.<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|flipV(Vertical Flip)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a vertical flip. When true, this attribute defines that the group is flipped<br>vertically about the center of its bounding box.<br>[_Example_: The following illustrates the effect of a vertical flip.<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|
|rot(Rotation)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the rotation of the Graphic Frame. The units for which this attribute is specified<br>in reside within the simple type definition referenced below.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Anglesimple type (Part 1,<br>§20.1.10.3).|

## **16.6.28 Changed attribute for control element (Part 1, §19.3.2.1)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship ID)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship id that is used to identify this Embedded object from within a<br>slide.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **16.6.29 Changed attribute for oleObj element (Part 1, §19.3.2.4)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship ID)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship id that is used to identify this Embedded object from within a<br>slide.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **16.6.30 Changed attribute for pos element (Part 1, §19.4.5)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|x(X-Axis<br>Coordinate)|Specifies a coordinate on the x-axis. The origin point for this coordinate shall be specified<br>by the parent XML element.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|[_Example_: Consider the following point on a basic wrapping polygon for a DrawingML<br>object:<br>`<… x="0" y="100" />`<br>Thexattribute defines an x-coordinate of`0`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Coordinatesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.16).|
|y(Y-Axis<br>Coordinate)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a coordinate on the y-axis. The origin point for this coordinate shall be specified<br>by the parent XML element.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following point on a basic wrapping polygon for a DrawingML<br>object:<br>`<… x="0" y="100" />`<br>Theyattribute defines a y-coordinate of`100`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Coordinatesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.16).|

## **16.6.31 Changed attribute for snd element (Part 1, §19.5.68)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|embed(Embedded<br>Audio File<br>Relationship ID)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the identification information for an embedded audio file. This attribute is used<br>to specify the location of an object that resides locally within the file. [_Note_: A list of<br>suggested audio types is provided in Part 1, §15.2.2._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|
|name(Sound<br>Name)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the original name or given short name for the corresponding sound. This is used<br>to distinguish this sound from others by providing a human readable name for the<br>attached sound should the user need to identify the sound among others within the UI.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|embed(Embedded<br>Audio File<br>Relationship ID)|Specifies the identification information for an embedded audio file. This attribute is used<br>to specify the location of an object that resides locally within the file. [_Note_: A list of<br>suggested audio types is provided in Part 1, §15.2.2._end note_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

This is used<br>to distinguish this sound from others by providing a human readable name for the<br>attached sound should the user need to identify the sound among others within the UI.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

## **17. DrawingML - Framework Reference Material**

## **17.1 General**

[ _Note_ : For further information on the mapping of elements and attributes to OPC parts, see the Bibliography entry, “Information on elements, attributes, and OPC parts in ISO/IEC 29500 (OOXML)”. _end note_ ]

## **17.2 DrawingML - Main**

## **17.2.2 Simple Types**

The following additional simple type information in the

http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/main namespace is used for documents of a transitional conformance class.

## 17.2.2.1 Additional member types for the union in ST_FixedPercentage (Part 1, §20.1.10.24)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

- The ST_FixedPercentageDecimal simple type (§17.2.2.7).

## 17.2.2.2 Additional member types for the union in ST_Percentage (Part 1, §20.1.10.40)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

   - The ST_PercentageDecimal simple type (Part 4, §12.1.2.12).

- 17.2.2.3 Additional member types for the union in ST_PositiveFixedPercentage (Part 1, §20.1.10.45)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

   - The ST_PositiveFixedPercentageDecimal simple type (§17.2.2.8).

- 17.2.2.4 Additional member types for the union in ST_PositivePercentage (Part 1, §20.1.10.46)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

- The ST_PositivePercentageDecimal simple type (§17.2.2.9).

## 17.2.2.5 Additional member types for the union in ST_TextFontScalePercentOrPercentString (Part 1, §20.1.10.67)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

- The ST_TextFontScalePercent simple type (§17.2.2.10).

## 17.2.2.6 Additional member types for the union in ST_TextSpacingPercentOrPercentString (Part 1, §20.1.10.77)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

- The ST_TextSpacingPercent simple type (§17.2.2.11).

## 17.2.2.7 ST_FixedPercentageDecimal (Fixed Percentage)

This simple type represents a fixed percentage in 1000ths of a percent. Range from [-100%, 100%].

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the ST_PercentageDecimal datatype (Part 1, §20.1.10.41).

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

- This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to -100000

- This simple type has a maximum value of less than or equal to 100000 .

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_FixedPercentageDecimal) is located in §A.5.1. _end note_ ]

## 17.2.2.8 ST_PositiveFixedPercentageDecimal (Positive Fixed Percentage)

This simple type represents a positive fixed percentage in 1000ths of a percent. Range from [0%, 100%].

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the ST_PercentageDecimal datatype (Part 1, §20.1.10.41).

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

- This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to 0 .

- This simple type has a maximum value of less than or equal to 100000

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model

(ST_PositiveFixedPercentageDecimal) is located in §A.5.1. _end note_ ]

## 17.2.2.9 ST_PositivePercentageDecimal (Positive Percentage as Decimal Number)

This simple type represents a positive percentage in 1000ths of a percent. Range from 0% up to and including infinity.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the ST_PercentageDecimal datatype (Part 1, §20.1.10.41).

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

- This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to 0 .

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_PositivePercentageDecimal) is located in §A.5.1. _end note_ ]

## 17.2.2.10 ST_TextFontScalePercent (Text Font Scale Percentage)

This simple type specifies the percentage range text can be scaled to in order to fit, in 1000ths of a percent.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the ST_PercentageDecimal datatype (Part 1, §20.1.10.41).

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

- This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to 1000

- This simple type has a maximum value of less than or equal to 100000 .

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_TextFontScalePercent) is located in §A.5.1. _end note_ ]

## 17.2.2.11 ST_TextSpacingPercent (Text Spacing Percent)

This type specifies the range of text spacing in thousandths of a percent, in terms of a line.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the ST_PercentageDecimal datatype (Part 1, §20.1.10.41).

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

- This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to 0 .

- This simple type has a maximum value of less than or equal to 13200000

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_TextSpacingPercent) is located in §A.5.1. _end note_ ]

## 17.2.2.12 ST_PercentageDecimal (Percentage as Decimal Number)

This simple type represents a percentage in 1000ths of a percent, e.g., a value of 1 represents 0.001% == 0.00001; a value of 100000 is equal to 100%. Percentages have no intrinsic units, but are used to scale other values with units.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema int datatype.

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_PercentageDecimal) is located in §A.4.1. _end note_ ]

## 17.2.2.13 Additional member types for the union in ST_PrSetCustVal (Part 1, §21.4.7.66)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

The W3C XML Schema int datatype.

## 17.2.2.14 ST_TextBulletSizeDecimal (Bullet Size Percentage)

This simple type specifies the range that the bullet percent can be. A bullet percent is the size of the bullet with respect to the text that should follow it. 25000 = 25%, 400000 = 400%

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the ST_PercentageDecimal datatype (Part 4, §12.1.2.12).

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to 25000 .

This simple type has a maximum value of less than or equal to 400000 .

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_TextBulletSizeDecimal) is located in §A.4.1. _end note_ ]

## 17.2.2.15 Additional member types for the union in ST_TextBulletSize (Part 1, §20.1.10.86)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

The ST_TextBulletSizeDecimal simple type (Part 4, §12.1.2.14).

## **17.3 DrawingML - Legacy Compatibility**

Within the context of DrawingML, it shall be possible (for considerations to legacy compatibility) to be able to include explicit references to specific shapes within VML Drawing parts.

[ _Example_ : A VML Drawing part is used to define ink on a PresentationML slide, but the resulting ink is referenced from the slide by its shape ID using the elements of this namespace. _end example_ ]
