## **17.3.2 Basics**

Legacy Compatibility is part of the shape definitions and properties of the DrawingML framework.

## 17.3.2.1 legacyDrawing (Legacy Drawing Object)

This element specifies the shape ID for a legacy drawing object. These legacy drawing objects all have a shape ID associated with them that is unique across the entire document. In order to store these legacy shape IDs as well as new shape IDs this legacyDrawing element should be used.

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|spid(Shape ID)|Legacy Shape ID that is unique throughout the entire document. Legacy shape IDs should<br>be assigned based on which portion of the document the drawing resides on. The<br>assignment of these ids is broken down into clusters of 1024 values. The first cluster is 1-<br>1024, the second 1025-2048 and so on.<br>This optional attribute shall be present if the parent element does not contain a child<br>pictureelement.<br>[_Example_: Within a word processing application thespidshould be assigned based on the<br>page that the drawing resides on. If the drawing resides on the second page then the<br>assignedspidshould be a value between 1025 and 2048._end example_]<br>[_Example_: Within a spreadsheet application thespidshould be assigned based on the<br>sheet that the drawing resides on. If the drawing resides on the second sheet then the<br>assignedspidshould be a value between 1025 and 2048._end example_]<br>[_Example_: Within a presentation application thespidshould be assigned based on the<br>slide that the drawing resides on. If the drawing resides on the second slide then the<br>assignedspidshould be a value between 1025 and 2048._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ShapeIDsimple type (Part 1,<br>§20.1.10.55).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Compat) is located in §A.1. _end note_ ]

## **17.4 Changed attributes**

The following attributes, which are defined in subclauses within Part 1, §20, “DrawingML - Framework Reference Material”, have different source relationships when used in documents of the Transitional conformance class:

## **17.4.1 Changed attribute for hlinkHover element (Part 1, §20.1.2.2.23)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Drawing Object<br>Hyperlink Target)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship id that when looked up in this slides relationship file contains<br>the target of this hyperlink. This attribute cannot be omitted.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **17.4.2 Changed attribute for snd element (Part 1, §20.1.2.2.32)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|embed(Embedded<br>Audio File<br>Relationship ID)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the identification information for an embedded audio file. This attribute is used<br>to specify the location of an object that resides locally within the file. [_Note_: A list of<br>suggested audio types is provided in Part 1, §15.2.2._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **17.4.3 Changed attribute for audioFile element (Part 1, §20.1.3.2)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|link(Linked<br>Relationship ID)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the identification information for a linked object. This attribute is used to<br>specify the location of an object that does not reside within this file.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

**17.4.4 Changed attribute for quickTimeFile element (Part 1, §20.1.3.4)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|link(Linked<br>Relationship ID)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the identification information for a linked object. This attribute is used to<br>specify the location of an object that does not reside within this file.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **17.4.5 Changed attribute for videoFile element (Part 1, §20.1.3.6)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|link(Linked<br>Relationship ID)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the identification information for a linked object. This attribute is used to<br>specify the location of an object that does not reside within this file.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

**17.4.6 Changed attribute for wavAudioFile element (Part 1, §20.1.3.7)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|embed(Embedded<br>Audio File<br>Relationship ID)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the identification information for an embedded audio file. This attribute is used<br>to specify the location of an object that resides locally within the file. [_Note_: A list of<br>suggested audio types is provided in Part 1, §15.2.2._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

**17.4.7 Changed attribute for blip element (Part 1, §20.1.8.13)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|embed(Embedded<br>Picture Reference)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the identification information for an embedded picture. This attribute is used to<br>specify an image that resides locally within the file.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|link(Linked Picture<br>Reference)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the identification information for a linked picture. This attribute is used to<br>specify an image that does not reside within this file.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **17.4.8 Changed attribute for blipFill element (Part 1, §20.2.2.1)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|dpi(DPI Setting)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the DPI (dots per inch) used to calculate the size of the blip. If not present or<br>zero, the DPI in the blip is used.<br>[_Note_: This attribute is primarily used to keep track of the picture quality within a<br>document. There are different levels of quality needed for print than on-screen viewing<br>and thus a need to track this information._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML SchemaunsignedInt<br>datatype.|
|rotWithShape<br>(Rotate With Shape)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies that the fill should rotate with the shape. That is, when the shape that has been<br>filled with a picture and the containing shape (say a rectangle) is transformed with a<br>rotation then the fill is transformed with the same rotation.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

## **17.4.9 Changed attribute for cNvPicPr element (Part 1, §20.2.2.2)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|preferRelativeResi<br>ze(Relative Resize<br>Preferred)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies if the user interface should show the resizing of the picture based on the<br>picture's current size or its original size. If this attribute is set to true, then scaling is<br>relative to the original picture size as opposed to the current picture size.<br>[_Example_: Consider the case where a picture has been resized within a document and is<br>now 50% of the originally inserted picture size. Now if the user chooses to make a later<br>adjustment to the size of this picture within the generating application, then the value of<br>this attribute should be checked.<br>If this attribute is set to true then a value of 50% is shown. Similarly, if this attribute is set<br>to false, then a value of 100% should be shown because the picture has not yet been<br>resized from its current (smaller) size._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

## **17.4.10 Changed attribute for cNvPr element (Part 1, §20.2.2.3)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|descr(Alternative<br>Text for Object)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies alternative text for the current DrawingML object, for use by assistive<br>technologies or applications that do not display the current object.<br>If this element is omitted, then no alternative text is present for the parent object.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`<… descr="A picture of a bowl of fruit">`<br>Thedescrattribute contains alternative text that can be used in place of the actual<br>DrawingML object._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|hidden(Hidden)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies whether this DrawingML object is displayed. When a DrawingML object is<br>displayed within a document, that object can be hidden (i.e., present, but not visible).<br>This attribute determines whether the object is rendered or made hidden. [_Note_: An<br>application can have settings that allow this object to be viewed._end note_]<br>If this attribute is omitted, then the parent DrawingML object shall be displayed (i.e., not<br>hidden).<br>[_Example_: Consider an inline DrawingML object that must be hidden within the<br>document's content. This setting would be specified as follows:<br>`<… hidden="true" />`<br>Thehiddenattribute has a value of`true`, which specifies that the DrawingML object is<br>hidden and not displayed when the document is displayed._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|
|id(Unique<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a unique identifier for the current DrawingML object within the current<br>document. This ID can be used to assist in uniquely identifying this object so that it can<br>be referred to by other parts of the document.<br>If multiple objects within the same document share the sameidattribute value, then the<br>document shall be considered non-conformant.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`<… id="10" … >`<br>Theidattribute has a value of`10`, which is the unique identifier for this DrawingML|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||object._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_DrawingElementIdsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.21).|
|name(Name)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the name of the object. [_Note_: Typically, this is used to store the original file<br>name of a picture object._end note_]<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`< … name="foo.jpg" >`<br>Thenameattribute has a value of`foo.jpg`, which is the name of this DrawingML object.<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|title(Title)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the title (caption) of the current DrawingML object.<br>If this attribute is omitted, then no title text is present for the parent object.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`<… title="Process Flow Diagram">`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

## **17.4.11 Changed attribute for spPr element (Part 1, §20.2.2.6)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bwMode(Black and<br>White Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies that the picture should be rendered using only black and white coloring. That is<br>the coloring information for the picture should be converted to either black or white<br>when rendering the picture.<br>No gray is to be used in rendering this image, only stark black and stark white.<br>[_Note_: This does not mean that the picture itself that is stored within the file is<br>necessarily a black and white picture. This attribute instead sets the rendering mode that<br>the picture has applied to when rendering._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BlackWhiteModesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.10).|

## **17.4.12 Changed attribute for docPr element (Part 1, §20.4.2.5)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|descr(Alternative<br>Text for Object)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies alternative text for the current DrawingML object, for use by assistive<br>technologies or applications that do not display the current object.<br>If this element is omitted, then no alternative text is present for the parent object.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`<… descr="A picture of a bowl of fruit">`<br>Thedescrattribute contains alternative text that can be used in place of the actual<br>DrawingML object._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|hidden(Hidden)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies whether this DrawingML object is displayed. When a DrawingML object is<br>displayed within a document, that object can be hidden (i.e., present, but not visible).<br>This attribute determines whether the object is rendered or made hidden. [_Note_: An<br>application can have settings that allow this object to be viewed._end note_]<br>If this attribute is omitted, then the parent DrawingML object shall be displayed (i.e., not<br>hidden).<br>[_Example_: Consider an inline DrawingML object that must be hidden within the<br>document's content. This setting would be specified as follows:<br>`<… hidden="true" />`<br>Thehiddenattribute has a value of`true`, which specifies that the DrawingML object is<br>hidden and not displayed when the document is displayed._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|
|id(Unique<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a unique identifier for the current DrawingML object within the current<br>document. This ID can be used to assist in uniquely identifying this object so that it can<br>be referred to by other parts of the document.<br>If multiple objects within the same document share the sameidattribute value, then the<br>document shall be considered non-conformant.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`<… id="10" … >`<br>Theidattribute has a value of`10`, which is the unique identifier for this DrawingML|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||object._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_DrawingElementIdsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.21).|
|name(Name)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the name of the object. [_Note_: Typically, this is used to store the original file<br>name of a picture object._end note_]<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`< … name="foo.jpg" >`<br>Thenameattribute has a value of`foo.jpg`, which is the name of this DrawingML object.<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|title(Title)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the title (caption) of the current DrawingML object.<br>If this attribute is omitted, then no title text is present for the parent object.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`<… title="Process Flow Diagram">`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

## **17.4.13 Changed attribute for extent element (Part 1, §20.4.2.7)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|cx(Extent Width)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the width of the extents rectangle in EMUs. This rectangle shall dictate the size<br>of the object as displayed (the result of any scaling to the original object).<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object specified as follows:<br>`<… cx="1828800" cy="200000"/>`<br>Thecxattribute specifies that this object has a width of`1828800`EMUs (English Metric<br>Units)._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_PositiveCoordinatesimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.42).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|cy(Extent Height)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the height of the extents rectangle in EMUs. This rectangle shall dictate the size<br>of the object as displayed (the result of any scaling to the original object).<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object specified as follows:<br>`< … cx="1828800" cy="200000"/>`<br>Thecyattribute specifies that this object has a height of`200000`EMUs (English Metric<br>Units)._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_PositiveCoordinatesimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.42).|

## **17.4.14 Changed attribute for lineTo element (Part 1, §20.4.2.9)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|x(X-Axis<br>Coordinate)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a coordinate on the x-axis. The origin point for this coordinate shall be specified<br>by the parent XML element.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following point on a basic wrapping polygon for a DrawingML<br>object:<br>`<… x="0" y="100" />`<br>Thexattribute defines an x-coordinate of`0`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Coordinatesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.16).|
|y(Y-Axis<br>Coordinate)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a coordinate on the y-axis. The origin point for this coordinate shall be specified<br>by the parent XML element.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following point on a basic wrapping polygon for a DrawingML<br>object:<br>`<… x="0" y="100" />`<br>Theyattribute defines a y-coordinate of`100`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Coordinatesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.16).|

## **17.4.15 Changed attribute for simplePos element (Part 1, §20.4.2.13)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|x(X-Axis<br>Coordinate)|Specifies a coordinate on the x-axis. The origin point for this coordinate shall be specified<br>by the parent XML element.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|[_Example_: Consider the following point on a basic wrapping polygon for a DrawingML<br>object:<br>`<… x="0" y="100" />`<br>Thexattribute defines an x-coordinate of`0`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Coordinatesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.16).|
|y(Y-Axis<br>Coordinate)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a coordinate on the y-axis. The origin point for this coordinate shall be specified<br>by the parent XML element.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following point on a basic wrapping polygon for a DrawingML<br>object:<br>`<… x="0" y="100" />`<br>Theyattribute defines a y-coordinate of`100`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Coordinatesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.16).|

## **17.4.16 Changed attribute for start element (Part 1, §20.4.2.14)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|x(X-Axis<br>Coordinate)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a coordinate on the x-axis. The origin point for this coordinate shall be specified<br>by the parent XML element.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following point on a basic wrapping polygon for a DrawingML<br>object:<br>`<… x="0" y="100" />`<br>Thexattribute defines an x-coordinate of`0`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Coordinatesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.16).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|y(Y-Axis<br>Coordinate)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a coordinate on the y-axis. The origin point for this coordinate shall be specified<br>by the parent XML element.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following point on a basic wrapping polygon for a DrawingML<br>object:<br>`<… x="0" y="100" />`<br>Theyattribute defines a y-coordinate of`100`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Coordinatesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.16).|

## **17.4.17 Changed attribute for blipFill element (Part 1, §20.5.2.2)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|dpi(DPI Setting)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the DPI (dots per inch) used to calculate the size of the blip. If not present or<br>zero, the DPI in the blip is used.<br>[_Note_: This attribute is primarily used to keep track of the picture quality within a<br>document. There are different levels of quality needed for print than on-screen viewing<br>and thus a need to track this information._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML SchemaunsignedInt<br>datatype.|
|rotWithShape<br>(Rotate With Shape)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies that the fill should rotate with the shape. That is, when the shape that has been<br>filled with a picture and the containing shape (say a rectangle) is transformed with a<br>rotation then the fill is transformed with the same rotation.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

## **17.4.18 Changed attribute for cNvPicPr element (Part 1, §20.5.2.7)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|preferRelativeResi<br>ze(Relative Resize<br>Preferred)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies if the user interface should show the resizing of the picture based on the<br>picture's current size or its original size. If this attribute is set to true, then scaling is<br>relative to the original picture size as opposed to the current picture size.<br>[_Example_: Consider the case where a picture has been resized within a document and is<br>now 50% of the originally inserted picture size. Now if the user chooses to make a later<br>adjustment to the size of this picture within the generating application, then the value of<br>this attribute should be checked.<br>If this attribute is set to true then a value of 50% is shown. Similarly, if this attribute is set<br>to false, then a value of 100% should be shown because the picture has not yet been|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||resized from its current (smaller) size._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

## **17.4.19 Changed attribute for cNvPr element (Part 1, §20.5.2.8)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|descr(Alternative<br>Text for Object)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies alternative text for the current DrawingML object, for use by assistive<br>technologies or applications that do not display the current object.<br>If this element is omitted, then no alternative text is present for the parent object.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`<… descr="A picture of a bowl of fruit">`<br>Thedescrattribute contains alternative text that can be used in place of the actual<br>DrawingML object._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|hidden(Hidden)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies whether this DrawingML object is displayed. When a DrawingML object is<br>displayed within a document, that object can be hidden (i.e., present, but not visible).<br>This attribute determines whether the object is rendered or made hidden. [_Note_: An<br>application can have settings that allow this object to be viewed._end note_]<br>If this attribute is omitted, then the parent DrawingML object shall be displayed (i.e., not<br>hidden).<br>[_Example_: Consider an inline DrawingML object that must be hidden within the<br>document's content. This setting would be specified as follows:<br>`<… hidden="true" />`<br>Thehiddenattribute has a value of`true`, which specifies that the DrawingML object is<br>hidden and not displayed when the document is displayed._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Unique<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a unique identifier for the current DrawingML object within the current<br>document. This ID can be used to assist in uniquely identifying this object so that it can<br>be referred to by other parts of the document.<br>If multiple objects within the same document share the sameidattribute value, then the<br>document shall be considered non-conformant.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`<… id="10" … >`<br>Theidattribute has a value of`10`, which is the unique identifier for this DrawingML<br>object._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_DrawingElementIdsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.21).|
|name(Name)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the name of the object. [_Note_: Typically, this is used to store the original file<br>name of a picture object._end note_]<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`< … name="foo.jpg" >`<br>Thenameattribute has a value of`foo.jpg`, which is the name of this DrawingML object.<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the type W3C XML Schemastring.|
|title(Title)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the title (caption) of the current DrawingML object.<br>If this attribute is omitted, then no title text is present for the parent object.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`<… title="Process Flow Diagram">`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

## **17.4.20 Changed attribute for cNvSpPr element (Part 1, §20.5.2.9)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|txBox(Text Box)|Specifies that the corresponding shape is a text box and thus should be treated as such<br>by the generating application. If this attribute is omitted then it is assumed that the|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|corresponding shape is not specifically a text box.<br>[_Note_: Because a shape is not specified to be a text box does not mean that it cannot<br>have text attached to it. A text box is merely a specialized shape with specific properties.<br>_end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

## **17.4.21 Changed attribute for contentPart element (Part 1, §20.5.2.12)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship to<br>Part)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship ID to a content part.<br>[_Example_: Consider an XML element that has the followingidattribute:<br>`<… r:id="rId1" />`<br>The markup specifies the associated relationship part with relationship ID`rId1`contains<br>the corresponding relationship information for the parent XML element._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **17.4.22 Changed attribute for ext element (Part 1, §20.5.2.14)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|cx(Extent Width)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the width of the extents rectangle in EMUs. This rectangle shall dictate the size<br>of the object as displayed (the result of any scaling to the original object).<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object specified as follows:<br>`<… cx="1828800" cy="200000"/>`<br>Thecxattribute specifies that this object has a width of`1828800`EMUs (English Metric<br>Units)._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_PositiveCoordinatesimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.42).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|cy(Extent Height)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the height of the extents rectangle in EMUs. This rectangle shall dictate the size<br>of the object as displayed (the result of any scaling to the original object).<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object specified as follows:<br>`< … cx="1828800" cy="200000"/>`<br>Thecyattribute specifies that this object has a height of`200000`EMUs (English Metric<br>Units)._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_PositiveCoordinatesimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.42).|

## **17.4.23 Changed attribute for grpSpPr element (Part 1, §20.5.2.18)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bwMode(Black and<br>White Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies that the group shape should be rendered using only black and white coloring.<br>That is the coloring information for the group shape should be converted to either black<br>or white when rendering the corresponding shapes.<br>No gray is to be used in rendering this image, only stark black and stark white.<br>[_Note_: This does not mean that the group shapes themselves are stored with only black<br>and white color information. This attribute instead sets the rendering mode that the<br>shapes use when rendering._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BlackWhiteModesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.10).|

## **17.4.24 Changed attribute for pos element (Part 1, §20.5.2.26)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|x(X-Axis<br>Coordinate)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a coordinate on the x-axis. The origin point for this coordinate shall be specified<br>by the parent XML element.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following point on a basic wrapping polygon for a DrawingML<br>object:<br>`<… x="0" y="100" />`<br>Thexattribute defines an x-coordinate of`0`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Coordinatesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.16).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|y(Y-Axis<br>Coordinate)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a coordinate on the y-axis. The origin point for this coordinate shall be specified<br>by the parent XML element.<br>[_Example_: Consider the following point on a basic wrapping polygon for a DrawingML<br>object:<br>`<… x="0" y="100" />`<br>Theyattribute defines a y-coordinate of`100`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Coordinatesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.16).|

## **17.4.25 Changed attribute for spPr element (Part 1, §20.5.2.30)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bwMode(Black and<br>White Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies that the picture should be rendered using only black and white coloring. That is<br>the coloring information for the picture should be converted to either black or white<br>when rendering the picture.<br>No gray is to be used in rendering this image, only stark black and stark white.<br>[_Note_: This does not mean that the picture itself that is stored within the file is<br>necessarily a black and white picture. This attribute instead sets the rendering mode that<br>the picture has applied to when rendering._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BlackWhiteModesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.10).|

## **17.4.26 Changed attribute for xfrm element (Part 1, §20.5.2.36)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|flipH(Horizontal<br>Flip)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a horizontal flip. When true, this attribute defines that the shape is flipped<br>horizontally about the center of its bounding box.<br>[_Example_: The following illustrates the effect of a horizontal flip.<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|flipV(Vertical Flip)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a vertical flip. When true, this attribute defines that the group is flipped<br>vertically about the center of its bounding box.<br>[_Example_: The following illustrates the effect of a vertical flip.<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|
|rot(Rotation)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the rotation of the Graphic Frame. The units for that this attribute is specified in<br>reside within the simple type definition referenced below.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Anglesimple type (Part 1,<br>§20.1.10.3).|

## **18.1.2 Elements**

## 18.1.2.1 legacyDrawingHF (Legacy Drawing for Headers and Footers)

This element specifies the VML Drawing part that contains any pictures used in the header or footer of the chart.

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship<br>Reference)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship ID for the relationship for this Chart or Chart Drawing part. The<br>type of relationship needed is specified by the parent element.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_RelId) is located in §A.6.1. _end note_ ]

## **18.1.3 Simple Types**

## 18.1.3.1 Additional member types for union in ST_DepthPercent

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

 The ST_DepthPercentUShort simple type (§13.1.3.2).

## 18.1.3.2 ST_DepthPercentUShort (Depth Percent UnsignedShort) (Part 1, §21.2.3.9)

This simple type specifies that its contents contain a whole number between 20 and 2000, whose contents are a percentage. This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema unsignedShort datatype.

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to 20 .

This simple type has a maximum value of less than or equal to 2000 .

## 18.1.3.3 Additional member types for union in ST_HPercent (Part 1, §21.2.3.19)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

- The ST_HPercentUShort simple type (§13.1.3.4).

18.1.3.4 ST_HPercentUShort (Depth Percent UnsignedShort)

This simple type specifies that its contents contain a whole number between 5 and 500, whose contents are a percentage.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema unsignedShort datatype.

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to 5 .

This simple type has a maximum value of less than or equal to 500 .

18.1.3.5 Additional member types for union in ST_GapAmount (Part 1, §21.2.3.16)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

- The ST_GapAmountUShort simple type (§13.1.3.6).

18.1.3.6 ST_GapAmountUShort (Gap Amount UnsignedShort)

This simple type specifies that its contents contain a whole number between 0 and 500, whose contents are a percentage.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema unsignedShort datatype.

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to 0 .

This simple type has a maximum value of less than or equal to 500 .

## 18.1.3.7 Additional member types for union in ST_SecondPieSize (Part 1, §21.2.3.41)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

- The ST_SecondPieSizeUShort simple type (§13.1.3.10).

18.1.3.8 ST_SecondPieSizeUShort (Second Pie Size UnsignedShort)

This simple type specifies that its contents contain a whole number between 5 and 200, whose contents are a percentage.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema unsignedShort datatype.

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to 5 .

This simple type has a maximum value of less than or equal to 200 .

## 18.1.3.9 Additional member types for union in ST_HoleSize (Part 1, §21.2.3.18)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

- The ST_HoleSizeUByte simple type (§13.1.3.12).

## 18.1.3.10 ST_HoleSizeUByte (Hole Size UnsignedByte)

This simple type specifies that its contents contain a whole number between 10 and 90, whose contents are a percentage.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema unsignedByte datatype.

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to 10 .

This simple type has a maximum value of less than or equal to 90 .

## 18.1.3.11 Additional member types for union in ST_LblOffset (Part 1, §21.2.3.23)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

- The ST_LblOffsetUShort simple type (§13.1.3.14).

## 18.1.3.12 ST_LblOffsetUShort (Label Offset UnsignedShort)

This simple type specifies that its contents contain a whole number between 0 and 1000, whose contents are a percentage.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema unsignedShort datatype.

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to 0 .

This simple type has a maximum value of less than or equal to 1000 .

## 18.1.3.13 Additional member types for union in ST_Overlap (Part 1, §21.2.3.31)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

- The ST_OverlapByte simple type (§13.1.3.16).

## 18.1.3.14 ST_OverlapByte (Overlap Byte)

This simple type specifies that its contents contain a whole number between -100 and 100, whose contents are a percentage.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema byte datatype.

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

- This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to -100.

This simple type has a maximum value of less than or equal to 100.

## 18.1.3.15 Additional member types for union in ST_BubbleScale (Part 1, §21.2.3.5)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

- The ST_BubbleScaleUInt simple type (§13.1.3.18).

## 18.1.3.16 ST_BubbleScaleUInt (Bubble Scale UnsignedInt)

This simple type specifies that its contents contain a whole number between 0 and 300, whose contents are a percentage.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema unsignedInt datatype.

This simple type also specifies the following restrictions:

This simple type has a minimum value of greater than or equal to 0 .

This simple type has a maximum value of less than or equal to 300 .

## 18.1.3.17 Additional member types for union in ST_Thickness (Part 1, §21.2.3.206)

The value space of the following additional member types can be used within the context of this simple type for a document of a transitional conformance class.

- The W3C XML Schema unsignedInt datatype.

## **18.2 Changed attributes**

The following attributes, which are defined in subclauses within Part 1, §21, “DrawingML - Components Reference Material”, have different source relationships when used in documents of the Transitional conformance class:

## **18.2.1 Changed attribute for hlinkClick element (Part 1, §21.1.2.3.5)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Drawing Object<br>Hyperlink Target)|Specifies the relationship id that when looked up in this slides relationship file contains<br>the target of this hyperlink. This attribute cannot be omitted.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **18.2.2 Changed attribute for hlinkMouseOver element (Part 1, §21.1.2.3.6)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Drawing Object<br>Hyperlink Target)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship id that when looked up in this slides relationship file contains<br>the target of this hyperlink. This attribute cannot be omitted.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|
|**18.2.3**<br>**Changed attribute for chart element (Part 1, §21.2.2.26)**||

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Relationship<br>Reference)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship ID for the relationship for this Chart or Chart Drawing part. The<br>type of relationship needed is specified by the parent element.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|

|accent1(Accent 1)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined that is associated as the accent 1 color.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|

|accent4(Accent 4)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined that is associated as the accent 4 color.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|

|bg2(Background 2)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined that is associated as the second background color.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|
|folHlink(Followed<br>Hyperlink)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a color defined that is associated as the color for a followed hyperlink.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorSchemeIndexsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.14).|

The relationship explicitly<br>targeted by this attribute shall be of type<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/package.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **18.2.6 Changed attribute for spPr element (Part 1, §21.2.2.197)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bwMode(Black and<br>White Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies that the picture should be rendered using only black and white coloring. That is<br>the coloring information for the picture should be converted to either black or white<br>when rendering the picture.<br>No gray is to be used in rendering this image, only stark black and stark white.<br>[_Note_: This does not mean that the picture itself that is stored within the file is<br>necessarily a black and white picture. This attribute instead sets the rendering mode that<br>the picture has applied to when rendering._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BlackWhiteModesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.10).|

**18.2.7 Changed attribute for userShapes element (Part 1, §21.2.2.221)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|

The<br>type of relationship needed is specified by the parent element.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|

## **18.2.8 Changed attribute for blipFill element (Part 1, §21.3.2.2)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|dpi(DPI Setting)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the DPI (dots per inch) used to calculate the size of the blip. If not present or<br>zero, the DPI in the blip is used.<br>[_Note_: This attribute is primarily used to keep track of the picture quality within a<br>document. There are different levels of quality needed for print than on-screen viewing<br>and thus a need to track this information._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML SchemaunsignedInt<br>datatype.|
|rotWithShape<br>(Rotate With Shape)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies that the fill should rotate with the shape. That is, when the shape that has been<br>filled with a picture and the containing shape (say a rectangle) is transformed with a<br>rotation then the fill is transformed with the same rotation.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

## **18.2.9 Changed attribute for cNvPicPr element (Part 1, §21.3.2.6)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|preferRelativeResi<br>ze(Relative Resize<br>Preferred)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies if the user interface should show the resizing of the picture based on the<br>picture's current size or its original size. If this attribute is set to true, then scaling is<br>relative to the original picture size as opposed to the current picture size.<br>[_Example_: Consider the case where a picture has been resized within a document and is<br>now 50% of the originally inserted picture size. Now if the user chooses to make a later<br>adjustment to the size of this picture within the generating application, then the value of<br>this attribute should be checked.<br>If this attribute is set to true then a value of 50% is shown. Similarly, if this attribute is set<br>to false, then a value of 100% should be shown because the picture has not yet been<br>resized from its current (smaller) size._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||datatype.|

## **18.2.10 Changed attribute for cNvPr element (Part 1, §21.3.2.7)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|descr(Alternative<br>Text for Object)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies alternative text for the current DrawingML object, for use by assistive<br>technologies or applications that do not display the current object.<br>If this element is omitted, then no alternative text is present for the parent object.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`<… descr="A picture of a bowl of fruit">`<br>Thedescrattribute contains alternative text that can be used in place of the actual<br>DrawingML object._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|hidden(Hidden)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies whether this DrawingML object is displayed. When a DrawingML object is<br>displayed within a document, that object can be hidden (i.e., present, but not visible).<br>This attribute determines whether the object is rendered or made hidden. [_Note_: An<br>application can have settings that allow this object to be viewed._end note_]<br>If this attribute is omitted, then the parent DrawingML object shall be displayed (i.e., not<br>hidden).<br>[_Example_: Consider an inline DrawingML object that must be hidden within the<br>document's content. This setting would be specified as follows:<br>`<… hidden="true" />`<br>Thehiddenattribute has a value of`true`, which specifies that the DrawingML object is<br>hidden and not displayed when the document is displayed._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|
|id(Unique<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies a unique identifier for the current DrawingML object within the current<br>document. This ID can be used to assist in uniquely identifying this object so that it can<br>be referred to by other parts of the document.<br>If multiple objects within the same document share the sameidattribute value, then the<br>document shall be considered non-conformant.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<… id="10" … >`<br>Theidattribute has a value of`10`, which is the unique identifier for this DrawingML<br>object._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_DrawingElementIdsimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.21).|
|name(Name)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the name of the object. [_Note_: Typically, this is used to store the original file<br>name of a picture object._end note_]<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`< … name="foo.jpg" >`<br>Thenameattribute has a value of`foo.jpg`, which is the name of this DrawingML object.<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|title(Title)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the title (caption) of the current DrawingML object.<br>If this attribute is omitted, then no title text is present for the parent object.<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object defined as follows:<br>`<… title="Process Flow Diagram">`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

## **18.2.11 Changed attribute for cNvSpPr element (Part 1, §21.3.2.8)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|txBox(Text Box)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies that the corresponding shape is a text box and thus should be treated as such<br>by the generating application. If this attribute is omitted then it is assumed that the<br>corresponding shape is not specifically a text box.<br>[_Note_: Because a shape is not specified to be a text box does not mean that it cannot<br>have text attached to it. A text box is merely a specialized shape with specific properties.<br>_end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

## **18.2.12 Changed attribute for ext element (Part 1, §21.3.2.10)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|cx(Extent Width)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the width of the extents rectangle in EMUs. This rectangle shall dictate the size<br>of the object as displayed (the result of any scaling to the original object).<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object specified as follows:<br>`<… cx="1828800" cy="200000"/>`<br>Thecxattribute specifies that this object has a width of`1828800`EMUs (English Metric<br>Units)._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_PositiveCoordinatesimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.42).|
|cy(Extent Height)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies the height of the extents rectangle in EMUs. This rectangle shall dictate the size<br>of the object as displayed (the result of any scaling to the original object).<br>[_Example_: Consider a DrawingML object specified as follows:<br>`< … cx="1828800" cy="200000"/>`<br>Thecyattribute specifies that this object has a height of`200000`EMUs (English Metric<br>Units)._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_PositiveCoordinatesimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.42).|

## **18.2.13 Changed attribute for grpSpPr element (Part 1, §21.3.2.14)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bwMode(Black and<br>White Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies that the group shape should be rendered using only black and white coloring.<br>That is the coloring information for the group shape should be converted to either black<br>or white when rendering the corresponding shapes.<br>No gray is to be used in rendering this image, only stark black and stark white.<br>[_Note_: This does not mean that the group shapes themselves are stored with only black<br>and white color information. This attribute instead sets the rendering mode that the<br>shapes use when rendering._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BlackWhiteModesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.10).|

## **18.2.14 Changed attribute for spPr element (Part 1, §21.3.2.23)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bwMode(Black and<br>White Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Specifies that the picture should be rendered using only black and white coloring. That is<br>the coloring information for the picture should be converted to either black or white<br>when rendering the picture.<br>No gray is to be used in rendering this image, only stark black and stark white.<br>[_Note_: This does not mean that the picture itself that is stored within the file is<br>necessarily a black and white picture. This attribute instead sets the rendering mode that<br>the picture has applied to when rendering._end note_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BlackWhiteModesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.10).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|

When true, this attribute defines that the shape is flipped<br>horizontally about the center of its bounding box.<br>[_Example_: The following illustrates the effect of a horizontal flip.<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

When true, this attribute defines that the group is flipped<br>vertically about the center of its bounding box.<br>[_Example_: The following illustrates the effect of a vertical flip.<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaboolean<br>datatype.|

The units for which this attribute is specified<br>in reside within the simple type definition referenced below.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Anglesimple type (Part 1,<br>§20.1.10.3).|

## **18.2.19 Changed attribute for sp3d element (Part 1, §21.4.5.6)**

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|contourW(Contour<br>Width)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Defines the width of the contour on the shape.<br>[_Example_:  Consider the following example of acontourWin use within thesp3d<br>element:<br>`<a:sp3d extrusionH="165100" contourW="50800"`<br>`prstMaterial="plastic">`<br>`<a:bevelT w="254000" h="254000"/>`<br>`<a:bevelB w="254000" h="254000"/>`<br>`<a:extrusionClr>`<br>`<a:srgbClr val="FF0000"/>`<br>`</a:extrusionClr>`<br>`<a:contourClr>`<br>`<a:schemeClr val="accent3"/>`<br>`</a:contourClr>`<br>`</a:sp3d>`<br>In this example, we see acountourWdefined as`50800`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_PositiveCoordinatesimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.42).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|extrusionH<br>(Extrusion Height)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Defines the height of the extrusion applied to the shape.<br>[_Example_:  Consider the following example of anextrusionHin use within thesp3d<br>element:<br>`<a:sp3d extrusionH="165100" contourW="50800"`<br>`prstMaterial="plastic">`<br>`<a:bevelT w="254000" h="254000"/>`<br>`<a:bevelB w="254000" h="254000"/>`<br>`<a:extrusionClr>`<br>`<a:srgbClr val="FF0000"/>`<br>`</a:extrusionClr>`<br>`<a:contourClr>`<br>`<a:schemeClr val="accent3"/>`<br>`</a:contourClr>`<br>`</a:sp3d>`<br>In this example, we see aextrusionHdefined as`165100`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_PositiveCoordinatesimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.42).|
|prstMaterial<br>(Preset Material<br>Type)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Defines the preset material that is combined with the lighting properties to give the final<br>look and feel of a shape.<br>[_Example_:  Consider the following example of aprstMaterialin use within thesp3d<br>element:<br>`<a:sp3d extrusionH="165100" contourW="50800"`<br>`prstMaterial="plastic">`<br>`<a:bevelT w="254000" h="254000"/>`<br>`<a:bevelB w="254000" h="254000"/>`<br>`<a:extrusionClr>`<br>`<a:srgbClr val="FF0000"/>`<br>`</a:extrusionClr>`<br>`<a:contourClr>`<br>`<a:schemeClr val="accent3"/>`<br>`</a:contourClr>`<br>`</a:sp3d>`<br>In this example, we see aprstMaterialdefined as`plastic`._end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_PresetMaterialTypesimple<br>type (Part 1, §20.1.10.50).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|z(Shape Depth)<br>Namespace:<br>.../drawingml/200<br>6/main|Defines the z coordinate for the 3D shape.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Coordinatesimple type<br>(Part 1, §20.1.10.16).|

## **19. VML Reference Material**

## **19.1 VML**

VML is a language for defining graphical objects in cases where DrawingML does not apply, such as text boxes and shapes in WordprocessingML documents and comments and controls in SpreadsheetML documents. The urn:schemas-microsoft-com:vml namespace provides the base elements and attributes for defining shape primitives. The urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:office , urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:word , urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:excel and urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:powerpoint namespaces define elements that layer on information beyond the baseline graphical definition. To maintain backward compatibility, all VML namespaces defined in ECMA-376 maintain the legacy namespace structure used by the existing corpus of binary documents.

[ _Note_ : The VML format is a legacy format used in an existing corpus of binary documents and is included and fully defined in ECMA-376 for backwards compatibility reasons. The DrawingML format is a newer and richer format created with the goal of eventually replacing any uses of VML in the Office Open XML formats. VML should be considered a transitional format included in Office Open XML for legacy reasons only and new applications that need a file format for drawings are strongly encouraged to use preferentially DrawingML . _end note_ ]

[ _Example_ : Assume the following shapes exist in a WordprocessingML document:

The drawing consists of four shapes.  The arrows are specified by extending the shape type base definition in the shapetype element.  Each shape representing an arrow references the shapetype it is extending via its type attribute.

- `<v:shapetype id="_x0000_t32" coordsize="21600,21600" o:spt="32" o:oned="t" path="m,l21600,21600e" filled="f">`

- `<v:path arrowok="t" fillok="f" o:connecttype="none"/>`

- `<o:lock v:ext="edit" shapetype="t"/>`

- `</v:shapetype>`

- `<v:shape id="_x0000_s1030" type="#_x0000_t32" style="position:absolute;left:0; text-align:left;margin-left:105pt;margin-top:36pt;width:48pt;height:0;flip:x; z-index:251661312" o:connectortype="straight">`

- `<v:stroke endarrow="block"/>`

- `</v:shape>`

- `<v:shape id="_x0000_s1029" type="#_x0000_t32" style="position:absolute;left:0; text-align:left;margin-left:105pt;margin-top:21.75pt;width:48pt;height:0;`

- `z-index:251660288" o:connectortype="straight">`

- `<v:stroke endarrow="block"/>`

- `</v:shape>`

The rounded rectangles use the VML roundrect element.

- `<v:roundrect id="_x0000_s1028" style="position:absolute;left:0;`

- `text-align:left;margin-left:153pt;margin-top:8.25pt;width:68.25pt;height:42pt; z-index:251659264" arcsize="10923f"/>`

- `<v:roundrect id="_x0000_s1027" style="position:absolute;left:0; text-align:left;margin-left:36.75pt;margin-top:8.25pt;width:68.25pt; height:42pt;z-index:251658240" arcsize="10923f"/>`

_end example_ ]

Throughout VML, numeric values that are allowed to take units can be specified in: cm (centimeters), mm (millimeters), in (inches), pt (points), pc (picas), px (pixels).

**This subclause is informative.**

|**19.1.2**<br>**Elements ...................................................................................................................................... 298**|
|---|
|19.1.2.1 arc (Arc Segment) .............................................................................................................................. 298|
|19.1.2.2 background (Document Background)................................................................................................ 325|
|19.1.2.3 curve (Bezier Curve) .......................................................................................................................... 328|
|19.1.2.4 f (Single Formula) ............................................................................................................................... 355|
|19.1.2.5 fill (Shape Fill Properties) ................................................................................................................... 359|
|19.1.2.6 formulas (Set of Formulas) ................................................................................................................ 370|
|19.1.2.7 group (Shape Group) ......................................................................................................................... 371|
|19.1.2.8 h (Shape Handle) ............................................................................................................................... 393|
