**End of informative text.**

## **19.1.2 Elements**

The following elements comprise the contents of the urn:schemas-microsoft-com:vml namespace:

[ _Note_ : As the VML format is a format provided for backward compatibility, many VML elements are defined in the same urn:schemas-microsoft-com:vml namespace that is already used by millions of documents already using VML. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.2.1 arc (Arc Segment)

This element specifies an arc defined as a segment of an oval.  The CSS2 style content width and height define the width and height of that oval.  The arc is defined by the intersection of the oval with the start and end radius vectors given by the angles.   The angles are calculated on the basis of a circle (width equal to height) which is then scaled anisotropically to the desired width and height.

[ _Example_ : The following specifies a simple half-circle arc open at the top:

`<v:arc`

```
  style="position:relative;top:120;left:20;width:200;height:200"
  startangle="90" endangle="270">
```

```
</v:arc>
```

The shape looks like this:

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|allowincell(Allow<br>in Table Cell)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a shape can be placed in a table.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:allowincell="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|allowoverlap<br>(Allow Shape<br>Overlap)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a shape can overlap another shape.  Default is`true`.  If`false`, the<br>shape shifts left or right so as not to overlap another shape, similar to the behavior of the<br>HTML float attribute.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:allowoverlap="false" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|alt(Alternate Text)|Specifies alternative text describing the graphical object.  This text should provide a brief<br>description of the shape for use by accessibility tools.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_: The alt text describes the basic shape:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="red"`<br>`alt="Red rectangle">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>The alt text describes the contents of a shape displaying an image:<br>`<v:shape … alt="Picture of a sunset">`<br>`</v:shape>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|borderbottomcolor<br>(Bottom Border<br>Color)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the bottom border color of an inline shape.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:borderbottomcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|borderleftcolor<br>(Border Left Color)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the left border color of an inline shape.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:borderleftcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|borderrightcolor<br>(Border Right Color)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the right border color of an inline shape.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:borderrightcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|bordertopcolor<br>(Border Top Color)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the top border color of an inline shape.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:bordertopcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|bullet(Graphical<br>Bullet)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the shape is a graphical bullet.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:bullet="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|button(Button<br>Behavior Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a shape exhibits button press behavior on click.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:button="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|bwmode(Black-<br>and-White Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies how a shape renders for black-and-white output devices.  When a shape is<br>printed on a black-and-white printer or displayed in a black-and-white view in an<br>application, several options are possible.  Default is`auto`, which useso:bwnormalfor<br>normal black-and-white rendering ando:bwpurefor pure black-and-white rendering.<br>bwnormal and bwpure are subordinate to bwmode.  If bwmode is "auto" then the value<br>for bwnormal or bwpure is used depending on what the output format is.  An application<br>can define for itself what, if any, difference there is between normal B&W and pure<br>B&W.  [_Example_: Normal B&W might allow greyscale and pure B&W might not._end_<br>_example_]<br>[_Example_: This shape renders in grayscale in a black-and-white environment:<br>`<v:shape … o:bwmode="grayscale" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BWModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.3).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bwnormal(Normal<br>Black-and-White<br>Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the black-and-white mode for normal black-and-white output devices.  Default<br>is`auto`.<br>[_Example_: This shape renders in a pale grayscale in a normal black-and-white<br>environment:<br>`<v:shape …  o:bwmode="auto" o:bwnormal="lightgrayscale" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BWModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.3).|
|bwpure(Pure<br>Black-and-White<br>Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the black-and-white mode for pure black-and-white output devices.  Default is<br>`auto`.<br>[_Example_: This shape renders in high contrast when in a pure black-and-white<br>environment:<br>`<v:shape … o:bwmode="auto" o:bwpure="highcontrast" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BWModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.3).|
|chromakey(Image<br>Transparency Color)|Specifies a color value that is transparent and show anything behind the shape.  Default<br>is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:image … chromakey="white" …>`<br>`</v:image>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|class(CSS<br>Reference)|Specifies a reference to the definition of a CSS style.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_: The snippets below are equivalent:<br>`…`<br>`.narrowstyle {width:50;height:100}`<br>`…`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:shape … class="narrowstyle"`<br>`style="top:1;left:1">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>`<v:shape … style="top:1;left:1;`<br>`width:50;height:100">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|clip(Clipping<br>Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies that the clipping region is active.  This is used in conjunction with theclippath<br>(§19.2.2.3) element to create a clipping region.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:clip="true">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|cliptowrap(Clip to<br>Wrapping Polygon)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies that the clipping region for the shape aligns with the wrapping polygon that<br>tightly surrounds the entire shape (essentially, that the shape shall not be drawn beyond<br>its wrapping polygon's extents – if it does, the shape shall be clipped).  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:cliptowrap="true">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|connectortype<br>(Shape Connector<br>Type)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-|Specifies the kind of connector used for joining shapes.  Default is`straight`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:connectortype="elbow" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|com:office:office|_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ConnectorTypesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.7).|
|coordorigin<br>(Coordinate Space<br>Origin)|Specifies the coordinate of the top left corner of the shape's coordinate space.  This<br>determines the position of the (0,0) coordinate space origin within the shape's bounding<br>box.  Default is "0,0", which places the (0,0) origin at the top left corner of the bounding<br>box.<br>This affects shape properties that specify coordinate positions, such as thepath<br>attribute.  Thus a path can be defined against a generic (0,0) origin and thecoordorigin<br>value translates the entire path within the shape's bounding space.<br>[_Example_: The horizontal and vertical coordinate space ranges from -100 to +100 because<br>the coordinate space (coordsize) is 200 by 200 and the top left coordinate is (-100,-100).<br>The (0,0) origin lies at the center of the shape's bounding box, as evidenced by the<br>position of the shape's path within the coordinate space:<br>`<v:shape … coordsize="200,200"`<br>`coordorigin="-100,-100"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,50, 50,50, 50,0 x e">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|coordsize<br>(Coordinate Space<br>Size)|Specifies the size of the shape's coordinate space in coordinate units.  Default is<br>"1000,1000".<br>The physical size of a coordinate unit length is determined by both the size of the<br>coordinate space (coordsize) and the size of the shape (stylewidth and height).  The<br>coordsizeattribute defines the number of horizontal and vertical subdivisions into which<br>the shape's bounding box is divided.  The combination ofcoordsizeandstyle<br>width/height effective scales the shape anisotropically.<br>[_Example_: The path is 50 units wide and tall, which is 25% of the size of the coordinate<br>space:<br>`<v:shape … coordsize="200,200"`<br>`coordorigin="-100,-100"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,50, 50,50, 50,0 x e">`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|dgmlayout<br>(Diagram Node<br>Layout Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the kind of automatic layout to apply to the diagram node.  This is only<br>meaningful if the shape is a node in an organization chart, which is denoted by the<br>`orgchart`value of theeditasattribute of thegroupelement.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … dgmlayout="1">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_DiagramLayoutsimple type<br>(§19.2.3.10).|
|dgmlayoutmru<br>(Diagram Node<br>Recent Layout<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the kind of automatic layout most recently used on the child elements of the<br>diagram node.  This is only meaningful if the shape is a node in anorganization chart,<br>which is denoted by the`orgchart`value of theeditasattribute of thegroupelement.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … dgmlayout="1">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_DiagramLayoutsimple type<br>(§19.2.3.10).|
|dgmnodekind<br>(Diagram Node<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies an optional, application-defined parameter that is intended to be used by the<br>application to tag different types of nodes in a diagram.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … dgmnodekind="1">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemainteger<br>datatype.|
|doubleclicknotify<br>(Double-click<br>Notification Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies that an event message is sent when a shape is double-clicked.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:doubleclicknotify="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|endAngle(Ending<br>Angle)|Specifies the angle that defines the endpoint of the arc.  The angle is measured in<br>degrees clockwise from the vertical.  Default is`90`.<br>[_Example_: This arc ends at the bottom center of the shape's region:<br>`<v:arc … endangle="180" … >`<br>`</v:arc>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemadecimal<br>datatype.|
|fillcolor(Fill Color)|Specifies the color to use for the fill.  Default is`white`.  If thefillelement (§19.1.2.5) is<br>present, itscolorattribute takes precedence.  Colors are typically specified as either a<br>named color, such as`red`, or six hexadecimal digits representing the red, green and blue<br>values of the color, such as`#00FF30`.  Full details are specified in the simple type<br>description.<br>[_Example_: This shape is red if its fill is visible:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>This is equivalent to:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="#ff0000" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||(§20.1.2.3).|
|filled(Shape Fill<br>Toggle)|Specifies whether the closed path is filled.  Default is`true`.  This attribute is overridden<br>by the fillonattribute.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … filled="f"`<br>`fillcolor="red" …>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|forcedash(Force<br>Dashed Outline)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a dashed outline is used to draw a shape when a shape has no line or<br>fill.  Default is`false`.<br>Used by PresentationML placeholders to draw a dashed outline when there is no line and<br>no fill for a shape.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:forcedash="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|hr(Horizontal Rule<br>Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies that a shape is a horizontal rule.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:hr="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|hralign(Horizontal|Specifies the alignment of a horizontal rule.  Default is`left`.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Rule Alignment)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:hralign="center" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_HrAlignsimple type<br>(§19.2.3.16).|
|href(Hyperlink<br>Target)|Specifies a hyperlink URL target for the shape.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … href="http://www.openxmlformats.org" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|hrnoshade<br>(Horizontal Rule 3D<br>Shading Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies that the horizontal rule does not have 3-D shading.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:hrnoshade="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|hrpct(Horizontal<br>Rule Length<br>Percentage)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the length of a horizontal rule as a percentage of page width.  Default is 0.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:hrpct="85" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemafloatdatatype.|
|hrstd(Horizontal<br>Rule Standard<br>Display Toggle)|Specifies whether a shape is displayed as a standard horizontal rule.  Only applies ifhris<br>`true`.  Default is`false`.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:hrstd="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|id(Unique<br>Identifier)|Specifies a unique identifier that can be used to reference a VML object.<br>Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … id="myShape" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|insetmode(Text<br>Inset Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the application calculates the internal text margin instead of using the<br>insetattribute.  Default is`custom`.  This attribute is only meaningful for text boxes.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:insetmode="auto" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_InsetModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.17).|
|insetpen(Inset<br>Border From Path)|Specifies that the border shall be displayed inside of the path defining the shape, rather<br>than along the path (the default border placement), or outside of the path as might be<br>done with an image.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … insetpen="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|ole(Embedded<br>Object Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the shape is an embedded object.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:ole="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalseBlanksimple type<br>(§20.1.2.6).|
|oleicon(Embedded<br>Object Icon Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether an embedded object is displayed as an icon.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:oleicon="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|oned(Shape Handle<br>Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the extra handles of a shape are hidden.  If`true`, hides all shape<br>handles except the top left and bottom right; that is, the same handles that are used for a<br>straight line segment.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:oned="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|opacity(Fill Color<br>Opacity)|Specifies the opacity of the primary fill color.  Default is 1.0.  This numeric value can also<br>be specified in 1/65536-ths if a trailing "f" is supplied.  [_Example_: A value of "52429f"<br>represents 52429/65536 or 0.8._end example_]<br>[_Example_: The red color is 25% opaque:<br>`<v:fill type="gradient" color="red"`<br>`color2="blue" opacity=".25">`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`</v:fill>`<br>`opacity="1"`<br>`opacity=".25"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|preferrelative<br>(Relative Resize<br>Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the original size of an object is saved after reformatting.  Default is<br>`false`.  If`true`, the original size of the object is stored and all resizing is based on a<br>percentage of that original size.  Otherwise, each resizing resets the scale to 100%.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:preferrelative="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|print(Print Toggle)|Specifies whether the shape is printed.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … print="false" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|regroupid(Regroup<br>ID)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies a previous group for a shape.  An ID number is used to identify groups of shapes<br>that are no longer grouped.  This allows shapes to be regrouped programmatically.<br>[_Example_: The shape was part of a group identified by the ID 040754:<br>`<v:shape … o:regroupid="040754" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemainteger<br>datatype.|
|spid(Optional<br>String)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies an optional string that an application can use to Identify the particular shape.<br>Default is no value.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|spt(Optional<br>Number)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies an optional number that an application can use to associate the particular shape<br>with a defined shape type.  Default is 0.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemafloatdatatype.|
|startAngle(Starting<br>Angle)|Specifies an angle that defines the starting point of the arc. The angle is measured in<br>degrees clockwise from the vertical.<br>Default is`0`.<br>[_Example_: This arc begins in the upper-right quadrant:<br>`<v:arc … startangle="45" … >`<br>`</v:arc>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemadecimal<br>datatype.|
|strokecolor(Shape<br>Stroke Color)|Specifies the primary color of the brush to use to stroke the path of the shape.  Default is<br>`black`.  Thecolorattribute of thestrokeelement (§19.1.2.21) overrides this.  Colors are<br>typically specified as either a named color, such as`red`, or six hexadecimal digits<br>representing the red, green and blue values of the color, such as`#00FF30`.  Full details<br>are specified in the simple type description.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … strokecolor="red" …>`<br>`</v:shape>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|stroked(Shape<br>Stroke Toggle)|Specifies whether the path defining the shape is stroked with a solid line.  Thestroke<br>element (§19.1.2.21) defines other strokes.  Theonattribute of thestrokeelement<br>overrides this attribute.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="red"`<br>`stroked="false" strokecolor="blue"…>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|strokeweight<br>(Shape Stroke<br>Weight)|Specifies the width of the brush to use to stroke the path.  Default is 1 point.  If a number<br>is given without units, the emu is used.  Theweightattribute of thestrokeelement<br>(§19.1.2.21) overrides this attribute.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … strokeweight="3pt" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|style(Shape Styling<br>Properties)|Specifies the CSS2 styling properties of the shape. The CSS2 (Cascading Style Sheets, Level<br>2) specification, a Recommendation of the World Wide Web Consortium, is available|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|||here:http://www.w3.org/TR/REC-CSS2.<br>This attribute uses a semi-colon delimited list of “name:value” pairs, the syntax defined<br>by section 4.1.8 of the CSS2 specification for grouped declarations (without the<br>surrounding braces). The following tables define the allowed properties and the VML<br>treatment of each.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … style=’position:absolute;width:100pt;height:50pt’ …`<br>`>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>**Property**<br>**Description**<br>`flip`<br>Specifies that the orientation of a shape is flipped.  Default is no<br>value.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`x`- Flip along the y-axis, reversing the x-coordinates.<br><br>`y`- Flip along the x-axis, reversing the y-coordinates.<br><br>`xy`- Flip along both the y- and x-axis.<br><br>`yx`- Flip along both the x- and y-axis.<br>`height`<br>Specifies the height of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.<br>`left`<br>Specifies the position of the left of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element left of it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units> - A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as apercentage of the|||
|||**Property**||**Description**|
|||`flip`||Specifies that the orientation of a shape is flipped.  Default is no<br>value.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`x`- Flip along the y-axis, reversing the x-coordinates.<br><br>`y`- Flip along the x-axis, reversing the y-coordinates.<br><br>`xy`- Flip along both the y- and x-axis.<br><br>`yx`- Flip along both the x- and y-axis.|
|||`height`||Specifies the height of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.|
|||`left`||Specifies the position of the left of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element left of it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units> - A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as apercentage of the|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||parent object's width.||
|||`margin-bottom`|Specifies the position of the bottom of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`margin-left`|Specifies the position of the left of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`margin-right`|Specifies the position of the right of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units> - A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`margin-top`|Specifies the position of the top of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||<br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`horizontal`|Specifies the horizontal positioning data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`absolute`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`absolute`<br><br>`left`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`right`<br><br>`inside`<br><br>`outside`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`horizontal-`<br>`relative`|Specifies relative horizontal position data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  This modifies the`mso-`<br>`position-horizontal`property.  Default is`text`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`margin`<br><br>`page`<br><br>`text`<br><br>`char`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`vertical`|Specifies the vertical positioning data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`absolute`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`absolute`<br><br>`top`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`bottom`<br><br>`inside`<br><br>`outside`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`vertical-`<br>`relative`|Specifies relative vertical position data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  This modifies the`mso-`<br>`position-vertical`property.  Default is`text`.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`margin`<br><br>`page `||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`text`<br><br>`line`||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-`<br>`bottom`|Specifies the distance from the bottom of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-left`|Specifies the distance from the left side of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-right`|Specifies the distance from the right side of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-top`|Specifies the distance from the top of the shape to the text that<br>wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different from<br>the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the shape<br>to include the margin areas.  This property does not change the<br>origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`edited`|Specifies whether the wrap coordinates were customized by the<br>user.  If the wrap coordinates are generated by an editor, this<br>property is`true`; otherwise they were customized by a user.<br>Default is`false`.||
|||`mso-wrap-style`|Specifies the wrapping mode for text in shapes in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`square`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`square`- Wraps text inside the shape in a square.<br><br>`none`- Text does not wrap.||
|||`position`|Specifies the kind of positioning used to place an element.<br>Default is`static`.  When the element is contained inside a group,<br>this property shall be`absolute`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`static`- The element is positioned according to the<br>normal flow of the page.  The`top`and`left`properties<br>are ignored.  If the object is anchored inline, this value is<br>used.<br><br>`absolute`- The element is positioned relative to the<br>parent, using the`top`and`left`properties.<br><br>`relative`- The element ispositioned accordingto the||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||normal flow of the page, but the`top`and`left`properties<br>are used.  The overlap of overlapping elements is<br>governed by the`z-index`property.||
|||`rotation`|Specifies the angle that a shape is rotated, in degrees.  Default is<br>0.  Positive angles are clockwise.||
|||`top`|Specifies the position of the top of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element above it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`visibility`|Specifies whether a shape is displayed.  Only`inherit`and`hidden`<br>are used; any other values are mapped to`inherit`.  Default is<br>`inherit`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`hidden`- The shape is not visible, but is still part of the<br>flow of the objects in the browser.  Mouse events are not<br>processed.<br><br>`inherit`- The visibility state is inherited from the parent<br>of the shape.||
|||`width`|Specifies the width of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`z-index`|Specifies the display order of overlapping shapes.  Default is 0.<br>This property shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Uses the order that the shapes appear in thepage,||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||bottom to top.<br><br><order>- A number that represents the stacking<br>precedence.  Shapes with higher numbers are placed on<br>top of those with lower numbers.  Negative numbers are<br>allowed.|
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`direction`|Specifies the direction of the text in the textbox.  Default is`ltr`.<br>This property is superceded by the`mso-direction-alt`property<br>if that is specified.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`ltr`- Text is displayed left-to-right.<br><br>`rtl`- Text is displayed right-to-left.|
|||`layout-flow`|Determines the flow of the text layout in a textbox.  Default is<br>`horizontal`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`horizontal`- Text is displayed horizontally.<br><br>`vertical`- Text is displayed vertically.<br><br>`vertical-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>vertically.<br><br>`horizontal-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>horizontally.|
|||`mso-direction-`<br>`alt`|Specifies an alternate direction for text in textboxes.  Overrides<br>the`direction`property.  The only allowed value is`context`.|
|||`mso-fit-shape-`<br>`to-text`|Specifies whether the shape stretches to fit the text in the<br>textbox.  Default is`false`.|
|||`mso-fit-text-`<br>`to-shape`|Specifies whether the text stretches to fit the textbox.  Default is<br>`false`.|
|||`mso-layout-`<br>`flow-alt`|Specifies the alternate layout flow for text in textboxes.  This<br>property is used instead of`layout-flow`when the layout flow is<br>from bottom to top for non-ideographic languages.  Its only value<br>is`bottom-to-top`.|
|||`mso-next-`<br>`textbox`|Specifies the ID of the next textbox in a series.  Used to keep track<br>of a set of linked textboxes.  Default is no value.|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|||`mso-rotate`|Specifies a specific rotation value for text in a textbox.  Default is<br>0.  Allowed values are:<br><br>0<br><br>90<br><br>180<br><br>-90|
|||`mso-text-scale`|Specifies the scaling factor for fitting text to shapes.  Default is 0.<br>This property is only used if`mso-fit-text-to-shape`is`true`.|
|||`v-text-anchor`|Specifies the vertical anchoring of text in a textbox.  Default is<br>`top`.  The alignment of a text anchor only becomes evident if`mso-`<br>`fit-text-to-shape`is`false`.  This property is different from the<br>`vertical-align`CSS property, which is used for ideographic<br>languages. Allowed values are:<br><br>`top`<br><br>`middle`<br><br>`bottom`<br><br>`top-center`<br><br>`middle-center`<br><br>`bottom-center`<br><br>`top-baseline`<br><br>`bottom-baseline`<br><br>`top-center-baseline`<br><br>`bottom-center-baseline`|
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`font`|Specifies a compound value of font settings.  Default is no value.<br>The values are the same as those of the CSS font property.  The<br>order of definitions in the string is: font-style, font-variant, font-<br>weight, font-size, line-height, font-family.|
|||`font-family`|Specifies the family of the font.  Default is no value.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS font-family property.|
|||`font-size`|Specifies the size of the font.  Default is no value.  The font size is<br>defined in points.  The values are the same as those of the CSS<br>font-size property.|
|||`font-style`|Specifies the amount of slant for a font.  Default is`normal`.  The<br>values are the same as those of the CSS font-style property.<br>Allowed values are:|

|**Attributes**||||**Description**|**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|||||<br>`normal`<br><br>`italic`<br><br>`oblique`- Treated the same as italic.|||
|||`font-variant`||Specifies the variant style of a font.  Default is`normal`.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS font-variant property.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`normal`<br><br>`small-caps`|||
|||`font-weight`||Specifies the thickness of the letters of the font.  Default is<br>`normal`.  The values are the same as those of the CSS font-weight<br>property.  Allowed values are:<br>**Value**<br>**Description**<br>`normal`<br>`lighter`<br>`100`<br>`200`<br>`300`<br>`400`<br>Treated as non-bold.<br>`bold`<br>`bolder`<br>`500`<br>`600`<br>`700`<br>`800`<br>`900`<br>Treated as bold.|||
|||||**Value**|**Description**||
|||||`normal`<br>`lighter`<br>`100`<br>`200`<br>`300`<br>`400`|Treated as non-bold.||
|||||`bold`<br>`bolder`<br>`500`<br>`600`<br>`700`<br>`800`<br>`900`|Treated as bold.||
|||`mso-text-`<br>`shadow`||Specifies whether a shadow is applied to the text on a text path.<br>Default is`false`.|||
|||`text-`<br>`decoration`||Specifies the style of text decoration.  Default is`none`.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS text-decoration property.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`|||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`underline`<br><br>`overline`<br><br>`line-through`<br><br>`blink`|
|||`v-rotate-`<br>`letters`|Specifies whether the letters of the text are rotated<br>counterclockwise by 90 degrees.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-same-letter-`<br>`heights`|Specifies whether all letters are the same height regardless of<br>initial case.  If`true`, the lowercase letters are stretched to the<br>height of the uppercase letters.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-text-align`|Specifies the alignment of text.  Default is`left`.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`left`<br><br>`right`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`justify`<br><br>`letter-justify`- Distributes the extra space between<br>the letters.<br><br>`stretch-justify`- Stretches the letters to fill in the<br>space.|
|||`v-text-kern`|Specifies whether kerning is turned on.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-text-reverse`|Specifies whether the layout order of rows is reversed.  Default is<br>`false`.  This is used for vertical text layout.|
|||`v-text-`<br>`spacing-mode`|Specifies the mode for letter spacing.  Default is`tightening`.<br>This property determines whether space is removed between<br>each letter (`tightening`) or added between each letter<br>(`tracking`).  The amount of letter spacing change is defined by<br>the`v-text-spacing`property.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`tightening`<br><br>`tracking`|
|||`v-text-spacing`|Specifies the amount of spacing for text in 100ths of single line<br>spacing.  Default is 100.|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|||The following properties are not inherited by an element that references ashapetype<br>element (§19.1.2.20) via theidattribute:<br><br>`flip`<br><br>`height`<br><br>`left`<br><br>`margin-left`<br><br>`margin-top`<br><br>`position`<br><br>`rotation`<br><br>`top`<br><br>`visibility`<br><br>`width`<br><br>`z-index`<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.||
|target(Hyperlink<br>Display Target)||Specifies a frame<br>values are:||
|||**Value**|**Description**|
|||<targetname>|String containing the name of the frame or window in which to load<br>the document.|
|||`_blank`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into a new blank<br>window. This window is not named.|
|||`_media`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the browser’s<br>multimedia pane.|
|||`_parent`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the immediate<br>parent of the document containing the link.|
|||`_search`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the browser's<br>search pane.|
|||`_self`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the window in<br>which the link was clicked (the active window).|
|||`_top`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the topmost<br>window.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|title(Shape Title)|Specifies the text displayed when the mouse pointer moves over the shape.  Default is no<br>value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … title="tooltip" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|userdrawn(Exists<br>In Master Slide)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the user has added the shape to a master slide.  Default is`false`.  Used<br>by PresentationML.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:userdrawn="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|userhidden(Hide<br>Script Anchors)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a script anchor is hidden.  Default is`false`.  If`true`, script anchors stay<br>hidden even if the shape is otherwise visible.  A script anchor is the visual representation<br>of a script that when displayed in an application.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:userhidden="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|wrapcoords(Shape<br>Bounding Polygon)|Specifies the bounding polygon that surrounds a shape.  This is specified using a comma-<br>delimited list of x and y coordinates: "x1,y1,x2,y2,x3,y3,…"  This is used when text is|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||tightly wrapped around a shape.  Default is no value until themso-wrap-mode style<br>attribute is set to`tight`or`through`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape …`<br>`wrapcoords="0,0 0,200, 200,200, 200,0" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

- [ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Arc) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.2.2 background (Document Background)

This element describes the fill of the background of a page using vector graphics fills.  Fills consist of simple colors, more advanced effects defined through the fill element (§19.1.2.5), or images.

[ _Example_ : The following shades the page background a pale red:

```
<v:background fillcolor="#c0504d">
```

```
</v:background>
```

This uses the fill element (§19.1.2.5) to create a gradient background fill:

`<v:background>`

```
 <v:fill type="gradient" color="#c0504d" color2="#f0504d" angle="45"/>
```

```
</v:background>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bwmode(Black-<br>and-White Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies how a shape renders for black-and-white output devices.  When a shape is<br>printed on a black-and-white printer or displayed in a black-and-white view in an<br>application, several options are possible.  Default is`auto`, which useso:bwnormalfor<br>normal black-and-white rendering ando:bwpurefor pure black-and-white rendering<br>[_Example_: This shape renders in grayscale in a black-and-white environment:<br>`<v:shape … o:bwmode="grayscale" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BWModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.3).|
|bwnormal(Normal<br>Black-and-White<br>Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the black-and-white mode for normal black-and-white output devices.  Default<br>is`auto`.<br>[_Example_: This shape renders in a pale grayscale in a normal black-and-white<br>environment:<br>`<v:shape …`<br>`o:bwmode="lightgrayscale" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BWModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.3).|
|bwpure(Pure<br>Black-and-White<br>Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the black-and-white mode for pure black-and-white output devices.  Default is<br>`auto`.<br>[_Example_: This shape renders in high contrast when in a pure black-and-white<br>environment:<br>`<v:shape … o:bwmode="highcontrast" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BWModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.3).|
|fillcolor(Fill Color)|Specifies the color to use for the fill.  Default is`white`.  If thefillelement (§19.1.2.5) is<br>present, itscolorattribute takes precedence.  Colors are typically specified as either a<br>named color, such as`red`, or six hexadecimal digits representing the red, green and blue<br>values of the color, such as`#00FF30`.  Full details are specified in the simple type<br>description.<br>[_Example_: This shape is red if its fill is visible:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>This is equivalent to:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="#ff0000" … >`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|filled(Shape Fill<br>Toggle)|Specifies whether the closed path is filled.  Default is`true`.  This attribute is overridden<br>by the fillonattribute.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … filled="f"`<br>`fillcolor="red" …>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|id(Unique<br>Identifier)|Specifies a unique identifier that can be used to reference a VML object.<br>Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … id="myShape" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|targetscreensize<br>(Target Screen Size)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the target resolution used for WordprocessingML documents with a gradient or<br>picture filled background.  Default is no value.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`544,376`<br><br>`640,480`<br><br>`720,512`<br><br>`800,600`<br><br>`1024,768`<br><br>`1152,862`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ScreenSizesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.23).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Background) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.2.3 curve (Bezier Curve)

This element is used to draw a cubic bézier curve.

The following properties of the style attribute are ignored:

- `top`

- `margin-top`

- `center-y`

- `left`

- `margin-left`

- `center-x`

- `width`

- `height`

[ _Example_ : The following specifies a simple curve that opens upward:

```
<v:curve id="mycurve"
```

```
from="10pt,10pt" to="100pt,10pt"
control1="40pt,30pt" control2="85pt,30pt">
</v:curve>
```

This shape is created:

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|allowincell(Allow<br>in Table Cell)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a shape can be placed in a table.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:allowincell="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|allowoverlap<br>(Allow Shape<br>Overlap)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a shape can overlap another shape.  Default is`true`.  If`false`, the<br>shape shifts left or right so as not to overlap another shape, similar to the behavior of the<br>HTML float attribute.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:allowoverlap="false" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|alt(Alternate Text)|Specifies alternative text describing the graphical object.  This text should provide a brief<br>description of the shape for use by accessibility tools.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_: The alt text describes the basic shape:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="red"`<br>`alt="Red rectangle">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>The alt text describes the contents of a shape displaying an image:<br>`<v:shape … alt="Picture of a sunset">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|borderbottomcolor<br>(Bottom Border<br>Color)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the bottom border color of an inline shape.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:borderbottomcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|borderleftcolor|Specifies the left border color of an inline shape.  Default is no value.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|(Border Left Color)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:borderleftcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|borderrightcolor<br>(Border Right Color)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the right border color of an inline shape.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:borderrightcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|bordertopcolor<br>(Border Top Color)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the top border color of an inline shape.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:bordertopcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|bullet(Graphical<br>Bullet)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the shape is a graphical bullet.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:bullet="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|button(Button<br>Behavior Toggle)|Specifies whether a shape exhibits button press behavior on click.  Default is`false`.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:button="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|bwmode(Black-<br>and-White Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies how a shape renders for black-and-white output devices.  When a shape is<br>printed on a black-and-white printer or displayed in a black-and-white view in an<br>application, several options are possible.  Default is`auto`, which useso:bwnormalfor<br>normal black-and-white rendering ando:bwpurefor pure black-and-white rendering.<br>bwnormal and bwpure are subordinate to bwmode.  If bwmode is "auto" then the value<br>for bwnormal or bwpure is used depending on what the output format is.  An application<br>can define for itself what, if any, difference there is between normal B&W and pure<br>B&W.  [_Example_: Normal B&W might allow greyscale and pure B&W might not._end_<br>_example_]<br>[_Example_: This shape renders in grayscale in a black-and-white environment:<br>`<v:shape … o:bwmode="grayscale" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BWModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.3).|
|bwnormal(Normal<br>Black-and-White<br>Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the black-and-white mode for normal black-and-white output devices.  Default<br>is`auto`.<br>[_Example_: This shape renders in a pale grayscale in a normal black-and-white<br>environment:<br>`<v:shape …  o:bwmode="auto" o:bwnormal="lightgrayscale" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BWModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.3).|
|bwpure(Pure<br>Black-and-White<br>Mode)|Specifies the black-and-white mode for pure black-and-white output devices.  Default is<br>`auto`.<br>[_Example_: This shape renders in high contrast when in a pure black-and-white|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|environment:<br>`<v:shape … o:bwmode="auto" o:bwpure="highcontrast" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_BWModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.3).|
|chromakey(Image<br>Transparency Color)|Specifies a color value that is transparent and show anything behind the shape.  Default<br>is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:image … chromakey="white" …>`<br>`</v:image>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|class(CSS<br>Reference)|Specifies a reference to the definition of a CSS style.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_: The snippets below are equivalent:<br>`…`<br>`.narrowstyle {width:50;height:100}`<br>`…`<br>`<v:shape … class="narrowstyle"`<br>`style="top:1;left:1">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>`<v:shape … style="top:1;left:1;`<br>`width:50;height:100">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|clip(Clipping<br>Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-|Specifies that the clipping region is active.  This is used in conjunction with theclippath<br>(§19.2.2.3) element to create a clipping region.<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|microsoft-<br>com:office:office|`<v:shape … o:clip="true">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|cliptowrap(Clip to<br>Wrapping Polygon)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies that the clipping region for the shape aligns with the wrapping polygon that<br>tightly surrounds the entire shape (essentially, that the shape shall not be drawn beyond<br>its wrapping polygon's extents – if it does, the shape shall be clipped).  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:cliptowrap="true">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|connectortype<br>(Shape Connector<br>Type)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the kind of connector used for joining shapes.  Default is`straight`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:connectortype="elbow" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ConnectorTypesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.7).|
|control1(First<br>Curve Control Point)|Specifies the first control point for the curve, given in the coordinate space of the parent<br>element.  Default is "10,10".  If the parent is not a VML element, the default unit is a<br>pixel.  Allowed units are in, cm, mm, pt, pc and px.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:curve … control1="20,30" … >`<br>`</v:curve>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|control2(Second<br>Curve Control Point)|Specifies the second control point for the curve, given in the coordinate space of the<br>parent element.  Default is "20,0".  If the parent is not a VML element, the default unit is<br>a pixel.  Allowed units are in, cm, mm, pt, pc and px.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:curve … control2="50,20" … >`<br>`</v:curve>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|coordorigin<br>(Coordinate Space<br>Origin)|Specifies the coordinate of the top left corner of the shape's coordinate space.  This<br>determines the position of the (0,0) coordinate space origin within the shape's bounding<br>box.  Default is "0,0", which places the (0,0) origin at the top left corner of the bounding<br>box.<br>This affects shape properties that specify coordinate positions, such as thepath<br>attribute.  Thus a path can be defined against a generic (0,0) origin and thecoordorigin<br>value translates the entire path within the shape's bounding space.<br>[_Example_: The horizontal and vertical coordinate space ranges from -100 to +100 because<br>the coordinate space (coordsize) is 200 by 200 and the top left coordinate is (-100,-100).<br>The (0,0) origin lies at the center of the shape's bounding box, as evidenced by the<br>position of the shape's path within the coordinate space:<br>`<v:shape … coordsize="200,200"`<br>`coordorigin="-100,-100"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,50, 50,50, 50,0 x e">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|coordsize<br>(Coordinate Space<br>Size)|Specifies the size of the shape's coordinate space in coordinate units.  Default is<br>"1000,1000".<br>The physical size of a coordinate unit length is determined by both the size of the<br>coordinate space (coordsize) and the size of the shape (stylewidth and height).  The<br>coordsizeattribute defines the number of horizontal and vertical subdivisions into which|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||the shape's bounding box is divided.  The combination ofcoordsizeandstyle<br>width/height effective scales the shape anisotropically.<br>[_Example_: The path is 50 units wide and tall, which is 25% of the size of the coordinate<br>space:<br>`<v:shape … coordsize="200,200"`<br>`coordorigin="-100,-100"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,50, 50,50, 50,0 x e">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|dgmlayout<br>(Diagram Node<br>Layout Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the kind of automatic layout to apply to the diagram node.  This is only<br>meaningful if the shape is a node in an organization chart, which is denoted by the<br>`orgchart`value of theeditasattribute of thegroupelement.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … dgmlayout="1">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_DiagramLayoutsimple type<br>(§19.2.3.10).|
|dgmlayoutmru<br>(Diagram Node<br>Recent Layout<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the kind of automatic layout most recently used on the child elements of the<br>diagram node.  This is only meaningful if the shape is a node in anorganization chart,<br>which is denoted by the`orgchart`value of theeditasattribute of thegroupelement.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … dgmlayout="1">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_DiagramLayoutsimple type<br>(§19.2.3.10).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|dgmnodekind<br>(Diagram Node<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies an optional, application-defined parameter that is intended to be used by the<br>application to tag different types of nodes in a diagram.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … dgmnodekind="1">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemainteger<br>datatype.|
|doubleclicknotify<br>(Double-click<br>Notification Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies that an event message is sent when a shape is double-clicked.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:doubleclicknotify="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|fillcolor(Fill Color)|Specifies the color to use for the fill.  Default is`white`.  If thefillelement (§19.1.2.5) is<br>present, itscolorattribute takes precedence.  Colors are typically specified as either a<br>named color, such as`red`, or six hexadecimal digits representing the red, green and blue<br>values of the color, such as`#00FF30`.  Full details are specified in the simple type<br>description.<br>[_Example_: This shape is red if its fill is visible:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>This is equivalent to:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="#ff0000" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|filled(Shape Fill<br>Toggle)|Specifies whether the closed path is filled.  Default is`true`.  This attribute is overridden<br>by the fillonattribute.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … filled="f"`<br>`fillcolor="red" …>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|forcedash(Force<br>Dashed Outline)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a dashed outline is used to draw a shape when a shape has no line or<br>fill.  Default is`false`.<br>Used by PresentationML placeholders to draw a dashed outline when there is no line and<br>no fill for a shape.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:forcedash="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|from(Curve<br>Starting Point)|Specifies the starting point of the line in the coordinate space of the parent element.<br>Default is "0,0".  If the parent is not a VML element, the default unit is a pixel.  Allowed<br>units are in, cm, mm, pt, pc and px.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:curve … from="10,10" … >`<br>`</v:curve>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|hr(Horizontal Rule<br>Toggle)|Specifies that a shape is a horizontal rule.  Default is`false`.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:hr="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|hralign(Horizontal<br>Rule Alignment)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the alignment of a horizontal rule.  Default is`left`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:hralign="center" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_HrAlignsimple type<br>(§19.2.3.16).|
|href(Hyperlink<br>Target)|Specifies a hyperlink URL target for the shape.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … href="http://www.openxmlformats.org" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|hrnoshade<br>(Horizontal Rule 3D<br>Shading Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies that the horizontal rule does not have 3-D shading. |hrpct(Horizontal<br>Rule Length<br>Percentage)|Specifies the length of a horizontal rule as a percentage of page width.  Default is 0.<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|`<v:shape … o:hrpct="85" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemafloatdatatype.|
|hrstd(Horizontal<br>Rule Standard<br>Display Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a shape is displayed as a standard horizontal rule.  Only applies ifhris<br>`true`.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:hrstd="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|id(Unique<br>Identifier)|Specifies a unique identifier that can be used to reference a VML object.<br>Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … id="myShape" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|insetmode(Text<br>Inset Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the application calculates the internal text margin instead of using the<br>insetattribute.  Default is`custom`.  This attribute is only meaningful for text boxes.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:insetmode="auto" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_InsetModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.17).|
|insetpen(Inset|Specifies that the border shall be displayed inside of the path defining the shape, rather|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Border From Path)|than along the path (the default border placement), or outside of the path as might be<br>done with an image.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … insetpen="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|ole(Embedded<br>Object Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the shape is an embedded object.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:ole="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalseBlanksimple type<br>(§20.1.2.6).|
|oleicon(Embedded<br>Object Icon Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether an embedded object is displayed as an icon.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:oleicon="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|oned(Shape Handle<br>Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the extra handles of a shape are hidden.  If`true`, hides all shape<br>handles except the top left and bottom right; that is, the same handles that are used for a<br>straight line segment.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:oned="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|opacity(Fill Color<br>Opacity)|Specifies the opacity of the primary fill color.  Default is 1.0.  This numeric value can also<br>be specified in 1/65536-ths if a trailing "f" is supplied.  [_Example_: A value of "52429f"<br>represents 52429/65536 or 0.8._end example_]<br>[_Example_: The red color is 25% opaque:<br>`<v:fill type="gradient" color="red"`<br>`color2="blue" opacity=".25">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>`opacity="1"`<br>`opacity=".25"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|preferrelative<br>(Relative Resize<br>Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the original size of an object is saved after reformatting.  Default is<br>`false`.  If`true`, the original size of the object is stored and all resizing is based on a<br>percentage of that original size.  Otherwise, each resizing resets the scale to 100%.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:preferrelative="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|print(Print Toggle)|Specifies whether the shape is printed.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … print="false" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|regroupid(Regroup<br>ID)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies a previous group for a shape.  An ID number is used to identify groups of shapes<br>that are no longer grouped.  This allows shapes to be regrouped programmatically.<br>[_Example_: The shape was part of a group identified by the ID 040754:<br>`<v:shape … o:regroupid="040754" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemainteger<br>datatype.|
|spid(Optional<br>String)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies an optional string that an application can use to Identify the particular shape.<br>Default is no value.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|spt(Optional<br>Number)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies an optional number that an application can use to associate the particular shape<br>with a defined shape type.  Default is 0.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemafloatdatatype.|
|strokecolor(Shape<br>Stroke Color)|Specifies the primary color of the brush to use to stroke the path of the shape.  Default is<br>`black`.  Thecolorattribute of thestrokeelement (§19.1.2.21) overrides this.  Colors are<br>typically specified as either a named color, such as`red`, or six hexadecimal digits<br>representing the red, green and blue values of the color, such as`#00FF30`.  Full details<br>are specified in the simple type description.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … strokecolor="red" …>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|stroked(Shape<br>Stroke Toggle)|Specifies whether the path defining the shape is stroked with a solid line.  Thestroke<br>element (§19.1.2.21) defines other strokes.  Theonattribute of thestrokeelement<br>overrides this attribute.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="red"`<br>`stroked="false" strokecolor="blue"…>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|strokeweight<br>(Shape Stroke<br>Weight)|Specifies the width of the brush to use to stroke the path.  Default is 1 point.  If a number<br>is given without units, the emu is used.  Theweightattribute of thestrokeelement<br>(§19.1.2.21) overrides this attribute.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … strokeweight="3pt" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|style(Shape Styling<br>Properties)|Specifies the CSS2 styling properties of the shape. The CSS2 (Cascading Style Sheets, Level<br>2) specification, a Recommendation of the World Wide Web Consortium, is available<br>here:http://www.w3.org/TR/REC-CSS2.<br>This attribute uses a semi-colon delimited list of “name:value” pairs, the syntax defined<br>by section 4.1.8 of the CSS2 specification for grouped declarations (without the<br>surrounding braces). The following tables define the allowed properties and the VML<br>treatment of each.|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|||[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … style=’position:absolute;width:100pt;height:50pt’ …`<br>`>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>**Property**<br>**Description**<br>`flip`<br>Specifies that the orientation of a shape is flipped.  Default is no<br>value.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`x`- Flip along the y-axis, reversing the x-coordinates.<br><br>`y`- Flip along the x-axis, reversing the y-coordinates.<br><br>`xy`- Flip along both the y- and x-axis.<br><br>`yx`- Flip along both the x- and y-axis.<br>`height`<br>Specifies the height of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.<br>`left`<br>Specifies the position of the left of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element left of it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units> - A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.<br>`margin-bottom`<br>Specifies the position of the bottom of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:||
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`flip`|Specifies that the orientation of a shape is flipped.  Default is no<br>value.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`x`- Flip along the y-axis, reversing the x-coordinates.<br><br>`y`- Flip along the x-axis, reversing the y-coordinates.<br><br>`xy`- Flip along both the y- and x-axis.<br><br>`yx`- Flip along both the x- and y-axis.|
|||`height`|Specifies the height of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.|
|||`left`|Specifies the position of the left of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element left of it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.
