Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units> - A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.|
|||`margin-bottom`|Specifies the position of the bottom of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`margin-left`|Specifies the position of the left of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`margin-right`|Specifies the position of the right of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units> - A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`margin-top`|Specifies the position of the top of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|||`mso-position-`<br>`horizontal`|Specifies the horizontal positioning data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`absolute`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`absolute`<br><br>`left`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`right`<br><br>`inside`<br><br>`outside`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`horizontal-`<br>`relative`|Specifies relative horizontal position data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  This modifies the`mso-`<br>`position-horizontal`property.  Default is`text`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`margin`<br><br>`page`<br><br>`text`<br><br>`char`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`vertical`|Specifies the vertical positioning data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`absolute`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`absolute`<br><br>`top`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`bottom`<br><br>`inside`<br><br>`outside`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`vertical-`<br>`relative`|Specifies relative vertical position data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  This modifies the`mso-`<br>`position-vertical`property.  Default is`text`.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`margin`<br><br>`page`<br><br>`text`<br><br>`line`||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-`<br>`bottom`|Specifies the distance from the bottom of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-left`|Specifies the distance from the left side of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-right`|Specifies the distance from the right side of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-top`|Specifies the distance from the top of the shape to the text that<br>wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different from<br>the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the shape<br>to include the margin areas.  This property does not change the<br>origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`edited`|Specifies whether the wrap coordinates were customized by the<br>user.  If the wrap coordinates are generated by an editor, this<br>property is`true`; otherwise they were customized by a user.<br>Default is`false`.||
|||`mso-wrap-style`|Specifies the wrapping mode for text in shapes in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`square`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`square`- Wraps text inside the shape in a square.<br><br>`none`- Text does not wrap.||
|||`position`|Specifies the kind of positioning used to place an element.<br>Default is`static`.  When the element is contained inside a group,<br>this property shall be`absolute`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`static`- The element is positioned according to the<br>normal flow of the page.  The`top`and`left`properties<br>are ignored.  If the object is anchored inline, this value is<br>used.<br><br>`absolute`- The element is positioned relative to the<br>parent, using the`top`and`left`properties.<br><br>`relative`- The element is positioned according to the<br>normal flow of the page, but the`top`and`left`properties<br>are used.  The overlap of overlapping elements is<br>governed by the`z-index`property.||
|||`rotation`|Specifies the angle that a shape is rotated, in degrees.  Default is<br>0.  Positive angles are clockwise.||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|||`top`|Specifies the position of the top of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element above it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`visibility`|Specifies whether a shape is displayed.  Only`inherit`and`hidden`<br>are used; any other values are mapped to`inherit`.  Default is<br>`inherit`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`hidden`- The shape is not visible, but is still part of the<br>flow of the objects in the browser.  Mouse events are not<br>processed.<br><br>`inherit`- The visibility state is inherited from the parent<br>of the shape.||
|||`width`|Specifies the width of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`z-index`|Specifies the display order of overlapping shapes.  Default is 0.<br>This property shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Uses the order that the shapes appear in the page,<br>bottom to top.<br><br><order>- A number that represents the stacking<br>precedence.  Shapes with higher numbers are placed on<br>top of those with lower numbers.  Negative numbers are<br>allowed.||

|**Attributes**||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|||The following properties are only used by thetextboxelement (§19.1.2.22):<br>**Property**<br>**Description**<br>`direction`<br>Specifies the direction of the text in the textbox.  Default is`ltr`.<br>This property is superceded by the`mso-direction-alt`property<br>if that is specified.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`ltr`- Text is displayed left-to-right.<br><br>`rtl`- Text is displayed right-to-left.<br>`layout-flow`<br>Determines the flow of the text layout in a textbox.  Default is<br>`horizontal`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`horizontal`- Text is displayed horizontally.<br><br>`vertical`- Text is displayed vertically.<br><br>`vertical-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>vertically.<br><br>`horizontal-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>horizontally.<br>`mso-direction-`<br>`alt`<br>Specifies an alternate direction for text in textboxes.  Overrides<br>the`direction`property.  The only allowed value is`context`.<br>`mso-fit-shape-`<br>`to-text`<br>Specifies whether the shape stretches to fit the text in the<br>textbox.  Default is`false`.<br>`mso-fit-text-`<br>`to-shape`<br>Specifies whether the text stretches to fit the textbox.  Default is<br>`false`.<br>`mso-layout-`<br>`flow-alt`<br>Specifies the alternate layout flow for text in textboxes.  This<br>property is used instead of`layout-flow`when the layout flow is<br>from bottom to top for non-ideographic languages.  Its only value<br>is`bottom-to-top`.<br>`mso-next-`<br>`textbox`<br>Specifies the ID of the next textbox in a series.  Used to keep track<br>of a set of linked textboxes.  Default is no value.<br>`mso-rotate`<br>Specifies a specific rotation value for text in a textbox.  Default is<br>0.  Allowed values are:<br><br>0<br><br>90<br><br>180||
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`direction`|Specifies the direction of the text in the textbox.  Default is`ltr`.<br>This property is superceded by the`mso-direction-alt`property<br>if that is specified.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`ltr`- Text is displayed left-to-right.<br><br>`rtl`- Text is displayed right-to-left.|
|||`layout-flow`|Determines the flow of the text layout in a textbox.  Default is<br>`horizontal`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`horizontal`- Text is displayed horizontally.<br><br>`vertical`- Text is displayed vertically.<br><br>`vertical-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>vertically.<br><br>`horizontal-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>horizontally.|
|||`mso-direction-`<br>`alt`|Specifies an alternate direction for text in textboxes.  Overrides<br>the`direction`property.  The only allowed value is`context`.|
|||`mso-fit-shape-`<br>`to-text`|Specifies whether the shape stretches to fit the text in the<br>textbox.  Default is`false`.|
|||`mso-fit-text-`<br>`to-shape`|Specifies whether the text stretches to fit the textbox.  Default is<br>`false`.|
|||`mso-layout-`<br>`flow-alt`|Specifies the alternate layout flow for text in textboxes.  This<br>property is used instead of`layout-flow`when the layout flow is<br>from bottom to top for non-ideographic languages.  Its only value<br>is`bottom-to-top`.|
|||`mso-next-`<br>`textbox`|Specifies the ID of the next textbox in a series.  Used to keep track<br>of a set of linked textboxes.  Default is no value.|
|||`mso-rotate`|Specifies a specific rotation value for text in a textbox.  Default is<br>0.  Allowed values are:<br><br>0<br><br>90<br><br>180|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>-90|
|||`mso-text-scale`|Specifies the scaling factor for fitting text to shapes.  Default is 0.<br>This property is only used if`mso-fit-text-to-shape`is`true`.|
|||`v-text-anchor`|Specifies the vertical anchoring of text in a textbox.  Default is<br>`top`.  The alignment of a text anchor only becomes evident if`mso-`<br>`fit-text-to-shape`is`false`.  This property is different from the<br>`vertical-align`CSS property, which is used for ideographic<br>languages. Allowed values are:<br><br>`top`<br><br>`middle`<br><br>`bottom`<br><br>`top-center`<br><br>`middle-center`<br><br>`bottom-center`<br><br>`top-baseline`<br><br>`bottom-baseline`<br><br>`top-center-baseline`<br><br>`bottom-center-baseline`|
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`font`|Specifies a compound value of font settings.  Default is no value.<br>The values are the same as those of the CSS font property.  The<br>order of definitions in the string is: font-style, font-variant, font-<br>weight, font-size, line-height, font-family.|
|||`font-family`|Specifies the family of the font.  Default is no value.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS font-family property.|
|||`font-size`|Specifies the size of the font.  Default is no value.  The font size is<br>defined in points.  The values are the same as those of the CSS<br>font-size property.|
|||`font-style`|Specifies the amount of slant for a font.  Default is`normal`.  The<br>values are the same as those of the CSS font-style property.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`normal`<br><br>`italic`<br><br>`oblique`- Treated the same as italic.|
|||`font-variant`|Specifies the variant style of a font.  Default is`normal`.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS font-variant property.  Allowed<br>values are:|

|**Attributes**||||**Description**|**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|||||<br>`normal`<br><br>`small-caps`|||
|||`font-weight`||Specifies the thickness of the letters of the font.  Default is<br>`normal`.  The values are the same as those of the CSS font-weight<br>property.  Allowed values are:<br>**Value**<br>**Description**<br>`normal`<br>`lighter`<br>`100`<br>`200`<br>`300`<br>`400`<br>Treated as non-bold.<br>`bold`<br>`bolder`<br>`500`<br>`600`<br>`700`<br>`800`<br>`900`<br>Treated as bold.|||
|||||**Value**|**Description**||
|||||`normal`<br>`lighter`<br>`100`<br>`200`<br>`300`<br>`400`|Treated as non-bold.||
|||||`bold`<br>`bolder`<br>`500`<br>`600`<br>`700`<br>`800`<br>`900`|Treated as bold.||
|||`mso-text-`<br>`shadow`||Specifies whether a shadow is applied to the text on a text path.<br>Default is`false`.|||
|||`text-`<br>`decoration`||Specifies the style of text decoration.  Default is`none`.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS text-decoration property.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`underline`<br><br>`overline`<br><br>`line-through`<br><br>`blink`|||
|||`v-rotate-`||Specifies whether the letters of the text are rotated<br>counterclockwise by90 degrees.  Default is`false`.|||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|||`letters`||
|||`v-same-letter-`<br>`heights`|Specifies whether all letters are the same height regardless of<br>initial case.  If`true`, the lowercase letters are stretched to the<br>height of the uppercase letters.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-text-align`|Specifies the alignment of text.  Default is`left`.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`left`<br><br>`right`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`justify`<br><br>`letter-justify`- Distributes the extra space between<br>the letters.<br><br>`stretch-justify`- Stretches the letters to fill in the<br>space.|
|||`v-text-kern`|Specifies whether kerning is turned on.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-text-reverse`|Specifies whether the layout order of rows is reversed.  Default is<br>`false`.  This is used for vertical text layout.|
|||`v-text-`<br>`spacing-mode`|Specifies the mode for letter spacing.  Default is`tightening`.<br>This property determines whether space is removed between<br>each letter (`tightening`) or added between each letter<br>(`tracking`).  The amount of letter spacing change is defined by<br>the`v-text-spacing`property.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`tightening`<br><br>`tracking`|
|||`v-text-spacing`|Specifies the amount of spacing for text in 100ths of single line<br>spacing.  Default is 100.|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|||<br>`height`<br><br>`left`<br><br>`margin-left`<br><br>`margin-top`<br><br>`position`<br><br>`rotation`<br><br>`top`<br><br>`visibility`<br><br>`width`<br><br>`z-index`<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.||
|target(Hyperlink<br>Display Target)||Specifies a frame<br>values are:||
|||**Value**|**Description**|
|||<targetname>|String containing the name of the frame or window in which to load<br>the document.|
|||`_blank`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into a new blank<br>window. This window is not named.|
|||`_media`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the browser’s<br>multimedia pane.|
|||`_parent`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the immediate<br>parent of the document containing the link.|
|||`_search`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the browser's<br>search pane.|
|||`_self`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the window in<br>which the link was clicked (the active window).|
|||`_top`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the topmost<br>window.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||datatype.|
|title(Shape Title)|Specifies the text displayed when the mouse pointer moves over the shape.  Default is no<br>value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … title="tooltip" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|to(Curve Ending<br>Point)|Specifies the ending point of the line in the coordinate space of the parent element.<br>Default is "30,20". If the parent is not a VML element, the default unit is a pixel.  Allowed<br>units are in, cm, mm, pt, pc and px.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:curve … to="40,40" … >`<br>`</v:curve>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|userdrawn(Exists<br>In Master Slide)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the user has added the shape to a master slide.  Default is`false`.  Used<br>by PresentationML.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:userdrawn="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|userhidden(Hide<br>Script Anchors)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a script anchor is hidden.  Default is`false`.  If`true`, script anchors stay<br>hidden even if the shape is otherwise visible.  A script anchor is the visual representation<br>of a script that when displayed in an application.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:userhidden="true" … >`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|wrapcoords(Shape<br>Bounding Polygon)|Specifies the bounding polygon that surrounds a shape.  This is specified using a comma-<br>delimited list of x and y coordinates: "x1,y1,x2,y2,x3,y3,…"  This is used when text is<br>tightly wrapped around a shape.  Default is no value until themso-wrap-mode style<br>attribute is set to`tight`or`through`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape …`<br>`wrapcoords="0,0 0,200, 200,200, 200,0" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Curve) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.2.4 f (Single Formula)

This element defines a single value as the result of the evaluation of an expression. The expression is defined by the eqn attribute and has the general form of an operation followed by up to three arguments, which consist of adjustment values (see the adj attribute of the shape element (§19.1.2.19)), the results of earlier formulas, fixed numbers or pre-defined values.  Each f value is referenced using "@" followed by a number corresponding to the zero-based index for that value in the list of f elements.  [ _Example_ : For example, the value of the second f element is referenced as "@2". _end example_ ]

[ _Example_ : The following defines a blue arrow pointing to the right:

- `<v:shape coordsize="21600,21600" adj="18000,5400,10800"`

- `path="m @0,0 l @0,@1 0,@1 0,@3 @0,@3 @0,21600 21600,10800 x e" style='left:50pt;top:50pt;width:90pt;height:30pt'`

- `fillcolor="#4f81bd" strokecolor="#4f81bd" strokeweight="2pt">`

- `<v:formulas>`

- `<v:f eqn="val #0"/>`

- `<v:f eqn="val #1"/>`

- `<v:f eqn="val #2"/>`

```
   <v:f eqn="sum height 0 #1"/>
```

```
   <v:f eqn="sum #2 0 #1"/>
   <v:f eqn="sum width 0 #0"/>
```

```
   <v:f eqn="prod @5 @4 #2"/>
   <v:f eqn="sum width 0 @6"/>
  </v:formulas>
</v:shape>
```

The shape looks like this:

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|eqn(Equation)||Specifies a single formula, which consists of a named operation followed by up to three<br>parameters, typically described as v, P1 and P2.  Up to 128 formulas can be specified.<br>These operations are defined (calculation accuracy is discussed below):<br>Operation<br>Description<br>`val`<br>v<br>Returns the supplied value.  Exact.<br>`sum`<br>v + P1 −P2<br>Addition and subtraction.  Exact.<br>`product`<br>v × P1/P2<br>Multiplication and division.  Rounds up.<br>`mid`<br>(v + P1)/2<br>Simple average.  Rounds toward zero.<br>`abs`<br>|v|<br>Absolute value.  Exact.<br>`min`<br>min(v, P1)<br>The lesser of two values.  Exact.<br>`max`<br>max(v, P1)<br>The greater of two values.  Exact.<br>`if`<br>v > 0 ? 𝑃1 ∶𝑃2<br>Conditional slection.  Exact.<br>`mod`<br>√v2 + P12 + P12<br>Modulus.  Inexact.<br>`atan2`<br>atan2(P1, v)<br>Trigonometric arc tangent of a quotient.  Result is<br>in"fd"units or fractional degrees-degrees × 216.||
|||Operation|Description|
|||`val`|v<br>Returns the supplied value.  Exact.|
|||`sum`|v + P1 −P2<br>Addition and subtraction.  Exact.|
|||`product`|v × P1/P2<br>Multiplication and division.  Rounds up.|
|||`mid`|(v + P1)/2<br>Simple average.  Rounds toward zero.|
|||`abs`||v|<br>Absolute value.  Exact.|
|||`min`|min(v, P1)<br>The lesser of two values.  Exact.|
|||`max`|max(v, P1)<br>The greater of two values.  Exact.|
|||`if`|v > 0 ? 𝑃1 ∶𝑃2<br>Conditional slection.  Exact.|
|||`mod`|√v2 + P12 + P12<br>Modulus.  Inexact.|
|||`atan2`|atan2(P1, v)<br>Trigonometric arc tangent of a quotient.  Result is<br>in"fd"units or fractional degrees-degrees × 216.|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||Inexact.|
|||`sin`|v × sin(P1)<br>Sine.  Argument is in "fd" units or fractional<br>degrees - degrees× 216.  Inexact.|
|||`cos`|v × cos(P1)<br>Cosine.  Argument is in "fd" units or fractional<br>degrees - degrees× 216.  Inexact.|
|||`cosatan2`|v × cos(atan2(P2, P1))<br>Preserves full accuracy in the intermediate<br>calculation.  Inexact.|
|||`sinatan2`|v × sin(atan2(P2, P1))<br>Preserves full accuracy in the intermediate<br>calculation.  Inexact.|
|||`sqrt`|√v<br>Square root.  Result is positive and rounds down.<br>Inexact.|
|||`sumangle`|v + P1 × 216 −P2 × 216<br>Adds an existing angle in fd units (v) to two other<br>angles specified in degrees.  P1 and P2 are scaled<br>by216.  Exact.|
|||`ellipse`|P2√1 −~~(~~<br>v<br>P1<br>~~)~~<br>2<br>The eccentricity formula for an ellipse, where v is<br>length of the semiminor axis and P1 is the length<br>of the semimajor axis.  Inexact.|
|||`tan`|v × tan(P1)<br>Tangent.  Argument is in "fd" units or fractional<br>degrees - degrees× 216.  Inexact.|

**Attributes Description** down (towards -infinity) and should perform internal calculations with this form of rounding. The arguments used in the evaluation of a formula are normally either fixed numbers, the result of the evaluation of a previous formula or an adjust value - the value of the corresponding entry in the shape adj attribute.  Fixed numbers shall be positive integral values in the range 0 to 65535 (unsigned 16-bit numbers).  The following named values are defined:

|Value|Description|
|---|---|
|`@n`|The value of formula n, where n is the zero-<br>based index of the formula in the list of<br>formulas.  n shall be less than the current<br>formula index.|
|`#n`|Adjustment (adj) value n.  n shall be in the<br>range 0 to 7.|
|`width`|The width defined by thecoordsizeattribute.|
|`height`|The height defined by thecoordsize<br>attribute.|
|`xcenter`|The x ordinate of the center of the coordinate<br>space defined bycoordoriginandcoordsize.|
|`ycenter`|The y ordinate of the center of the coordinate<br>space defined bycoordoriginandcoordsize.|
|`xlimo`|The x value of thelimoattribute (see also the<br>pathelement (§19.1.2.14)).|
|`ylimo`|The y value of thelimoattribute (see also the<br>pathelement (§19.1.2.14)).|
|`hasstroke`|1 if the shape has a stroke operation, 0 if it<br>does not, as determined by theonattribute<br>of thestrokeelement (§19.1.2.21).|
|`hasfill`|1 if the shape has a fill operation, 0 if it does<br>not, as determined by theonattribute of the<br>fillelement (§19.1.2.5).|
|`pixellinewid`<br>`th`|The line width in output device pixels.  This is<br>used to outset lines from the edge of a<br>rectangle on the assumption that the<br>implementation draws to lower right pixel in<br>preference to the upper left pixel when a line<br>is on a pixel boundary.|
|`pixelwidth`|The width of the shape in device pixels (i.e.,<br>thecoordsizewidth transformed into device|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||space).|
|||`pixelheight`|The height of thecoordsizein device pixels.|
|||`emuwidth`|The width of thecoordsizein EMUs.|
|||`emuheight`|The height of thecoordsizein EMUs.|
|||`emuwidth2`|Half the width of thecoordsizein EMUs.|
|||`emuheight2`|Half the height of thecoordsizein EMUs.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_F) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.2.5 fill (Shape Fill Properties)

This element specifies how the path should be filled if something beyond a solid color fill is desired.  The attributes of the fill element can be used to describe a powerful set of image- or gradient-based fill patterns.   Extensions to the VML fill definition are encoded as sub-elements of fill .

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|alignshape(Align<br>Image With Shape)|Specifies whether an image aligns with the shape.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_: The image displayed in the shape is not rotated even though the shape is<br>rotated 30 degrees:<br>`<v:shape coordorigin="0,0"`<br>`coordsize="200,200"`<br>`style="top:1;left:1;width:50;`<br>`height:50;rotation:30"`<br>`path="m 1,1 l 1,200, 200,200, 200,1 x e">`<br>`<v:fill alignshape="false" type="frame"`<br>`src="myimage.gif">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>`</v:shape>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||Applied to a simple square the fill looks like this:<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|althref(Alternate<br>Image Reference<br>Location)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Defines an alternate reference for an image in Macintosh PICT format.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … althref="myimage.pcz" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|angle(Gradient<br>Angle)|Specifies the direction of a gradient.  The vector of a gradient is perpendicular to the<br>vector of the blend direction from one color to another.  The default value is zero<br>degrees, which is a horizontal vector from left to right.  Positive angles rotate the<br>gradient in a counter-clockwise direction.<br>[_Example_: The fill is composed of a 45-degree gradient of two colors.  Blue is in the top<br>left corner and red is in the bottom right corner.<br>`<v:fill type="gradient" color="red"`<br>`color2="blue" angle="45">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemadecimal<br>datatype.|
|aspect(Image<br>Aspect Ratio)|Specifies how the fill image aspect ratio is preserved.  Default is`ignore`.  Allowed values<br>are:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||<br>`ignore`- Ignore aspect ratio.<br><br>`atleast`- At least as large as defined by thesizeattribute.<br><br>`atmost`- No larger than that defined by thesizeattribute.<br>In each case, thesizeattribute is adjusted to preserve the aspect ratio of the image.<br>[_Example_: The image that makes up the fill is no larger than 20 points by 20 points,<br>limiting the size of the tiles inside the shape.<br>`<v:fill type="tile" aspect="atmost"`<br>`size="20pt,20pt" src="myimage.gif">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ImageAspectsimple type<br>(§19.1.3.5).|
|color(Primary<br>Color)|Specifies the main fill color; functions the same as thefillcolorattribute of theshape<br>element (§19.1.2.19). This attribute overrides the shape'sfillcolor.  Default is`white`.<br>Colors are typically specified as either a named color, such as`red`, or six hexadecimal<br>digits representing the red, green and blue values of the color, such as`#00FF30`.  Full<br>details are specified in the simple type description.<br>[_Example_: The shape is blue:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="red" … >`<br>`<v:fill color="blue"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|color2(Secondary<br>Color)|Specifies the secondary fill color, used when a filltypeis a pattern or a gradient.  Default<br>is`white`.  Colors are typically specified as either a named color, such as`red`, or six<br>hexadecimal digits representing the red, green and blue values of the color, such as<br>`#00FF30`.  Full details are specified in the simple type description.<br>[_Example_: The shape is filled with a horizontal gradient with red at the bottom and blue<br>on top:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:fill type="gradient"`<br>`color="red" color2="blue">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|colors<br>(Intermediate<br>Colors)|Specifies an array of comma-separated percentage-color pairs that define intermediate<br>colors and their positions in a gradient fill.  The primary color, specified either by the<br>fillcolorattribute of theshapeelement (§19.1.2.19) or thecolorattribute of thefill<br>element (§19.1.2.5), is used at the 0% endpoint.  The secondary color, specified by the<br>color2attribute of thefillelement (§19.1.2.5), is used at the 100% endpoint.  The<br>numeric values can also be specified in 1/65536-ths if a trailing "f" is supplied.  [_Example_:<br>For example, a value of "52429f" represents 52429/65536 or 0.8._end example_]<br>[_Example_: The shape is filled with a horizontal gradient colored, from bottom to top, red,<br>yellow, green, blue:<br>`<v:fill type="gradient"`<br>`color="red" color2="blue"`<br>`colors="30% yellow,70% green">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|detectmouseclick<br>(Detect Mouse<br>Click)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a mouse click is detected on the fill of a shape.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|focus(Gradient<br>Center)|Specifies the center starting position of a gradient.  Valuesare in therange 100% to -<br>100%.  Default is 0.<br>A value of 100% or -100% reverses the direction of the gradient (in effect swappingcolor<br>andcolor2).  A value of 50% changes the gradient so thatcoloris at both ends and|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||color2is in the middle.  A value of -50% changes the gradient so thatcolor2is at both<br>ends andcoloris in the middle.<br>[_Example_: The shape is filled with a horizontal gradient with red at both ends and blue in<br>the middle:<br>`<v:fill type="gradient"`<br>`color="red" color2="blue"`<br>`focus="50%">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|focusposition<br>(Radial Gradient<br>Center)|Specifies the position of the center rectangle of a radial gradient.  The vector is a fraction<br>of the width and height of the shape.  The first is a percentage of the fill to the left edge;<br>the second is a percentage of the fill to the top.  Default is 0,0.  To position a radial fill at<br>the center of a shape, use a value of 50%,50%.<br>[_Example_: The shape is filled with a rectangular gradient positioned in the top-left<br>quadrant of the shape.  The interior of the gradient is blue and the exterior is red:<br>`<v:fill type="gradientradial"`<br>`color="red" color2="blue"`<br>`focusposition="25%,25%">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|focussize(Radial<br>Gradient Size)|Specifies the size of the center rectangle of a radial gradient.  The vector is a fraction of<br>the width and height of the shape.  The first is a percentage of the fill to the right edge;<br>the second is a percentage of the fill to the bottom.  Default is 0,0.<br>Afocussizevalue of 100%,100% and afocuspositionof 0,0 makescolor2dominate the<br>gradient completely.  Small values of around 10%,10% are recommended for balanced|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||gradients.<br>[_Example_: The shape is filled with a rectangular gradient positioned in the top-left<br>quadrant of the shape.  The interior of the gradient is blue and the exterior is red.  The<br>red portion is wider on the bottom and right sides of the blue region.  The pure blue<br>region is 25% the width and 25% the height of the shape:<br>`<v:fill type="gradientradial"`<br>`color="red" color2="blue"`<br>`focussize="25%,25%"`<br>`focusposition="25%,25%">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|href(Hyperlink<br>Target)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the URL to the original image file.  Used only if the picture has been linked and<br>embedded.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … o:href="myimage.gif" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|id(Relationship to<br>Part)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the relationship ID of the relationship to the image used for this fill.  The<br>specified relationship shall be of type<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/imageor<br>the document shall be considered non-conformant.<br>[_Example_: The markup specifies the associated relationship part with relationship ID<br>`rId10`contains the corresponding relationship information for the fill:<br>`< … r:id="rId10" />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|
|id(Unique<br>Identifier)|Specifies a unique identifier that can be used to reference a VML object.<br>Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … id="myShape" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|method(Gradient<br>Fill Method)|Specifies the method used to generate the transition fromcolortocolor2in a gradient<br>fill.  Default is`sigma`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill type="gradient"`<br>`color="red" color2="blue" method="any">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_FillMethodsimple type<br>(§19.1.3.3).|
|on(Fill Toggle)|Specifies whether to fill the shape.  Default is`true`.  This attribute overrides the shape's<br>fillattribute.<br>[_Example_: The shape has a transparent fill:<br>`<v:shape … fill="true" … >`<br>`<v:fill color="red" on="false">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|opacity(Primary<br>Color Opacity)|Specifies the opacity of the primary fill color.  Default is 1.0.  This numeric value can also<br>be specified in 1/65536-ths if a trailing "f" is supplied.  For example, a value of "52429f"<br>represents 52429/65536 or 0.8.<br>[_Example_: The red color is 25% opaque:<br>`<v:fill type="gradient" color="red"`<br>`color2="blue" opacity=".25">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>`opacity="1"`<br>`opacity=".25"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|opacity2<br>(Secondary Color<br>Opacity)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the opacity of the secondary fill color.  Default is 1.0.  This numeric value can<br>also be specified in 1/65536-ths if a trailing "f" is supplied.  [_Example_: For example, a<br>value of "52429f" represents 52429/65536 or 0.8._end example_]<br>[_Example_: The blue color is 25% opaque:<br>`<v:fill type="gradient" color="red"`<br>`color2="blue" o:opacity2=".25">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>`opacity2="1"`<br>`opacity2=".25"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|origin(Fill Image<br>Origin)|Specifies the position of the origin of a fill image as a point relative to the top left corner<br>of the image.  The vector is a fraction of the width and height of the image.  Default is the<br>center of the image.  These numeric values can also be specified in 1/65536-ths if a|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||trailing "f" is supplied.  [_Example_: For example, a value of "52429f" represents<br>52429/65536 or 0.8._end example_]<br>[_Example_: The origin of the image is 25% to the right and 25% above the image's top left<br>corner:<br>`<v:fill type="tile" src="myimage.gif"`<br>`origin="0.25,-0.25">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|position(Fill Image<br>Position)|Specifies the position of the origin of a fill image as a point within its containing shape.<br>The vector is a fraction of the width and height of the shape.  These numeric values can<br>also be specified in 1/65536-ths if a trailing "f" is supplied.  For example, a value of<br>"52429f" represents 52429/65536 or 0.8.<br>[_Example_: The origin of the image is positioned 25% to the right of the left edge of the<br>shape and 25% down from the shape's top:<br>`<v:fill type="tile" src="myimage.gif"`<br>`position="0.25,0.25">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|recolor(Recolor Fill<br>as Picture)|Specifies that the fill uses an image.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill r:id="rId4" o:title="MyPic" recolor="true"`<br>`type="frame">`<br>`</v:fill>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|relid(Relationship<br>to Part)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the relationship ID of the relationship to the image.  The specified relationship<br>shall be of type<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/imageor<br>the document shall be considered non-conformant.<br>[_Example_: The markup specifies the associated relationship part with relationship ID<br>`rId10`contains the corresponding relationship information:<br>`<v:fill … o:relid="rId10" …>`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|
|rotate(Rotate Fill<br>with Shape)|Specifies whether the fill is rotated with the shape.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_: The gradient is rotated with the shape:<br>`<v:fill color2="white" focus="100%" rotate="true"`<br>`type="gradient">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>`rotate="true"`<br>`rotate="false"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|size(Fill Image Size)|Specifies the size of the fill image.  Default is the native image pixel size.<br>[_Example_: The image is reduced in size disproportionately:<br>`<v:fill type="tile" src="myimage.gif"`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`size="25pt,15pt">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|src(Fill Image<br>Source)|Specifies the URL specifying the fill image to use.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … src="myimage.gif" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|title(Title)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the title of an embedded fill image.  This is typically set to the comment<br>property of the image, which is often blank.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … o:title="alt text" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|type(Fill Type)|Specifies the kind of fill.  Default is`solid`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`solid`<br><br>`gradient`<br><br>`gradientradial`<br><br>`tile`<br><br>`pattern`<br><br>`frame`<br>[_Example_: Applied to a simple square using the following fill element, the three gradient|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|
|---|---|---|
|||types look like this:<br>`<v:fill color="red" color2="blue"`<br>`type="solid">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>`type="solid"`<br>`type="gradient"`<br>`type="gradientradial"`<br>Applied to a simple square using the following fill elements, the three image types look<br>like this:<br>`<v:fill src="myimage.gif"`<br>`type="tile" size="50%,50%">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>`<v:fill src="myimage.gif"`<br>`type="frame" size="50%,50%">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>`<v:fill src="myimage.gif"`<br>`color="red" color2="blue"`<br>`type="pattern">`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_FillTypesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.4).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Fill) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_

## 19.1.2.6 formulas (Set of Formulas)

This element defines a set of formulas whose calculated values are referenced by other attributes.  Each formula is contained in a child f element (§19.1.2.4).

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Formulas) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.2.7 group (Shape Group)

This element is used to collect shapes and groups so they can be positioned and transformed as a single unit.  A group contains group , shapetype , shape, pre-defined shape - arc , curve , image , line , oval , polyline , rect , roundrect - and lock elements.

[ _Example_ : The following example defines a few basic parts of a flying saucer graphic.  The group consists of five shapes.  Each shape's position is determined within the coordinate space of the group, which is defined by the group's attributes.

- `<v:group id="saucer"`

- `style='position:relative;left:200;top:200;width:50;height:50'`

- `coordorigin="0,0" coordsize="6000,6000">`

- `<v:shape id="body"`

- `style='position:relative;left:234.75pt;top:208.875pt;`

- `width:235.25pt;height:128.875pt' coordsize="3765,2060"`

- `path="m1285,251l1126,469,580,1009,,1285,25,1412,93,1547,194,1673,`

- `1017,2026,2312,2060,3209,1756,3765,1388,3278,680,3059,319,2976,, 1285,251,1285,251xe"`

- `fillcolor="#bcbcd6" stroked="f">`

- `<v:path arrowok="t"/>`

- `</v:shape>`

- `<v:shape id="canopy"`

- `style='position:relative;left:314.625pt;top:140.5pt;`

- `width:104pt;height:102pt' coordsize="1663,1633"`

- `path="m0,1355l177,1498,353,1582,840,1633,1378,1498,1663,1295,`

- `1545,456,1260,10,1025,,656,260,253,874,,1355,,1355xe" fillcolor="#99ebff" stroked="f">`

- `<v:path arrowok="t"/>`

- `</v:shape>`

- `<v:shape id="light1"`

```
    style='position:relative;left:408.625pt;top:268.75pt;
```

- `width:24.25pt;height:27.375pt' coordsize="388,437"`

- `path="m209,0l34,101,,302,125,437,329,327,388,152,209,,209,0xe" fillcolor="#fff27f" stroked="f">`

- `<v:path arrowok="t"/>`

- `</v:shape>`

- `<v:shape id="light2"`

```
    style='position:relative;left:356.625pt;top:279.25pt;
```

- `width:28.875pt;height:30pt' coordsize="462,479"`

```
    path="m135,0l0,186,59,422,344,479,462,228,135,,135,0xe"
```

```
    fillcolor="#fff27f" stroked="f">
```

```
    <v:path arrowok="t"/>
```

```
  </v:shape>
```

```
  <v:shape id="light3"
```

```
    style='position:relative;left:302.625pt;top:274pt;
```

```
    width:23pt;height:23.625pt' coordsize="369,378"
```

```
    path="m0,59l226,,369,186,243,378,32,363,,59,,59xe"
```

```
    fillcolor="#fff27f" stroked="f">
```

```
    <v:path arrowok="t"/>
```

```
  </v:shape>
```

```
</v:group>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|allowincell(Allow<br>in Table Cell)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a shape can be placed in a table.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:allowincell="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|allowoverlap<br>(Allow Shape<br>Overlap)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a shape can overlap another shape.  Default is`true`.  If`false`, the<br>shape shifts left or right so as not to overlap another shape, similar to the behavior of the<br>HTML float attribute.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:allowoverlap="false" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|alt(Alternate Text)|Specifies alternative text describing the graphical object.  This text should provide a brief<br>description of the shape for use by accessibility tools.  Default is no value.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||[_Example_: The alt text describes the basic shape:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="red"`<br>`alt="Red rectangle">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>The alt text describes the contents of a shape displaying an image:<br>`<v:shape … alt="Picture of a sunset">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|borderbottomcolor<br>(Bottom Border<br>Color)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the bottom border color of an inline shape.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:borderbottomcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|borderleftcolor<br>(Border Left Color)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the left border color of an inline shape.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:borderleftcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|borderrightcolor<br>(Border Right Color)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the right border color of an inline shape.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:borderrightcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|bordertopcolor<br>(Border Top Color)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the top border color of an inline shape.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:bordertopcolor="red" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|bullet(Graphical<br>Bullet)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the shape is a graphical bullet.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:bullet="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|button(Button<br>Behavior Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a shape exhibits button press behavior on click.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:button="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|class(CSS<br>Reference)|Specifies a reference to the definition of a CSS style.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_: The snippets below are equivalent:<br>`…`<br>`.narrowstyle {width:50;height:100}`<br>`…`<br>`<v:shape … class="narrowstyle"`<br>`style="top:1;left:1">`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`</v:shape>`<br>`<v:shape … style="top:1;left:1;`<br>`width:50;height:100">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|coordorigin<br>(Coordinate Space<br>Origin)|Specifies the coordinate of the top left corner of the shape's coordinate space.  This<br>determines the position of the (0,0) coordinate space origin within the shape's bounding<br>box.  Default is "0,0", which places the (0,0) origin at the top left corner of the bounding<br>box.<br>This affects shape properties that specify coordinate positions, such as thepath<br>attribute.  Thus a path can be defined against a generic (0,0) origin and thecoordorigin<br>value translates the entire path within the shape's bounding space.<br>[_Example_: The horizontal and vertical coordinate space ranges from -100 to +100 because<br>the coordinate space (coordsize) is 200 by 200 and the top left coordinate is (-100,-100).<br>The (0,0) origin lies at the center of the shape's bounding box, as evidenced by the<br>position of the shape's path within the coordinate space:<br>`<v:shape … coordsize="200,200"`<br>`coordorigin="-100,-100"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,50, 50,50, 50,0 x e">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|coordsize<br>(Coordinate Space<br>Size)|Specifies the size of the shape's coordinate space in coordinate units.  Default is<br>"1000,1000".<br>The physical size of a coordinate unit length is determined by both the size of the<br>coordinate space (coordsize) and the size of the shape (stylewidth and height).  The<br>coordsizeattribute defines the number of horizontal and vertical subdivisions into which<br>the shape's bounding box is divided.  The combination ofcoordsizeandstyle<br>width/height effective scales the shape anisotropically.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||[_Example_: The path is 50 units wide and tall, which is 25% of the size of the coordinate<br>space:<br>`<v:shape … coordsize="200,200"`<br>`coordorigin="-100,-100"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,50, 50,50, 50,0 x e">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|dgmlayout<br>(Diagram Node<br>Layout Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the kind of automatic layout to apply to the diagram node.  This is only<br>meaningful if the shape is a node in an organization chart, which is denoted by the<br>`orgchart`value of theeditasattribute of thegroupelement.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … dgmlayout="1">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_DiagramLayoutsimple type<br>(§19.2.3.10).|
|dgmlayoutmru<br>(Diagram Node<br>Recent Layout<br>Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the kind of automatic layout most recently used on the child elements of the<br>diagram node.  This is only meaningful if the shape is a node in anorganization chart,<br>which is denoted by the`orgchart`value of theeditasattribute of thegroupelement.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … dgmlayout="1">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_DiagramLayoutsimple type<br>(§19.2.3.10).|
|dgmnodekind<br>(Diagram Node|Specifies an optional, application-defined parameter that is intended to be used by the<br>application to tag different types of nodes in a diagram.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Identifier)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … dgmnodekind="1">`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemainteger<br>datatype.|
|doubleclicknotify<br>(Double-click<br>Notification Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies that an event message is sent when a shape is double-clicked.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:doubleclicknotify="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|editas(Group<br>Diagram Type)|Specifies which diagram type the contained shapes represent.  This is used in conjunction<br>with thediagramelement (§19.2.2.8).  A value of`canvas`indicates that the group is a<br>regular group of shapes and does not represent a diagram.  Other values indicate that the<br>diagramelement and its children contain semantic information relevant to that type of<br>diagram, which is represented by the shapes in the group.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:group … editas="orgchart">`<br>`</v:group>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_EditAssimple type<br>(§19.1.3.1).|
|fillcolor(Fill Color)|Specifies the color to use for the fill.  Default is`white`.  If thefillelement (§19.1.2.5) is<br>present, itscolorattribute takes precedence.  Colors are typically specified as either a<br>named color, such as`red`, or six hexadecimal digits representing the red, green and blue<br>values of the color, such as`#00FF30`.  Full details are specified in the simple type<br>description.<br>[_Example_: This shape is red if its fill is visible:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="red" … >`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`</v:shape>`<br>This is equivalent to:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="#ff0000" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|filled(Shape Fill<br>Toggle)|Specifies whether the closed path is filled.  Default is`true`.  This attribute is overridden<br>by the fillonattribute.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … filled="f"`<br>`fillcolor="red" …>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|hr(Horizontal Rule<br>Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies that a shape is a horizontal rule.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:hr="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|hralign(Horizontal<br>Rule Alignment)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the alignment of a horizontal rule.  Default is`left`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:hralign="center" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_HrAlignsimple type<br>(§19.2.3.16).|
|href(Hyperlink<br>Target)|Specifies a hyperlink URL target for the shape.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … href="http://www.openxmlformats.org" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|hrnoshade<br>(Horizontal Rule 3D<br>Shading Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies that the horizontal rule does not have 3-D shading.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:hrnoshade="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|hrpct(Horizontal<br>Rule Length<br>Percentage)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the length of a horizontal rule as a percentage of page width.  Default is 0.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:hrpct="85" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemafloatdatatype.|
|hrstd(Horizontal<br>Rule Standard<br>Display Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a shape is displayed as a standard horizontal rule.  Only applies ifhris<br>`true`.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:hrstd="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|id(Unique<br>Identifier)|Specifies a unique identifier that can be used to reference a VML object.<br>Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … id="myShape" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|insetmode(Text<br>Inset Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the application calculates the internal text margin instead of using the<br>insetattribute.  Default is`custom`.  This attribute is only meaningful for text boxes.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:insetmode="auto" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_InsetModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.17).|
|oned(Shape Handle<br>Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the extra handles of a shape are hidden.  If`true`, hides all shape<br>handles except the top left and bottom right; that is, the same handles that are used for a<br>straight line segment.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:oned="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|print(Print Toggle)|Specifies whether the shape is printed.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|||`<v:shape … print="false" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).||
|regroupid(Regroup<br>ID)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office||Specifies a previous group for a shape.  An ID number is used to identify groups of shapes<br>that are no longer grouped.  This allows shapes to be regrouped programmatically.<br>[_Example_: The shape was part of a group identified by the ID 040754:<br>`<v:shape … o:regroupid="040754" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemainteger<br>datatype.||
|spid(Optional<br>String)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office||Specifies an optional string that an application can use to Identify the particular shape.<br>Default is no value.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.||
|style(Shape Styling<br>Properties)||Specifies the CSS2 styling properties of the shape. The CSS2 (Cascading Style Sheets, Level<br>2) specification, a Recommendation of the World Wide Web Consortium, is available<br>here:http://www.w3.org/TR/REC-CSS2.<br>This attribute uses a semi-colon delimited list of “name:value” pairs, the syntax defined<br>by section 4.1.8 of the CSS2 specification for grouped declarations (without the<br>surrounding braces). The following tables define the allowed properties and the VML<br>treatment of each.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … style=’position:absolute;width:100pt;height:50pt’ …`<br>`>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>**Property**<br>**Description**<br>`flip`<br>Specifies that the orientation of a shape is flipped.  Default is no||
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`flip`|Specifies that the orientation of a shape is flipped.  Default is no|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||value.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`x`- Flip along the y-axis, reversing the x-coordinates.<br><br>`y`- Flip along the x-axis, reversing the y-coordinates.<br><br>`xy`- Flip along both the y- and x-axis.<br><br>`yx`- Flip along both the x- and y-axis.||
|||`height`|Specifies the height of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`left`|Specifies the position of the left of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element left of it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units> - A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`margin-bottom`|Specifies the position of the bottom of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`margin-left`|Specifies the position of the left of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`margin-right`|Specifies the position of the right of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units> - A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`margin-top`|Specifies the position of the top of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`horizontal`|Specifies the horizontal positioning data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`absolute`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`absolute`<br><br>`left`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`right`<br><br>`inside`||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`outside`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`horizontal-`<br>`relative`|Specifies relative horizontal position data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  This modifies the`mso-`<br>`position-horizontal`property.  Default is`text`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`margin`<br><br>`page`<br><br>`text`<br><br>`char`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`vertical`|Specifies the vertical positioning data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`absolute`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`absolute`<br><br>`top`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`bottom`<br><br>`inside`<br><br>`outside`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`vertical-`<br>`relative`|Specifies relative vertical position data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  This modifies the`mso-`<br>`position-vertical`property.  Default is`text`.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`margin`<br><br>`page`<br><br>`text`<br><br>`line`||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-`<br>`bottom`|Specifies the distance from the bottom of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-left`|Specifies the distance from the left side of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`|Specifies the distance from the right side of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0pt.  Thispropertyis different||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|||`distance-right`|from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-top`|Specifies the distance from the top of the shape to the text that<br>wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different from<br>the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the shape<br>to include the margin areas.  This property does not change the<br>origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`edited`|Specifies whether the wrap coordinates were customized by the<br>user.  If the wrap coordinates are generated by an editor, this<br>property is`true`; otherwise they were customized by a user.<br>Default is`false`.||
|||`mso-wrap-style`|Specifies the wrapping mode for text in shapes in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`square`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`square`- Wraps text inside the shape in a square.<br><br>`none`- Text does not wrap.||
|||`position`|Specifies the kind of positioning used to place an element.<br>Default is`static`.  When the element is contained inside a group,<br>this property shall be`absolute`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`static`- The element is positioned according to the<br>normal flow of the page.  The`top`and`left`properties<br>are ignored.  If the object is anchored inline, this value is<br>used.<br><br>`absolute`- The element is positioned relative to the<br>parent, using the`top`and`left`properties.<br><br>`relative`- The element is positioned according to the<br>normal flow of the page, but the`top`and`left`properties<br>are used.  The overlap of overlapping elements is<br>governed by the`z-index`property.||
|||`rotation`|Specifies the angle that a shape is rotated, in degrees.  Default is<br>0.  Positive angles are clockwise.||
|||`top`|Specifies the position of the top of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element above it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator(cm,||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.|
|||`visibility`|Specifies whether a shape is displayed.  Only`inherit`and`hidden`<br>are used; any other values are mapped to`inherit`.  Default is<br>`inherit`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`hidden`- The shape is not visible, but is still part of the<br>flow of the objects in the browser.  Mouse events are not<br>processed.<br><br>`inherit`- The visibility state is inherited from the parent<br>of the shape.|
|||`width`|Specifies the width of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.|
|||`z-index`|Specifies the display order of overlapping shapes.  Default is 0.<br>This property shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Uses the order that the shapes appear in the page,<br>bottom to top.<br><br><order>- A number that represents the stacking<br>precedence.  Shapes with higher numbers are placed on<br>top of those with lower numbers.  Negative numbers are<br>allowed.|
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`direction`|Specifies the direction of the text in the textbox.  Default is`ltr`.<br>This property is superceded by the`mso-direction-alt`property<br>if that is specified.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`ltr`-Text is displayed left-to-right.|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`rtl`- Text is displayed right-to-left.||
|||`layout-flow`|Determines the flow of the text layout in a textbox.  Default is<br>`horizontal`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`horizontal`- Text is displayed horizontally.<br><br>`vertical`- Text is displayed vertically.<br><br>`vertical-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>vertically.<br><br>`horizontal-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>horizontally.||
|||`mso-direction-`<br>`alt`|Specifies an alternate direction for text in textboxes.  Overrides<br>the`direction`property.  The only allowed value is`context`.||
|||`mso-fit-shape-`<br>`to-text`|Specifies whether the shape stretches to fit the text in the<br>textbox.  Default is`false`.||
|||`mso-fit-text-`<br>`to-shape`|Specifies whether the text stretches to fit the textbox.  Default is<br>`false`.||
|||`mso-layout-`<br>`flow-alt`|Specifies the alternate layout flow for text in textboxes.  This<br>property is used instead of`layout-flow`when the layout flow is<br>from bottom to top for non-ideographic languages.  Its only value<br>is`bottom-to-top`.||
|||`mso-next-`<br>`textbox`|Specifies the ID of the next textbox in a series.  Used to keep track<br>of a set of linked textboxes.  Default is no value.||
|||`mso-rotate`|Specifies a specific rotation value for text in a textbox.  Default is<br>0.  Allowed values are:<br><br>0<br><br>90<br><br>180<br><br>-90||
|||`mso-text-scale`|Specifies the scaling factor for fitting text to shapes.  Default is 0.<br>This property is only used if`mso-fit-text-to-shape`is`true`.||
|||`v-text-anchor`|Specifies the vertical anchoring of text in a textbox.  Default is<br>`top`.  The alignment of a text anchor only becomes evident if`mso-`<br>`fit-text-to-shape`is`false`.  This property is different from the<br>`vertical-align`CSS property, which is used for ideographic<br>languages. Allowed values are:||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`top`<br><br>`middle`<br><br>`bottom`<br><br>`top-center`<br><br>`middle-center`<br><br>`bottom-center`<br><br>`top-baseline`<br><br>`bottom-baseline`<br><br>`top-center-baseline`<br><br>`bottom-center-baseline`|||
|||**Property**||**Description**||
|||`font`||Specifies a compound value of font settings.  Default is no value.<br>The values are the same as those of the CSS font property.  The<br>order of definitions in the string is: font-style, font-variant, font-<br>weight, font-size, line-height, font-family.||
|||`font-family`||Specifies the family of the font.  Default is no value.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS font-family property.||
|||`font-size`||Specifies the size of the font.  Default is no value.  The font size is<br>defined in points.  The values are the same as those of the CSS<br>font-size property.||
|||`font-style`||Specifies the amount of slant for a font.  Default is`normal`.  The<br>values are the same as those of the CSS font-style property.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`normal`<br><br>`italic`<br><br>`oblique`- Treated the same as italic.||
|||`font-variant`||Specifies the variant style of a font.  Default is`normal`.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS font-variant property.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`normal`<br><br>`small-caps`||
|||`font-weight`||Specifies the thickness of the letters of the font.  Default is<br>`normal`.  The values are the same as those of the CSS font-weight<br>property.  Allowed values are:<br>**Value**<br>**Description**||
|||||**Value**|**Description**|

|**Attributes**|||||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|||||`normal`<br>`lighter`<br>`100`<br>`200`<br>`300`<br>`400`|Treated as non-bold.<br>Treated as bold.||
|||||`bold`<br>`bolder`<br>`500`<br>`600`<br>`700`<br>`800`<br>`900`|Treated as bold.||
|||`mso-text-`<br>`shadow`||Specifies whether a shadow is applied to the text on a text path.<br>Default is`false`.|||
|||`text-`<br>`decoration`||Specifies the style of text decoration.  Default is`none`.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS text-decoration property.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`underline`<br><br>`overline`<br><br>`line-through`<br><br>`blink`|||
|||`v-rotate-`<br>`letters`||Specifies whether the letters of the text are rotated<br>counterclockwise by 90 degrees.  Default is`false`.|||
|||`v-same-letter-`<br>`heights`||Specifies whether all letters are the same height regardless of<br>initial case.  If`true`, the lowercase letters are stretched to the<br>height of the uppercase letters.  Default is`false`.|||
|||`v-text-align`||Specifies the alignment of text.  Default is`left`.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`left`|||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`right`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`justify`<br><br>`letter-justify`- Distributes the extra space between<br>the letters.<br><br>`stretch-justify`- Stretches the letters to fill in the<br>space.|
|||`v-text-kern`|Specifies whether kerning is turned on.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-text-reverse`|Specifies whether the layout order of rows is reversed.  Default is<br>`false`.  This is used for vertical text layout.|
|||`v-text-`<br>`spacing-mode`|Specifies the mode for letter spacing.  Default is`tightening`.<br>This property determines whether space is removed between<br>each letter (`tightening`) or added between each letter<br>(`tracking`).  The amount of letter spacing change is defined by<br>the`v-text-spacing`property.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`tightening`<br><br>`tracking`|
|||`v-text-spacing`|Specifies the amount of spacing for text in 100ths of single line<br>spacing.  Default is 100.|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|
|---|---|---|
|||<br>`z-index`<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|tablelimits(Table<br>Row Height Limits)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office||Specifies a list of minimum height values for each row in a table.  Default is no value.<br>Used by PresentationML for native tables.  This attribute is only useful when the table is<br>made up of shapes that are grouped.  When text is added to table cells, the row height<br>can increase.  Thetablelimitsattribute stores the original row height so that if text is<br>deleted, the row height does not fall below the original value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:tablelimits="30pt 20pt" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|tableproperties<br>(Table Properties)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office||Specifies a bitmask, represented as an integer, that determines table properties.  Only<br>the first three bits of this integer are used.  Default is 0.<br>Used by PresentationML for native tables.  This attribute is only useful when the table is<br>made up of shapes that are grouped.
