|||`mso-position-`<br>`vertical-`<br>`relative`|Specifies relative vertical position data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  This modifies the`mso-`<br>`position-vertical`property.  Default is`text`.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`margin`<br><br>`page`<br><br>`text`<br><br>`line`||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-`<br>`bottom`|Specifies the distance from the bottom of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-left`|Specifies the distance from the left side of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`|Specifies the distance from the right side of the shape to the text||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|||`distance-right`|that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-top`|Specifies the distance from the top of the shape to the text that<br>wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different from<br>the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the shape<br>to include the margin areas.  This property does not change the<br>origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`edited`|Specifies whether the wrap coordinates were customized by the<br>user.  If the wrap coordinates are generated by an editor, this<br>property is`true`; otherwise they were customized by a user.<br>Default is`false`.||
|||`mso-wrap-style`|Specifies the wrapping mode for text in shapes in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`square`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`square`- Wraps text inside the shape in a square.<br><br>`none`- Text does not wrap.||
|||`position`|Specifies the kind of positioning used to place an element.<br>Default is`static`.  When the element is contained inside a group,<br>this property shall be`absolute`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`static`- The element is positioned according to the<br>normal flow of the page.  The`top`and`left`properties<br>are ignored.  If the object is anchored inline, this value is<br>used.<br><br>`absolute`- The element is positioned relative to the<br>parent, using the`top`and`left`properties.<br><br>`relative`- The element is positioned according to the<br>normal flow of the page, but the`top`and`left`properties<br>are used.  The overlap of overlapping elements is<br>governed by the`z-index`property.||
|||`rotation`|Specifies the angle that a shape is rotated, in degrees.  Default is<br>0.  Positive angles are clockwise.||
|||`top`|Specifies the position of the top of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element above it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||<br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.|
|||`visibility`|Specifies whether a shape is displayed.  Only`inherit`and`hidden`<br>are used; any other values are mapped to`inherit`.  Default is<br>`inherit`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`hidden`- The shape is not visible, but is still part of the<br>flow of the objects in the browser.  Mouse events are not<br>processed.<br><br>`inherit`- The visibility state is inherited from the parent<br>of the shape.|
|||`width`|Specifies the width of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.|
|||`z-index`|Specifies the display order of overlapping shapes.  Default is 0.<br>This property shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Uses the order that the shapes appear in the page,<br>bottom to top.<br><br><order>- A number that represents the stacking<br>precedence.  Shapes with higher numbers are placed on<br>top of those with lower numbers.  Negative numbers are<br>allowed.|
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`direction`|Specifies the direction of the text in the textbox.  Default is`ltr`.<br>This property is superceded by the`mso-direction-alt`property<br>if that is specified.  Allowed values are:|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`ltr`- Text is displayed left-to-right.<br><br>`rtl`- Text is displayed right-to-left.||
|||`layout-flow`|Determines the flow of the text layout in a textbox.  Default is<br>`horizontal`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`horizontal`- Text is displayed horizontally.<br><br>`vertical`- Text is displayed vertically.<br><br>`vertical-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>vertically.<br><br>`horizontal-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>horizontally.||
|||`mso-direction-`<br>`alt`|Specifies an alternate direction for text in textboxes.  Overrides<br>the`direction`property.  The only allowed value is`context`.||
|||`mso-fit-shape-`<br>`to-text`|Specifies whether the shape stretches to fit the text in the<br>textbox.  Default is`false`.||
|||`mso-fit-text-`<br>`to-shape`|Specifies whether the text stretches to fit the textbox.  Default is<br>`false`.||
|||`mso-layout-`<br>`flow-alt`|Specifies the alternate layout flow for text in textboxes.  This<br>property is used instead of`layout-flow`when the layout flow is<br>from bottom to top for non-ideographic languages.  Its only value<br>is`bottom-to-top`.||
|||`mso-next-`<br>`textbox`|Specifies the ID of the next textbox in a series.  Used to keep track<br>of a set of linked textboxes.  Default is no value.||
|||`mso-rotate`|Specifies a specific rotation value for text in a textbox.  Default is<br>0.  Allowed values are:<br><br>0<br><br>90<br><br>180<br><br>-90||
|||`mso-text-scale`|Specifies the scaling factor for fitting text to shapes.  Default is 0.<br>This property is only used if`mso-fit-text-to-shape`is`true`.||
|||`v-text-anchor`|Specifies the vertical anchoring of text in a textbox.  Default is<br>`top`.  The alignment of a text anchor only becomes evident if`mso-`<br>`fit-text-to-shape`is`false`.  This property is different from the<br>`vertical-align`CSS property, which is used for ideographic<br>languages. Allowed values are:||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`top`<br><br>`middle`<br><br>`bottom`<br><br>`top-center`<br><br>`middle-center`<br><br>`bottom-center`<br><br>`top-baseline`<br><br>`bottom-baseline`<br><br>`top-center-baseline`<br><br>`bottom-center-baseline`|||
|||**Property**||**Description**||
|||`font`||Specifies a compound value of font settings.  Default is no value.<br>The values are the same as those of the CSS font property.  The<br>order of definitions in the string is: font-style, font-variant, font-<br>weight, font-size, line-height, font-family.||
|||`font-family`||Specifies the family of the font.  Default is no value.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS font-family property.||
|||`font-size`||Specifies the size of the font.  Default is no value.  The font size is<br>defined in points.  The values are the same as those of the CSS<br>font-size property.||
|||`font-style`||Specifies the amount of slant for a font.  Default is`normal`.  The<br>values are the same as those of the CSS font-style property.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`normal`<br><br>`italic`<br><br>`oblique`- Treated the same as italic.||
|||`font-variant`||Specifies the variant style of a font.  Default is`normal`.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS font-variant property.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`normal`<br><br>`small-caps`||
|||`font-weight`||Specifies the thickness of the letters of the font.  Default is<br>`normal`.  The values are the same as those of the CSS font-weight<br>property.  Allowed values are:<br>**Value**<br>**Description**||
|||||**Value**|**Description**|

|**Attributes**|||||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|||||`normal`<br>`lighter`<br>`100`<br>`200`<br>`300`<br>`400`|Treated as non-bold.<br>Treated as bold.||
|||||`bold`<br>`bolder`<br>`500`<br>`600`<br>`700`<br>`800`<br>`900`|Treated as bold.||
|||`mso-text-`<br>`shadow`||Specifies whether a shadow is applied to the text on a text path.<br>Default is`false`.|||
|||`text-`<br>`decoration`||Specifies the style of text decoration.  Default is`none`.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS text-decoration property.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`underline`<br><br>`overline`<br><br>`line-through`<br><br>`blink`|||
|||`v-rotate-`<br>`letters`||Specifies whether the letters of the text are rotated<br>counterclockwise by 90 degrees.  Default is`false`.|||
|||`v-same-letter-`<br>`heights`||Specifies whether all letters are the same height regardless of<br>initial case.  If`true`, the lowercase letters are stretched to the<br>height of the uppercase letters.  Default is`false`.|||
|||`v-text-align`||Specifies the alignment of text.  Default is`left`.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`left`|||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`right`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`justify`<br><br>`letter-justify`- Distributes the extra space between<br>the letters.<br><br>`stretch-justify`- Stretches the letters to fill in the<br>space.|
|||`v-text-kern`|Specifies whether kerning is turned on.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-text-reverse`|Specifies whether the layout order of rows is reversed.  Default is<br>`false`.  This is used for vertical text layout.|
|||`v-text-`<br>`spacing-mode`|Specifies the mode for letter spacing.  Default is`tightening`.<br>This property determines whether space is removed between<br>each letter (`tightening`) or added between each letter<br>(`tracking`).  The amount of letter spacing change is defined by<br>the`v-text-spacing`property.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`tightening`<br><br>`tracking`|
|||`v-text-spacing`|Specifies the amount of spacing for text in 100ths of single line<br>spacing.  Default is 100.|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|||<br>`z-index`<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.||
|target(Hyperlink<br>Display Target)||Specifies a frame<br>values are:||
|||**Value**|**Description**|
|||<targetname>|String containing the name of the frame or window in which to load<br>the document.|
|||`_blank`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into a new blank<br>window. This window is not named.|
|||`_media`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the browser’s<br>multimedia pane.|
|||`_parent`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the immediate<br>parent of the document containing the link.|
|||`_search`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the browser's<br>search pane.|
|||`_self`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the window in<br>which the link was clicked (the active window).|
|||`_top`|Specifies that the linked document is loaded into the topmost<br>window.|
|title(Shape Title)||Specifies the text displayed when the mouse pointer moves over the shape.  Default is no<br>value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … title="tooltip" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`||

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|userdrawn(Exists<br>In Master Slide)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the user has added the shape to a master slide.  Default is`false`.  Used<br>by PresentationML.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:userdrawn="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|userhidden(Hide<br>Script Anchors)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a script anchor is hidden.  Default is`false`.  If`true`, script anchors stay<br>hidden even if the shape is otherwise visible.  A script anchor is the visual representation<br>of a script that when displayed in an application.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:userhidden="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|wrapcoords(Shape<br>Bounding Polygon)|Specifies the bounding polygon that surrounds a shape.  This is specified using a comma-<br>delimited list of x and y coordinates: "x1,y1,x2,y2,x3,y3,…"  This is used when text is<br>tightly wrapped around a shape.  Default is no value until themso-wrap-mode style<br>attribute is set to`tight`or`through`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape …`<br>`wrapcoords="0,0 0,200, 200,200, 200,0" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Shapetype) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.2.21 stroke (Line Stroke Settings)

This element describes how to draw the path if something beyond solid line with a solid color is desired.  The attributes of the stroke element can be used to describe a powerful set of stroke properties.   Extensions to the VML stroke definition are encoded as sub-elements of stroke .

[ _Example_ :

- `<v:polyline points="0pt,0pt,50pt,0pt,50pt,35pt,15pt,35pt,`

- `15pt,15pt,75pt,15pt">`

- `<v:stroke startarrow="classic" endarrow="classic"`

```
    startarrowwidth="wide" endarrowwidth="wide" dashstyle="dashdot"
```

```
    weight="2pt" color="teal" linestyle="thinThin"/>
```

```
</v:polyline>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|althref(Alternate<br>Image Reference)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies an alternate reference for an image in Macintosh PICT format.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … althref="myimage.pcz" … >`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|color(Stroke Color)|Specifies the stroke color.  Overrides thestrokecolorattribute of a shape.  Default is<br>`black`.  See thefillcolorattribute for a list of supported named colors.<br>[_Example_: The shape stroke is blue:<br>`<v:shape … strokecolor="red" … >`<br>`<v:stroke color="blue"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|color2(Stroke<br>Alternate Pattern<br>Color)|Specifies a second color for strokes, used whenfilltypeis`pattern`.  Default is no value.<br>When a pattern fill is used for the stroke, the stroke color is used in colored parts of the<br>source image.  The color2 defines an alternate color to use in place of black in the source<br>image.<br>[_Example_: This unusual example is intended to demonstrate how the image and colors<br>interact to create a patterned stroke.  The yellow background shows transparency.  The<br>non-square shape and square image create an effective offset.  The heavy stroke weight<br>shows more of the image.  The green shape fill shows how the stroke is overlaid on the<br>shape.<br>`<v:background fillcolor="yellow"/>`<br>`<v:shape style="width:60;height:50"`<br>`strokecolor="red" fillcolor="lime"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke filltype="pattern" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="myimage.gif" color2="blue"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>, where myimage.gif is:<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|dashstyle(Stroke<br>Dash Pattern)|Specifies the dot and dash pattern for a stroke.  Default is`solid`.  Pre-defined values are:<br><br>`solid`<br><br>`shortdash`<br><br>`shortdot`<br><br>`shortdashdot`<br><br>`shortdashdotdot`<br><br>`dot`<br><br>`dash`<br><br>`longdash`<br><br>`dashdot`<br><br>`longdashdot`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||<br>`longdashdotdot`<br>A custom-defined dash pattern can also be specified using a series of numbers.  These<br>define the length of the dash (the drawn part of the stroke) and the length of the space<br>between the dashes.  The lengths are relative to the line width: a length of 1 is equal to<br>the line width.  Theendcapstyle is applied to each dash but the arrow style is not.  The<br>string defines the length of the dash then the length of the space.  This can be repeated<br>to form complex dash styles.  The string should always contain a pair of numbers; if it<br>contains an odd number of numbers the last is disregarded.  0 implies a dot that is<br>fourfold symmetrical (with round end caps, this is a circle).<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke dashstyle="0 2" weight="3pt"`<br>`endcap="round">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>`<v:stroke dashstyle="longdashdotdot"`<br>`weight="2pt">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|endarrow(Line End<br>Arrowhead)|Specifies an arrowhead for the end of a line.  Default is`none`.  The path shall not be<br>closed with the x command for the arrowhead to show.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`block`<br><br>`classic`<br><br>`diamond`<br><br>`oval`<br><br>`open`<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:stroke endarrow="classic"/>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowTypesimple<br>type (§19.1.3.8).|
|endarrowlength<br>(Line End<br>Arrowhead Length)|Specifies the length of the arrowhead at the end of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`short`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`long`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endarrowlength="long" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowLengthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.7).|
|endarrowwidth<br>(Line End<br>Arrowhead Width)|Specifies the width of the arrowhead at the end of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`narrow`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`wide`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endarrowwidth="wide" … />`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowWidthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.9).|
|endcap(Line End<br>Cap)|Specifies the cap style for the end of a stroke.  Default is`flat`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`flat`<br><br>`square`<br><br>`round`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endcap="round" weight="10pt" … />`<br>`endcap="flat"`<br>`endcap="square"`<br>`endcap="round"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeEndCapsimple type<br>(§19.1.3.10).|
|filltype(Stroke<br>Image Style)|Specifies the kind offill used for the background of a stroke.  Default is`solid`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`solid`- The fill pattern is solid.<br><br>`tile`- The fill image is tiled.<br><br>`pattern`- The fill image is stretched to form a pattern.<br><br>`frame`- The fill image becomes a border for the shape.<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:shape style="width:50;height:50"`<br>`strokecolor="red"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke filltype="frame" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="border.gif"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>, where border.gif is:<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_FillTypesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.4).|
|forcedash(Force<br>Dashed Outline)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether a dashed outline is used to draw a shape when a shape has no line or<br>fill.  Default is`false`.<br>Used by PresentationML placeholders to draw a dashed outline when there is no line and<br>no fill for a shape.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:forcedash="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|href(Original Image<br>Reference)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the URL to the original image file.  Used only if the picture has been linked and<br>embedded.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … o:href="myimage.gif" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|id(Relationship)|Specifies the relationship ID of the relationship to the image used for the stroke.  The<br>specified relationship shall be of type|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/imageor<br>the document shall be considered non-conformant.<br>[_Example_: The markup specifies the associated relationship part with relationship ID<br>`rId10`contains the corresponding relationship information:<br>`< … r:id="rId10" />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|
|id(Unique<br>Identifier)|Specifies a unique identifier that can be used to reference a VML object.<br>Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … id="myShape" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|imagealignshape<br>(Stoke Image<br>Alignment)|Specifies the alignment of the stroke image.  If`true`, the image is aligned with the shape.<br>Otherwise, it is aligned with the containing scope.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_: The top position offset shifts the image alignment relative to the containing<br>window:<br>`<v:shape fillcolor="silver"`<br>`style="top:20;width:50;height:50"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke imagealignshape="false" weight="20pt"`<br>`filltype="tile" src="myimage.gif"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>`imagealignshape="false"`|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|
|---|---|---|
|||`imagealignshape="false"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|imageaspect<br>(Stroke Image<br>Aspect Ratio)||Specifies how the stroke image aspect ratio is preserved.  Default is`ignore`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br>Value<br>Description<br>`ignore`<br>Ignore aspect issues.<br>`atleast`<br>Image is at least as big asimagesize.<br>`atmost`<br>Image is no bigger thanimagesize.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke filltype="frame" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="border.gif" imagealignshape="true"`<br>`imageaspect="atleast">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>`imagealignshape="ignore"`<br>`imagealignshape="atleast"`<br>`imagealignshape="atmost"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ImageAspectsimple type<br>(§19.1.3.5).|
|imagesize(Stroke<br>Image Size)||Specifies the size of the image for the stroke.  Default is the size of the image.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … imagesize="10pt,10pt" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|insetpen(Inset<br>Border From Path)|Specifies that the border shall be displayed inside of the path defining the shape, rather<br>than along the path (the default border placement), or outside of the path as might be<br>done with an image.<br>[_Example:_<br>`<v:shape … insetpen="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|joinstyle(Line End<br>Join Style)|Specifies the join style for line ends.  Default is`round`.<br><br>`round`<br><br>`bevel`<br><br>`miter`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:polyline strokeweight="10pt" strokecolor="navy"`<br>`points="10pt,10pt,50pt,50pt,90pt,10pt">`<br>`<v:stroke joinstyle="bevel"/>`<br>`</v:polyline>`<br>`joinstyle="round"`<br>`joinstyle="bevel"`<br>`joinstyle="miter"`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeJoinStylesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.11).|
|linestyle(Stroke<br>Line Style)|Specifies the line style of the stroke.  Default is`single`.<br><br>`single`<br><br>`thinThin`<br><br>`thinThick`<br><br>`thickThin`<br><br>`thickBetweenThin`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke linestyle="thickThin" weight="5pt">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeLineStylesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.12).|
|miterlimit(Miter<br>Joint Limit)|Specifies the smoothness of the miter joint, or the maximum distance between the inner<br>point and outer point of a joint.  This number is a multiple of the thickness of the line.<br>Default is 8.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke joinstyle="miter" weight="10pt"`<br>`miterlimit="2">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemadecimal<br>datatype.|
|on(Stroke Toggle)|Specifies whether the stroke is displayed.  Default is`true`.  This attribute overrides the|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||shape'sstrokeattribute.<br>[_Example:_<br>`<v:rect style="width:50;height:50" stroked="true"`<br>`fillcolor="lime" strokecolor="red">`<br>`<v:stroke on="false" weight="5pt"/>`<br>`</v:rect>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|opacity(Stroke<br>Opacity)|Specifies the amount of transparency of a stroke.  Default is 1.0.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:rect style="width:50;height:50"`<br>`fillcolor="lime" strokecolor="red">`<br>`<v:stroke weight="5pt" opacity="50%"/>`<br>`</v:rect>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|relid(Relationship<br>to Part)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the relationship ID of the relationship to the image.  The specified relationship<br>shall be of type<br>http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships/imageor<br>the document shall be considered non-conformant.<br>[_Example_: The markup specifies the associated relationship part with relationship ID<br>`rId10`contains the corresponding relationship information:<br>`<v:stroke … o:relid="rId10" …>`<br>`</v:stroke>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|
|src(Stroke Image<br>Location)|Specifies the source image to load for a stroke fill.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … src="myimage.gif" … >`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|startarrow(Line<br>Start Arrowhead)|Specifies an arrowhead for the start of a line.  Default is`none`.  The path shall not be<br>closed with the x command for the arrowhead to show.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`block`<br><br>`classic`<br><br>`diamond`<br><br>`oval`<br><br>`open`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke startarrow="classic"/>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowTypesimple<br>type (§19.1.3.8).|
|startarrowlength<br>(Line Start<br>Arrowhead Length)|Specifies the length of the arrowhead at the start of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`short`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`long`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … startarrowlength="long" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowLengthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.7).|
|startarrowwidth<br>(Line Start<br>Arrowhead Width)|Specifies the width of the arrowhead at the start of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`narrow`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`wide`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … startarrowwidth="wide" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowWidthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.9).|
|title(Stroke Title)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies the title of an embedded stroke image.  This is typically set to the comment<br>property of the image, which is often blank.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … o:title="alt text" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|weight(Stroke<br>Weight)|Specifies the thickness of a stroke.  Default is 1.  This attribute overrides the shape's<br>strokeweightattribute.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Stroke) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.2.22 textbox (Text Box)

This element is used to define text that appears inside the shape.  This text can contain rich formatting and is rendered to fit inside the textboxrect defined by the path element (§19.1.2.14).

[ _Example_ :

- `<v:shape style="width=200;height=200" coordsize="400,400"`

- `fillcolor="yellow" strokecolor="maroon"`

- `path="m 119,0 l 148,86 238,86 166,140 192,226 119,175 46,226`

- `72,140 0,86 90,86 x e">`

```
  <v:textbox inset="32pt,35pt,,">VML</v:textbox>
```

- `</v:shape>`

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Unique<br>Identifier)|Specifies a unique identifier that can be used to reference a VML object.<br>Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … id="myShape" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|inset(Text Box<br>Inset)|Specifies inner margin values for textbox text.  Default is "0.1in, 0.05in, 0.1in, 0.05in".<br>Missing values are set to the default.  This is used ifinsetmodeis`custom`.<br>The internal text margin value is specified as a string containing four values, each<br>separated by commas or spaces. The values measure inset from the left, top, right, and<br>bottom edges of the box specified by thetextboxrectattribute of thepathelement<br>(§19.1.2.14).<br>[_Example_: The text is set toward the lower right of a small square:<br>`<v:textbox inset="20pt,30pt,10pt,10pt">`<br>`VML</v:textbox>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|insetmode(Text<br>Inset Mode)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether the application calculates the internal text margin instead of using the<br>insetattribute.  Default is`custom`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:textbox … o:insetmode="auto" … >`<br>`</v:textbox>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_InsetModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.17).|
|singleclick(Text<br>Box Single-Click<br>Selection Toggle)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-<br>com:office:office|Specifies whether text is selectable with a single click. Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|style(Shape Styling<br>Properties)||Specifies the CSS2 styling properties of the shape. The CSS2 (Cascading Style Sheets, Level<br>2) specification, a Recommendation of the World Wide Web Consortium, is available<br>here:http://www.w3.org/TR/REC-CSS2.<br>This attribute uses a semi-colon delimited list of “name:value” pairs, the syntax defined<br>by section 4.1.8 of the CSS2 specification for grouped declarations (without the<br>surrounding braces). The following tables define the allowed properties and the VML<br>treatment of each.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … style=’position:absolute;width:100pt;height:50pt’ …`<br>`>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>**Property**<br>**Description**<br>`flip`<br>Specifies that the orientation of a shape is flipped.  Default is no<br>value.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`x`- Flip along the y-axis, reversing the x-coordinates.<br><br>`y`- Flip along the x-axis, reversing the y-coordinates.<br><br>`xy`- Flip along both the y- and x-axis.<br><br>`yx`- Flip along both the x- and y-axis.<br>`height`<br>Specifies the height of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.<br>`left`<br>Specifies the position of the left of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element left of it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units> - A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm,in, pt, pc,orpx)or a relative units designator(em or||
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`flip`|Specifies that the orientation of a shape is flipped.  Default is no<br>value.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`x`- Flip along the y-axis, reversing the x-coordinates.<br><br>`y`- Flip along the x-axis, reversing the y-coordinates.<br><br>`xy`- Flip along both the y- and x-axis.<br><br>`yx`- Flip along both the x- and y-axis.|
|||`height`|Specifies the height of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.|
|||`left`|Specifies the position of the left of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element left of it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units> - A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm,in, pt, pc,orpx)or a relative units designator(em or|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`margin-bottom`|Specifies the position of the bottom of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`margin-left`|Specifies the position of the left of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`margin-right`|Specifies the position of the right of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units> - A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`margin-top`|Specifies the position of the top of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`horizontal`|Specifies the horizontal positioning data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`absolute`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`absolute`<br><br>`left`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`right`<br><br>`inside`<br><br>`outside`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`horizontal-`<br>`relative`|Specifies relative horizontal position data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  This modifies the`mso-`<br>`position-horizontal`property.  Default is`text`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`margin`<br><br>`page`<br><br>`text`<br><br>`char`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`vertical`|Specifies the vertical positioning data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`absolute`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`absolute`<br><br>`top`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`bottom`<br><br>`inside`<br><br>`outside`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`vertical-`<br>`relative`|Specifies relative vertical position data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  This modifies the`mso-`<br>`position-vertical`property.  Default is`text`.  Allowed values<br>are:||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`margin`<br><br>`page`<br><br>`text`<br><br>`line`||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-`<br>`bottom`|Specifies the distance from the bottom of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-left`|Specifies the distance from the left side of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-right`|Specifies the distance from the right side of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-top`|Specifies the distance from the top of the shape to the text that<br>wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different from<br>the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the shape<br>to include the margin areas.  This property does not change the<br>origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`edited`|Specifies whether the wrap coordinates were customized by the<br>user.  If the wrap coordinates are generated by an editor, this<br>property is`true`; otherwise they were customized by a user.<br>Default is`false`.||
|||`mso-wrap-style`|Specifies the wrapping mode for text in shapes in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`square`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`square`- Wraps text inside the shape in a square.<br><br>`none`- Text does not wrap.||
|||`position`|Specifies the kind of positioning used to place an element.<br>Default is`static`.  When the element is contained inside a group,<br>this property shall be`absolute`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`static`- The element is positioned according to the<br>normal flow of the page.  The`top`and`left`properties<br>are ignored.  If the object is anchored inline, this value is<br>used.<br><br>`absolute`- The element ispositioned relative to the||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||parent, using the`top`and`left`properties.<br><br>`relative`- The element is positioned according to the<br>normal flow of the page, but the`top`and`left`properties<br>are used.  The overlap of overlapping elements is<br>governed by the`z-index`property.||
|||`rotation`|Specifies the angle that a shape is rotated, in degrees.  Default is<br>0.  Positive angles are clockwise.||
|||`top`|Specifies the position of the top of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element above it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`visibility`|Specifies whether a shape is displayed.  Only`inherit`and`hidden`<br>are used; any other values are mapped to`inherit`.  Default is<br>`inherit`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`hidden`- The shape is not visible, but is still part of the<br>flow of the objects in the browser.  Mouse events are not<br>processed.<br><br>`inherit`- The visibility state is inherited from the parent<br>of the shape.||
|||`width`|Specifies the width of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`z-index`|Specifies the display order of overlapping shapes.  Default is 0.<br>This property shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.<br>Allowed values are:||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`auto`- Uses the order that the shapes appear in the page,<br>bottom to top.<br><br><order>- A number that represents the stacking<br>precedence.  Shapes with higher numbers are placed on<br>top of those with lower numbers.  Negative numbers are<br>allowed.|
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`direction`|Specifies the direction of the text in the textbox.  Default is`ltr`.<br>This property is superceded by the`mso-direction-alt`property<br>if that is specified.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`ltr`- Text is displayed left-to-right.<br><br>`rtl`- Text is displayed right-to-left.|
|||`layout-flow`|Determines the flow of the text layout in a textbox.  Default is<br>`horizontal`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`horizontal`- Text is displayed horizontally.<br><br>`vertical`- Text is displayed vertically.<br><br>`vertical-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>vertically.<br><br>`horizontal-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>horizontally.|
|||`mso-direction-`<br>`alt`|Specifies an alternate direction for text in textboxes.  Overrides<br>the`direction`property.  The only allowed value is`context`.|
|||`mso-fit-shape-`<br>`to-text`|Specifies whether the shape stretches to fit the text in the<br>textbox.  Default is`false`.|
|||`mso-fit-text-`<br>`to-shape`|Specifies whether the text stretches to fit the textbox.  Default is<br>`false`.|
|||`mso-layout-`<br>`flow-alt`|Specifies the alternate layout flow for text in textboxes.  This<br>property is used instead of`layout-flow`when the layout flow is<br>from bottom to top for non-ideographic languages.  Its only value<br>is`bottom-to-top`.|
|||`mso-next-`|Specifies the ID of the next textbox in a series.  Used to keep track<br>of a set of linked textboxes.  Default is no value.|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|||`textbox`||
|||`mso-rotate`|Specifies a specific rotation value for text in a textbox.  Default is<br>0.  Allowed values are:<br><br>0<br><br>90<br><br>180<br><br>-90|
|||`mso-text-scale`|Specifies the scaling factor for fitting text to shapes.  Default is 0.<br>This property is only used if`mso-fit-text-to-shape`is`true`.|
|||`v-text-anchor`|Specifies the vertical anchoring of text in a textbox.  Default is<br>`top`.  The alignment of a text anchor only becomes evident if`mso-`<br>`fit-text-to-shape`is`false`.  This property is different from the<br>`vertical-align`CSS property, which is used for ideographic<br>languages. Allowed values are:<br><br>`top`<br><br>`middle`<br><br>`bottom`<br><br>`top-center`<br><br>`middle-center`<br><br>`bottom-center`<br><br>`top-baseline`<br><br>`bottom-baseline`<br><br>`top-center-baseline`<br><br>`bottom-center-baseline`|
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`font`|Specifies a compound value of font settings.  Default is no value.<br>The values are the same as those of the CSS font property.  The<br>order of definitions in the string is: font-style, font-variant, font-<br>weight, font-size, line-height, font-family.|
|||`font-family`|Specifies the family of the font.  Default is no value.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS font-family property.|
|||`font-size`|Specifies the size of the font.  Default is no value.  The font size is<br>defined in points.  The values are the same as those of the CSS<br>font-size property.|
|||`font-style`|Specifies the amount of slant for a font.  Default is`normal`.  The<br>values are the same as those of the CSS font-styleproperty.|

|**Attributes**||||**Description**|**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|||||Allowed values are:<br><br>`normal`<br><br>`italic`<br><br>`oblique`- Treated the same as italic.|||
|||`font-variant`||Specifies the variant style of a font.  Default is`normal`.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS font-variant property.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`normal`<br><br>`small-caps`|||
|||`font-weight`||Specifies the thickness of the letters of the font.  Default is<br>`normal`.  The values are the same as those of the CSS font-weight<br>property.  Allowed values are:<br>**Value**<br>**Description**<br>`normal`<br>`lighter`<br>`100`<br>`200`<br>`300`<br>`400`<br>Treated as non-bold.<br>`bold`<br>`bolder`<br>`500`<br>`600`<br>`700`<br>`800`<br>`900`<br>Treated as bold.|||
|||||**Value**|**Description**||
|||||`normal`<br>`lighter`<br>`100`<br>`200`<br>`300`<br>`400`|Treated as non-bold.||
|||||`bold`<br>`bolder`<br>`500`<br>`600`<br>`700`<br>`800`<br>`900`|Treated as bold.||
|||`mso-text-`<br>`shadow`||Specifies whether a shadow is applied to the text on a text path.<br>Default is`false`.|||
|||`text-`<br>`decoration`||Specifies the style of text decoration.  Default is`none`.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS text-decoration property.<br>Allowed values are:|||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`none`<br><br>`underline`<br><br>`overline`<br><br>`line-through`<br><br>`blink`|
|||`v-rotate-`<br>`letters`|Specifies whether the letters of the text are rotated<br>counterclockwise by 90 degrees.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-same-letter-`<br>`heights`|Specifies whether all letters are the same height regardless of<br>initial case.  If`true`, the lowercase letters are stretched to the<br>height of the uppercase letters.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-text-align`|Specifies the alignment of text.  Default is`left`.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`left`<br><br>`right`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`justify`<br><br>`letter-justify`- Distributes the extra space between<br>the letters.<br><br>`stretch-justify`- Stretches the letters to fill in the<br>space.|
|||`v-text-kern`|Specifies whether kerning is turned on.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-text-reverse`|Specifies whether the layout order of rows is reversed.  Default is<br>`false`.  This is used for vertical text layout.|
|||`v-text-`<br>`spacing-mode`|Specifies the mode for letter spacing.  Default is`tightening`.<br>This property determines whether space is removed between<br>each letter (`tightening`) or added between each letter<br>(`tracking`).  The amount of letter spacing change is defined by<br>the`v-text-spacing`property.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`tightening`<br><br>`tracking`|
|||`v-text-spacing`|Specifies the amount of spacing for text in 100ths of single line<br>spacing.  Default is 100.|

**Attributes Description**  `width`  `height` The following properties are not inherited by an element that references a shapetype element (§19.1.2.20) via the id attribute:  `flip`  `height`  `left`  `margin-left`  `margin-top`  `position`  `rotation`  `top`  `visibility`  `width`  `z-index`

The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Textbox) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.2.23 textpath (Text Layout Path)

This element is used to define a vector path based on the text data, font and font styles supplied.  The path which results is then mapped into the region defined by the v attribute of the shape's path (§19.1.2.14).

[ _Example_ :

- `<v:curve from="50,100" to="400,100" control1="200,200" control2="300,200">`

```
  <v:stroke color="blue"/>
```

- `<v:fill color="yellow" color2="green" type="gradient"/>`

- `<v:path textpathok="true"/>`

- `<v:textpath on="true" style="font:normal normal normal 36pt Arial" fitpath="true" string="Hello, VML!"/>`

- `</v:curve>`

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**||**Description**|
|---|---|---|
|fitpath(Path Fit<br>Toggle)||Specifies whether the text fits the path of a shape.  If`true`, sizes the text to fill the path it<br>lies out on.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:textpath on="true" fitpath="true"`<br>`string="VML">`<br>`</v:textpath>`<br>`fitpath="true"`<br>`fitpath="false"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|fitshape(Shape Fit<br>Toggle)||Specifies whether the text fits the bounding box of a shape.  If`true`, the text is stretched<br>out to the edges of the box that defines the entire shape.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_: Whenfitshapeis`false`, the text is drawn along the first part of the path.<br>When`true`, the text is stretched to fit the entire enclosed area of the shape.<br>`<v:shape style="width:100;height:100"`<br>`path="m 0,500 c 250,0 750,0 1000,500 e`<br>`m 0,600 c 250,900 750,900 1000,600 e"`<br>`fillcolor="yellow" strokecolor="maroon">`<br>`<v:path textpathok="t"/>`<br>`<v:textpath on="t" fitshape="t" string="VML"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The raw path stroke is:<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|id(Unique<br>Identifier)|Specifies a unique identifier that can be used to reference a VML object.<br>Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … id="myShape" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|on(Text Path<br>Toggle)|Specifies whether the text is displayed on the textpath.  Default is`false`.  The<br>textpathokattribute of thepathelement (§19.1.2.14) overrides this.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:line from="50,100" to="100,100">`<br>`<v:path textpathok="false"/>`<br>`<v:textpath on="true" string="VML"/>`<br>`</v:line>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|string(Text Path<br>Text)|Specifies the text of the text path.  Default is no value.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|style(Shape Styling<br>Properties)|Specifies the CSS2 styling properties of the shape. The CSS2 (Cascading Style Sheets, Level<br>2) specification, a Recommendation of the World Wide Web Consortium, is available|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|||here:http://www.w3.org/TR/REC-CSS2.<br>This attribute uses a semi-colon delimited list of “name:value” pairs, the syntax defined<br>by section 4.1.8 of the CSS2 specification for grouped declarations (without the<br>surrounding braces). The following tables define the allowed properties and the VML<br>treatment of each.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … style=’position:absolute;width:100pt;height:50pt’ …`<br>`>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>**Property**<br>**Description**<br>`flip`<br>Specifies that the orientation of a shape is flipped.  Default is no<br>value.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`x`- Flip along the y-axis, reversing the x-coordinates.<br><br>`y`- Flip along the x-axis, reversing the y-coordinates.<br><br>`xy`- Flip along both the y- and x-axis.<br><br>`yx`- Flip along both the x- and y-axis.<br>`height`<br>Specifies the height of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.<br>`left`<br>Specifies the position of the left of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element left of it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units> - A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as apercentage of the|||
|||**Property**||**Description**|
|||`flip`||Specifies that the orientation of a shape is flipped.  Default is no<br>value.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`x`- Flip along the y-axis, reversing the x-coordinates.<br><br>`y`- Flip along the x-axis, reversing the y-coordinates.<br><br>`xy`- Flip along both the y- and x-axis.<br><br>`yx`- Flip along both the x- and y-axis.|
|||`height`||Specifies the height of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.|
|||`left`||Specifies the position of the left of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element left of it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units> - A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as apercentage of the|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||parent object's width.||
|||`margin-bottom`|Specifies the position of the bottom of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex). Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).
