It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||<br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`horizontal`|Specifies the horizontal positioning data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`absolute`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`absolute`<br><br>`left`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`right`<br><br>`inside`<br><br>`outside`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`horizontal-`<br>`relative`|Specifies relative horizontal position data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  This modifies the`mso-`<br>`position-horizontal`property.  Default is`text`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`margin`<br><br>`page`<br><br>`text`<br><br>`char`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`vertical`|Specifies the vertical positioning data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`absolute`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`absolute`<br><br>`top`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`bottom`<br><br>`inside`<br><br>`outside`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`vertical-`<br>`relative`|Specifies relative vertical position data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  This modifies the`mso-`<br>`position-vertical`property.  Default is`text`.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`margin`<br><br>`page `||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`text`<br><br>`line`||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-`<br>`bottom`|Specifies the distance from the bottom of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-left`|Specifies the distance from the left side of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-right`|Specifies the distance from the right side of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-top`|Specifies the distance from the top of the shape to the text that<br>wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different from<br>the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the shape<br>to include the margin areas.  This property does not change the<br>origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`edited`|Specifies whether the wrap coordinates were customized by the<br>user.  If the wrap coordinates are generated by an editor, this<br>property is`true`; otherwise they were customized by a user.<br>Default is`false`.||
|||`mso-wrap-style`|Specifies the wrapping mode for text in shapes in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`square`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`square`- Wraps text inside the shape in a square.<br><br>`none`- Text does not wrap.||
|||`position`|Specifies the kind of positioning used to place an element.<br>Default is`static`.  When the element is contained inside a group,<br>this property shall be`absolute`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`static`- The element is positioned according to the<br>normal flow of the page.  The`top`and`left`properties<br>are ignored.  If the object is anchored inline, this value is<br>used.<br><br>`absolute`- The element is positioned relative to the<br>parent, using the`top`and`left`properties.<br><br>`relative`- The element ispositioned accordingto the||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||normal flow of the page, but the`top`and`left`properties<br>are used.  The overlap of overlapping elements is<br>governed by the`z-index`property.||
|||`rotation`|Specifies the angle that a shape is rotated, in degrees.  Default is<br>0.  Positive angles are clockwise.||
|||`top`|Specifies the position of the top of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element above it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`visibility`|Specifies whether a shape is displayed.  Only`inherit`and`hidden`<br>are used; any other values are mapped to`inherit`.  Default is<br>`inherit`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`hidden`- The shape is not visible, but is still part of the<br>flow of the objects in the browser.  Mouse events are not<br>processed.<br><br>`inherit`- The visibility state is inherited from the parent<br>of the shape.||
|||`width`|Specifies the width of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`z-index`|Specifies the display order of overlapping shapes.  Default is 0.<br>This property shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Uses the order that the shapes appear in thepage,||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||bottom to top.<br><br><order>- A number that represents the stacking<br>precedence.  Shapes with higher numbers are placed on<br>top of those with lower numbers.  Negative numbers are<br>allowed.|
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`direction`|Specifies the direction of the text in the textbox.  Default is`ltr`.<br>This property is superceded by the`mso-direction-alt`property<br>if that is specified.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`ltr`- Text is displayed left-to-right.<br><br>`rtl`- Text is displayed right-to-left.|
|||`layout-flow`|Determines the flow of the text layout in a textbox.  Default is<br>`horizontal`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`horizontal`- Text is displayed horizontally.<br><br>`vertical`- Text is displayed vertically.<br><br>`vertical-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>vertically.<br><br>`horizontal-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>horizontally.|
|||`mso-direction-`<br>`alt`|Specifies an alternate direction for text in textboxes.  Overrides<br>the`direction`property.  The only allowed value is`context`.|
|||`mso-fit-shape-`<br>`to-text`|Specifies whether the shape stretches to fit the text in the<br>textbox.  Default is`false`.|
|||`mso-fit-text-`<br>`to-shape`|Specifies whether the text stretches to fit the textbox.  Default is<br>`false`.|
|||`mso-layout-`<br>`flow-alt`|Specifies the alternate layout flow for text in textboxes.  This<br>property is used instead of`layout-flow`when the layout flow is<br>from bottom to top for non-ideographic languages.  Its only value<br>is`bottom-to-top`.|
|||`mso-next-`<br>`textbox`|Specifies the ID of the next textbox in a series.  Used to keep track<br>of a set of linked textboxes.  Default is no value.|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|||`mso-rotate`|Specifies a specific rotation value for text in a textbox.  Default is<br>0.  Allowed values are:<br><br>0<br><br>90<br><br>180<br><br>-90|
|||`mso-text-scale`|Specifies the scaling factor for fitting text to shapes.  Default is 0.<br>This property is only used if`mso-fit-text-to-shape`is`true`.|
|||`v-text-anchor`|Specifies the vertical anchoring of text in a textbox.  Default is<br>`top`.  The alignment of a text anchor only becomes evident if`mso-`<br>`fit-text-to-shape`is`false`.  This property is different from the<br>`vertical-align`CSS property, which is used for ideographic<br>languages. Allowed values are:<br><br>`top`<br><br>`middle`<br><br>`bottom`<br><br>`top-center`<br><br>`middle-center`<br><br>`bottom-center`<br><br>`top-baseline`<br><br>`bottom-baseline`<br><br>`top-center-baseline`<br><br>`bottom-center-baseline`|
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`font`|Specifies a compound value of font settings.  Default is no value.<br>The values are the same as those of the CSS font property.  The<br>order of definitions in the string is: font-style, font-variant, font-<br>weight, font-size, line-height, font-family.|
|||`font-family`|Specifies the family of the font.  Default is no value.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS font-family property.|
|||`font-size`|Specifies the size of the font.  Default is no value.  The font size is<br>defined in points.  The values are the same as those of the CSS<br>font-size property.|
|||`font-style`|Specifies the amount of slant for a font.  Default is`normal`.  The<br>values are the same as those of the CSS font-style property.<br>Allowed values are:|

|**Attributes**||||**Description**|**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|||||<br>`normal`<br><br>`italic`<br><br>`oblique`- Treated the same as italic.|||
|||`font-variant`||Specifies the variant style of a font.  Default is`normal`.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS font-variant property.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`normal`<br><br>`small-caps`|||
|||`font-weight`||Specifies the thickness of the letters of the font.  Default is<br>`normal`.  The values are the same as those of the CSS font-weight<br>property.  Allowed values are:<br>**Value**<br>**Description**<br>`normal`<br>`lighter`<br>`100`<br>`200`<br>`300`<br>`400`<br>Treated as non-bold.<br>`bold`<br>`bolder`<br>`500`<br>`600`<br>`700`<br>`800`<br>`900`<br>Treated as bold.|||
|||||**Value**|**Description**||
|||||`normal`<br>`lighter`<br>`100`<br>`200`<br>`300`<br>`400`|Treated as non-bold.||
|||||`bold`<br>`bolder`<br>`500`<br>`600`<br>`700`<br>`800`<br>`900`|Treated as bold.||
|||`mso-text-`<br>`shadow`||Specifies whether a shadow is applied to the text on a text path.<br>Default is`false`.|||
|||`text-`<br>`decoration`||Specifies the style of text decoration.  Default is`none`.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS text-decoration property.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`|||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`underline`<br><br>`overline`<br><br>`line-through`<br><br>`blink`|
|||`v-rotate-`<br>`letters`|Specifies whether the letters of the text are rotated<br>counterclockwise by 90 degrees.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-same-letter-`<br>`heights`|Specifies whether all letters are the same height regardless of<br>initial case.  If`true`, the lowercase letters are stretched to the<br>height of the uppercase letters.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-text-align`|Specifies the alignment of text.  Default is`left`.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`left`<br><br>`right`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`justify`<br><br>`letter-justify`- Distributes the extra space between<br>the letters.<br><br>`stretch-justify`- Stretches the letters to fill in the<br>space.|
|||`v-text-kern`|Specifies whether kerning is turned on.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-text-reverse`|Specifies whether the layout order of rows is reversed.  Default is<br>`false`.  This is used for vertical text layout.|
|||`v-text-`<br>`spacing-mode`|Specifies the mode for letter spacing.  Default is`tightening`.<br>This property determines whether space is removed between<br>each letter (`tightening`) or added between each letter<br>(`tracking`).  The amount of letter spacing change is defined by<br>the`v-text-spacing`property.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`tightening`<br><br>`tracking`|
|||`v-text-spacing`|Specifies the amount of spacing for text in 100ths of single line<br>spacing.  Default is 100.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The following properties are not inherited by an element that references ashapetype<br>element (§19.1.2.20) via theidattribute:<br><br>`flip`<br><br>`height`<br><br>`left`<br><br>`margin-left`<br><br>`margin-top`<br><br>`position`<br><br>`rotation`<br><br>`top`<br><br>`visibility`<br><br>`width`<br><br>`z-index`<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|trim(Text Path Trim<br>Toggle)|Specifies whether extra space is removed above and below the text.  If`true`, space<br>reserved for ascenders and descenders is removed.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_: The shape path is duplicated as a second shape and overlaid on the textpath<br>for illustrative purposes:<br>`<v:shape style=" width:100;height:100"`<br>`path="m 0,500 c 250,0 750,0 1000,500 e`<br>`m 0,600 c 250,900 750,900 1000,600 e"`<br>`fillcolor="yellow" strokecolor="maroon">`<br>`<v:path textpathok="true"/>`<br>`<v:textpath on="true" fitshape="true"`<br>`string="vml" trim="true"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>`trim="true"`<br>`trim="false"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||(§20.1.2.5).|
|xscale(Text X-<br>Scaling)|Specifies whether a straight text path is used instead of the shape path.  If`true`, the text<br>runs along a path from left to right along the x value of the lower boundary of the shape.<br>Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_TextPath) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## **19.1.3 Simple Types**

The following additional simple type information in the urn:schemas-microsoft-com:vml namespace is used for documents of a transitional conformance class.

## 19.1.3.1 ST_EditAs (Shape Grouping Types)

This simple type specifies the different meanings of a group of shapes.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bullseye(Bullseye Diagram)|Specifies that the group represents a bulls-eye<br>diagram.|
|canvas(Shape Canvas)|Specifies that the group is a regular group and does<br>not represent a diagram.|
|cycle(Cycle Diagram)|Specifies that the group represents a cycle diagram.|
|orgchart(Organization Chart Diagram)|Specifies that the group represents an organization<br>chart.|
|radial(Radial Diagram)|Specifies that the group represents a radial diagram.|
|stacked(Pyramid Diagram)|Specifies that the group represents a pyramid diagram.|
|venn(Venn Diagram)|Specifies that the group represents a Venn diagram.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_EditAs) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.3.2 ST_Ext (VML Extension Handling Behaviors)

This simple type specifies VML extension handling behaviors.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|backwardCompatible(Renderable)|Specifies that the VML entity may be rendered by<br>ignoring the extension information.  If edited, the<br>extension information must be discarded.|
|edit(Editable)|Specifies that the VML entity may be safely rendered<br>and edited without invalidating the extension<br>information.|
|view(Not renderable)|Specifies that the VML entity is not be renderable<br>without understanding the extension information.  If<br>the extension information cannot be understood, the<br>downlevel image should be used to render the object.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_Ext) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.3.3 ST_FillMethod (Gradient Fill Computation Type)

This simple type specifies ways in which a gradient fill is computed.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|any(Application Default Fill)|Default blend|
|linear(Linear Fill)|Linear blend|
|linear sigma(Linear Sigma Fill)|Linear sigma blend|
|none(No Gradient Fill)|No blend|
|sigma(Sigma Fill)|Sigma blend|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_FillMethod) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.3.4 ST_FillType (Shape Fill Type)

This simple type specifies the types for fills applied to a shape.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|frame(Stretch Image to Fit)|The image is stretched to fill the shape.|
|gradient(Linear Gradient)|The fill colors blend together in a linear gradient from<br>bottom to top.|
|gradientRadial(Radial Gradient)|The fill colors blend together in a radial gradient.|
|pattern(Image Pattern)|The image is used to create a pattern using the fill<br>colors.|
|solid(Solid Fill)|The fill pattern is a solid color.|
|tile(Tiled Image)|The fill image is tiled.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_FillType) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.3.5 ST_ImageAspect (Image Scaling Behavior)

This simple type specifies the scaling behaviors for an image applied to a stroke.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|atLeast(At Least)|Image is at least as big asimagesize.|
|atMost(At Most)|Image is no bigger thanimagesize.|
|ignore(Ignore Aspect Ratio)|Ignore aspect issues.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_ImageAspect) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.3.6 ST_ShadowType (Shadow Type)

This simple type specifies the types of shadows applied to a shape.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|double(Double Shadow)|Double shadow.color2andoffset2are used for the<br>second shadow's color and offset.|
|emboss(Embossed Shadow)|The shadow has an embossed look.  Similar todouble.|
|perspective(Perspective Shadow)|Perspective shadow.|
|single(Single Shadow)|Single shadow.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_ShadowType) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.3.7 ST_StrokeArrowLength (Stroke Arrowhead Length)

This simple type specifies the lengths of a stroke arrowhead.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|long(Long Arrowhead)|Long length|

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|medium(Medium Arrowhead)|Medium length|
|short(Short Arrowhead)|Short length|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_StrokeArrowLength) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.3.8 ST_StrokeArrowType (Stroke Arrowhead Type)

This simple type specifies the types of arrowhead for a stroke.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|block(Block Arrowhead)|Block arrowhead|
|classic(Classic Arrowhead)|Classic curved arrowhead|
|diamond(Diamond Arrowhead)|Diamond arrowhead|
|none(No Arrowhead)|No arrowhead|
|open(Open Arrowhead)|Open arrowhead|
|oval(Oval Arrowhead)|Round arrowhead|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_StrokeArrowType) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.3.9 ST_StrokeArrowWidth (Stroke Arrowhead Width)

This simple type specifies the widths of a stroke arrowhead.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|medium(Medium Arrowhead)|Medium width|
|narrow(Narrow Arrowhead)|Narrow width|

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|wide(Wide Arrowhead)|Wide width|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_StrokeArrowWidth) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.3.10 ST_StrokeEndCap (Stroke End Cap Type)

This simple type specifies the styles for the end of a stroke.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|flat(Flat End)|Flat end|
|round(Round End)|Round end|
|square(Square End)|Square end|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_StrokeEndCap) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.3.11 ST_StrokeJoinStyle (Line Join Type)

This simple type specifies the join styles for a polyline (§19.1.2.15).

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bevel(Bevel Joint)|Bevel joint|

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|miter(Miter Joint)|Miter joint|
|round(Round Joint)|Round joint|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_StrokeJoinStyle) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## 19.1.3.12 ST_StrokeLineStyle (Stroke Line Style)

This simple type specifies the line styles for a stroke.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|single(Single Line)|Single line|
|thickBetweenThin(Thck Line Between Thin Lines)|Thick line between thin lines|
|thickThin(Thick Line Outside Thin Line)|Thick line outside thin line|
|thinThick(Thin Line Outside Thick Line)|Thin line outside thick line|
|thinThin(Two Thin Lines)|Two thin lines|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_StrokeLineStyle) is located in §A.7.1. _end note_ ]

## **19.2 VML - Office Drawing**

It is possible to include graphical VML objects in Office Open XML documents.  The elements describing the core graphical objects are defined in the VML namespace.  Additional elements that describe certain advanced shape effects, metadata and relationships are defined in this namespace.

[ _Note_ : The VML format is a legacy format originally introduced with Office 2000 and is included and fully defined in ECMA-376 for backwards compatibility reasons. The DrawingML format is a newer and richer format created with the goal of eventually replacing any uses of VML in the Office Open XML formats. VML should be considered a transitional format included in Office Open XML for legacy reasons only and new applications that need a file format for drawings are strongly encouraged to use preferentially DrawingML . _end note_ ]

[ _Example:_ Assume the following shape exists in a document:

The basic speech bubble shape is defined using VML.  The 3-D effect is defined using the extrusion element in this namespace.  The specularity attribute defines the subtle sharp reflection on the edge of the shape.  The color attribute sets the extrusion to a different color than the face of the shape.  The rotationangle attribute sets the shape's rotation about the X- and Y-axes.  The lightposition and lightposition2 attributes set the positions of the light sources that illuminate the shape.

```
  <o:extrusion v:ext="view" specularity="80000f" color="#c4bc96 [2414]" on="t"
  rotationangle="-5,15" lightposition="0,-50000" lightposition2="0,50000"
  type="perspective"/>
```

This element is a child of the primary shape definition:

- `<v:shape id="_x0000_s1030" type="#_x0000_t62"`

- `style="position:absolute;left:0;text-align:left;margin-left:35.25pt; margin-top:60pt;width:69pt;height:57pt;z-index:251658240" adj="1675,27171" fillcolor="#ddd8c2 [2894]">`

- `<o:extrusion … />`

- `</v:shape>`

_end example_ ]

Throughout VML, numeric values that are allowed to take units can be specified in: cm (centimeters), mm (millimeters), in (inches), pt (points), pc (picas), px (pixels).

## **19.2.2 Elements**

The following elements comprise the contents of the urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:office namespace:

[ _Note_ : As the VML format is a format provided for backward compatibility, those VML elements defined in the same urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:office namespace remain in that namespace as it is already used by millions of documents already using VML. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.1 bottom (Text Box Bottom Stroke)

This element specifies the stroke properties for the bottom border of a text box.  It entirely supercedes its parent stroke element if its on attribute is true.  Thus the default value of an unspecified attribute overrides a value specified in the parent.  If the on attribute is `false` or not specified, the border is not shown.

[ _Example_ : The text box borders are set independently.  Note that the bottom border does not inherit the weight from the parent stroke element.

- `<v:stroke weight="2.25pt">`

- `<o:left v:ext="view" dashstyle="1 1" color="blue" weight="5pt" on="t"/>`

- `<o:top v:ext="view" color="black" weight="2.25pt" on="t"/>`

- `<o:right v:ext="view" dashstyle="1 1" color="blue" weight="5pt" on="t"/>`

- `<o:bottom v:ext="view" color="black" on="t"/>`

- `<o:column v:ext="view" color="#f60" on="t"/>`

- `</v:stroke>`

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|althref(Alternate<br>Image Reference)|Specifies an alternate reference for an image in Macintosh PICT format.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … althref="myimage.pcz" … >`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|color(Stroke Color)|Specifies the stroke color.  Overrides thestrokecolorattribute of a shape.  Default is<br>`black`.  See thefillcolorattribute for a list of supported named colors.<br>[_Example_: The shape stroke is blue:<br>`<v:shape … strokecolor="red" … >`<br>`<v:stroke color="blue"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|color2(Stroke<br>Alternate Pattern<br>Color)|Specifies a second color for strokes, used whenfilltypeis`pattern`.  Default is no value.<br>When a pattern fill is used for the stroke, the stroke color is used in colored parts of the<br>source image.  The color2 defines an alternate color to use in place of black in the source<br>image.<br>[_Example_: This unusual example is intended to demonstrate how the image and colors<br>interact to create a patterned stroke.  The yellow background shows transparency.  The<br>non-square shape and square image create an effective offset.  The heavy stroke weight<br>shows more of the image.  The green shape fill shows how the stroke is overlaid on the<br>shape.<br>`<v:background fillcolor="yellow"/>`<br>`<v:shape style="width:60;height:50"`<br>`strokecolor="red" fillcolor="lime"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke filltype="pattern" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="myimage.gif" color2="blue"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||, where myimage.gif is:<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|dashstyle(Stroke<br>Dash Pattern)|Specifies the dot and dash pattern for a stroke.  Default is`solid`.  Pre-defined values are:<br><br>`solid`<br><br>`shortdash`<br><br>`shortdot`<br><br>`shortdashdot`<br><br>`shortdashdotdot`<br><br>`dot`<br><br>`dash`<br><br>`longdash`<br><br>`dashdot`<br><br>`longdashdot`<br><br>`longdashdotdot`<br>A custom-defined dash pattern can also be specified using a series of numbers.  These<br>define the length of the dash (the drawn part of the stroke) and the length of the space<br>between the dashes.  The lengths are relative to the line width: a length of 1 is equal to<br>the line width.  Theendcapstyle is applied to each dash but the arrow style is not.  The<br>string defines the length of the dash then the length of the space.  This can be repeated<br>to form complex dash styles.  The string should always contain a pair of numbers; if it<br>contains an odd number of numbers the last is disregarded.  0 implies a dot that is<br>fourfold symmetrical (with round end caps, this is a circle).<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke dashstyle="0 2" weight="3pt"`<br>`endcap="round">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>`<v:stroke dashstyle="longdashdotdot"`<br>`weight="2pt">`<br>`</v:stroke>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|endarrow(Line End<br>Arrowhead)|Specifies an arrowhead for the end of a line.  Default is`none`.  The path shall not be<br>closed with the x command for the arrowhead to show.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`block`<br><br>`classic`<br><br>`diamond`<br><br>`oval`<br><br>`open`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke endarrow="classic"/>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowTypesimple<br>type (§19.1.3.8).|
|endarrowlength<br>(Line End<br>Arrowhead Length)|Specifies the length of the arrowhead at the end of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`short`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`long`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endarrowlength="long" … />`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowLengthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.7).|
|endarrowwidth<br>(Line End<br>Arrowhead Width)|Specifies the width of the arrowhead at the end of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`narrow`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`wide`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endarrowwidth="wide" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowWidthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.9).|
|endcap(Line End<br>Cap)|Specifies the cap style for the end of a stroke.  Default is`flat`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`flat`<br><br>`square`<br><br>`round`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endcap="round" weight="10pt" … />`<br>`endcap="flat"`<br>`endcap="square"`<br>`endcap="round"`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeEndCapsimple type<br>(§19.1.3.10).|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|
|filltype(Stroke<br>Image Style)|Specifies the kind of fill used for the background of a stroke.  Default is`solid`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`solid`- The fill pattern is solid.<br><br>`tile`- The fill image is tiled.<br><br>`pattern`- The fill image is stretched to form a pattern.<br><br>`frame`- The fill image becomes a border for the shape.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape style="width:50;height:50"`<br>`strokecolor="red"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke filltype="frame" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="border.gif"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>, where border.gif is:<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_FillTypesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.4).|
|forcedash(Force<br>Dashed Outline)|Specifies whether a dashed outline is used to draw a shape when a shape has no line or<br>fill.  Default is`false`.<br>Used by PresentationML placeholders to draw a dashed outline when there is no line and<br>no fill for a shape.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:forcedash="true" … >`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|href(Original Image<br>Reference)|Specifies the URL to the original image file.  Used only if the picture has been linked and<br>embedded.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … o:href="myimage.gif" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|imagealignshape<br>(Stoke Image<br>Alignment)|Specifies the alignment of the stroke image.  If`true`, the image is aligned with the shape.<br>Otherwise, it is aligned with the containing scope.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_: The top position offset shifts the image alignment relative to the containing<br>window:<br>`<v:shape fillcolor="silver"`<br>`style="top:20;width:50;height:50"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke imagealignshape="false" weight="20pt"`<br>`filltype="tile" src="myimage.gif"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>`imagealignshape="false"`<br>`imagealignshape="false"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|
|---|---|---|
|||(§20.1.2.5).|
|imageaspect<br>(Stroke Image<br>Aspect Ratio)||Specifies how the stroke image aspect ratio is preserved.  Default is`ignore`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br>Value<br>Description<br>`ignore`<br>Ignore aspect issues.<br>`atleast`<br>Image is at least as big asimagesize.<br>`atmost`<br>Image is no bigger thanimagesize.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke filltype="frame" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="border.gif" imagealignshape="true"`<br>`imageaspect="atleast">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>`imagealignshape="ignore"`<br>`imagealignshape="atleast"`<br>`imagealignshape="atmost"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ImageAspectsimple type<br>(§19.1.3.5).|
|imagesize(Stroke<br>Image Size)||Specifies the size of the image for the stroke.  Default is the size of the image.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … imagesize="10pt,10pt" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|insetpen(Inset<br>Border From Path)||Specifies that the border shall be displayed inside of the path defining the shape, rather<br>than along the path (the default border placement), or outside of the path as might be<br>done with an image.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … insetpen="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|joinstyle(Line End<br>Join Style))|Specifies the join style for line ends.  Default is`round`.<br><br>`round`<br><br>`bevel`<br><br>`miter`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:polyline strokeweight="10pt" strokecolor="navy"`<br>`points="10pt,10pt,50pt,50pt,90pt,10pt">`<br>`<v:stroke joinstyle="bevel"/>`<br>`</v:polyline>`<br>`joinstyle="round"`<br>`joinstyle="bevel"`<br>`joinstyle="miter"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeJoinStylesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.11).|
|linestyle(Stroke<br>Line Style)|Specifies the line style of the stroke.  Default is`single`.<br><br>`single`<br><br>`thinThin`<br><br>`thinThick`<br><br>`thickThin`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||<br>`thickBetweenThin`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke linestyle="thickThin" weight="5pt">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeLineStylesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.12).|
|miterlimit(Miter<br>Joint Limit)|Specifies the smoothness of the miter joint, or the maximum distance between the inner<br>point and outer point of a joint.  This number is a multiple of the thickness of the line.<br>Default is 8.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke joinstyle="miter" weight="10pt"`<br>`miterlimit="2">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemadecimal<br>datatype.|
|on(Stroke Toggle)|Specifies whether the stroke is displayed.  Default is`true`.  This attribute overrides the<br>shape'sstrokeattribute.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:rect style="width:50;height:50" stroked="true"`<br>`fillcolor="lime" strokecolor="red">`<br>`<v:stroke on="false" weight="5pt"/>`<br>`</v:rect>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|opacity(Stroke<br>Opacity)|Specifies the amount of transparency of a stroke.  Default is 1.0.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:rect style="width:50;height:50"`<br>`fillcolor="lime" strokecolor="red">`<br>`<v:stroke weight="5pt" opacity="50%"/>`<br>`</v:rect>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|src(Stroke Image<br>Location)|Specifies the source image to load for a stroke fill.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … src="myimage.gif" … >`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|startarrow(Line<br>Start Arrowhead)|Specifies an arrowhead for the start of a line.  Default is`none`.  The path shall not be<br>closed with the x command for the arrowhead to show.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`block`<br><br>`classic`<br><br>`diamond`<br><br>`oval`<br><br>`open`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke startarrow="classic"/>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowTypesimple<br>type (§19.1.3.8).|
|startarrowlength<br>(Line Start<br>Arrowhead Length)|Specifies the length of the arrowhead at the start of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`short`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`long`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … startarrowlength="long" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowLengthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.7).|
|startarrowwidth<br>(Line Start<br>Arrowhead Width)|Specifies the width of the arrowhead at the start of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`narrow`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`wide`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … startarrowwidth="wide" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowWidthsimple|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||type (§19.1.3.9).|
|title(Stroke Title)|Specifies the title of an embedded stroke image.  This is typically set to the comment<br>property of the image, which is often blank.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … o:title="alt text" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|weight(Stroke<br>Weight)|Specifies the thickness of a stroke.  Default is 1.  This attribute overrides the shape's<br>strokeweightattribute.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_StrokeChild) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.2 callout (Callout)

This element specifies the automatic behavior and layout parameters of callout shapes. Callout shapes are standard VML shapes that behave as callouts, providing an additional callout object which can be used to point at another location:

[ _Example_ : Consider the following VML shape:

If this shape is made a callout shape by adding the callout element to its shape definition, then the shape has a callout object, for example:

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|accentbar(Callout<br>accent bar toggle)|Specifies whether an accent bar is used with the callout.  Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|angle(Callout<br>angle)|Specifies the angle that the callout makes with respect to the bounding box of the shape.<br>Default is no value.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Anglesimple type (§19.2.3.2).|
|distance(Callout<br>drop distance)|Specifies the drop distance of a callout.  The drop distance of a callout is measured from<br>the edge of the shape where the pointer line starts and continues the absolute length of<br>the distance value.  If specified with no units, EMUs are assumed.  Default is no value.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|drop(Callout drop<br>position)|Specifies where the drop of a callout is placed.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_CalloutDropsimple type<br>(§19.2.3.4).|
|dropauto(Callout<br>automatic drop<br>toggle)|Specifies whether the callout has an automatic drop.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|
|gap(Callout gap)|Specifies the distance of the callout line from the bounding rectangle of the callout.<br>Default value is one-twelfth of an inch, in EMUs (76200).<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|length(Callout<br>length)|Specifies the length of the first part of a multi-segmented callout line.  If specified with no<br>units, EMUs are assumed.  Default is 0.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|lengthspecified<br>(Callout length<br>toggle)|Specifies whether thelengthattribute is used for the callout.  Default is`false`.  If`true`,<br>the length attribute is used.  If`false`, a best fit is used.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|minusx(Callout flip<br>x)|Specifies whether the callout flips to the other side of the drop tip along the x-axis when<br>moved or resized.  Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|minusy(Callout flip<br>y)|Specifies whether the callout flips to the other side of the drop tip along the y-axis when<br>moved or resized.  Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|on(Callout toggle)|Specifies whether a shape is a callout.  Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|textborder(Callout<br>text border toggle)|Specifies whether a callout has a text border.  Default is`true`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|type(Callout type)|Specifies the type of callout.  Default is`rectangle`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`rectangle`<br><br>`roundedrectangle`<br><br>`oval`<br><br>`cloud`<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Callout) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.3 clippath (Shape Clipping Path)

This element specifies the path of the clipping polygon for the shape.

[ _Example_ :

`<v:rect … wrapcoords="-207 -433 -207 21925 21807 21925 21807 -433 -207 -433"`

- `o:clip="t" o:cliptowrap="t">`

```
  <o:clippath o:v="m-207,-433r,22358l21807,21925r,-22358l-207,-433xe"/>
```

```
</v:rect>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**||**Description**|**Description**|**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|v(Path Definition)||Specifies a string containing the commands that define the shape's path.  This value<br>consists of commands followed by zero or more parameters.  Default is no value.<br>The following rules apply to path strings:<br><br>Commas or spaces delimit parameters for each command.  Both "m 0,0" and "m0<br>0" are acceptable.<br><br>A parameter that is omitted using commas is treated as having a value of zero.<br>Thus, "c 10,10,0,0,25,13" and "c 10,10,,,25,13" are equivalent.<br><br>Parameterized paths are also allowed.  In this case, the shape shall also have a<br>formulaselement (§19.1.2.6) with a list of formulas that are substituted into the<br>path using the @ symbol followed by the number of the formula.  Theadj<br>property of the shape contains the input parameters for these formulas.  For<br>example, "moveto @1@4".   The evaluations of the formulas are substituted into<br>the appropriate positions.  @ also serves as a delimiter.<br>The allowed commands are given below.  An asterisk (*) indicates that the command is<br>allowed to be repeated.  For the`qb`command, the controlpoint parameter is also allowed<br>to be repeated.<br>**Command**<br>**Name**<br>**Parameters**<br>**Description**<br>`m`<br>moveto<br>2<br>Start a new sub-path at the given (x,y)<br>coordinate.<br>`l`<br>lineto<br>2*<br>Draw a line from the current point to<br>the given (x,y) coordinate which<br>becomes the new current point.<br>Specifying a number of coordinate<br>pairs forms a polyline.<br>`c`<br>curveto<br>6*<br>Draw a cubic bézier curve from the<br>current point to the coordinate given<br>by the final two parameters.  The<br>control points are given by the first<br>four parameters.<br>`x`<br>close<br>0<br>Close the current sub-path by drawing<br>a straight line from the current point<br>to the original moveto point.<br>`e`<br>end<br>0<br>End the current set of sub-paths.  A<br>given set of sub-paths (as delimited by<br>end) is filled.  Subsequent sets of sub-<br>paths are filled independently and<br>superimposed on existing ones.||||
|||**Command**|**Name**|**Parameters**|**Description**|
|||`m`|moveto|2|Start a new sub-path at the given (x,y)<br>coordinate.|
|||`l`|lineto|2*|Draw a line from the current point to<br>the given (x,y) coordinate which<br>becomes the new current point.<br>Specifying a number of coordinate<br>pairs forms a polyline.|
|||`c`|curveto|6*|Draw a cubic bézier curve from the<br>current point to the coordinate given<br>by the final two parameters.  The<br>control points are given by the first<br>four parameters.|
|||`x`|close|0|Close the current sub-path by drawing<br>a straight line from the current point<br>to the original moveto point.|
|||`e`|end|0|End the current set of sub-paths.  A<br>given set of sub-paths (as delimited by<br>end) is filled.  Subsequent sets of sub-<br>paths are filled independently and<br>superimposed on existing ones.|

|**Attributes**||||**Description**|**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|||`t`|rmoveto|2*|Start a new sub-path at a coordinate<br>relative to the current point, cp<br>(cpx+x, cpy+y).||
|||`r`|rlineto|2*|Draw a line from the current point to<br>the given relative coordinate (cpx+x,<br>cpy+y).||
|||`v`|rcurveto|6*|Cubic bézier curve using the given<br>coordinate relative to the current<br>point.||
|||`nf`|nofill|0|The current set of sub-paths<br>(delimited by e) is not filled.||
|||`ns`|nostroke|0|The current set of sub-paths<br>(delimited by e) is not stroked.||
|||`ae`|angleellipseto|6*|Draws a segment of an ellipse as<br>described using these parameters.  A<br>straight line is drawn from the current<br>point to the start point of the<br>segment.  The parameters are: center<br>(x,y), size(w,h), start angle, end angle.||
|||`al`|angleellipse|6*|Same as angleellipseto except that<br>there is an implied moveto the starting<br>point of the segment.||
|||`at`|arcto|8*|A segment of the ellipse is drawn<br>which starts at the angle defined by<br>the start radius vector and ends at the<br>angle defined by the end vector.  A<br>straight line is drawn from the current<br>point to the start of the arc.  The arc is<br>always drawn in a counterclockwise<br>direction.  The parameters are: left,<br>top, right, bottom, start(x,y), end(x,y).<br>The first four values define the<br>bounding box of an ellipse.  The last<br>four define two radial vectors.||
|||`ar`|arc|8*|Same as arcto except there is an<br>implied moveto the start point of the<br>arc.||
|||`wa`|clockwisearct<br>o|8*|Same as arcto but the arc is drawn in a<br>clockwise direction.||
|||`wr`|clockwisearc|8*|Same as arc but the arc is drawn in a<br>clockwise direction||

|**Attributes**||||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|||`qx`|ellipticalqaudr<br>antx|2*|A quarter ellipse is drawn from the<br>current point to the given end point.<br>The elliptical segment is initially<br>tangential to a line parallel to the x-<br>axis. (i.e. the segment starts out<br>horizontal).  The parameters are:<br>end(x,y).|
|||`qy`|ellipticalquadr<br>anty|2*|Same as ellipticalquadrantx except<br>that the elliptical segment is initially<br>tangential to a line parallel to the y-<br>axis (i.e. the segment starts out<br>vertical).|
|||`qb`|quadraticbezi<br>er|2+2*|Defines one or more quadratic bézier<br>curves by means of control points and<br>an end point.  Intermediate (on-curve)<br>points are obtained by interpolation<br>between successive control points as<br>in the OpenType font<br>specification.  The sub-path need not<br>be started in which case the sub-path<br>is closed.  In this case the last point of<br>the sub-path defines the start point of<br>the quadratic bézier.  The parameters<br>are: controlpoint(x,y)*, end(x,y).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_ClipPath) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.4 colormenu (UI Default Colors)

This element determines the default colors for different types of colors that can be applied to VML shapes. [ _Rationale_ : An application can choose to retain default colors or the last color choices a user made and present those in parts of its user interface. _end rationale_ ]

[ _Example_ :

`<o:shapedefaults … >`

- `<o:colormenu v:ext="edit" fillcolor="none" extrusioncolor="#36f"/> </o:shapedefaults>`

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|
|extrusioncolor<br>(Default extrusion<br>color)|The default color associated with the 3D extrusion of a VML shape.  Default is "#000000".<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|fillcolor(Default fill<br>color)|The default color associated with the fill of a VML shape.  Default is "#0000FF".<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|shadowcolor<br>(Default shadow<br>color)|The default color associated with the shadow of a VML shape.  Default is "#80800C".<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|strokecolor<br>(Default stroke<br>color)|The default color associated with the stroke of a VML shape.  Default is "#FFFF00".<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_ColorMenu) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.5 colormru (Most Recently Used Colors)

This element defines a list of up to eight colors which represent the colors most recently used by the user. [ _Rationale_ : An application can choose to retain the last color choices a user made, regardless of where on VML shapes they are used, and present those in parts of its user interface. _end rationale_ ]

[ _Example_ :

`<o:shapedefaults … >`

```
  <o:colormru v:ext="edit" colors="#a01aae,#456b69,#06f,#a1ae24,#d57811"/>
</o:shapedefaults>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|colors(Recent<br>colors)|A comma-separated list of up to eight most recently used colors.  Default is no value.<br>Colors should be defined using hexadecimal notation - see theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3) for a full description.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:colormru v:ext="edit"`<br>`colors="#a01aae,#456b69,#06f,#a1ae24,#d57811"/>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_ColorMru) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.6 column (Text Box Interior Stroke)

This element specifies the stroke properties for the interior border of a text box.  It entirely supercedes its parent stroke element if its on attribute is true.  Thus the default value of an unspecified attribute overrides a value specified in the parent.  If the on attribute is `false` or not specified, the border is not shown.  [ _Note_ : This element is ignored if an implementation does not support multi-column text boxes. _end note_ ]

[ _Example_ : The text box borders are set independently.  Note that the bottom border does not inherit the weight from the parent stroke element.

- `<v:stroke weight="2.25pt">`

- `<o:left v:ext="view" dashstyle="1 1" color="blue" weight="5pt" on="t"/>`

- `<o:top v:ext="view" color="black" weight="2.25pt" on="t"/>`

- `<o:right v:ext="view" dashstyle="1 1" color="blue" weight="5pt" on="t"/>`

- `<o:bottom v:ext="view" color="black" on="t"/>`

- `<o:column v:ext="view" color="#f60" on="t"/>`

- `</v:stroke>`

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|althref(Alternate<br>Image Reference)|Specifies an alternate reference for an image in Macintosh PICT format.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … althref="myimage.pcz" … >`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|color(Stroke Color)|Specifies the stroke color.  Overrides thestrokecolorattribute of a shape.  Default is<br>`black`.  See thefillcolorattribute for a list of supported named colors.<br>[_Example_: The shape stroke is blue:<br>`<v:shape … strokecolor="red" … >`<br>`<v:stroke color="blue"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|color2(Stroke<br>Alternate Pattern<br>Color)|Specifies a second color for strokes, used whenfilltypeis`pattern`.  Default is no value.<br>When a pattern fill is used for the stroke, the stroke color is used in colored parts of the<br>source image.  The color2 defines an alternate color to use in place of black in the source<br>image.<br>[_Example_: This unusual example is intended to demonstrate how the image and colors<br>interact to create a patterned stroke.  The yellow background shows transparency.  The<br>non-square shape and square image create an effective offset.  The heavy stroke weight<br>shows more of the image.  The green shape fill shows how the stroke is overlaid on the<br>shape.<br>`<v:background fillcolor="yellow"/>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:shape style="width:60;height:50"`<br>`strokecolor="red" fillcolor="lime"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke filltype="pattern" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="myimage.gif" color2="blue"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>, where myimage.gif is:<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|dashstyle(Stroke<br>Dash Pattern)|Specifies the dot and dash pattern for a stroke.  Default is`solid`.  Pre-defined values are:<br><br>`solid`<br><br>`shortdash`<br><br>`shortdot`<br><br>`shortdashdot`<br><br>`shortdashdotdot`<br><br>`dot`<br><br>`dash`<br><br>`longdash`<br><br>`dashdot`<br><br>`longdashdot`<br><br>`longdashdotdot`<br>A custom-defined dash pattern can also be specified using a series of numbers.  These<br>define the length of the dash (the drawn part of the stroke) and the length of the space<br>between the dashes.  The lengths are relative to the line width: a length of 1 is equal to<br>the line width.  Theendcapstyle is applied to each dash but the arrow style is not.  The<br>string defines the length of the dash then the length of the space.  This can be repeated<br>to form complex dash styles.  The string should always contain a pair of numbers; if it<br>contains an odd number of numbers the last is disregarded.  0 implies a dot that is<br>fourfold symmetrical (with round end caps, this is a circle).<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke dashstyle="0 2" weight="3pt"`<br>`endcap="round">`<br>`</v:stroke>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:stroke dashstyle="longdashdotdot"`<br>`weight="2pt">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|endarrow(Line End<br>Arrowhead)|Specifies an arrowhead for the end of a line.  Default is`none`.  The path shall not be<br>closed with the x command for the arrowhead to show.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`block`<br><br>`classic`<br><br>`diamond`<br><br>`oval`<br><br>`open`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke endarrow="classic"/>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowTypesimple<br>type (§19.1.3.8).|
|endarrowlength<br>(Line End<br>Arrowhead Length)|Specifies the length of the arrowhead at the end of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`short`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`long`<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:stroke … endarrowlength="long" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowLengthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.7).|
|endarrowwidth<br>(Line End<br>Arrowhead Width)|Specifies the width of the arrowhead at the end of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`narrow`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`wide`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endarrowwidth="wide" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowWidthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.9).|
|endcap(Line End<br>Cap)|Specifies the cap style for the end of a stroke.  Default is`flat`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`flat`<br><br>`square`<br><br>`round`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endcap="round" weight="10pt" … />`<br>`endcap="flat"`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`endcap="square"`<br>`endcap="round"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeEndCapsimple type<br>(§19.1.3.10).|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|
|filltype(Stroke<br>Image Style)|Specifies the kind of fill used for the background of a stroke.  Default is`solid`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`solid`- The fill pattern is solid.<br><br>`tile`- The fill image is tiled.<br><br>`pattern`- The fill image is stretched to form a pattern.<br><br>`frame`- The fill image becomes a border for the shape.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape style="width:50;height:50"`<br>`strokecolor="red"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke filltype="frame" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="border.gif"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>, where border.gif is:<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_FillTypesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.4).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|forcedash(Force<br>Dashed Outline)|Specifies whether a dashed outline is used to draw a shape when a shape has no line or<br>fill.  Default is`false`.<br>Used by PresentationML placeholders to draw a dashed outline when there is no line and<br>no fill for a shape.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:forcedash="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|href(Original Image<br>Reference)|Specifies the URL to the original image file.  Used only if the picture has been linked and<br>embedded.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … o:href="myimage.gif" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|imagealignshape<br>(Stoke Image<br>Alignment)|Specifies the alignment of the stroke image.  If`true`, the image is aligned with the shape.<br>Otherwise, it is aligned with the containing scope.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_: The top position offset shifts the image alignment relative to the containing<br>window:<br>`<v:shape fillcolor="silver"`<br>`style="top:20;width:50;height:50"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke imagealignshape="false" weight="20pt"`<br>`filltype="tile" src="myimage.gif"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>`imagealignshape="false"`|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|
|---|---|---|
|||`imagealignshape="false"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|imageaspect<br>(Stroke Image<br>Aspect Ratio)||Specifies how the stroke image aspect ratio is preserved.  Default is`ignore`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br>Value<br>Description<br>`ignore`<br>Ignore aspect issues.<br>`atleast`<br>Image is at least as big asimagesize.<br>`atmost`<br>Image is no bigger thanimagesize.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke filltype="frame" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="border.gif" imagealignshape="true"`<br>`imageaspect="atleast">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>`imagealignshape="ignore"`<br>`imagealignshape="atleast"`<br>`imagealignshape="atmost"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ImageAspectsimple type<br>(§19.1.3.5).|
|imagesize(Stroke<br>Image Size)||Specifies the size of the image for the stroke.  Default is the size of the image.<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:stroke … imagesize="10pt,10pt" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|insetpen(Inset<br>Border From Path)|Specifies that the border shall be displayed inside of the path defining the shape, rather<br>than along the path (the default border placement), or outside of the path as might be<br>done with an image.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … insetpen="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|joinstyle(Line End<br>Join Style))|Specifies the join style for line ends.  Default is`round`.<br><br>`round`<br><br>`bevel`<br><br>`miter`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:polyline strokeweight="10pt" strokecolor="navy"`<br>`points="10pt,10pt,50pt,50pt,90pt,10pt">`<br>`<v:stroke joinstyle="bevel"/>`<br>`</v:polyline>`<br>`joinstyle="round"`<br>`joinstyle="bevel"`<br>`joinstyle="miter"`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeJoinStylesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.11).|
|linestyle(Stroke<br>Line Style)|Specifies the line style of the stroke.  Default is`single`.<br><br>`single`<br><br>`thinThin`<br><br>`thinThick`<br><br>`thickThin`<br><br>`thickBetweenThin`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke linestyle="thickThin" weight="5pt">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeLineStylesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.12).|
|miterlimit(Miter<br>Joint Limit)|Specifies the smoothness of the miter joint, or the maximum distance between the inner<br>point and outer point of a joint.  This number is a multiple of the thickness of the line.<br>Default is 8.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke joinstyle="miter" weight="10pt"`<br>`miterlimit="2">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemadecimal<br>datatype.|
|on(Stroke Toggle)|Specifies whether the stroke is displayed.  Default is`true`.  This attribute overrides the<br>shape'sstrokeattribute.<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:rect style="width:50;height:50" stroked="true"`<br>`fillcolor="lime" strokecolor="red">`<br>`<v:stroke on="false" weight="5pt"/>`<br>`</v:rect>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|opacity(Stroke<br>Opacity)|Specifies the amount of transparency of a stroke.  Default is 1.0.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:rect style="width:50;height:50"`<br>`fillcolor="lime" strokecolor="red">`<br>`<v:stroke weight="5pt" opacity="50%"/>`<br>`</v:rect>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|src(Stroke Image<br>Location)|Specifies the source image to load for a stroke fill.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … src="myimage.gif" … >`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|startarrow(Line<br>Start Arrowhead)|Specifies an arrowhead for the start of a line.  Default is`none`.  The path shall not be<br>closed with the x command for the arrowhead to show.  Allowed values are:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||<br>`none`<br><br>`block`<br><br>`classic`<br><br>`diamond`<br><br>`oval`<br><br>`open`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke startarrow="classic"/>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowTypesimple<br>type (§19.1.3.8).|
|startarrowlength<br>(Line Start<br>Arrowhead Length)|Specifies the length of the arrowhead at the start of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`short`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`long`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … startarrowlength="long" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowLengthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.7).|
|startarrowwidth<br>(Line Start<br>Arrowhead Width)|Specifies the width of the arrowhead at the start of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`narrow`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`wide`<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:stroke … startarrowwidth="wide" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowWidthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.9).|
|title(Stroke Title)|Specifies the title of an embedded stroke image.  This is typically set to the comment<br>property of the image, which is often blank.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … o:title="alt text" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|weight(Stroke<br>Weight)|Specifies the thickness of a stroke.  Default is 1.  This attribute overrides the shape's<br>strokeweightattribute.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_StrokeChild) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.7 complex (Complex)

This element specifies that a shapetype contains fragments.

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Complex) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.8 diagram (VML Diagram)

This element specifies semantic information for a limited set of structured diagrams that have VML representations.  Diagrams should be defined using DrawingML; this representation is included for compatibility with applications that rely on VML.  The following diagram types have VML representations:

|**Diagram Type **|**Example(non-normative)**|
|---|---|
|Organization chart||
|Radial||
|Cycle||
|Pyramid||
|Venn||
|Bulls-eye||

Each of these types of diagrams contains shapes that are positioned relative to one another.  Each shape also has optional associated text.

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|autoformat<br>(Diagram Automatic<br>Format)|Specifies whether the diagram is formatted automatically by the application and user<br>overrides are locked.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:diagram … autoformat="true">`<br>`</o:diagram>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|autolayout<br>(Diagram Automatic<br>Layout)|Specifies whether the diagram elements are laid out automatically by the application and<br>user overrides are locked.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:diagram … autolayout="false">`<br>`</o:diagram>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|constrainbounds<br>(Diagram Layout<br>Extents)|Specifies an optional, application-specific parameter related to the diagram's extents<br>intended to be used by the application to assist laying out the diagram.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:diagram … constrainbounds="2910,2696,9773,9558">`<br>`</o:diagram>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|dgmbasetextscale<br>(Diagram Base Font<br>Size)|Specifies the diagram's original font size.  This is used in subsequent font size<br>recalculations.  If the most recent diagram font size is used to calculate the font size after<br>a rescale, the font size would be wrong after non-isometric diagram rescalings.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:diagram … dgmbasetextscale="12">`<br>`</o:diagram>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemainteger<br>datatype.|
|dgmfontsize<br>(Diagram Font Size)|Specifies the font size for attached text when a new diagram node is added.<br>[_Example:_<br>`<o:diagram … dgmfontsize="12">`<br>`</o:diagram>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemainteger<br>datatype.|
|dgmscalex<br>(Diagram Layout X<br>Scale)|Specifies an optional, application-specific parameter related to the horizontal scaling of<br>the diagram that is intended to be used by the application to assist laying out the<br>diagram.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:diagram … dgmscalex="50000">`<br>`</o:diagram>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemainteger<br>datatype.|
|dgmscaley<br>(Diagram Layout Y<br>Scale)|Specifies an optional, application-specific parameter related to the vertical scaling of the<br>diagram that is intended to be used by the application to assist laying out the diagram.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:diagram … dgmscaley="75000">`<br>`</o:diagram>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemainteger<br>datatype.|
|dgmstyle(Diagram<br>Style Options)|Specifies an optional, application-specific parameter related to the styling of the diagram<br>that is intended to be used by the application to assist in formatting the diagram.<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<o:diagram … dgmstyle="1">`<br>`</o:diagram>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemainteger<br>datatype.|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|
|reverse(Diagram<br>Reverse Direction)|Specifies whether the order of the diagram nodes is reversed.  This is only relevant to<br>diagrams that have linear ordering.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:diagram … reverse="true">`<br>`</o:diagram>`<br>`reverse="false"`<br>`reverse="true"`<br>_end example_<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Diagram) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.9 entry (Regroup Entry)

This element specifies a single entry in a regrouptable (§19.2.2.23).  Each entry is a pair mapping a current regroupid value to an old one.  This is used to restore regrouping information on the regrouped object.  A value of zero indicates no previous group.

[ _Example_ : The zero value of the old attribute indicates that if the shapes with regroupid 1 are regrouped, the restored group was not previously grouped with any other shapes:

`<o:regrouptable v:ext="edit">`

```
  <o:entry new="1" old="0"/>
```

```
</o:regrouptable>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|new(New Group<br>ID)|Specifies the ID of the new group.  Default is 0.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaintdatatype.|
|old(Old Group ID)|Specifies the ID of the old group.  Default is 0.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaintdatatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Entry) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.10 equationxml (Storage for Alternate Math Content)

This element specifies XML markup for mathematical text which can be used in place of the shape data. [ _Note:_ Applications are encouraged to use an open format, such as the Math format defined in ECMA-376-1, or the MathML format, a Recommendation from the World Wide Web Consortium, available at http://www.w3.org/TR/MathML/. _end note_ ]

[ _Example_ : Consider a VML object which specifies alternate math content using MathML. This object might contain the following XML markup:

```
<v:shape>
```

```
  …
```

```
  <o:equationXml contentType="mathml">
```

```
    <mrow>
```

```
      <mrow>
```

```
        <msup>
          <mi>x</mi>
          <mn>2</mn>
```

```
        </msup>
```

```
        <mo>+</mo>
        <mrow>
          <mn>4</mn>
          <mo>*</mo>
          <mi>x</mi>
        </mrow>
        <mo>+</mo>
        <mn>4</mn>
      </mrow>
      <mo>=</mo>
      <mn>0</mn>
    </mrow>
  </o:equationXml>
</v:shape>
```

The embedded MathML markup is stored within the equationxml element. _end example_ ]

If a producer that wants interoperability supports equations, it should use one of the following standard formats:

- Office Open XML Math (Part 1, §22.1

- W3C MathML 2.0

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|contentType<br>(Content Type of<br>Alternate Math<br>Content)|Specifies the syntax of the markup used for the alternate math content stored in the<br>equationxmlattribute.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_AlternateMathContentType<br>simple type (§19.2.3.1).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_EquationXml) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.11 extrusion (3D Extrusion)

This element specifies a parallel or perspective extrusion of a 2-D shape, creating the appearance of a 3-D shape. Lighting is controlled via two independent point light sources.  Extrusions are defined as either perspective or parallel.

[ _Example_ :

- `<v:polyline points="0pt,75pt 20pt,45pt 10pt,50pt 30pt,10pt`

- `50pt,50pt 40pt,45pt 60pt,75pt 0pt,75pt" fillcolor="#00a000">`

- `<o:extrusion on="t" backdepth="20pt"`

```
    lightposition="30000,10000,10000"/>
```

```
</v:polyline>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|autorotationcenter<br>(Center of Rotation<br>Toggle)|Specifies whether the center of rotation is the geometric center of the extrusion.  Default<br>is`false`.  If`true`, the geometric center of an extruded shape is (0,0,0).  If`false`, the<br>center of rotation is determined by therotationcenterattribute.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|backdepth<br>(Backward Extrusion<br>Depth)|Specifies the amount of backward extrusion.  Default is 36 pt, default units are points.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" backdepth="15pt">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|brightness<br>(Brightness)|Specifies the overall brightness of a scene.  Default is 0.3.
