This numeric value can also be<br>specified in 1/65536-ths if a trailing "f" is supplied (as "f" indicates the value is a fraction).<br>For example, a value of "52429f" represents 52429/65536 or 0.8.<br>This quantity is not specified using units.  The numeric values are in the range 0 to 1 (0f to<br>65536f), where 0 implies darkness and 1 implies light saturation.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" brightness="0.4">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`brightness="0"`<br>`brightness="25000f"`<br>`brightness="0.4"`<br>`brightness="0.75"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|color(Extrusion<br>Color)|Specifies the color of the extrusion faces.  This attribute is only used whencolormodeis<br>`custom`.  Colors are typically specified as either a named color, such as`red`, or six<br>hexadecimal digits representing the red, green and blue values of the color, such as<br>`#00FF30`.  Full details are specified in the simple type description.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" color="lime"`<br>`colormode="custom">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|colormode<br>(Extrusion Color<br>Mode)|Specifies whether the extrusion color is defined by thecolorattribute or is the same as<br>the shape's fill color.  Default is`auto`.<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<o:extrusion on="true" color="lime"`<br>`colormode="auto">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.6).|
|diffusity(Diffuse<br>Reflection)|Specifies the amount of diffusion of reflected light from an extruded shape, defined as<br>the ratio of incident light to diffused reflected light.  Default is 1.  Normal values are in<br>the range 0 to 1.  This numeric value can also be specified in 1/65536-ths if a trailing "f" is<br>supplied (as "f" indicates the value is a fraction).  [_Example_: A value of "52429f"<br>represents 52429/65536 or 0.8._end example_]<br>This quantity is not specified using units.  The numeric values are in the range 0 to 1 (0f to<br>65536f), where 0 implies all reflected light is diffuse and 1 implies no reflected light is<br>diffuse.<br>Specularity and diffusity should be considered together as it is possible, though physically<br>incorrect, to define more reflected light than incident light.  This is the case if the amount<br>of specularly reflected light and diffusely reflected light add up to more than the amount<br>of incident light.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" diffusity=".75">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>`diffusity="0"`<br>`diffusity="0.5"`<br>`diffusity="0.75"`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`diffusity="1"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|edge(Simulated<br>Bevel)|Specifies the apparent bevel of the extrusion edges.  Default is 1 point.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" edge="2pt">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|
|facet(Faceting<br>Quality)|Specifies the quality with which the application approximates curved surfaces of an<br>extrusion.  A higher facet value produces shapes with smoother curves.  A lower value<br>reduces smoothing, resulting in curves with sharper, jagged edges.  Default is 30000.<br>Allowed values are in the range 1 to 65536, where 1 implies extremely low quality curve<br>approximation and 65536 implies extremely high quality.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" facet="65536">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`facet="65536"`<br>`facet="100"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|foredepth(Forward<br>Extrusion)|Specifies the amount of forward extrusion.  Default is 0 pt, default units are points.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" foredepth="25pt">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|lightface(Shape<br>Face Lighting<br>Toggle)|Specifies whether the front face of the extrusion responds to changes in the lighting.  If<br>`false`, the front face does not respond when a lighting value changes.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_: The front face is colored as if the shape were not extruded and lit by a 3-D light<br>source:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" lightface="false">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|lightharsh(Primary<br>Light Harshness<br>Toggle)|Specifies whether the primary light source is harsh.  If`false`, shadow boundaries are<br>diffused.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_: The secondary light source is turned off so only the primary has an effect:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" lightharsh="false"`<br>`lightlevel2="0">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>`lightharsh="false"`<br>`lightharsh="true"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|lightharsh2<br>(Secondary Light<br>Harshness Toggle)|Specifies whether the secondary light source is harsh.  If`false`, shadow boundaries<br>defined by the secondary light source are diffused.  Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|lightlevel(Primary<br>Light Intensity)|Specifies the intensity of the primary light source for the scene.  Default is 0.6.  This<br>numeric value can also be specified in 1/65536-ths if a trailing "f" is supplied (as "f"<br>indicates the value is a fraction).  [_Example_: A value of "52429f" represents 52429/65536<br>or 0.8._end example_]<br>This quantity is not specified using units.  The numeric values are in the range 0 to 1 (0f to<br>65536f), where 0 implies no direct light and 1 implies saturated direct light.<br>[_Example_: The secondary light source is turned off so only the primary has an effect:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" lightlevel=".5"`<br>`lightlevel2="0">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`lightlevel="1"`<br>`lightlevel="0.5"`<br>`lightlevel="0"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|lightlevel2<br>(Secondary Light<br>Intensity)|Specifies the intensity of the secondary light source for the scene.  Default is 0.6.  This<br>numeric value can also be specified in 1/65536-ths if a trailing "f" is supplied (as "f"<br>indicates the value is a fraction).  [_Example_: A value of "52429f" represents 52429/65536<br>or 0.8._end example_]<br>This quantity is not specified using units.  The numeric values are in the range 0 to 1 (0f to<br>65536f), where 0 implies no direct light and 1 implies saturated direct light.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|lightposition<br>(Primary Light<br>Position)|Specifies the normalized X,Y,Z position of the primary light in a scene in 1/65536-ths.<br>Default is "50000,0,10000".  The use of a normalized vector from the shape origin<br>effectively establishes the direction of the light relative to the shape.  The distance of the<br>light from the shape is irrelevant as the light source is treated as a directional light.<br>The position "0,0,0" is at the center of the shape.  Positive numbers move the light to the<br>right, down and toward the viewer, respectively.<br>[_Example_: The secondary light source is turned off so only the primary has an effect:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" lightlevel2="0"`<br>`lightposition="7000,-13000,20000">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|lightposition2<br>(Secondary Light<br>Position)|Specifies the X,Y,Z position of the secondary light in a scene in 1/65536-ths.  Default is "-<br>50000,0,10000".  The use of a normalized vector from the shape origin effectively<br>establishes the direction of the light relative to the shape.  The distance of the light from<br>the shape is irrelevant as the light source is treated as a directional light.<br>The position "0,0,0" is at the center of the shape.  Positive numbers move the light to the<br>right, down and toward the viewer, respectively.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|lockrotationcenter<br>(Rotation Toggle)|Specifies whether the rotation of the extruded object is specified by therotationangle<br>attribute.  If`false`, the rotation is specified by theorientationattribute.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_: The following snippets are equivalent:<br>`<o:extrusion lockrotationcenter="false"`<br>`orientationangle="45" orientation="0,1,0">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>`<o:extrusion lockrotationcenter=true`<br>`rotationangle="45"/>`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|metal(Metallic<br>Surface Toggle)|Specifies whether the surface of the extruded shape resembles metal.  Default is`false`.<br>If`true`, this attribute causes the specularly reflected light to be the material color instead<br>of the light source color, making the object seem more metallic.  To further approximate<br>a metallic material requires that specularity be relatively high (about 1.2) and diffusity be<br>relatively low (about 0.6).<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" metal="true"`<br>`lightposition="10000,-10000,10000"`<br>`lightlevel2="0" specularity="1.2"`<br>`diffusity="0.6">`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`</o:extrusion>`<br>`metal="true"`<br>`metal="false"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|on(Extrusion<br>Toggle)|Specifies whether an extrusion is displayed.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:rect style="width=50;height=50">`<br>`<o:extrusion />`<br>`</v:rect>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|orientation<br>(Rotation Axis)|Specifies a vector in 3D space around which the shape is rotated, as given by the<br>orientationangleattribute.  Default is "100,0,0".<br>The position "0,0,0" is at the center of the shape.  Positive numbers are to the right,<br>down and toward the viewer, respectively.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion … orientation="200,0,0">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|||datatype.||
|orientationangle<br>(Rotation Around<br>Axis)||Specifies the angle, in degrees, that an extrusion rotates around theorientation.  Default<br>is 0.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion … orientationangle="30">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemafloatdatatype.||
|plane(Extrusion<br>Direction)||Specifies the plane that is at right angles to the extrusion.  Default is`xy`.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`xy`<br><br>`zx`<br><br>`yz`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" plane="yz"`<br>`backdepth="100pt">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ExtrusionPlanesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.11).||
|render(Extrusion<br>Render Mode)||Specifies the rendering mode of the extrusion.  Default is`solid`.  Allowed values are:<br>Value<br>Description<br>`solid`<br>Rendering displays a solid shape.<br>`wireframe`<br>Rendering displays a wireframe shape.<br>`boundingcube`Rendering displays the bounding cube that<br>contains the shape.||
|||Value|Description|
|||`solid`|Rendering displays a solid shape.|
|||`wireframe`|Rendering displays a wireframe shape.|
|||`boundingcube`|Rendering displays the bounding cube that<br>contains the shape.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" render="wireframe">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ExtrusionRendersimple type<br>(§19.2.3.12).|
|rotationangle(X-Y<br>Rotation Angle)|Specifies the rotation of the object about the x- and y-axes, in degrees.  Default is "0,0".<br>Positive angles are measured clockwise around the axis (as if viewing from the positive<br>axis).<br>The rotation of the object is defined by a rotation angle about the y-axis followed by the<br>rotation angle about the x-axis.  The z-axis angle is controlled by the value of the CSS<br>style attribute's`rotation`property.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="t" lockrotationcenter="true"`<br>`rotationangle="10,20">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|rotationcenter<br>(Rotation Center)|Specifies the center of rotation for a shape ifautorotationcenteris`false`.  The offset of<br>the rotation is specified in terms of fractions of the shape's size.  Default is "0,0,0".<br>The position "0,0,0" is at the center of the shape.  Positive numbers are to the right,<br>down and toward the viewer, respectively.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|shininess<br>(Shininess)|Specifies the concentration of the reflected light on an extrusion surface.  Default is 5.<br>The range of values should be constrained to 0-10.  Reflection intensity typically grows<br>exponentially with theshininessvalue.<br>High values (8-10) approximate the shininess of a mirror and low values (2-3)<br>approximate a speckled effect.  Reflections do not mirror other objects; only pinpoint<br>light sources are reflected.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemafloatdatatype.|
|skewamt(Extrusion<br>Skew)|Specifies the amount of skew, or length, of a parallel extrusion.  Default is 50%.  Applies<br>only if the extrusiontypeis`parallel`.  This attribute andbackdepthinteract to create<br>the actual extrusion length.  Allowed values are in the range 0 (0%) to 1 (100%).<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" skewamt="100%">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|skewangle<br>(Extrusion Skew<br>Angle)|Specifies the angle of the skew of a parallel extrusion.  Default is 225 degrees.  Angles are<br>measured in degrees, counterclockwise from the negative x-axis.  Applies only if the<br>extrusiontypeis`parallel`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" skewangle="25">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemafloatdatatype.|
|specularity|Specifies the specularity of an extruded shape, defined as the ratio of incident light to|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|(Specularity)||specularly reflected light.  Default is 0.  Normal values are in the range 0 to 1.  This<br>numeric value can also be specified in 1/65536-ths if a trailing "f" is supplied (as "f"<br>indicates the value is a fraction).  [_Example_: A value of "52429f" represents 52429/65536<br>or 0.8._end example_]<br>Specularity and diffusity should be considered together as it is possible, though physically<br>incorrect, to define more reflected light than incident light.  This is the case if the amount<br>of specularly reflected light and diffusely reflected light add up to more than the amount<br>of incident light.<br>[_Example_: The secondary light source is turned off so only the primary has an effect.<br>Although the effect is subtle, the first cylinder has a sharper specular reflection on its<br>edge:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" specularity="1"`<br>`lightposition="10000,-10000,10000"`<br>`lightlevel2="0">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>`specularity="1"`<br>`specularity="0"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.||
|type(Extrusion<br>Type)||Specifies the way|that the shape is extruded.  Default is`parallel`.  Allowed values are:<br>Description<br>Extrusion is rendered so that the center of<br>projection is infinitely far away; that is, the<br>extrusion lines do not converge (unlike<br>perspective projections).<br>Extrusion is rendered to a center of projection,<br>which is the same as the vanishing point for<br>unrotated objects.|
|||Value|Description|
|||`parallel`|Extrusion is rendered so that the center of<br>projection is infinitely far away; that is, the<br>extrusion lines do not converge (unlike<br>perspective projections).|
|||`perspective`|Extrusion is rendered to a center of projection,<br>which is the same as the vanishing point for<br>unrotated objects.|
|||[_Example_:||

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<o:extrusion on="true" type="parallel"`<br>`backdepth="100pt">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>`type="parallel"`<br>`type="perspective"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ExtrusionTypesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.13).|
|viewpoint<br>(Extrusion<br>Viewpoint)|Specifies the viewpoint of the observer in EMUs.  This is effectively the end of a vector<br>extending from theviewpointorigin.<br>The position "0,0,0" is at the center of the shape.  Positive numbers are to the right,<br>down and toward the viewer, respectively.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" type="perspective"`<br>`viewpoint="500000,-100000,100000">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|viewpointorigin<br>(Extrusion<br>Viewpoint Origin)|Specifies the origin of the viewpoint vector for perspective extrusions.  This is the origin<br>of the vector whose opposite end is given by theviewpointattribute.  This origin is<br>always within the bounding box of the shape.  Default is "0.5,-0.5".<br>The viewpoint is specified in terms of the x and y values of the original shape.  The x and y|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||values are in the range 0.5 to -0.5 (50% to -50% of the shape's coordinate origin).  Larger<br>numbers move the viewpoint outside the bounding box.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:extrusion on="true" type="perspective"`<br>`viewpoint="500000,-100000,100000"`<br>`viewpointorigin="0,1">`<br>`</o:extrusion>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Extrusion) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.12 FieldCodes (WordprocessingML Field Switches)

This element specifies the WordprocessingML field switches which shall be stored with an embedded object, using the set of field switches defined by the `LINK` field, as specified in Part 1, §17.16. This element shall only be used within a WordprocessingML document, and shall specify the exact field switches for the field which represents the object..

[ _Rationale_ : Legacy word processors used fields to represent embedded objects – this element stores the field switches not explicitly defined using individual Office VML Drawing elements for embeddings so as not to use the fidelity of their contents. _end rationale_ ]

[ _Example_ : The following example inserts an embedded object and specifies additional properties as defined by the LINK field.

- `<o:OLEObject …>`

- `<o:FieldCodes>\f 0</o:FieldCodes>`

- `</o:OLEObject>`

This embedded object specifies additional `LINK` field code values of `\f 0` , which specifies that the embedded object shall retain its source formatting (as defined in Part 1, §17.16).

_end example_ ]

The possible values for this element are defined by the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

## 19.2.2.13 fill (Shape Fill Extended Properties)

This element specifies additional properties for fills.  It is used to identify additional types of gradient fills beyond those specified in the fill element (§19.1.2.5).

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|
|type(Fill Type)|Specifies the type of fill.  If specified, this overrides the value of thetypeattribute in the<br>parentfillelement.<br>[_Example_: The`gradientCenter`value overrides`gradientRadial`:<br>`<v:fill color2="black" focus="100%" type="gradientRadial">`<br>`<o:fill v:ext="view" type="gradientCenter"/>`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_FillTypesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.14).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Fill) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.14 idmap (Shape ID Map)

This element specifies how shape IDs in the document have been generated.  This is an optional element included to allow applications a mechanism for storing information they need to persist related to generating shape IDs.

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|data(Shape IDs)|Specifies the data the application uses to generate shape IDs.<br>[_Example_: An application might choose to reserve blocks of shape ID numbers for each<br>part in the package.  Each block of 1024 shape IDs could be referred to by index and this<br>index stored in thedataattribute.  The data value for a given part might then be:<br>`<o:idmap v:ext="edit" data="1"/>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||indicating that all the IDs in block 1 are reserved by this part ( meaning shape IDs from 1<br>to 1024 cannot be used ).  The application's internal constraint would be that each part<br>reserve a different set of IDs.  Another part, that contains more shapes, might use:<br>`<o:idmap v:ext="edit" data="2,3"/>`<br>In this case, shape IDs from 1025 to 3072 [ 3 x 1024 ] cannot be used ).<br>Another implementation might choose to store more verbose information in this<br>attribute.  Yet another implementation might ignore this element completely.<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_IdMap) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.15 ink (Ink)

This element specifies the presence of an ink object.  An ink object is a VML object which allows applications to store data for ink annotations. [ _Note:_ Applications are encouraged to use an open-ink format, such as the Ink Markup Language (InkML). _end note_ ]

[ _Example_ :

```
<v:shape … >
```

```
  <o:ink i="…" annotation="t" contentType="application/inkml+xml"/>
```

```
</v:shape>
```

```
<v:shape … >
```

```
  <o:ink i="AMgFHQSWC+YFASAAaAwAAAAAAMA…" annotation="t"
    contentType="application/x-ms-ink"/>
```

```
</v:shape>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|annotation<br>(Annotation Flag)|Specifies whether the ink object was created as an annotation rather than through pen<br>input.  Default is`false`.  [_Rationale_This allows an application to treat annotation ink<br>objects as any other annotation.  For example, if annotations are hidden, the application<br>can hide the ink object.  An ink object that represents primary user input through a pen<br>can be left visible._end rationale_]<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:ink … annotation="true">`<br>`</o:ink>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|contentType<br>(Content Type)|Specifies the format of the ink content stored in theiattribute. The syntax is a content<br>type as defined in IETF RFC 2616.<br>If this attribute is omitted, the application should attempt to determine the content type<br>by reading the contents of theielement.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ContentTypesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.9).|
|i(Ink Data)|Specifies additional ink object information which shall be associated with the parent VML<br>shape. The VML shape specifies the information necessary to render the ink, and this<br>attribute can be used to store additional data about the VML shape(s) representing ink.<br>This attribute's contents are formatted as specified by thecontentTypeattribute, but are<br>optional and can be ignored if not recognized.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:ink … i="…">`<br>`</o:ink>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Ink) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.16 left (Text Box Left Stroke)

This element specifies the stroke properties for the left border of a text box.  It entirely supercedes its parent stroke element if its on attribute is true.  Thus the default value of an unspecified attribute overrides a value specified in the parent.  If the on attribute is `false` or not specified, the border is not shown.

[ _Example_ : The text box borders are set independently.  The bottom border does not inherit the weight from the parent stroke element.

- `<v:stroke weight="2.25pt">`

- `<o:left v:ext="view" dashstyle="1 1" color="blue" weight="5pt" on="t"/>`

- `<o:top v:ext="view" color="black" weight="2.25pt" on="t"/>`

- `<o:right v:ext="view" dashstyle="1 1" color="blue" weight="5pt" on="t"/>`

- `<o:bottom v:ext="view" color="black" on="t"/>`

- `<o:column v:ext="view" color="#f60" on="t"/>`

- `</v:stroke>`

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|althref(Alternate<br>Image Reference)|Specifies an alternate reference for an image in Macintosh PICT format.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … althref="myimage.pcz" … >`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|color(Stroke Color)|Specifies the stroke color.  Overrides thestrokecolorattribute of a shape.  Default is<br>`black`.  See thefillcolorattribute for a list of supported named colors.<br>[_Example_: The shape stroke is blue:<br>`<v:shape … strokecolor="red" … >`<br>`<v:stroke color="blue"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|color2(Stroke<br>Alternate Pattern<br>Color)|Specifies a second color for strokes, used whenfilltypeis`pattern`.  Default is no value.<br>When a pattern fill is used for the stroke, the stroke color is used in colored parts of the<br>source image.  The color2 defines an alternate color to use in place of black in the source<br>image.<br>[_Example_: This unusual example is intended to demonstrate how the image and colors<br>interact to create a patterned stroke.  The yellow background shows transparency.  The<br>non-square shape and square image create an effective offset.  The heavy stroke weight<br>shows more of the image.  The green shape fill shows how the stroke is overlaid on the<br>shape.<br>`<v:background fillcolor="yellow"/>`<br>`<v:shape style="width:60;height:50"`<br>`strokecolor="red" fillcolor="lime"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke filltype="pattern" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="myimage.gif" color2="blue"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>, where myimage.gif is:<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|dashstyle(Stroke<br>Dash Pattern)|Specifies the dot and dash pattern for a stroke.  Default is`solid`.  Pre-defined values are:<br><br>`solid`<br><br>`shortdash`<br><br>`shortdot`<br><br>`shortdashdot`<br><br>`shortdashdotdot`<br><br>`dot`<br><br>`dash`<br><br>`longdash`<br><br>`dashdot`<br><br>`longdashdot`<br><br>`longdashdotdot`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||A custom-defined dash pattern can also be specified using a series of numbers.  These<br>define the length of the dash (the drawn part of the stroke) and the length of the space<br>between the dashes.  The lengths are relative to the line width: a length of 1 is equal to<br>the line width.  Theendcapstyle is applied to each dash but the arrow style is not.  The<br>string defines the length of the dash then the length of the space.  This can be repeated<br>to form complex dash styles.  The string should always contain a pair of numbers; if it<br>contains an odd number of numbers the last is disregarded.  0 implies a dot that is<br>fourfold symmetrical (with round end caps, this is a circle).<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke dashstyle="0 2" weight="3pt"`<br>`endcap="round">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>`<v:stroke dashstyle="longdashdotdot"`<br>`weight="2pt">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|endarrow(Line End<br>Arrowhead)|Specifies an arrowhead for the end of a line.  Default is`none`.  The path shall not be<br>closed with the x command for the arrowhead to show.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`block`<br><br>`classic`<br><br>`diamond`<br><br>`oval`<br><br>`open`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke endarrow="classic"/>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowTypesimple<br>type (§19.1.3.8).|
|endarrowlength<br>(Line End<br>Arrowhead Length)|Specifies the length of the arrowhead at the end of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`short`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`long`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endarrowlength="long" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowLengthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.7).|
|endarrowwidth<br>(Line End<br>Arrowhead Width)|Specifies the width of the arrowhead at the end of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`narrow`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`wide`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endarrowwidth="wide" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowWidthsimple|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||type (§19.1.3.9).|
|endcap(Line End<br>Cap)|Specifies the cap style for the end of a stroke.  Default is`flat`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`flat`<br><br>`square`<br><br>`round`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endcap="round" weight="10pt" … />`<br>`endcap="flat"`<br>`endcap="square"`<br>`endcap="round"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeEndCapsimple type<br>(§19.1.3.10).|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|
|filltype(Stroke<br>Image Style)|Specifies the kind of fill used for the background of a stroke.  Default is`solid`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`solid`- The fill pattern is solid.<br><br>`tile`- The fill image is tiled.<br><br>`pattern`- The fill image is stretched to form a pattern.<br><br>`frame`- The fill image becomes a border for the shape.<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:shape style="width:50;height:50"`<br>`strokecolor="red"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke filltype="frame" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="border.gif"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>, where border.gif is:<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_FillTypesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.4).|
|forcedash(Force<br>Dashed Outline)|Specifies whether a dashed outline is used to draw a shape when a shape has no line or<br>fill.  Default is`false`.<br>Used by PresentationML placeholders to draw a dashed outline when there is no line and<br>no fill for a shape.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:forcedash="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|href(Original Image<br>Reference)|Specifies the URL to the original image file.  Used only if the picture has been linked and<br>embedded.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … o:href="myimage.gif" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|imagealignshape<br>(Stoke Image|Specifies the alignment of the stroke image.  If`true`, the image is aligned with the shape.<br>Otherwise, it is aligned with the containing scope.  Default is`true`.|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|
|---|---|---|
|Alignment)||[_Example_: The top position offset shifts the image alignment relative to the containing<br>window:<br>`<v:shape fillcolor="silver"`<br>`style="top:20;width:50;height:50"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke imagealignshape="false" weight="20pt"`<br>`filltype="tile" src="myimage.gif"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>`imagealignshape="false"`<br>`imagealignshape="false"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|imageaspect<br>(Stroke Image<br>Aspect Ratio)||Specifies how the stroke image aspect ratio is preserved.  Default is`ignore`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br>Value<br>Description<br>`ignore`<br>Ignore aspect issues.<br>`atleast`<br>Image is at least as big asimagesize.<br>`atmost`<br>Image is no bigger thanimagesize.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke filltype="frame" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="border.gif" imagealignshape="true"`<br>`imageaspect="atleast">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>`imagealignshape="ignore"`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`imagealignshape="atleast"`<br>`imagealignshape="atmost"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ImageAspectsimple type<br>(§19.1.3.5).|
|imagesize(Stroke<br>Image Size)|Specifies the size of the image for the stroke.  Default is the size of the image.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … imagesize="10pt,10pt" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|insetpen(Inset<br>Border From Path)|Specifies that the border shall be displayed inside of the path defining the shape, rather<br>than along the path (the default border placement), or outside of the path as might be<br>done with an image.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … insetpen="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|joinstyle(Line End<br>Join Style))|Specifies the join style for line ends.  Default is`round`.<br><br>`round`<br><br>`bevel`<br><br>`miter`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:polyline strokeweight="10pt" strokecolor="navy"`<br>`points="10pt,10pt,50pt,50pt,90pt,10pt">`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:stroke joinstyle="bevel"/>`<br>`</v:polyline>`<br>`joinstyle="round"`<br>`joinstyle="bevel"`<br>`joinstyle="miter"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeJoinStylesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.11).|
|linestyle(Stroke<br>Line Style)|Specifies the line style of the stroke.  Default is`single`.<br><br>`single`<br><br>`thinThin`<br><br>`thinThick`<br><br>`thickThin`<br><br>`thickBetweenThin`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke linestyle="thickThin" weight="5pt">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeLineStylesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.12).|
|miterlimit(Miter<br>Joint Limit)|Specifies the smoothness of the miter joint, or the maximum distance between the inner<br>point and outer point of a joint.  This number is a multiple of the thickness of the line.<br>Default is 8.<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:stroke joinstyle="miter" weight="10pt"`<br>`miterlimit="2">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemadecimal<br>datatype.|
|on(Stroke Toggle)|Specifies whether the stroke is displayed.  Default is`true`.  This attribute overrides the<br>shape'sstrokeattribute.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:rect style="width:50;height:50" stroked="true"`<br>`fillcolor="lime" strokecolor="red">`<br>`<v:stroke on="false" weight="5pt"/>`<br>`</v:rect>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|opacity(Stroke<br>Opacity)|Specifies the amount of transparency of a stroke.  Default is 1.0.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:rect style="width:50;height:50"`<br>`fillcolor="lime" strokecolor="red">`<br>`<v:stroke weight="5pt" opacity="50%"/>`<br>`</v:rect>`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|src(Stroke Image<br>Location)|Specifies the source image to load for a stroke fill.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … src="myimage.gif" … >`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|startarrow(Line<br>Start Arrowhead)|Specifies an arrowhead for the start of a line.  Default is`none`.  The path shall not be<br>closed with the x command for the arrowhead to show.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`block`<br><br>`classic`<br><br>`diamond`<br><br>`oval`<br><br>`open`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke startarrow="classic"/>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowTypesimple<br>type (§19.1.3.8).|
|startarrowlength<br>(Line Start<br>Arrowhead Length)|Specifies the length of the arrowhead at the start of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`short`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`long`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … startarrowlength="long" … />`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowLengthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.7).|
|startarrowwidth<br>(Line Start<br>Arrowhead Width)|Specifies the width of the arrowhead at the start of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`narrow`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`wide`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … startarrowwidth="wide" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowWidthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.9).|
|title(Stroke Title)|Specifies the title of an embedded stroke image.  This is typically set to the comment<br>property of the image, which is often blank.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … o:title="alt text" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|weight(Stroke<br>Weight)|Specifies the thickness of a stroke.  Default is 1.  This attribute overrides the shape's<br>strokeweightattribute.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_StrokeChild) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.17 LinkType (Embedded Object Alternate Image Request)

This element specifies the kind of image which shall be requested from an embedded object's host application when the contents of a linked image are updated within a document. When linked images are stored in documents, the only items stored in the document are an image representation and a link to the source. This element specifies the kind of image which shall be requested from the source on update.

[ _Note_ : The formats available can vary based on the kind of embedded object - this information is typically queried from the embedded object's application before it is stored. This setting can be omitted, and is usually stored for performance reasons, so it is not queried on each update of the linked object. _end note_ ]

The possible values for this element are defined by the ST_OLELinkType simple type (§19.2.3.19).

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (ST_OLELinkType) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.18 lock (Shape Protections)

This element specifies locks against actions that can be effected in the UI of an authoring application or programmatically through an object model.

[ _Example_ : The following snippet locks the shape's aspect ratio and text from user edits.

```
<v:shape … >
```

```
  <o:lock v:ext="edit" aspectratio="t" text="t"/>
```

```
</v:shape>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|adjusthandles<br>(Handles Lock)|Specifies whether the handles of a shape are locked from being edited.  Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|aspectratio(Aspect<br>Ratio Lock)|Specifies whether the aspect ratio of a shape is locked from being edited.  Default is<br>`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|cropping(Cropping<br>Lock)|Specifies whether cropping of a shape is locked from being edited.  Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|
|grouping(Grouping<br>Lock)|Specifies whether a shape is locked from being grouped.  Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|position(Position<br>Lock)|Specifies whether the position of a shape is locked from being edited.  Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|rotation(Rotation<br>Lock)|Specifies whether the rotation of a shape is locked from being edited.  Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|selection(Selection<br>Lock)|Specifies whether the shape is locked from being selectable in an editor.  Default is<br>`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|shapetype<br>(AutoShape Type<br>Lock)|Specifies whether the AutoShape type is locked from being edited.  Default is`false`.  If<br>`true`, the type of an AutoShape cannot be changed in a graphical editor.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|text(Text Lock)|Specifies whether the text attached to a shape is locked from being edited.  Default is<br>`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|ungrouping<br>(Ungrouping Lock)|Specifies whether a grouped shape is locked from being ungrouped.  Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|verticies(Vertices<br>Lock)|Specifies whether the vertices of a path are locked from being edited.  Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Lock) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.19 LockedField (Embedded Object Cannot Be Refreshed)

This element specifies that the embedded object's appearance is locked - that is, that the object's current representation shall be locked to prevent any user interaction or automatic application behavior from modifying its contents.

This element shall contain no content - its presence indicates that the embedded object is locked, and its omission allows the field to be updated.

The possible values for this element are defined by the ST_TrueFalseBlank simple type (§20.1.2.6).

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (ST_TrueFalseBlank) is located in §A.8.9. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.20 OLEObject (Embedded OLE Object)

This element specifies an embedded object.

[ _Example_ : The following markup defines a reference to an embedded object using Bonobo. The ProgId attribute contains the shared library that contains the widget. The content type of the referred part identifies the referenced Bonobo object.

```
<OLEObject r:id="rb1" ProgId="OAFIID:Bonobo_Sample_Calculator">
```

- `…`

`</OLEObject>`

The target of the relationship with ID `rb1` , defines the Bonobo object itself. This example shows a link to a - sample Bonobo widget taken from the  article http://www.ibm.com/developerworks/webservices/library/co bnbo2.html, which also provides an introduction to Bonobo. _end example_ ]

[ _Example_ : The following demonstrates a video file embedded in a WordprocessingML document:

- `<w:object … >`

- `<v:shape id="_x0000_i1025" type="#_x0000_t75"`

```
  style="width:1in;height:24pt" o:ole="">
```

```
  <v:imagedata r:id="rId4" o:title=""/>
```

```
  </v:shape>
```

```
  <o:OLEObject Type="Embed" ProgID="AVIFile" ShapeID="_x0000_i1025"
  DrawAspect="Content" ObjectID="_1219561732" r:id="rId5"/>
```

```
</w:object>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|DrawAspect<br>(Embedded Object<br>Representation)|Specifies how the embedded object is represented visually in the application.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:OLEObject … DrawAspect="Content">`<br>`</o:OLEObject>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_OLEDrawAspectsimple type<br>(§19.2.3.18).|
|id(Relationship)<br>Namespace:<br>.../officeDocument<br>/2006/relationshi<br>ps|Specifies the actual OLE object using a standard part relationship lookup.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:OLEObject … r:id="rId5">`<br>`</o:OLEObject>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RelationshipIdsimple type<br>(Part 1, §22.8.2.1).|
|ObjectID(Unique ID<br>for Embedded<br>Object)|Specifies a unique ID identifying the embedded object.<br>[_Example_: The following markup defines a reference to a linked object using KParts. The<br>name attribute contains the shared library that contains the plugin. The item element<br>contains the name of the plugin. The content type of the referred part would identifies<br>the referenced KParts object.<br>`<oleLink r:id="rKp1" progId="libhtmlvalidatorplugin">`<br>`…`<br>`</oleLink>`<br>The following XML, contained in the target of the relationship with ID`rKp1`, defines the<br>KPart object, and follows the kpartgui DTD:<br>`<!DOCTYPE kpartgui SYSTEM "kpartgui.dtd">`<br>`<kpartgui library="libhtmlvalidatorplugin"`<br>`name="htmlvalidatorplugin" version="1" >`<br>`<MenuBar>`<br>`<Menu name="tools"><Text>&amp;Tools</Text>`<br>`<Action name="validatewebpage"/>`<br>`</Menu>`<br>`</MenuBar>`<br>`</kpartgui>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|ProgID(Object Link<br>Identifier)|Specifies the embedded object server application associated with the embedded object.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:OLEObject … ProgID="AVIFile">`<br>`</o:OLEObject>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|ShapeID<br>(Embedded Object<br>Shape)|Specifies the shape with which the embedded object is associated.  A VML shape<br>provides the visual placeholder for an embedded object and this attribute is set to theid<br>of the placeholder shape.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:OLEObject … ShapeID="_x0000_i1025">`<br>`</o:OLEObject>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|Type(Embedded<br>Object Type)|Specifies the kind of embedded object connection.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:OLEObject … Type="Embed">`<br>`</o:OLEObject>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_OLETypesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.20).|
|UpdateMode<br>(Update Mode for<br>Embedded Object)|Specifies how the object is updated with new data if theTypeis`Link`- automatically or<br>on-demand by the user.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:OLEObject … UpdateMode="Always">`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`</o:OLEObject>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_OLEUpdateModesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.21).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_OLEObject) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.21 proxy (Shape Reference)

This element specifies an entry in a r element rule that contains a reference to one or more shapes that are participating in the rule.

[ _Example_ : The following rule defines a connection between two shapes.  The shape with id _s1036 connects shape _s1033 to _s1032:

- `<o:shapelayout v:ext="edit">`

- `<o:rules v:ext="edit">`

- `<o:r id="V:Rule1" type="connector" idref="#_s1036">`

- `<o:proxy start="" idref="#_s1033" connectloc="0"/>`

- `<o:proxy end="" idref="#_s1032" connectloc="2"/>`

- `</o:r>`

```
  </o:rules>
```

```
</o:shapelayout>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|connectloc<br>(Connection<br>Location)|Specifies the location on the shape where the connector is attached.  The value is an<br>index into the list of connection points defined in the shape - see theconnectlocs<br>attribute.  Default is 0.  Only used in a connector rule.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemaintdatatype.|
|end(End Point<br>Connection Flag)|Specifies whether the connector's end point is connected to the shape.  Default is`false`.<br>Only used in a connector rule.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalseBlanksimple type<br>(§20.1.2.6).|
|idref(Proxy Shape<br>Reference)|Specifies a reference to a shape in the current document.  Default is no value.  A shape<br>name is used as the reference mechanism; this is not a relationship ID.<br>This attribute indicates that the referenced shape is part of this rule.  Two or moreproxy|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||elements are used for an alignment rule.  A connector rule uses one or two, indicating<br>which shapes the connector is attached to.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|start(Start Point<br>Connection Flag)|Specifies whether the connector’s start point is connected to the shape.  Default is<br>`false`.  Only used in a connector rule.  If both start and end are specified the later one<br>takes precedence.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalseBlanksimple type<br>(§20.1.2.6).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Proxy) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.22 r (Rule)

This element specifies a rule entry in a rules element rule set that describes how a certain shape or set of shapes behaves during editing.

[ _Example_ : The following rule defines a connection between two shapes.  The shape with id _s1036 connects shape _s1033 to _s1032:

- `<o:shapelayout v:ext="edit">`

```
  <o:rules v:ext="edit">
```

```
    <o:r id="V:Rule1" type="connector" idref="#_s1036">
```

```
      <o:proxy start="" idref="#_s1033" connectloc="0"/>
```

```
      <o:proxy end="" idref="#_s1032" connectloc="2"/>
```

```
    </o:r>
```

```
  </o:rules>
```

```
</o:shapelayout>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|how(Alignment<br>Rule Type)|Specifies the kind of alignment for an alignment rule.  Default is no value.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`top`<br><br>`middle`<br><br>`bottom`<br><br>`left`<br><br>`center`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||<br>`right`<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Howsimple type<br>(§19.2.3.15).|
|id(Rule ID)|Specifies an identifier for the rule.  Default is no value.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|idref(Rule Shape<br>Reference)|Specifies a reference to a shape in the current document that is the primary shape in the<br>rule.  [_Example_: For a connector rule, the connector._end example_]<br>Default is no value.  A shape name is used as the reference mechanism; this is not a<br>relationship ID.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|type(Rule Type)|Specifies the kind of the rule.  Default is no value.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`arc`<br><br>`callout`<br><br>`connector`<br><br>`align`<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_RTypesimple type<br>(§19.2.3.22).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_R) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.23 regrouptable (Shape Grouping History)

This element specifies a list of entries which describe how shapes were previously grouped so they can be regrouped.  The regroupid attribute of shapes indicates which shapes belong together when a regroup is performed.  The regrouptable tracks the previous regroupid that should be assigned to all shapes with the given current regroupid .

[ _Example_ : Consider a document containing two rectangles and a circle.  The rectangles are grouped together, then that group is grouped with the circle.  This new group is then ungrouped, leaving the circle and grouped rectangles.  The document might contain the following snippets:

- `<v:oval … o:regroupid="1"/>`

- `<v:group … o:regroupid="1"/>`

- `<v:rect … />`

- `<v:rect … />`

- `</v:group>`

- `<o:regrouptable v:ext="edit">`

- `<o:entry new="1" old="0"/>`

- `</o:regrouptable>`

The regroupid attribute indicates that the shapes with regroupid 1 were previously grouped together.  The entry indicates that if those shapes are regrouped, the new group formed should not have a regroupid value as it was not previously ungrouped.

If the two rectangles are ungrouped, the document reflects that the rectangles were previously grouped and that their old group was previously grouped:

```
<v:oval … o:regroupid="1"/>
```

- `<v:rect … o:regroupid="2"/>`

- `<v:rect … o:regroupid="2"/>`

- `<o:regrouptable v:ext="edit">`

- `<o:entry new="1" old="0"/>`

- `<o:entry new="2" old="1"/>`

- `</o:regrouptable>`

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_RegroupTable) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.24 rel (Diagram Relationship)

This element specifies a relationship between two diagram nodes.  An optional third node that exists between the primary two can also be included.  The relationship has an implicit order since it describes the source and destination nodes.

[ _Example_ : In the cycle diagram below, shape 1036 (the shape that is the text box for the text "2") is the first node.  A relationship exists between shape 1036 and shape 1044 (the text box containing "1").  In between those shapes is shape 1038 (the yellow arrow).

```
<o:relationtable v:ext="edit">
```

```
  <o:rel v:ext="edit" idsrc="#_s1036" iddest="#_s1036"/>
  <o:rel v:ext="edit" idsrc="#_s1042" iddest="#_s1036" idcntr="#_s1043"/>
  <o:rel v:ext="edit" idsrc="#_s1044" iddest="#_s1042" idcntr="#_s1045"/>
  <o:rel v:ext="edit" idsrc="#_s1036" iddest="#_s1044" idcntr="#_s1038"/>
</o:relationtable>
```

```
<v:rect id="_s1036" … >
```

```
  <v:textbox … ><…>2</…></v:textbox>
</v:rect>
```

```
<v:rect id="_s1044" … >
  <v:textbox … ><…>1</…></v:textbox>
</v:rect>
```

```
<v:shape id="_s1038" … />
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|
|idcntr(Diagram<br>Relationship Center<br>Shape)|Specifies the optional identifier of the shape that exists between the source and<br>destination shapes.  This is omitted if the relationship does not have a shape between the<br>source and destination shapes.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:rel … idcntr="#s_1038">`<br>`</o:rel>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|iddest(Diagram<br>Relationship<br>Destination Shape)|Specifies the identifier of the shape at the destination of the relationship.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:rel … iddest="#s_1044">`<br>`</o:rel>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|idsrc(Diagram<br>Relationship Source<br>Shape)|Specifies the identifier of the shape at the source of the relationship.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:rel … idsrc="#s_1036">`<br>`</o:rel>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Relation) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.25 relationtable (Diagram Relationship Table)

This element specifies a list that describes the relationships among diagram nodes.

[ _Example_ : The following table describes the parent-child relationships for shapes in an organization chart.  The first entry describes the top-level shape in the diagram.  The next two rows describe that the shapes are subordinates to the first shape.  Shape 1029 is a subordinate of shape 1028.  Shape 1032, a connector in this case, is in between the two.

- `<o:relationtable v:ext="edit">`

- `<o:rel v:ext="edit" idsrc="#_s1028" iddest="#_s1028"/>`

- `<o:rel v:ext="edit" idsrc="#_s1029" iddest="#_s1028" idcntr="#_s1032"/>`

- `<o:rel v:ext="edit" idsrc="#_s1030" iddest="#_s1028" idcntr="#_s1033"/>`

`</o:relationtable>`

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_RelationTable) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.26 right (Text Box Right Stroke)

This element specifies the stroke properties for the right border of a text box.  It entirely supercedes its parent stroke element if its on attribute is true.  Thus the default value of an unspecified attribute overrides a value specified in the parent.  If the on attribute is `false` or not specified, the border is not shown.

[ _Example_ : The text box borders are set independently.  The bottom border does not inherit the weight from the parent stroke element.

```
<v:stroke weight="2.25pt">
```

```
  <o:left v:ext="view" dashstyle="1 1" color="blue" weight="5pt" on="t"/>
```

```
  <o:top v:ext="view" color="black" weight="2.25pt" on="t"/>
```

```
  <o:right v:ext="view" dashstyle="1 1" color="blue" weight="5pt" on="t"/>
```

```
  <o:bottom v:ext="view" color="black" on="t"/>
```

```
  <o:column v:ext="view" color="#f60" on="t"/>
```

```
</v:stroke>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|althref(Alternate<br>Image Reference)|Specifies an alternate reference for an image in Macintosh PICT format.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … althref="myimage.pcz" … >`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|color(Stroke Color)|Specifies the stroke color.  Overrides thestrokecolorattribute of a shape.  Default is<br>`black`.  See thefillcolorattribute for a list of supported named colors.<br>[_Example_: The shape stroke is blue:<br>`<v:shape … strokecolor="red" … >`<br>`<v:stroke color="blue"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|color2(Stroke<br>Alternate Pattern<br>Color)|Specifies a second color for strokes, used whenfilltypeis`pattern`.  Default is no value.<br>When a pattern fill is used for the stroke, the stroke color is used in colored parts of the<br>source image.  The color2 defines an alternate color to use in place of black in the source<br>image.<br>[_Example_: This unusual example is intended to demonstrate how the image and colors<br>interact to create a patterned stroke.  The yellow background shows transparency.  The<br>non-square shape and square image create an effective offset.  The heavy stroke weight<br>shows more of the image.  The green shape fill shows how the stroke is overlaid on the<br>shape.<br>`<v:background fillcolor="yellow"/>`<br>`<v:shape style="width:60;height:50"`<br>`strokecolor="red" fillcolor="lime"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke filltype="pattern" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="myimage.gif" color2="blue"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||, where myimage.gif is:<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|dashstyle(Stroke<br>Dash Pattern)|Specifies the dot and dash pattern for a stroke.  Default is`solid`.  Pre-defined values are:<br><br>`solid`<br><br>`shortdash`<br><br>`shortdot`<br><br>`shortdashdot`<br><br>`shortdashdotdot`<br><br>`dot`<br><br>`dash`<br><br>`longdash`<br><br>`dashdot`<br><br>`longdashdot`<br><br>`longdashdotdot`<br>A custom-defined dash pattern can also be specified using a series of numbers.  These<br>define the length of the dash (the drawn part of the stroke) and the length of the space<br>between the dashes.  The lengths are relative to the line width: a length of 1 is equal to<br>the line width.  Theendcapstyle is applied to each dash but the arrow style is not.  The<br>string defines the length of the dash then the length of the space.  This can be repeated<br>to form complex dash styles.  The string should always contain a pair of numbers; if it<br>contains an odd number of numbers the last is disregarded.  0 implies a dot that is<br>fourfold symmetrical (with round end caps, this is a circle).<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke dashstyle="0 2" weight="3pt"`<br>`endcap="round">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>`<v:stroke dashstyle="longdashdotdot"`<br>`weight="2pt">`<br>`</v:stroke>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|endarrow(Line End<br>Arrowhead)|Specifies an arrowhead for the end of a line.  Default is`none`.  The path shall not be<br>closed with the x command for the arrowhead to show.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`block`<br><br>`classic`<br><br>`diamond`<br><br>`oval`<br><br>`open`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke endarrow="classic"/>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowTypesimple<br>type (§19.1.3.8).|
|endarrowlength<br>(Line End<br>Arrowhead Length)|Specifies the length of the arrowhead at the end of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`short`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`long`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endarrowlength="long" … />`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowLengthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.7).|
|endarrowwidth<br>(Line End<br>Arrowhead Width)|Specifies the width of the arrowhead at the end of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`narrow`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`wide`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endarrowwidth="wide" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowWidthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.9).|
|endcap(Line End<br>Cap)|Specifies the cap style for the end of a stroke.  Default is`flat`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`flat`<br><br>`square`<br><br>`round`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endcap="round" weight="10pt" … />`<br>`endcap="flat"`<br>`endcap="square"`<br>`endcap="round"`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeEndCapsimple type<br>(§19.1.3.10).|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|
|filltype(Stroke<br>Image Style)|Specifies the kind of fill used for the background of a stroke.  Default is`solid`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`solid`- The fill pattern is solid.<br><br>`tile`- The fill image is tiled.<br><br>`pattern`- The fill image is stretched to form a pattern.<br><br>`frame`- The fill image becomes a border for the shape.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape style="width:50;height:50"`<br>`strokecolor="red"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke filltype="frame" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="border.gif"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>, where border.gif is:<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_FillTypesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.4).|
|forcedash(Force<br>Dashed Outline)|Specifies whether a dashed outline is used to draw a shape when a shape has no line or<br>fill.  Default is`false`.<br>Used by PresentationML placeholders to draw a dashed outline when there is no line and<br>no fill for a shape.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:forcedash="true" … >`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|href(Original Image<br>Reference)|Specifies the URL to the original image file.  Used only if the picture has been linked and<br>embedded.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … o:href="myimage.gif" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|imagealignshape<br>(Stoke Image<br>Alignment)|Specifies the alignment of the stroke image.  If`true`, the image is aligned with the shape.<br>Otherwise, it is aligned with the containing scope.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_: The top position offset shifts the image alignment relative to the containing<br>window:<br>`<v:shape fillcolor="silver"`<br>`style="top:20;width:50;height:50"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke imagealignshape="false" weight="20pt"`<br>`filltype="tile" src="myimage.gif"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>`imagealignshape="false"`<br>`imagealignshape="false"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|
|---|---|---|
|imageaspect<br>(Stroke Image<br>Aspect Ratio)||Specifies how the stroke image aspect ratio is preserved.  Default is`ignore`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br>Value<br>Description<br>`ignore`<br>Ignore aspect issues.<br>`atleast`<br>Image is at least as big asimagesize.<br>`atmost`<br>Image is no bigger thanimagesize.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke filltype="frame" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="border.gif" imagealignshape="true"`<br>`imageaspect="atleast">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>`imagealignshape="ignore"`<br>`imagealignshape="atleast"`<br>`imagealignshape="atmost"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ImageAspectsimple type<br>(§19.1.3.5).|
|imagesize(Stroke<br>Image Size)||Specifies the size of the image for the stroke.  Default is the size of the image.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … imagesize="10pt,10pt" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|insetpen(Inset<br>Border From Path)||Specifies that the border shall be displayed inside of the path defining the shape, rather<br>than along the path (the default border placement), or outside of the path as might be<br>done with an image.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … insetpen="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|joinstyle(Line End<br>Join Style))|Specifies the join style for line ends.  Default is`round`.<br><br>`round`<br><br>`bevel`<br><br>`miter`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:polyline strokeweight="10pt" strokecolor="navy"`<br>`points="10pt,10pt,50pt,50pt,90pt,10pt">`<br>`<v:stroke joinstyle="bevel"/>`<br>`</v:polyline>`<br>`joinstyle="round"`<br>`joinstyle="bevel"`<br>`joinstyle="miter"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeJoinStylesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.11).|
|linestyle(Stroke<br>Line Style)|Specifies the line style of the stroke.  Default is`single`.<br><br>`single`<br><br>`thinThin`<br><br>`thinThick`<br><br>`thickThin`<br><br>`thickBetweenThin`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke linestyle="thickThin" weight="5pt">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeLineStylesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.12).|
|miterlimit(Miter<br>Joint Limit)|Specifies the smoothness of the miter joint, or the maximum distance between the inner<br>point and outer point of a joint.  This number is a multiple of the thickness of the line.<br>Default is 8.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke joinstyle="miter" weight="10pt"`<br>`miterlimit="2">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemadecimal<br>datatype.|
|on(Stroke Toggle)|Specifies whether the stroke is displayed.  Default is`true`.  This attribute overrides the<br>shape'sstrokeattribute.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:rect style="width:50;height:50" stroked="true"`<br>`fillcolor="lime" strokecolor="red">`<br>`<v:stroke on="false" weight="5pt"/>`<br>`</v:rect>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|opacity(Stroke<br>Opacity)|Specifies the amount of transparency of a stroke.  Default is 1.0.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:rect style="width:50;height:50"`<br>`fillcolor="lime" strokecolor="red">`<br>`<v:stroke weight="5pt" opacity="50%"/>`<br>`</v:rect>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|src(Stroke Image<br>Location)|Specifies the source image to load for a stroke fill.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … src="myimage.gif" … >`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|startarrow(Line<br>Start Arrowhead)|Specifies an arrowhead for the start of a line.  Default is`none`.  The path shall not be<br>closed with the x command for the arrowhead to show.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`block`<br><br>`classic`<br><br>`diamond`<br><br>`oval`<br><br>`open`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke startarrow="classic"/>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowTypesimple<br>type (§19.1.3.8).|
|startarrowlength<br>(Line Start<br>Arrowhead Length)|Specifies the length of the arrowhead at the start of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`short`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`long`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … startarrowlength="long" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowLengthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.7).|
|startarrowwidth<br>(Line Start<br>Arrowhead Width)|Specifies the width of the arrowhead at the start of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`narrow`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`wide`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … startarrowwidth="wide" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowWidthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.9).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|title(Stroke Title)|Specifies the title of an embedded stroke image.  This is typically set to the comment<br>property of the image, which is often blank.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … o:title="alt text" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|weight(Stroke<br>Weight)|Specifies the thickness of a stroke.  Default is 1.  This attribute overrides the shape's<br>strokeweightattribute.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_StrokeChild) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.27 rules (Rule Set)

This element specifies a list of rule entries which describe how a certain shape or sets of shapes should behave during editing.

[ _Example_ : The following rule defines a connection between two shapes.  The shape with id _s1036 connects shape _s1033 to _s1032:

```
<o:shapelayout v:ext="edit">
```

- `<o:rules v:ext="edit">`

```
    <o:r id="V:Rule1" type="connector" idref="#_s1036">
```

```
      <o:proxy start="" idref="#_s1033" connectloc="0"/>
```

```
      <o:proxy end="" idref="#_s1032" connectloc="2"/>
```

```
    </o:r>
```

```
  </o:rules>
```

```
</o:shapelayout>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Rules) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.28 shapedefaults (New Shape Defaults)

This element specifies the defaults that are used when creating new shapes.  These defaults are stored once per document.

[ _Example_ : Consider a case in which an application chooses to store the highest shape ID it has used in the document thus far.  This could be used to support the generation of new shape IDs:

```
<o:shapedefaults v:ext="edit" spidmax="1029"/>
```

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|allowincell(Allow<br>in Table Cell)|Specifies whether the shape is allowed to be placed in a table cell.  Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
