|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|
|fill(Shape Fill<br>Toggle)|Specifies whether the closed path is filled.  Default is`true`.  This attribute is overridden<br>by the fillonattribute.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … fill="f" fillcolor="red" …>`<br>`</v:shape>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|fillcolor(Default Fill<br>Color)|Specifies the default shape fill color.  Default is no value.  Colors are typically specified as<br>either a named color, such as`red`, or six hexadecimal digits representing the red, green<br>and blue values of the color, such as`#00FF30`.  Full details are specified in the simple type<br>description.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|spidmax(Shape ID<br>Optional Storage)|Specifies an optional value that allows applications a mechanism for storing information<br>they need to persist related to shape IDs.  Default is 0.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemainteger<br>datatype.|
|stroke(Shape<br>Stroke Toggle)|Specifies whether the path defining the shape is stroked with a solid line.  Thestroke<br>element (§19.1.2.21) defines other strokes.  Theonattribute of thestrokeelement<br>overrides this attribute.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … fillcolor="red"`<br>`stroke="false" strokecolor="blue"…>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|strokecolor(Shape<br>Stroke Color)|Specifies the primary color of the brush to use to stroke the path of the shape.  Default is<br>`black`.  Thecolorattribute of thestrokeelement (§19.1.2.21) overrides this.  Colors are<br>typically specified as either a named color, such as`red`, or six hexadecimal digits<br>representing the red, green and blue values of the color, such as`#00FF30`.  Full details<br>are specified in the simple type description.<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
|||`<v:shape … strokecolor="red" …>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).||
|style(Shape Styling<br>Properties)||Specifies the CSS2 styling properties of the shape. The CSS2 (Cascading Style Sheets, Level<br>2) specification, a Recommendation of the World Wide Web Consortium, is available<br>here:http://www.w3.org/TR/REC-CSS2.<br>This attribute uses a semi-colon delimited list of “name:value” pairs, the syntax defined<br>by section 4.1.8 of the CSS2 specification for grouped declarations (without the<br>surrounding braces). The following tables define the allowed properties and the VML<br>treatment of each.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … style=’position:absolute;width:100pt;height:50pt’ …`<br>`>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>**Property**<br>**Description**<br>`flip`<br>Specifies that the orientation of a shape is flipped.  Default is no<br>value.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`x`- Flip along the y-axis, reversing the x-coordinates.<br><br>`y`- Flip along the x-axis, reversing the y-coordinates.<br><br>`xy`- Flip along both the y- and x-axis.<br><br>`yx`- Flip along both the x- and y-axis.<br>`height`<br>Specifies the height of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as apercentage of the||
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`flip`|Specifies that the orientation of a shape is flipped.  Default is no<br>value.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`x`- Flip along the y-axis, reversing the x-coordinates.<br><br>`y`- Flip along the x-axis, reversing the y-coordinates.<br><br>`xy`- Flip along both the y- and x-axis.<br><br>`yx`- Flip along both the x- and y-axis.|
|||`height`|Specifies the height of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element. ||||parent object's height.||
|||`left`|Specifies the position of the left of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element left of it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0. |||`margin-right`|Specifies the position of the right of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0.  It is specified in<br>CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the coordinate system of<br>the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|

||||page.<br><br><units> - A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.||
|||`margin-top`|Specifies the position of the top of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the shape anchor.  Default is 0. Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`horizontal`|Specifies the horizontal positioning data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`absolute`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`absolute`<br><br>`left`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`right`<br><br>`inside`<br><br>`outside`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`horizontal-`<br>`relative`|Specifies relative horizontal position data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  This modifies the`mso-`<br>`position-horizontal`property.  Default is`text`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`margin`<br><br>`page`<br><br>`text`<br><br>`char`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`vertical`|Specifies the vertical positioning data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`absolute`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`absolute`<br><br>`top`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`bottom`||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||<br>`inside`<br><br>`outside`||
|||`mso-position-`<br>`vertical-`<br>`relative`|Specifies relative vertical position data for objects in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  This modifies the`mso-`<br>`position-vertical`property.  Default is`text`.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`margin`<br><br>`page`<br><br>`text`<br><br>`line`||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-`<br>`bottom`|Specifies the distance from the bottom of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-left`|Specifies the distance from the left side of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-right`|Specifies the distance from the right side of the shape to the text<br>that wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different<br>from the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the<br>shape to include the margin areas.  This property does not change<br>the origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`distance-top`|Specifies the distance from the top of the shape to the text that<br>wraps around it.  Default is 0 pt.  This property is different from<br>the CSS`margin`property, which changes the origin of the shape<br>to include the margin areas.  This property does not change the<br>origin.||
|||`mso-wrap-`<br>`edited`|Specifies whether the wrap coordinates were customized by the<br>user.  If the wrap coordinates are generated by an editor, this<br>property is`true`; otherwise they were customized by a user.<br>Default is`false`.||
|||`mso-wrap-style`|Specifies the wrapping mode for text in shapes in<br>WordprocessingML documents.  Default is`square`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`square`- Wraps text inside the shape in a square.<br><br>`none`- Text does not wrap.||
|||`position`|Specifies the kind of positioning used to place an element.<br>Default is`static`.  When the element is contained inside agroup,||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|
||||this property shall be`absolute`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`static`- The element is positioned according to the<br>normal flow of the page.  The`top`and`left`properties<br>are ignored.  If the object is anchored inline, this value is<br>used.<br><br>`absolute`- The element is positioned relative to the<br>parent, using the`top`and`left`properties.<br><br>`relative`- The element is positioned according to the<br>normal flow of the page, but the`top`and`left`properties<br>are used.  The overlap of overlapping elements is<br>governed by the`z-index`property.||
|||`rotation`|Specifies the angle that a shape is rotated, in degrees.  Default is<br>0.  Positive angles are clockwise.||
|||`top`|Specifies the position of the top of the containing block of the<br>shape relative to the element above it in the flow of the page.<br>Default is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group,<br>in the coordinate system of the parent element.  This property<br>shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm, in, pt, pc, or px) or a relative units designator (em or<br>ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's height.||
|||`visibility`|Specifies whether a shape is displayed.  Only`inherit`and`hidden`<br>are used; any other values are mapped to`inherit`.  Default is<br>`inherit`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`hidden`- The shape is not visible, but is still part of the<br>flow of the objects in the browser.  Mouse events are not<br>processed.<br><br>`inherit`- The visibility state is inherited from the parent<br>of the shape.||
|||`width`|Specifies the width of the containing block of the shape.  Default<br>is 0.  It is specified in CSS units or, for elements in a group, in the<br>coordinate system of the parent element.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Default position of an element in the flow of the<br>page.<br><br><units>- A number with an absolute units designator (cm,<br>mm,in, pt, pc,orpx)or a relative units designator(em or||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||ex).  If no units are given, pixels (px) is assumed.<br><br><percentage>- Value expressed as a percentage of the<br>parent object's width.|
|||`z-index`|Specifies the display order of overlapping shapes.  Default is 0.<br>This property shall not be used for shapes anchored inline.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`auto`- Uses the order that the shapes appear in the page,<br>bottom to top.<br><br><order>- A number that represents the stacking<br>precedence.  Shapes with higher numbers are placed on<br>top of those with lower numbers.  Negative numbers are<br>allowed.|
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`direction`|Specifies the direction of the text in the textbox.  Default is`ltr`.<br>This property is superceded by the`mso-direction-alt`property<br>if that is specified.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`ltr`- Text is displayed left-to-right.<br><br>`rtl`- Text is displayed right-to-left.|
|||`layout-flow`|Determines the flow of the text layout in a textbox.  Default is<br>`horizontal`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`horizontal`- Text is displayed horizontally.<br><br>`vertical`- Text is displayed vertically.<br><br>`vertical-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>vertically.<br><br>`horizontal-ideographic`- Ideographic text is displayed<br>horizontally.|
|||`mso-direction-`<br>`alt`|Specifies an alternate direction for text in textboxes.  Overrides<br>the`direction`property.  The only allowed value is`context`.|
|||`mso-fit-shape-`<br>`to-text`|Specifies whether the shape stretches to fit the text in the<br>textbox.  Default is`false`.|
|||`mso-fit-text-`<br>`to-shape`|Specifies whether the text stretches to fit the textbox.  Default is<br>`false`.|
|||`mso-layout-`<br>`flow-alt`|Specifies the alternate layout flow for text in textboxes.  This<br>propertyis used instead of`layout-flow`when the layout flow is|

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||from bottom to top for non-ideographic languages.  Its only value<br>is`bottom-to-top`.|
|||`mso-next-`<br>`textbox`|Specifies the ID of the next textbox in a series.  Used to keep track<br>of a set of linked textboxes.  Default is no value.|
|||`mso-rotate`|Specifies a specific rotation value for text in a textbox.  Default is<br>0.  Allowed values are:<br><br>0<br><br>90<br><br>180<br><br>-90|
|||`mso-text-scale`|Specifies the scaling factor for fitting text to shapes.  Default is 0.<br>This property is only used if`mso-fit-text-to-shape`is`true`.|
|||`v-text-anchor`|Specifies the vertical anchoring of text in a textbox.  Default is<br>`top`.  The alignment of a text anchor only becomes evident if`mso-`<br>`fit-text-to-shape`is`false`.  This property is different from the<br>`vertical-align`CSS property, which is used for ideographic<br>languages. Allowed values are:<br><br>`top`<br><br>`middle`<br><br>`bottom`<br><br>`top-center`<br><br>`middle-center`<br><br>`bottom-center`<br><br>`top-baseline`<br><br>`bottom-baseline`<br><br>`top-center-baseline`<br><br>`bottom-center-baseline`|
|||**Property**|**Description**|
|||`font`|Specifies a compound value of font settings.  Default is no value.<br>The values are the same as those of the CSS font property.  The<br>order of definitions in the string is: font-style, font-variant, font-<br>weight, font-size, line-height, font-family.|
|||`font-family`|Specifies the family of the font.  Default is no value.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS font-family property.|
|||`font-size`|Specifies the size of the font.  Default is no value.  The font size is<br>defined in points.  The values are the same as those of the CSS<br>font-size property.|
|||`font-style`|Specifies the amount of slant for a font.  Default is`normal`.  The|

|**Attributes**||||**Description**|**Description**|**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|||||values are the same as those of the CSS font-style property.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`normal`<br><br>`italic`<br><br>`oblique`- Treated the same as italic.|||
|||`font-variant`||Specifies the variant style of a font.  Default is`normal`.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS font-variant property.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`normal`<br><br>`small-caps`|||
|||`font-weight`||Specifies the thickness of the letters of the font.  Default is<br>`normal`.  The values are the same as those of the CSS font-weight<br>property.  Allowed values are:<br>**Value**<br>**Description**<br>`normal`<br>`lighter`<br>`100`<br>`200`<br>`300`<br>`400`<br>Treated as non-bold.<br>`bold`<br>`bolder`<br>`500`<br>`600`<br>`700`<br>`800`<br>`900`<br>Treated as bold.|||
|||||**Value**|**Description**||
|||||`normal`<br>`lighter`<br>`100`<br>`200`<br>`300`<br>`400`|Treated as non-bold.||
|||||`bold`<br>`bolder`<br>`500`<br>`600`<br>`700`<br>`800`<br>`900`|Treated as bold.||
|||`mso-text-`<br>`shadow`||Specifies whether a shadow is applied to the text on a text path.<br>Default is`false`.|||
|||`text-`<br>`decoration`||Specifies the style of text decoration.  Default is`none`.  The values<br>are the same as those of the CSS text-decoration property.<br>Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`underline`<br><br>`overline`<br><br>`line-through`<br><br>`blink`|||
|||`v-rotate-`<br>`letters`||Specifies whether the letters of the text are rotated<br>counterclockwise by 90 degrees.  Default is`false`.|||
|||`v-same-letter-`<br>`heights`||Specifies whether all letters are the same height regardless of|||

|**Attributes**|||**Description**|
|---|---|---|---|
||||initial case.  If`true`, the lowercase letters are stretched to the<br>height of the uppercase letters.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-text-align`|Specifies the alignment of text.  Default is`left`.  Allowed values<br>are:<br><br>`left`<br><br>`right`<br><br>`center`<br><br>`justify`<br><br>`letter-justify`- Distributes the extra space between<br>the letters.<br><br>`stretch-justify`- Stretches the letters to fill in the<br>space.|
|||`v-text-kern`|Specifies whether kerning is turned on.  Default is`false`.|
|||`v-text-reverse`|Specifies whether the layout order of rows is reversed.  Default is<br>`false`.  This is used for vertical text layout.|
|||`v-text-`<br>`spacing-mode`|Specifies the mode for letter spacing.  Default is`tightening`.<br>This property determines whether space is  removed between<br>each letter (`tightening`) or added between each letter<br>(`tracking`).  The amount of letter spacing change is defined by<br>the`v-text-spacing`property.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`tightening`<br><br>`tracking`|
|||`v-text-spacing`|Specifies the amount of spacing for text in 100ths of single line<br>spacing.  Default is 100.|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||<br>`rotation`<br><br>`top`<br><br>`visibility`<br><br>`width`<br><br>`z-index`<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_ShapeDefaults) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.29 shapelayout (Shape Layout Properties)

This element contains child elements that store information used in the editing and layout of shapes.

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_ShapeLayout) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.30 signatureline (Digital Signature Line)

This element specifies a signature line in a document.  A signature line provides a visual representation of a signature in a document that is digitally signed.  The signature line element indicates that the VML shape in which it appears acts as that visual representation.  Typically, the VML shape is an image.

[ _Example_ :

- `<v:shape … >`

- `<v:imagedata … />`

- `<o:signatureline v:ext="edit" id="{11979195-DE54-414B-ABD6-5F63607C648B}" provid="{00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000}" o:suggestedsigner="John Doe" o:suggestedsigner2="Manager" o:suggestedsigneremail=johndoe@example.com allowcomments="t" issignatureline="t"/>`

- `</v:shape>`

The signature line in the document might look like this:

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|addlxml(Additional<br>Signature<br>Information)|Specifies an optional string that is used to store additional information about the digital<br>signature.  Default is no value.  [_Rationale_: Some digital signature software stores, for<br>example, server and region information with the signature._end rationale_]<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:signatureline … o:addlxml="…">`<br>`</o:signatureline>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|allowcomments<br>(User-specified<br>Comments Flag)|Specifies whether the user can attach comments to the signature line at signing time.<br>Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:signatureline … allowcomments="true">`<br>`</o:signatureline>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|id(Unique ID)|Specifies a unique ID for the signature line.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:signatureline …`<br>`id="{11979195-DE54-414B-ABD6-5F63607C648B}">`<br>`</o:signatureline>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Guidsimple type (Part 1,<br>§22.9.2.4).|
|issignatureline<br>(Signature Line Flag)|Specifies whether the image is a signature line.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:signatureline … issignatureline="true">`<br>`</o:signatureline>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|provid(Signature<br>Provider ID)|Specifies a unique ID identifying which signature provider created the signature line.<br>Default is no value.  [_Guidance_The GUID is typically the CLSID of the provider COM add-<br>in._end guidance_]<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:signatureline …`<br>`provid="{00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000}">`<br>`</o:signatureline>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Guidsimple type (Part 1,<br>§22.9.2.4).|
|showsigndate<br>(Show Signed Date<br>Flag)|Specifies whether the signed signature line image generated should include the date of<br>signing.  Default is`true`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:signatureline … showsigndate="false">`<br>`</o:signatureline>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|signinginstructions<br>(Instructions for<br>Signing)|Specifies text shown to the user at signing time.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:signatureline … o:signinginstructions="Sign here">`<br>`</o:signatureline>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|signinginstructions<br>set(Use Signing<br>Instructions Flag)|Specifies whether there is data set in thesigninginstructionsattribute.  Default is`false`.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:signatureline … signinginstructionsset="true">`<br>`</o:signatureline>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|sigprovurl<br>(Signature Provider<br>Download URL)|Specifies the URL for downloading the signature provider.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:signatureline … o:sigprovurl="http://www.example.com">`<br>`</o:signatureline>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|suggestedsigner<br>(Suggested Signer<br>Line 1)|Specifies the first line of information of who should sign the signature line.  Default is no<br>value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:signatureline … o:suggestedsigner="John Doe">`<br>`</o:signatureline>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|suggestedsigner2<br>(Suggested Signer<br>Line 2)|Specifies the second line of information of who should sign the signature line.  Default is<br>no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:signatureline … o:suggestedsigner2="Title">`<br>`</o:signatureline>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|suggestedsignerem<br>ail(Suggested<br>Signer E-mail<br>Address)|Specifies the e-mail address of who should sign the signature line.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<o:signatureline …`<br>`o:suggestedsigneremail="johndoe@example.com">`<br>`</o:signatureline>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_SignatureLine) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.31 skew (Skew Transform)

This element specifies a perspective skew effect on a shape.  The skew is applied to vector graphics, not image data on the shape in picture fills or image elements.  The on attribute shall be true and a permitted value assigned to the matrix attribute.

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|
|id(Skew ID)|Specifies a name that provides a unique identifier for a skew.  Default is no value.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|matrix(Skew<br>Perspective Matrix)|Specifies a perspective transform of a skew.  Default is "1,0,0,1,0,0".<br>The matrix is given in the form "sxx,  sxy,  syx, syy, px, py" where s = scale and p =<br>perspective.  If theoffsetattribute is in absolute units thenpx, pyare in 1/EMU units;<br>otherwise they are an inverse fraction of the shape size.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|offset(Skew Offset)|Specifies the amount of x,y offset from the shape’s location.  Default is "2pt,2pt".<br>Positive values are measured from the upper left of the face of the shape.<br>Values are specified as either an absolute measurement or a fractional value of the<br>shape's dimensions (–0.5 to +0.5).<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|on(Skew Toggle)|Specifies whether a skew is displayed.  Default is`false`.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|origin(Skew Origin)|Specifies the origin of the skew.  Default is "0,0".<br>Values are typically a percentage of the shape's size and range from -0.5 to +0.5.  Larger<br>values are allowed that give offsets as multiples of the shape's size.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_Skew) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.2.32 top (Text Box Top Stroke)

This element specifies the stroke properties for the top border of a text box.  It entirely supercedes its parent stroke element if its on attribute is true.  Thus the default value of an unspecified attribute overrides a value specified in the parent.  If the on attribute is `false` or not specified, the border is not shown.

[ _Example_ : The text box borders are set independently.  Note that the bottom border does not inherit the weight from the parent stroke element.

- `<v:stroke weight="2.25pt">`

- `<o:left v:ext="view" dashstyle="1 1" color="blue" weight="5pt" on="t"/>`

- `<o:top v:ext="view" color="black" weight="2.25pt" on="t"/>`

- `<o:right v:ext="view" dashstyle="1 1" color="blue" weight="5pt" on="t"/>`

- `<o:bottom v:ext="view" color="black" on="t"/>`

- `<o:column v:ext="view" color="#f60" on="t"/>`

- `</v:stroke>`

_end example_ ]

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|althref(Alternate<br>Image Reference)|Specifies an alternate reference for an image in Macintosh PICT format.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … althref="myimage.pcz" … >`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|color(Stroke Color)|Specifies the stroke color.  Overrides thestrokecolorattribute of a shape.  Default is<br>`black`.  See thefillcolorattribute for a list of supported named colors.<br>[_Example_: The shape stroke is blue:<br>`<v:shape … strokecolor="red" … >`<br>`<v:stroke color="blue"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|color2(Stroke<br>Alternate Pattern<br>Color)|Specifies a second color for strokes, used whenfilltypeis`pattern`.  Default is no value.<br>When a pattern fill is used for the stroke, the stroke color is used in colored parts of the<br>source image.  The color2 defines an alternate color to use in place of black in the source<br>image.<br>[_Example_: This unusual example is intended to demonstrate how the image and colors<br>interact to create a patterned stroke.  The yellow background shows transparency.  The<br>non-square shape and square image create an effective offset.  The heavy stroke weight<br>shows more of the image.  The green shape fill shows how the stroke is overlaid on the<br>shape.<br>`<v:background fillcolor="yellow"/>`<br>`<v:shape style="width:60;height:50"`<br>`strokecolor="red" fillcolor="lime"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke filltype="pattern" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="myimage.gif" color2="blue"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>, where myimage.gif is:<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ColorTypesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.3).|
|dashstyle(Stroke<br>Dash Pattern)|Specifies the dot and dash pattern for a stroke.  Default is`solid`.  Pre-defined values are:<br><br>`solid`<br><br>`shortdash`<br><br>`shortdot`<br><br>`shortdashdot`<br><br>`shortdashdotdot`<br><br>`dot`<br><br>`dash`<br><br>`longdash`<br><br>`dashdot`<br><br>`longdashdot`<br><br>`longdashdotdot`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||A custom-defined dash pattern can also be specified using a series of numbers.  These<br>define the length of the dash (the drawn part of the stroke) and the length of the space<br>between the dashes.  The lengths are relative to the line width: a length of 1 is equal to<br>the line width.  Theendcapstyle is applied to each dash but the arrow style is not.  The<br>string defines the length of the dash then the length of the space.  This can be repeated<br>to form complex dash styles.  The string should always contain a pair of numbers; if it<br>contains an odd number of numbers the last is disregarded.  0 implies a dot that is<br>fourfold symmetrical (with round end caps, this is a circle).<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke dashstyle="0 2" weight="3pt"`<br>`endcap="round">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>`<v:stroke dashstyle="longdashdotdot"`<br>`weight="2pt">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|endarrow(Line End<br>Arrowhead)|Specifies an arrowhead for the end of a line.  Default is`none`.  The path shall not be<br>closed with the x command for the arrowhead to show.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`block`<br><br>`classic`<br><br>`diamond`<br><br>`oval`<br><br>`open`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke endarrow="classic"/>`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowTypesimple<br>type (§19.1.3.8).|
|endarrowlength<br>(Line End<br>Arrowhead Length)|Specifies the length of the arrowhead at the end of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`short`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`long`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endarrowlength="long" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowLengthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.7).|
|endarrowwidth<br>(Line End<br>Arrowhead Width)|Specifies the width of the arrowhead at the end of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`narrow`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`wide`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endarrowwidth="wide" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowWidthsimple|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||type (§19.1.3.9).|
|endcap(Line End<br>Cap)|Specifies the cap style for the end of a stroke.  Default is`flat`.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`flat`<br><br>`square`<br><br>`round`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … endcap="round" weight="10pt" … />`<br>`endcap="flat"`<br>`endcap="square"`<br>`endcap="round"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeEndCapsimple type<br>(§19.1.3.10).|
|ext(VML Extension<br>Handling Behavior)<br>Namespace:<br>urn:schemas-<br>microsoft-com:vml|Specifies an optional value that indicates how applications that implement VML should<br>interpret extensions not defined as part of the original specification of core VML.<br>[_Rationale_: This part of the original VML specification is included to assist applications<br>that leverage existing VML support in implementing the Office Open XML Format._end_<br>_rationale_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_Extsimple type (§19.1.3.2).|
|filltype(Stroke<br>Image Style)|Specifies the kind of fill used for the background of a stroke.  Default is`solid`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`solid`- The fill pattern is solid.<br><br>`tile`- The fill image is tiled.<br><br>`pattern`- The fill image is stretched to form a pattern.<br><br>`frame`- The fill image becomes a border for the shape.<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:shape style="width:50;height:50"`<br>`strokecolor="red"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke filltype="frame" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="border.gif"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>, where border.gif is:<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_FillTypesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.4).|
|forcedash(Force<br>Dashed Outline)|Specifies whether a dashed outline is used to draw a shape when a shape has no line or<br>fill.  Default is`false`.<br>Used by PresentationML placeholders to draw a dashed outline when there is no line and<br>no fill for a shape.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … o:forcedash="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|href(Original Image<br>Reference)|Specifies the URL to the original image file.  Used only if the picture has been linked and<br>embedded.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … o:href="myimage.gif" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|imagealignshape<br>(Stoke Image<br>Alignment)|Specifies the alignment of the stroke image.  If`true`, the image is aligned with the shape.<br>Otherwise, it is aligned with the containing scope.  Default is`true`.|

|**Attributes**||**Description**|
|---|---|---|
|||[_Example_: The top position offset shifts the image alignment relative to the containing<br>window:<br>`<v:shape fillcolor="silver"`<br>`style="top:20;width:50;height:50"`<br>`path="m 0,0 l 0,1000 1000,1000 1000,0 x e">`<br>`<v:stroke imagealignshape="false" weight="20pt"`<br>`filltype="tile" src="myimage.gif"/>`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>`imagealignshape="false"`<br>`imagealignshape="false"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|imageaspect<br>(Stroke Image<br>Aspect Ratio)||Specifies how the stroke image aspect ratio is preserved.  Default is`ignore`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br>Value<br>Description<br>`ignore`<br>Ignore aspect issues.<br>`atleast`<br>Image is at least as big asimagesize.<br>`atmost`<br>Image is no bigger thanimagesize.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke filltype="frame" weight="10pt"`<br>`src="border.gif" imagealignshape="true"`<br>`imageaspect="atleast">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>`imagealignshape="ignore"`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`imagealignshape="atleast"`<br>`imagealignshape="atmost"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_ImageAspectsimple type<br>(§19.1.3.5).|
|imagesize(Stroke<br>Image Size)|Specifies the size of the image for the stroke.  Default is the size of the image.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … imagesize="10pt,10pt" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|insetpen(Inset<br>Border From Path)|Specifies that the border shall be displayed inside of the path defining the shape, rather<br>than along the path (the default border placement), or outside of the path as might be<br>done with an image.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:shape … insetpen="true" … >`<br>`</v:shape>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|joinstyle(Line End<br>Join Style))|Specifies the join style for line ends.  Default is`round`.<br><br>`round`<br><br>`bevel`<br><br>`miter`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:polyline strokeweight="10pt" strokecolor="navy"`<br>`points="10pt,10pt,50pt,50pt,90pt,10pt">`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:stroke joinstyle="bevel"/>`<br>`</v:polyline>`<br>`joinstyle="round"`<br>`joinstyle="bevel"`<br>`joinstyle="miter"`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeJoinStylesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.11).|
|linestyle(Stroke<br>Line Style)|Specifies the line style of the stroke.  Default is`single`.<br><br>`single`<br><br>`thinThin`<br><br>`thinThick`<br><br>`thickThin`<br><br>`thickBetweenThin`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke linestyle="thickThin" weight="5pt">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeLineStylesimple type<br>(§19.1.3.12).|
|miterlimit(Miter<br>Joint Limit)|Specifies the smoothness of the miter joint, or the maximum distance between the inner<br>point and outer point of a joint.  This number is a multiple of the thickness of the line.<br>Default is 8.<br>[_Example_:|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||`<v:stroke joinstyle="miter" weight="10pt"`<br>`miterlimit="2">`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemadecimal<br>datatype.|
|on(Stroke Toggle)|Specifies whether the stroke is displayed.  Default is`true`.  This attribute overrides the<br>shape'sstrokeattribute.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:rect style="width:50;height:50" stroked="true"`<br>`fillcolor="lime" strokecolor="red">`<br>`<v:stroke on="false" weight="5pt"/>`<br>`</v:rect>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_TrueFalsesimple type<br>(§20.1.2.5).|
|opacity(Stroke<br>Opacity)|Specifies the amount of transparency of a stroke.  Default is 1.0.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:rect style="width:50;height:50"`<br>`fillcolor="lime" strokecolor="red">`<br>`<v:stroke weight="5pt" opacity="50%"/>`<br>`</v:rect>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||datatype.|
|src(Stroke Image<br>Location)|Specifies the source image to load for a stroke fill.  Default is no value.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … src="myimage.gif" … >`<br>`</v:stroke>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|startarrow(Line<br>Start Arrowhead)|Specifies an arrowhead for the start of a line.  Default is`none`.  The path shall not be<br>closed with the x command for the arrowhead to show.  Allowed values are:<br><br>`none`<br><br>`block`<br><br>`classic`<br><br>`diamond`<br><br>`oval`<br><br>`open`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke startarrow="classic"/>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowTypesimple<br>type (§19.1.3.8).|
|startarrowlength<br>(Line Start<br>Arrowhead Length)|Specifies the length of the arrowhead at the start of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`short`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`long`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … startarrowlength="long" … />`|

|**Attributes**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowLengthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.7).|
|startarrowwidth<br>(Line Start<br>Arrowhead Width)|Specifies the width of the arrowhead at the start of a line.  Default is`medium`.  Allowed<br>values are:<br><br>`narrow`<br><br>`medium`<br><br>`wide`<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:stroke … startarrowwidth="wide" … />`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by theST_StrokeArrowWidthsimple<br>type (§19.1.3.9).|
|title(Stroke Title)|Specifies the title of an embedded stroke image.  This is typically set to the comment<br>property of the image, which is often blank.<br>[_Example_:<br>`<v:fill … o:title="alt text" … >`<br>`</v:fill>`<br>_end example_]<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|
|weight(Stroke<br>Weight)|Specifies the thickness of a stroke.  Default is 1.  This attribute overrides the shape's<br>strokeweightattribute.<br>The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schemastring<br>datatype.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this element’s content model (CT_StrokeChild) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## **19.2.3 Simple Types**

The following additional simple type information in the urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:office namespace is used for documents of a transitional conformance class.

## 19.2.3.1 ST_AlternateMathContentType (Alternate Math Content Type)

This simple type specifies the content type of the XML markup stored within the equationxml element.

The following values are reserved:

|**Value**|**Meaning**|
|---|---|
|`officeopenxmlmath`|Specifies that the data has been stored using the Office Open XML Math syntax defined<br>in Part 1, §22.1.|
|`mathml`|Specifies that the data has been stored using the MathML syntax.|

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_AlternateMathContentType) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.2 ST_Angle (Callout Angles)

This simple type specifies values for the angle attribute of the callout element (§19.2.2.2).

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|30(30 degrees)|30 degrees.|
|45(45 degrees)|45 degrees.|
|60(60 degrees)|60 degrees.|
|90(90 degrees)|90 degrees.|
|any(Any Angle)|Unconstrained angle.|
|auto(Automatic Angle)|The application chooses an appropriate angle.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_Angle) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.3 ST_BWMode (Black And White Modes)

This simple type specifies the ways in which a shape renders in a black and white context.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|auto(Automatic)|Use thebwpureorbwnormalattributes based on the<br>type of output being generated.|
|black(Black)|Use black only.|
|blackTextAndLines(Black Text And Lines)|Use shades of gray, except for text and lines, which are<br>black.|
|color(Color)|Do not use grayscale or black and white.|
|grayOutline(Gray Outlines)|Use gray and white only.|
|grayScale(Grayscale)|Use shades of gray only.|
|hide(Hide Object When Displayed in Black and White)|Do not display the object when rendering in only black<br>and white.|
|highContrast(Black And White)|Use black and white only, no grays.|
|inverseGray(Inverse Grayscale)|Use shades of gray only, but invert light and dark<br>grays.|
|lightGrayscale(Light grayscale)|Use light shades of gray only.|
|undrawn(Do Not Show)|Do not show the object.|
|white(White)|Use white only.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_BWMode) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.4 ST_CalloutDrop (Callout Drop Location)

This simple type specifies location values for the drop attribute of the callout element (§19.2.2.2).

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_CalloutDrop) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.5 ST_CalloutPlacement (Callout Placement)

This type defines location values used by the drop attribute of the callout element (§19.2.2.2).

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bottom(Bottom placement)|Bottom of the shape.|
|center(Center placement)|Vertical center of the shape.|
|top(Top placement)|Top of the shape.|

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|user(User-defined placement)|User-defined placement.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_CalloutPlacement) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.6 ST_ColorMode (Extrusion Color Types)

This simple type specifies ways that the extrusion color is defined.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|auto(Use Shape Fill Color)|Specifies that the color of the extrusion is the same as<br>the fill color of the shape.|
|custom(Use Custom Color)|Specifies that the extrusion is the color of the color<br>attribute.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_ColorMode) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.7 ST_ConnectorType (Connector Type)

This simple type specifies types of connectors.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|curved(Curved Connector)|A curved connector.|
|elbow(Elbow Connector)|An elbow-shaped connector.|
|none(No Connector)|No connector.|
|straight(Straight Connector)|A straight connector.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_ConnectorType) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.8 ST_ConnectType (Connection Locations Type)

This simple type specifies types of connection locations.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|custom(Custom Connections)|A custom array of connection locations.|
|none(No)|No connection locations.|
|rect(Four Connections)|Standard four connection points at midpoints of top,<br>bottom, left, and right sides.|
|segments(Edit Point Connections)|The edit points of the shape are used.  Edit points are<br>the black dots in a graphical editor that are used to<br>select parts of a shape.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_ConnectType) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.9 ST_ContentType (Content Type)

This simple type specifies a content type.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_ContentType) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.10 ST_DiagramLayout (Diagram Layout Type)

This simple type specifies the style of automatic layout to apply to a node in a diagram.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema integer datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|0(Top-down Centered)|Top-down, centered layout.|
|1(Hanging Both Sides)|Hanging, both sides layout.|
|2(Hanging Right Side)|Hanging, right side layout.|
|3(Hanging Left Side)|Hanging, left side layout.|

**Enumeration Value Description**

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_DiagramLayout) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.11 ST_ExtrusionPlane (Extrusion Planes)

This simple type specifies three axis-aligned planes.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|XY(XY Plane)|The xy plane.|
|YZ(YZ Plane)|The yz plane.|
|ZX(ZX Plane)|The zx plane.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_ExtrusionPlane) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.12 ST_ExtrusionRender (Extrusion Rendering Types)

This simple type specifies different rendering modes.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|boundingCube(Bounding Cube)|Rendering displays the bounding cube that contains<br>the shape.|
|solid(Solid)|Rendering displays a solid shape.|
|wireFrame(Wireframe)|Rendering displays a wireframe shape.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_ExtrusionRender) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.13 ST_ExtrusionType (Extrusion Type)

This simple type specifies types of extrusions.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|parallel(Parallel Projection)|Extrusion is rendered so that the center of projection<br>is infinitely far away; the extrusion lines do not<br>converge.|
|perspective(Perspective Projection)|Extrusion is rendered to a center of projection, which<br>is the same as the vanishing point for unrotated<br>objects.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_ExtrusionType) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.14 ST_FillType (Shape Fill Type)

This simple type specifies the types for fills applied to a shape.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|background(Use Background Fill)|Use the fill properties of the background of the object<br>on which the shape exists, such as the page.|
|frame(Stretch Image to Fit)|The image is stretched to fill the shape.|
|gradient(Linear Gradient)|The fill colors blend together in a linear gradient from<br>bottom to top.|
|gradientCenter(Centered Radial Gradient)|This indicates that the gradient runs across the center<br>of the shape for a gradient that is defined as<br>`gradientRadial`in the parent fill element (§19.1.2.5)<br>that is defined in the VML namespace.|
|gradientRadial(Radial Gradient)|The fill colors blend together in a radial gradient.|
|gradientUnscaled(Unscaled Gradient)|The gradient angle is not scaled relative to the aspect<br>ratio of the shape.<br>[_Example_: The shapes below are twice as wide as they<br>are tall.  The first shape uses an unscaled gradient and<br>the second uses a regular scaled gradient:|

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||_end example_]|
|pattern(Image Pattern)|The image is used to create a pattern using the fill<br>colors.|
|solid(Solid Fill)|The fill pattern is a solid color.|
|tile(Tiled Image)|The fill image is tiled.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_FillType) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.15 ST_How (Alignment Type)

This simple type specifies types of alignment.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|bottom(Bottom Alignment)|Bottom vertical alignment.|
|center(Center Alignment)|Center horizontal alignment.|
|left(Left Alignment)|Left horizontal alignment.|
|middle(Middle Alignment)|Middle vertical alignment.|
|right(Right Alignment)|Right horizontal alignment.|
|top(Top Alignment)|Top vertical alignment.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_How) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.16 ST_HrAlign (Alignment Type)

This simple type specifies alignments for horiztonal rules.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|center(Center Alignment)|Center aligned.|
|left(Left Alignment)|Left aligned.|
|right(Right Alignment)|Right aligned.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_HrAlign) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.17 ST_InsetMode (Inset Margin Type)

This simple type specifies how inner text margins are obtained.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|auto(Automatic Margins)|Inner text margins are calculated by the application.|
|custom(Custom Margins)|Inner text margins are specified by the shape.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_InsetMode) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.18 ST_OLEDrawAspect ( Embedded Object Representations)

This simple type specifies the ways in which embedded objects are displayed in the application.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Content(Snapshot)|The object's presentation is a picture of the contained<br>document (provided by the embedded object server<br>technology).|
|Icon(Icon)|The object's presentation is an icon.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_OLEDrawAspect) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.19 ST_OLELinkType (Embedded Object Alternate Image Request Types)

This simple type specifies the kind of image that shall be requested from the application which hosts embedded object data for a linked object. This simple type alllows any image format to be specified; however, the following values are reserved:

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|`Bitmap`|Specifies that a bitmap should be requested.|
|`EnhancedMetaFile`|Specifies that a metafile (non-raster) image should be<br>requested.|
|`Jpeg`|Specifies an image which should use the JPEG format.|
|`Picture`|Specifies that any image format can be requested.<br>[_Example_: PNG or CGM (ISO/IEC 8632)._end example_]|
|`Png`|Specifies an image which should use the Portable<br>Network Graphics format.|

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_OLELinkType) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.20 ST_OLEType ( Embedded Connection Type)

This simple type specifies whether the embedded object is included in the package (that is, embedded) or is stored outside the package (that is, linked).

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Embed(Embedded Object)|Embedded object.|
|Link(Linked Object)|Linked object.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_OLEType) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.21 ST_OLEUpdateMode ( Embedded Object Update Method Type)

This simple type specifies how an embedded object is updated.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|Always(Server Application Update)|The object is updated whenever the server application<br>using the embedded object indicates there is new data|

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
||available.|
|OnCall(User Update)|The object is updated when the user chooses to<br>update it.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_OLEUpdateMode) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.22 ST_RType (Rule Type)

This simple type specifies types of rules.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|align(Alignment Rule)|Alignment rule.|
|arc(Arc Rule)|Arc rule.|
|callout(Callout Rule)|Callout rule.|
|connector(Connector Rule)|Connector rule.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_RType) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## 19.2.3.23 ST_ScreenSize (Screen Sizes Type)

This simple type specifies screen sizes.

This simple type's contents are a restriction of the W3C XML Schema string datatype.

|**Enumeration Value**|**Description**|
|---|---|
|1024,768(1024x768 pixels)|1024x768 pixels.|
|1152,862(1152x862 pixels)|1152x862 pixels.|
|544,376(544x376 pixels)|544x376 pixels.|
|640,480(640x480 pixels)|640x480 pixels.|
|720,512(720x512 pixels)|720x512 pixels.|
|800,600(800x600 pixels)|800x600 pixels.|

[ _Note_ : The W3C XML Schema definition of this simple type’s content model (ST_ScreenSize) is located in §A.7.2. _end note_ ]

## **19.3 VML - WordprocessingML Drawing**

Within a WordprocessingML document, it is possible to include graphical VML objects. When these objects are present in a word processing document, it is necessary to include information about the object which is specific to their presence in a word processing document.

[ _Note_ : The VML format is a legacy format originally introduced with Office 2000 and is included and fully defined in ECMA-376 for backwards compatibility reasons. The DrawingML format is a newer and richer format created with the goal of eventually replacing any uses of VML in the Office Open XML formats. VML should be considered a transitional format included in Office Open XML for legacy reasons only and new applications that need a file format for drawings are strongly encouraged to use preferentially DrawingML. _end note_ ]

The VML WordprocessingML Drawing namespace acts in this capacity, specifying all information necessary to anchor and display VML objects within a word processing document.

All elements defined in this subclause shall only appear in a WordprocessingML document.

[ _Example_ : Consider a 5-point star added to a WordprocessingML document, for example:

This object allows surrounding text to wrap around its top and bottom, but not to either side, so this interaction with the surrounding document text (which is specific to a word processing document) is stored in the WordprocessingML Drawing namespace as follows:

```
<v:shape … >
```

```
  …
```

`<wd:wrap wd:type="topAndBottom" /> </v:shape>`

The wrap element specifies how surrounding WordprocessingML document content must wrap around the floating VML object - in this case, by wrapping to its top and bottom extents via the type attribute value of `topAndBottom` . _end example_ ]

**This subclause is informative.**
